THE OHIO STATE UNIVERSITY

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANK-
FORT, KENTUCKY , ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE FIFTH ,
AND ENDED ON WEDNESDAY , MARCH THE SEVEN-
TEENTH , 1932 .
CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section No. 2518 , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition , providing that a period of
five years instead of two years , shall be the time when an
action upon a merchant's accounts for goods , wares and mer-
chandise sold and delivered , or any article charged in such
store account , shall be commenced next after the cause
of action accrued .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1 . That Section Two Thousand Five Hundred
Eighteen ( 2518 ) , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of
1922 , be amended by striking out the word " Two " ,
fixing the number of years at Five years as the time
when an action upon a merchant's account for goods ,
wares and merchandise sold and delivered , or any
article charged in such store account , shall be com-
menced next after the cause of action accrued , andbyinserting in lieu thereof the word " Five " , so that
said section , when amended , shall be re-enacted and
read as follows :
"Merchant's Account : Action to be brought
within Five years ; Penalty for post -dating
account.-

2 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
" An action upon a merchant's account for goods ,
wares and merchandise sold and delivered , or any
article charged in such store account shall be com-
menced within five years next after the cause of
action accrued .
"In every action upon such merchant's account as
last above described , the limitation shall be com-
puted from the first day of January next succeeding
the respective dates or times of the delivery of the
several articles charged in the account ; and judg-
ment shall be rendered for no more than the amount
of such articles as were actually charged or delivered
within five years preceding that in which the action
was brought ; and if any merchant or trader shall
willfully postdate any article charged in such
account , or the receipt for the delivery thereof, or
cause it to be done he shall forfeit tenfold the
amount of such article postdated , to be credited to
the person against whom the account may be ; and
such credit shall be allowed such person in any
action on the account without any written pleadings
setting it up ."
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
3. This Act shall become effective from and
after the date of its passage .
Neither approved or disapproved .
CHAPTER 2 .
AN ACT concerning the paying out of money of this Common-
wealth for salaries , board bills , traveling expenses and any
and all other expenses incident to the performance of any
duty of each and every officer , agent , appointee and employee
of this Commonwealth , and requiring reports to be made each
month by the heads of departments , commissioners , chairmen
of boards and all other persons authorized by law to approve
accounts of subordinates for traveling expenses , board bills ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 3
salaries and all other expenses before the payment of same ,
and prohibiting the expenditure of any monies of this Com-
monwealth for purposes other than those expressly authorized
by law of this Commonwealth ; providing for the liability on
the bonds of the officers whose duty it is to fix and pay
salaries or expense accounts , and prescribing the punishment
for a violation of the provisions of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shall be the duty of the Auditor , Secretary
of State , Treasurer , Attorney General , Superin-
tendent of Public Instruction , of this Common-
wealth , the Chairman of the State Road Commis-
sion, the Chairman of the State Board of Health ,
the Chairman of the State Board of Charities and
Corrections , Superintendents of the Feeble Minded
Institutions , and State Asylums , Wardens of the
State Penitentiaries , Superintendent of the Insti-
tution for the Blind and School of Reform , State
Inspector and Examiner , State Geologist and all
other officers , whose duty it is or may be to fix or
approve salaries or expense accounts of himself or
any other person in the employment of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky to make , itemize , verify and
file with the Auditor of Public Accounts of Ken-
tucky and copy thereof with the State Inspector and
Examiner on or before the 10th day of each and
every month a complete report of all salaries , ex-
pense accounts , board bills , traveling expenses , the
names of the persons to whom the salary is paid ,
the amount paid, the names of the person expending
the money , the purpose for which it is expended and
the date thereof and the service shall be described
for which the salary was paid . Such report shall
cover the preceding month before the filing of the
report on the 10th day of the month . Provided , how-
ever , that the Superintendents of the Feeble Minded.

4 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Institute , of the Hospital for the Insane , of the
School of Reform , of the Reformatory and the
Warden of the Penitentiary may file the monthly
statement required by this act with the State Board
of Charities and Corrections which Board shall
transmit the same to the State Inspector and
Examiner .
§ 2. It shall be unlawful for any state official ,
agent , appointee , employee of this commonwealth to
expend or use any money belonging to this com-
monwealth for his personal use or for any purpose
not expressly authorized by law , and any person so
using the same shall be guilty of converting trust
funds to his own use , and shall be punished therefor
as now provided by law for such offense .
§3 . It shall be unlawful for any officer to pay to
any person any sum under the disguise of payment
of salary or to pay a salary to himself or another
person a salary for services not actually rendered ,
and any person thus offending shall be guilty of a
misdemeanor and subject to indictment in the Frank-
lin Circuit Court and upon conviction be fined for
such offense note less than $ 500.00 nor more than
$1,000.00 , or imprisonment not less than 3 months
nor more than 12 months , or both so fined and im-
prisoned , in the discretion of the court or jury .
§ 4. Any officer of this commonwealth authorizing
or causing any person to be paid any money in viola-
tion of this act shall be liable therefor to the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky on his bond as such officer
to the Commonwealth of Kentucky . And an action

In view of the widespread delinquency of taxes
due the various drainage districts throughout the
commonwealth and since there are at present numer-
ous drainage districts which will be unable to
retire outstanding drainage bonds at their respec-
tive maturities , and in view of the purpose of this
bill , namely , to extend the time in which such install-
ments of drainage taxes can be paid , an emergency
is hereby declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect upon its passage . " If any word , line or para-
graph of this bill shall be declared to be unconstitu-
tional then , the sense of the entire bill shall become
unconstitutional ."
Neither approved or disapproved .
CHAPTER 4.
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Chapter 4 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of 1930 , and Section 2380b -43 , Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to the maintenance of public
ditches .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
$ 1. That Chapter 4 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1930 be and the same is hereby repealed
and re -enacted , and as thus re -enacted shall read as
follows :
Except as herein otherwise provided , all improve-
ments , established or constructed under the provi-
sions of this Act , as well as all public ditches or
drains established or constructed under pre- existing
laws , shall be under the control and supervision of
the drainage commissioner of the county having
jurisdiction of the proceedings ordering the estab-
lishment and construction thereof . It shall be the

14 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
3
•
duty of said drainage commissioner to keep all such
levees , ditches , drains or like public improvements
under his charge , open , free from obstruction and
in good repair . To accomplish that end, the said
board of drainage commissioners is hereby author-
ized and empowered to employ men and teams , and
to lease , rent , hire or purchase machinery, and
employ men to operate same . For the purpose of
providing funds to pay the compensation herein
provided to the drainage commissioner , his agents ,
servants and employees , outside of that portion of
same which shall be provided for and paid out of
the original assessment on any drainage district , as
well as to pay the cost and expense of repairing ,
keeping open and looking after the various public
drains , ditches and like improvements under the
charge of said commissioner , the lands in each
drainage district , which may be established under
this act , as well as the lands assessed for each public
ditch or system of drainage established under pre-
existing laws , shall be and the same are hereby
authoried to be assessed , in proportion to the orig-
inal assessment made against them for the original
establishment and construction of such system of
drainage .
Provided that where a drainage district has been
established diverting the course of the water, the
drainage commissioner may in his discretion in
order to relieve lands taxed and from which water
has been diverted, use funds of the district in main-
taining the original watercourse .
Provided , however , that no assessment for such
purpose in any one year shall exceed the following
amounts to-wit : In counties where there are
seventy -five (75 ) or more drainage districts , having
a cost construction of one million dollars ($ 1,000 ,-
000 ) or more , ten per centum ( 10 % ) of the original

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 15
assessment against said land , and in all other coun-
ties not more than two per centum ( 2 % ) of the orig-
inal assessment against said land , unless consent
is first obtained in writing from the owners of two-
thirds of the land affected , and in no event shall
such assessment exceed ten per centum ( 10% ) of
the original assessment .
Provided , that no more than one assessment
shall be made in any one year . Provided , further ,
that no assessment shall be made until the drain-
age commissioner shall prepare an assessment roll ,
in duplicate, giving therein the name of the owner
or owners of the land to be affected , and the amount
proposed to be assessment (assessed ) against each
tract of land . Said assessment roll and the dupli-
cate thereof shall be signed by the drainage commis-
sioner and his secretary , and the original thereof
shall be kept on file in the office of the drainage com-
missioner and the duplicate , aforesaid , shall be filed
with the collector of said tax . Assessments made
pursuant hereto shall have the same force and effect ,
as an original assessment and be collected in the
same manner, shall be kept separate and deposited
with the treasurer , who shall keep the accounts for
each district or system separate , and all warrants
drawn shall specify out of which account it shall be
paid , and to which it shall be charged .
§ 2. Section 2380b -43 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed and re - enacted , so that said section , when so
re-enacted shall read as follows :
To maintain and preserve and ditches , sewers ,
drains , levees , pumping stations , power plants , or
other improvements made pursuant to this act , and
to strengthen , repair , and restore the same , when
needed , and for the purpose of defraying the cur-
rent expenses of the district , the board of drainage

16 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
commissioners may , upon the completion of said im-
provements , and on the first Monday of December
in each year thereafter , levy an assessment upon
each tract or parcel of land and other property
within the district to be known as a " maintenance
assessment ." Said maintenance assessment shall be
apportioned upon the basis of benefits assessed on
account of original construction , shall not exceed
ten per centum ( 10 % ) thereof in any one year , and
shall be certified to the sheriff or collector of the
revenue of each county in which lands of said dis-
trict are situated in the same book , in like manner ,
and at the same time as the annual installment of
assessment is certified , but in a separate column
under the heading " maintenance assessment ." Pro-
vided, however , that said assessment shall not in any
one year exceed two per centum ( 2 % ) of the original
cost of construction , unless consent be first obtained.
in writing from the owners of two -thirds of the lands
affected thereby. Said sheriff or collector shall
demand and collect the maintenance assessment and
make return thereof and shall receive the same
compensation therefor and be liable for the same
penalties for failure or neglect so to do as is pro-
vided herein for the annual installments of assess-
ments . The " maintenance assessment " shall be
due and payable at the same time each year that the
other assessments of the district due that year are
payable , shall bear the same penalties and shall be
collected at the same time , and shall be enforced in
the same manner .
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict
herewith are hereby repealed .
Neither approved or disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 17
CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT to regulate the license of animals for public service .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That no one shall be allowed to license and
stand for public service any bull or boar except of
pure breeding . Proof of this breeding must be
established by the owner by showing certificate of
registration in one of the herd books of one of the
known breeds or by a sworn affidavit that the animal
to be licensed is purebred and that the ancestry
of such purebred animal has been registered or
was eligible to registration in the records of one of
the known breeds . In the case of the establishment
of a new breed shall have been purebred for at
least six generations .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict with this
Act are hereby repealed .
This Act shall take effect and be in force on and
after January 1 , 1933 .
Neither approved or disapproved .
CHAPTER 6 .
AN ACT to regulate the distribution and sale , the selling or
exposing for sale , of agricultural sced ; defining same , pro-
viding for inspection thereof and fixing penalties for violation
of this act ; naming the enforcing agent , prescribing his duties
and empowering him to make rules and regulations necessary
to carry out the intent and meaning of this act ; to provide
means for carrying out its provisions ; and to repeal all laws
or parts of laws in conflict with this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the term " agricultural seeds " or
"agricultural seed , " as used in this Act , shall be

18 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
defined as the seeds of Canada bluegrass , Kentucky
bluegrass , brome grass , fescues , millets , tall
meadow oat grass , orchard grass , crested dogstail ,
redtop , Italian ryegrass , perennial ryegrass , grain
sorghums , sweet sorghums , Sudan grass , timothy ,
alfalfa , alsike clover , crimson clover , red clover ,
sweet clover, white clover , Canada field peas , Aus-
trian peas , cowpeas , soybeans , vetches , lespedeza
sp . , buckwheat , flax , hemp , tobacco , rape , seed pota-
toes barley, corn of all kinds , oats rye , wheat , let-
tuce , peas , tomatoes , beans , onions , cabbage , carrots ,
melons and other truck crop seeds , grasses , cereals
and legumes which are sold , offered or exposed for
sale within this State for seeding purposes .
Label Requirements of Agricultural Seeds .
§ 2. Every lot of agricultural seeds , as defined
in section one of this Act except as herein otherwise
provided , when in bulk , packages or other container
of one pound or more , shall have affixed thereto , in
a conspicuous place on the exterior of the container
of such agricultural seeds , a plainly written or
printed tag or label in the English language , in a
manner prescribed by the Director of the Kentucky
Agricultural Experiment Station or his agent ,
stating :
(a ) Commonly accepted name of such agricul-
tural seeds .
(b ) The approximate percentage by weight of
purity ; meaning , the freedom of such agricultural
seeds from inert matter and from, other seeds dis-
tinguishable by their appearance .
(c ) The approximate total percentage by weight
of weed seeds ; the term " weed seeds , " as herein
used , being defined as the noxious weed seeds listed
in Section 2 , subsection ( d ) and all seeds not listed
in Section 1 as agricultural seeds .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 19
(d ) The name and approximate number per
ounce of each kind or the seeds or bulblets of the
noxious weeds provided for in Section 2 , subsection
(d ) of this Act which are present per ounce of agri-
cultural seeds .
Noxious weed seeds or bulblets are defined as
the seeds or bulblets of quack -grass (Agropyron
repens ) , dodder ( Cuscuta sp . ) , sorrel ( Rumex ace-
tosella ) , buckhorn ( Plantago lanceolata ) , corn
cockle ( Agrostemma githago ) , wild onion ( Allium
vineals ) , Canada thistle ( Cirsium arvense ) , and
such other weeds as the Director of the Kentucky
Agricultural Experiment Station or his agent may
from time to time determine to be noxious and a
menace to the best interests of the agriculture of
Kentucky : Provided , however , that twelve months
notice shall be given to the seed trade before any
addition to the noxious weed seed list shall become
effective .
(e) The aproximate percentage of germination
of such agricultural pure seeds designated together
with the month and year said seeds were tested .
(f) Full name and address of the vendor of
such agricultural seed .
(g) Name of State where such seed was grown ,
and if this is unknown , a statement to that effect .
Label Requirements of Mixtures of Agricultural
Seeds .
§ 3. Mixtures of agricultural seeds which con-
tain two or more kinds of such seed each present
in excess of five per cent by weight of the total mix-
ture , when sold , offered or exposed for sale as mix-
tures in containers of more than one pound, shall
have affixed thereto , in a conspicuous place on the
exterior of the container of such mixtures of seeds ,
a plainly written or printed tag or label , in the Eng-
lish language , stating :

20 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(a) That such seed is a mixture .
(b ) The name and approximate percentage by
weight of each kind of agricultural seed present in
such mixture if in excess of five ( 5 ) per cent by
weight of the total mixture .
(c ) Approximate percentage by weight of total
weed seeds as defined in Section 2 -c of this Act .
(d ) The name and approximate number per
ounce of each kind of seeds or bulblets of the nox-
ious weeds listed in Section 2 , subsection ( d ) of this
Act which are present .
(e ) Approximate percentage of germination of
each kind of agricultural seed present in such mix-
ture if in excess of five ( 5 ) per cent by weight ,
together with the month and year said seed was
tested .
(f) Full name and address of vendor of such
mixture .
(g) Name of State where such seed was grown ,
and if this is unknown , a statement to that effect .
Label Requirements of Special Mixtures .
§ 4. Special mixtures of agricultural seeds
except as specified in Section 3 of this Act , when
sold , offered or exposed for sale as mixtures in bulk ,
packages or other containers in excess of one pound
shall have affixed thereto in a conspicious place on
the exterior of the container of such mixture a
plainly written or printed tag or label in the Eng-
lish language , stating :
( a ) That such a seed is a mixture .
(b ) The name of each kind of agricultural seed
which is present in proportion of five ( 5 ) per cent
or more of the total mixture .
(c) The approximate total percentage by weight
of weed seeds as defined in Section 2 -c of this Act .
( d) Approximate percentage by weight of inert
matter .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 21
( e ) The name and approximate number per
ounce of each kind of seeds or bulblets of noxious
weeds listed in Section 2 , subsection ( d ) of this Act ,
which are present .
(f) Full name and address of the vendor of such
mixture .
Certified Seed .
§ 5. Séeds , roots or tubers bearing the certified
tag of any Growers ' Association whose require-
ments for certification are in any regard lower than
those of the Kentucky Seed Improvement Associa-
tion , shall not be sold or offered for sale in the State
of Kentucky . Standards announced by the Secre-
tary in the printed regulations governing the Ken-
tucky Seed Improvement Association in June of
each year shall be those prevailing for that month
and the eleven succeeding months .
Exemptions .
§ 6. Agricultural seeds or mixtures of same
shall be exempt from the provisions of this Act :
(a ) When possessed , exposed for sale or sold
for food or feed purposes only.
(b) When sold to merchants to be recleaned
before being sold or exposed for sale for seeding
purposes .
(c) When in store for the purpose of recleaning
and not possessed , sold or offered for sale for seed-
ing purposes .
(d ) When the seed is grown , sold and delivered
by a producer on his own premises to the purchaser
himself. If, however , the seed is advertised for sale
by the public press , circular or catalogue , or if the
seed is delivered by a common carrier , the seller
shall be deemed a vendor and said seed and seller
shall be subject to all the provisions and require-
ments of this Act .

22 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(e) Lawn grass seed mixtures sold in quantities
of five pounds or less shall be exempt from para-
graph ( d ) of section two , paragraph (d ) section
three , and paragraph ( e ) of section four.
Duties and Authority of Enforcing Officer .
§ 7. The duty of enforcing this Act and carry-
ing out its provisions and requirements shall be
vested in the Director of the Kentucky Agricultural

sions of this Act , and with the approval of the Board
of Trustees of the University of Kentucky , the
Director of the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment
Station shall fix the salaries of said analysts , inspec-
tors and assistants . It shall be the duty of the
Director of the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment
Station or his agent at his discretion , to publish ,
or cause to be published , the results of the examina-
tion , analysis and tests of any and all samples of
agricultural seed or mixtures of such seed , drawn
as provided for in Section 9 , together with any other
information he may deem advisable . The Director
of the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station
or his agent , is empowered to seize any seed sold
in or offered for sale in Kentucky which is not prop-
erly labeled and hold same until proper labeling
is affected by the shipper or seller of such seed .
§ 8. The word " approximate " as used in this
Act shall be defined in the rules and regulations

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 23
provided for in this Act , by the Director of the
Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station or his
agent who shall be guided by the " Rules and
Methods of Testing " adopted and approved by the
Association of Official Seed Analysts of North
America .
Inspection , Sampling and Testing .
9. It shall be the duty of the Director through
his authorized agents , to inspect , examine and make
analysis of and test any agricultural seeds sold ,
offered or exposed for sale within this State for
seeding purposes within this State , at such time and
place , and to such extent as he may determine . The
Director , and his agents , shall have free access at
all reasonable hours to any premises or structures ,
or any vehicle , truck , elevator or steamship company
and he is hereby given authority in person or by his
agents , to take for analysis a sample of such agri-
cultural seeds . In case a sample upon test or analy-
sis , is found to fall below the statement on the tag
or label attached to the lot from which said sample
was secured , or to violate any of the provisions of
this Act, the vendor or consignee of said lot of seed
shall be notified and a copy of said notice mailed to
the person, firm or corporation whose tag or label
was found affixed thereto .
Violations Defined .
§ 10. It shall be unlawful for any person , firm
or corporation to sell , offer or expose for sale within
this State any agricultural seeds , or mixtures of
agricultural seeds , as defined in this Act , for seed-
ing purposes without complying with the require-
ments of this Act , or falsely to mark , label any
agricultural seeds , or to interfere in anyway with
the inspectors or assistants in discharge of the
duties herein named .
Violations and Prosecutions .

24 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 11. Every violation of the provisions of this
Act shall be deemed a misdemeanor punishable by
a fine of not less than $25.00 and not more than
$100.00 and if the Director of the Kentucky Agri-
cultural Experiment Station or his agent shall find
upon examination , analysis or test , that any person ,
firm or corporation has violated any of the provi-
sions of this Act , he or his authorized agent or agents.
may institute proceedings in a court of competent
jurisdiction to have such person , firm , or corpora-
tion convicted therefor , or the Director or his agent
at his discretion may report the results of such
examination to the Attorney General of Kentucky
together with the sworn statement of the analyst ,
duly acknowledged , and such other evidence of said
violation as he shall deem necessary . Provided ,
however , that no prosecutions under this Act , shall
be instituted except in the manner following : When
the Director of the Kentucky Agricultural Experi-
ment Station or his agent finds that the Act has
been violated , he shall give notice to the person or
firm in whose hands the seed was found , designat-
ing a time and place for a hearing . This hearing
shall be private and the person or firm involved
shall have the right to introduce evidence either in
person , or by agent , or attorney . If, after said hear-
ing , or without such hearing , in case said person
fails or refuses to appear , the said Director or his
agent decides that the evidence warrants prosecu-
tion , he shall proceed as herein provided .
It shall be the duty of the Attorney General of
Kentucky or at his discretion he may act through
the Attorney of the Commonwealth of the County
or District or City in which said violation has oc-
curred , to institute proceedings at once in any court
of competent jurisdiction against the person , or per-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 25
sons , firms or corporations charged with such viola-
tion .
In any prosecutions arising from this Act a
certified analysis or test by the Kentucky Agricul-
tural Experiment Station shall be considered as
competent evidence in any court of this State in any
proceedings instituted under this Act , but upon
motion of the accused , the analyst whose signature
is attached to the certificate of analysis shall be re-
quired to appear as a witness and be subject to
cross - examination , or as a witness for the accused .
Provisions for Free Tests .
§ 12. Any citizen of this State shall have the
privilege of submitting to the Kentucky Agricul-
tural Experiment Station samples of agricultural
seed for test and analysis , subject to such rules and
regulations as may be adopted by said Director of
the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station .
The Director may by such regulations fix the maxi-
mum number of samples that may be tested free of
charge for any one citizen in any one period of time
and fix charges for tests of samples submitted in
excess of those tested free of charge . In the case of
nonresidents of Kentucky , a fee for the test of puri-
ty and for germination shall be paid in advance to
the Director of the Kentucky Agricultural Experi-
ment Station , said fees to be fixed by the Director
or his agent in accordance with rules and regula-
tions set forth in Section 7 of the Act . All
moneys received from such fees shall be paid over
to the Treasurer of the Board of Control of said
Station to be used in defraying the expenses of seed
analysis .
§ 13. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
inspection and analysis of agricultural seeds , the
tags or labels specified in sections 2 , 3 and 4 of this
Act must be purchased from the Director of the

26 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station or his
agent who shall receive 8 cents each for 100 pound
tags or labels , 6 cents each for 75 pound tags or
labels , 4 cents each for 50 pound tags or labels and
2 cents each for 25 pound tags or labels for alfalfa ,
sweet clover , and all grass and clover seeds or mix-
tures of any of these , and 4 cents each for 100 pound
tags or labels , 3 cents each for 75 pound tags or
labels , 2 cents each for 50 pound tags or labes , 1
cent each for 25 pound tags or labels , for all other
agricultural seeds included in this Act. Money col-
lected by the Director of the Kentucky Agricultural
Experiment Station or his agent from the sale of
tags shall be paid into the treasury of the State of
Kentucky and paid out on vouchers drawn by the
Director of the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment
Station , who shall , so much as may be necessary to
expend the same in meeting the necessary expenses
in carrying out the provisions of this act including
the employment of inspectors , analysts , and other
assistants . Any unexpended remainder thereof
shall revert to the general fund of the State of Ken-
tucky .
§ 14. The tag or label specified in sections 2 , 3
and 4 of this act must be delivered to the purchaser
with every sale of agricultural seed as defined in
this act if said sale amounts to one pound or more
whether said sale is in bulk or in package .
15. If any section or provision of the Act
shall be held invalid the remaining sections and pro-
visions shall not thereby be affected , showing a leg-
islative intention of adopting the Act by sections
as well as a whole .
§ 16. An Act , entitled An Act to regulate the
sale of Agricultural and other seeds , enacted by the
General Assembly at its regular session of 1916 , and

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 27
any other laws or parts of laws in conflict with this
Act, are hereby repealed .
17. This Act shall be effective on and after
July 1 , 1932 .
Neither approved or disapproved .
CHAPTER 7 .
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Chapter 107 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
enacted at the 1926 session , same being Sections 165-1 to
165-8 , inclusive , of Baldwin's 1930 Supplement to Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition , and also repealing and re-
enacting Chapter 11 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1930 , same being Sec-
tions 165-9 to 165-24 , inclusive , Baldwin's 1931 Kentucky Stat-
ute Service , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes . Said Acts hereby
repealed relate to the creation of an Air Board for Kentucky ;
provide the number , qualifications , term , powers and other
matters relating to said Board ; provide for the creation of
airports , buildings and facilities ; provide for the licensing
and registration of air men and aircrafts ; authorizing the
members of said Board to employ the necessary personnel ,
and to empower the Air Board of Kentucky to enforce laws
relating to aviation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 107 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , en-
acted at the 1926 session , same being sections 165-1
to 165-8 , inclusive , of Baldwin's 1930 Supplement
to the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition ,
and also Chapter 11 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky of
1930 , same being Sections 165-9 to 165-24 , inclusive ,
Baldwin's 1931 Kentucky Statute Service , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , be and the same are hereby
repealed and re -enacted so that as re-enacted said
laws shall read as follows :

28 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Section 1. There is hereby created a body cor-
porate to be known as the Air Board of Kentucky ,
the members of which shall be the Governor , Lieu-
tenant Governor , the Adjutant General , and two
other members to be appointed by the Governor with
the following qualifications :
(a ) Holding office at the pleasure of the Gover-
nor .
(b ) Male , white , and at least twenty -five ( 25 )
years of age .
Said Board may , in its corporate name , sue and
be sued , contract and be contracted with , acquire
real and personal property by gift , purchase or con-
demnation ; and may do all things and exercise all
rights and powers reasonably necessary to carry
out and perform the duties required by this Act .
The members of this Board shall serve without
compensation when attending meetings of the Board
but shall be allowed reasonable expenses so in-
curred . The members shall be allowed reasonable ex-
penses and a per diem of five dollars per day when
upon duty in connection with the business of the
said Board .
Said Board shall hold regular meetings at the
State Capitol at Frankfort Kentucky , on the first
Mondays in January and July of each year and
special meetings at the call of the Governor , after
due notice . A majority of the members shall con-
stitute a quorum for the consideration of business
but an affirmative vote of the majority of all mem-
bers shall be required for all contracts and agree-
ments creating liabilities or indebtedness .
In the absence of the Governor or Adjutant Gen-
eral the Lieutenant Governor or Assistant Adjutant
General may act for them respectively .
§ 2. This Air Board shall provide air ports and
air routes together with the necessary equipment ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 29
markings , personnel , etc. , for the State of Kentucky
as best suits the interests and future of aviation ,
both commercial and military , and for such pur-
poses shall be charged with the administration and
expenditure of the Air Fund provided for by the
biennial appropriation act .
§ 3. The Air Board may , either alone or in coop-
eration with counties , cities , or incorporated towns ,
private corporations , voluntary unincorporated
associations or individuals , construct or acquire by
purchase , contract , lease , donation or condemnation ,
air ports , buildings and grounds suitable for air
port purposes and the proper safeguards to flying
and to make additions and improvements in or to
such air ports and facilities and either alone or with
like cooperation of others provide personnel , heat ,
light , water , fuel , telephone service , drainage , run-
ways , fueling facilities , and other costs of operation.
and maintenance , including insurance , and further
to bear the expenses of removal or change of such
obstructions as shall exist to menace air travel .
§ 4. The Air Board of Kentucky shall be
attached to and be a part of the Military Depart-
ment of Kentucky . In addition to his other duties
the Adjutant General of Kentucky shall be the exec-
utive officer of the Department of Aviation , and
shall be responsible to the Governor for the proper
functioning of said Department , and shall audit and
approve any vouchers issued against the funds of
the Air Board before same shall be allowed by the
Auditor .
§ 5. In this Act " aircraft " means any contriv-
ance now known or hereafter invented , used , or
designed for navigation of or flight in the air , ex-
cept a parachute or other contrivance designed for
such navigation, but used primarily as safety

30 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
equipment . "Operating aircraft " means perform-
ing the services of aircraft pilot .
§ 6. The public safety requiring and the advan-
tages of uniform regulation making it desirable in
the interest of aeronautical progress that aircraft
operating within the State should conform with
respect to design , construction and airworthiness
to the standards prescribed by the United States
Government with respect to navigation of aircraft
subject to its jurisdiction , it shall be unlawful for
any person to navigate an aircraft within the State
unless it is licensed and registered by the Depart-
ment of Commerce of the United States in the man-
ner prescribed by the lawful rules and regulations
of the United States Government then in force .
§ 7. The public safety requiring and the advan-
tages of uniform regulation making it desirable in
the interest of aeronautical progress that person
engaging within this State in navigating the aircraft
described in Section 6 hereof in any form of naviga-
tion for which license to operate such aircraft would
be required by the United States Government shall
have the qualifications necesary for obtaining and
holding the class of license required by the United
States Government . It shall be unlawful for any
person to engage in operating such aircraft within
this State in any form of navigation unless he shall
have such license .
§ 8. The certificate of the license herein required
shall be kept in the personal possession of the
licensee when he is operating aircraft within this
State , and must be presented for inspection upon
the demand of any passenger , any peace officer of
this State , or any official , manager or person in
charge of any airport or landing field in this State
upon which he shall land .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 31
$ 9. The public safety requiring and the advan-
tages of uniform regulation making it desirable , it
shall be unlawful for any person to operate any air-
craft within this State in any form of navigation
whatsoever in violation of the air traffic rules pro-
mulgated by the Air Board of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky as hereinafter authorized .
§ 10. The Air Board of Kentucky shall, from time
to time , promulgate air traffic rules applicable to the
operation of aircraft within the Commonwealth ;
Provided , however , that such air traffic rules shall
be identical with the air traffic rules adopted by the
United States Government as nearly as may be and
in so far as same are applicable ; which said air
traffic rules shall be maintained in current condi-
tion by likewise promulgating any amendments
which may be made effective by the United States
Government .
§ 11. All aircraft and aircraft pilots operating in
the State of Kentucky shall be registered with the
Air Board of Kentucky in such a manner and such
detail as may be prescribed by said Board from time
to time. A period not to exceed thirty days after
the beginning of operation shall be allowed for such
registration , provided , however , that no pilot or
aircraft shall operate for hire until such application
for registration has been made . And provided
further that this shall not apply to transient pilots
and aircraft , unless such pilots and aircraft are
engaged in temporary exhibtions and carrying
passengers for hire from a base within the limts of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , nor to pilots and
aircraft operating in interstate commerce and not
using a point in Kentucky as a base for any opera-
tion . Registration fees for the registration pro-
vided for in this section shall be charged for at the
rate of five dollars ( $ 5.00 ) for each aircraft operated

32 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
in this State , either for hire or otherwise . A
registraton fee of five dollars ( $ 5.00 ) shall be
charged for all aircraft pilots operating in this
State. The registration of aircraft and aircraft
pilots shall be caused to be made annually . The
certificate of registration , as required in this sec-
tion , shall be displayed in a conspicuous place on the
aircraft or carried in the personal possession of the
pilot , as the case may be.
12. All fees collected from the registration of
pilots and aircraft under the provisions of this Act
shall be paid into the treasury of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky for the benefit of the General Expendi-
ture Fund , immediately upon receipts thereof .
13. The Air Board of Kentucky shall cause to
be inspected all air domes and landing fields of
whatsoever nature within the limits of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky for the purpose of determining
the safety and adequateness of such facilities for
the operation of aircraft . It shall be unlawful for
any person , firm or corporation to operate any air-
craft for hire from any airport or landing field of
any nature whatsoever if it has been declared unfit
for such operation by the Air Board of Kentucky .
§ 14. Nothing in this Act shall apply to public
aircraft of the Federal Government , or of a State ,
territory , or possession , or of a political subdivision
thereof, or to aircraft licenses by a foreign country
with which the United States has a reciprocal agree-
ment covering the operation of such licenses
(licensed ) aircraft .
§ 15. Any person who violates any provision
of this Act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
upon conviction shall be punished by a fine of not
more than one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) or by im-
prisonment for not more than ninety days , or both ;
Provided , however , that acts or omissions made un-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 33
lawful by this Act shall not be deemed to include
any act or omission which violates the laws or lawful
regulations of the United States , but it shall not be
necessary to allege or prove , as part of the case for
the people , that the defendant is not amenable , on
account of the alleged violation , to prosecution under
the laws of the United States . That he is amenable
to such prosecution shall be matter of defense , un-
less it affirmatively appear from the evidence
adduced by the people .
§ 16. It shall be unlawful for any person , firm or
corporation to navigate , or cause to be navigated ,
aircraft within this State except in conformity with
the rules and regulations laid down by the Air Board
of Kentucky and in conformity with this Act .
17. Any person who shall, without the consent
of the owner , go upon or enter , tamper with , or in
any way damage or injure any aircraft , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and shall be punished con-
sistent with the laws of the State .
§ 18. The Air Board of . Kentucky is hereby em-
powered to employ a reasonable and necessary
personnel , fix their compensation and prescribe their
duties . To administer the provisions of this Act
and for such purpose , the Board is authorized to
make such regulations as are necessary to execute
the functions vested in it by this Act .
$ 19. Should any section of this Act be found in
conflict with the Constitution of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , such unconstitutionality shall not
affect any other section herein .
§ 20. There being an urgent need for the promo-
tion of aviation in Kentucky and it being considered
that this Act should be placed in effect as soon as
possible , an emergency is hereby declared , and this
Act shall take effect from and after its passage as
required by law . Neither approved or disapproved .
Acts- 2

34 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 8.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Chapter 29 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1928
approved March 20 , 1928 , which was :
"An Act relating to fines and forfeitures in courts inferior
to the circuit court except police courts and fixing the per
centum of such fines and forfeitures to be paid to the county
and Commonwealth's Attorneys, respectively , and repealing
124 and 133 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1915 edition , and
section 2554a -43 , Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Supplement to
Carroll's 1922 edition , being section 43 of Chapter 33 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1922 , to the extent only of
the conflict between this act and of said sections ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 29 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
approved March 20 , 1928 be amended by striking
from the first literary paragraph thereof as same
appears on page 162 of the published acts of the
1928 General Assembly the words , " where actually
present and assisting in such prosecution , " and re-
enacted so that when amended and re -enacted said
Chapter 29 of the Acts of the General Assembly
passed at its 1928 session will read as follows :
" From all fines and forfeitures recovered in all
prsecutions before any county judge or magistrate ,
the county attorney shall receive from the treasurer
forty ( 40 % ) per centum of such fines and forfei-
tures (and the Commonwealth's attorney shall re-
ceive forty ( 40 % ) per centum of such fines and
forfeitures ) when recovered and paid into the treas-
ury , as provided by law , and provided , however ,
that if the judgment be for twenty-five ( $ 25.00 )
dollars or less , exclusive of costs , the county attor-
ney shall receive a fee of five ( $5 ) dollars which
shall be taxed as cost and belong to him.

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 35
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith and
especially Sections 124 and 133 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1915 edition, and section 2554a -43 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Baldwin's 1926 Supplement , being
section 43 of Chapter 33 , Acts of the General Assem-
bly of 1922 , hereby are repealed to the extent only
of the conflict between this act and of said sections . "
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 9.
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact section 124 Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to the Common-
wealth's Attorney , his fees and salary .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Commonwealth's attorney shall receive
from the State Treasury ( $ 500.00 ) five hundred
dollars in the payment of his salary as now provided
by law , and he shall receive in addition thereto
thirty five hundred ( $ 3,500.00 ) dollars per annum
out of the judgments for fines and forfeitures ren-
dered in favor of the commonwealth in the several
courts of his district and this shall be in lieu of all
taxed fees and perquisites ; the percentums of fines
and forfeiures . He shall be paid per the month of
such fines and forfeitures the proportion thereof
equal to all judgments for fines and forfeitures ren-
dered in favor of the commonwealth in the several
courts of his district per the month , and if at the
end of any year the judgments for fines and for-
feitures rendered in favor of the commonwealth in
the several counties in his district do not amount
to the sum of $3,500.00 per annum , and for the next
succeeding year there are rendered judgments for

36 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
fines and forfeitures in favor of the commonwealth
in the several courts in his district a sum during
such succeeding year in excess of the $ 3,500.00 for
that year there shall be paid to him out of such ex-
cess , if sufficient , a sum necessary to bring his com-
pensation from fines and forfeitures for the pre-
ceding year to the sum of $3,500.00 . The provisions
of this act shall in no wise affect any percentum of
judgments for fines and forfeitures now allowed by
law to any other officers of any county . This Act
shall not apply to Commonwealth's attorneys now in
office .
All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 10 .
AN ACT entitled an Act to authorize receivers , liquidating
agents and special Deputy Banking Commissioners of closed
and insolvent banks to borrow money and to secure same
pledge the assets of such bank or banks for the purpose of
making distribution to depositors , or to re -organize or re-
open such bank or banks .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kenucky :
§ 1. That receivers , liquidating agents and special
Deputy Banking Commissioners of closed and in-
solvent banks , by and with the consent and approval
of the State Banking Commissioner , are hereby
authorized and empowered to borrow money from
the Reconstruction Finance Corporation , the
National Credit Corporation , or any other agency
set up by the Government of the United States for
the purpose of lending money or from any person
or corporation , and to secure such money so bor-
rowed to pledge the assets of such bank or banks in

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 37
any amount for the purpose of facilitating liquida-
tion and making distribution to depositors , or for
the purpose of re - organizing or reopening such bank
or banks if, in the judgment of such receivers , liqui-
dating agents , special Deputy Banking Commis-
sioners and with the aproval of the Banking Com-
missioner , it is deemed prudent and advisable to
reorganize or re -open such bank or banks .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
§ 3. Whereas , on account of the widespread busi-
ness depression resulting in the closing of banks
with consequent distress to depositors and stock-
holders , an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
and this Act shall take effect from and after its
passage and upon its approval by the Governor .
Approved March 11 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 11 ,
AN ACT to amend and re -enact an Act of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1932 known as House
Bill No. 245 , approved by the Governor on February 2, 1932 ,
and entitled "An Act to amend and re -enact Section 4693 .
Carroll's Statutes , relating to depositories ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an Act of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky of 1932 known as
House Bill No. 245 , approved by the Governor on
February 2 , 1932 , and entitled " An Act to amend
and re -enact Section 4693 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , relating to depositories " be amended and re-
enacted so that , when amended and re -enacted , said
Act aproved February 2 , 1932 , and said Section
4693 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes Baldwin's 1930
Edition , shall read as follows :

38 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
" Before any bank shall be named as depository ,
or permitted to receive public funds , said bank shall
execute to the Commonwealth of Kentucky a bond ,
with good surety , worth severally or jointly , not
less than One Hundred Thousand Dollars ( $100 , -
000.00 ) to be aproved by the Treasurer and Gover-
nor , conditioned that said bank will pay over , when
demanded , all money deposited with it by the Treas-
urer , and otherwise faithfully comply with the re-
quirements of the law relating to it as a depository.
The bond shall be filed with and be recorded by the
Secretary of State , and shall be renewed at least
once in every two years and oftener if required by
the Treasurer . Or , in lieu of the bond herein pro-
vided for , said bank may pledge with the State
Treasurer , to secure said deposit , and for that pur-
pose the State Treasurer may accept , warrants
issued by the Auditor of Public Accounts against
the State Treasurer and/or bonds of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , including Bridge Revenue
Bonds , and/or such government bonds as are direct
obligations of the United States Government , pro-
vided that such warrants and/or bonds shall have
a total face value in an amount at least ten per
centum (10 % ) in excess of said deposit and pro-
vided further that the State Treasurer shall at all
times require such pledge of State warrants and/or
bonds of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , including
Bridge Revenue Bonds and/or United States bonds
as will have a currently quoted market value at least
equal to said deposit and for that purpose the Treas-
urer and Governor shall from time to time call for
additional security of such warrants or such bonds ,
as current market values may require . A written
receipt for which warrants and/or bonds so pledged
shall be delivered by the Treasurer to the depository
pledging same . And the Commonwealth of Ken-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 39
tucky shall be liable to said bank for the safe and/or
bonds of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , including
Bridge Revenue Bonds , keeping of State warrants
and/or United States bonds pledged under this Act ,
while so pledged , and to enforce said liability said
bank is hereby granted permission to maintain an
action against the Commonwealth in the Franklin
Circuit Court from the judgment of which court an
appeal by either party shall lie to the Court of
Appeals as in other cases provided . The execution
of this bond , for the deposit of the public funds in
banks named as depositories , or the pledge of and/or
bonds of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , including
Bridge Revenue Bonds, said State warrants and/or
Government bonds shall not in any wise diminish
the liability of the Treasurer and his sureties upon
his bond ; nor shall the execution of said bond nor
the pledge hereunder impair or delay the right of
the Commonwealth to recover on the Treasurer's
bond for any loss or misapplication of the public
funds or other delinquency in office . Neither shall
the execution of the bond by the bank nor said pledge
impair or delay in any way the right of the Com-
monwealth to recover from any delinquent or de-
faulting bank , or the officers or stockholders thereof ,
in the same manner as other depositories ."
All laws or parts of laws inconsistent with this
Ast are hereby repealed .
Whereas , because of the current financial strin-
gency and the practically insuperable difficulties now
encountered by State depository banks in securing
depository bonds , a flight of capital from the State ,
to the irreparable injury of agriculture and industry
is imminent , an emergency is declared to exist and
this bill shall take effect upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved March 28 , 1932 .

40 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 12 .
AN ACT to amend Section 861 of Chapter 144 of the Acts of
1918 as amended by the Acts of 1928 ; to amend Section 865
of Chapter 144 of the Acts of 1918 as amended by Acts of
1926 ; and to amend Section 874 -b of chapter 144 of the Acts
of 1918 ; being respectively Sections 861 , 865 and 874 -b of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , relating to building and
loan associations and providing for the purchase of stock and
bonds in Federal Home Loan Banks ; the borrowing of money
therefrom ; authorizing the State Banking Commissioner to
permit examination of the records of his office concerning
building and loan associations by such banks and providing
that building and loan associations of this State may do
business with Federal Home Loan Banks . Also to amend
Sections 868 and 868 -a of Chapter 144 of the Acts of 1918 ,

of 1930 , relating to profits and losses of building and loan

861 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , be and
the same is hereby amended by striking out the

vided , that any building association operating under
the provisions of this act may borrow of any
Federal Home Loan Bank or Banks such amounts
as may be permitted by the laws and rules regulat-
ing such banks , and for such length of time , and at
such rate of interest as may be permitted by such
banks , and the associations may pledge and assign
its notes and mortgages as security for such loans ,
and provided further that the debt limit fixed by
this section shall not be applicable to loans from such
banks and such loans may be in such amounts as may

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 41
1
be allowed by the laws and regulations governing
or established by such banks " , so that the same ,

" The directors shall have power to borrow money

in ; and that thereafter , the amount of such indebted-

bers , pass and record a resolution to that effect .
Provided , that any building association operating

bank, and the association may pledge and assign its

notes and mortgages as security for such loans , and

ing or established by such banks . "
§ 2. That Section 865 of Chapter 144 of the Acts
of 1918 as amended by the Acts of 1926 , being Sec-
tion 865 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes of 1930 , be

vest their funds in the stock of any Home Loan

by the laws and regulations governing said bank ”

laws . Each member , whose application for a loạn
has been approved , shall be entitled upon giving

mains after the weekly or monthly loans are granted
the directors may invest the same in good and safe
bonds or in such other securities in which banks are

tures of any Home Loan Bank established by Act

ing under this act may subscribe for and invest

§ 3. That Section 874 -b of Chapter 144 of the
Acts of 1918 , being Section 874 -b of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes of 1930 , be and the same is hereby
amended by adding thereto the following words ,
to -wit : " All reports and information in the hands of
the Banking Commissioner of and concerning build-
ing and loan associations operating under this act
shall be subject to inspection by the Federal Home
Loan Bank and Federal Home Loan Bank Board
and the duly authorized representative of either of

" The affairs of every domestic building and loan

sary, but at least once in every year , and without

and such examiner shall have the power and shall
make a thorough examination into the condition ,
workings and affairs generally of such association ,
and he shall have the power to examine any officer ,
agent or employee of such association or any other
person under oath touching the affairs thereof, and
for that purpose shall have the power to administer

such association shall be subject to his inspection.
-Said examiner shall make a report of his findings
and file the same in the office of the banking commis-

44 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
sioner and the said banking commissioner shall
furnish a copy of such report to the association
examined . Said examiner shall report any viola-
tion of law or any unauthorized or unfit practices or
any failure to keep and have correct accounts of
business of such association , and if he finds that any
director has willfully neglected to attend meetings
regularly , he shall recommend the discharge of such

All reports and information in the hands of the
Banking Commissioner of and concerning building
and loan associations operating under this act shall
be subject to inspection by the Federal Home Loan
Bank and Federal Home Loan Bank Board and the
duly authorized representative of either of them . "
4. That Section 868 of Acts of 1918 and being

relating to profits and losses of building and loan

by striking therefrom the following words in the

losses from sale of real estate as may be charged to

said Section , as amended shall read as follows :
" Dividends shall be declared , credited or paid on
stock in proportion to the amount paid in on such
stock , quarterly , semi -annually or annually, as the
by-laws may provide , but no dividends shall be
declared , credited or paid by any association , except
out of net profits collected , after deducting from
the earnings all expenses of operation and all losses
sustained , except such losses from sale of real
estate as may be charged to reserve fund as pro-
vided in Section 868 - a ."
§ 5. That Section 868 -a of Acts of 1918 , and
being Section 868 -a of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 45
of 1930 and relating to establishment of contingent
or reserve fund or building and loan associations be

" Provided further that any and all losses from
sale of real estate may be charged against this fund ,
and in the event of any such charges , then any and
all profits from sale of real estate shall , to the extent
of losses charged , be credited to said fund , " so that
said Section , as amended , shall read as follows :
"Every association shall set aside from the gross
profits at least one per cent thereof each year as a
sinking fund to provide against contingent losses
until the total amount of the fund so set aside shall
equal five ( 5 ) per cent of the assets of such associa-
tion , but this sinking fund shall in no case exceed ten
per centum of the assets of the association .
Provided , however , that no association shall be
required to reduce its dividend below five per cent
for that purpose.
Provided , further , that any and all losses from
sale of real estate may be charged against this fund
and in the event of any such charges , then any and
all profits from sale of real estate shall , to the extent
of losses charged , be credited to said fund . "
§ 6. Whereas , a bill is now pending in Congress
to create Federal Home Loan Banks , and´
Whereas , in view of the present financial strin-
gency it is desirable that Kentucky Building and
Loan Associations be empowered to obtain the privi-
leges and advantages to be derived from becoming
members of said Federal Home Loan Banks ;
Now , therefore , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this act shall become a law upon its
approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 13 .
AN ACT amending Section 579 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , relating to banks and banking , and empowering
banks , trust companies , and combined banks and trust com-
panies to pledge certain assets as security for public funds of
the United States , State , municipal , county , public schools ,
taxing districts , and other public funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 579 Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended so that said section as amended shall read
as follows : When the articles are filed and recorded
as provided by law it may commence business and
shall thereupon become a body corporate , and to be
known by and carry on its business in its corporate
name , and as such shall have power to adopt and use
a corporate seal ; to make contracts ; sue and be
sued ; to appoint , remove and elect officers , define
their duties and require from any of them a bond
for the faithful discharge of their duties , to pre-
scribe , by its board of directors , by -laws for the gov-
ernment of the bank not inconsistent with law ; to
exercise , subject to law, such powers as may be
necessary to carry on the business of banking by
discounting and negotiating notes , drafts , bills of
exchange , and other evidences of debt , and purchas-
ing bonds , receiving deposits , and allowing interest
thereon , buying and selling exchange , coin and
bullion , and lending money on personal or real
security, as provided in this article . Any bank or
trust company may accept for payment at a future
date drafts or bills of exchange drawn upon it by its
customers and issue letters of credit authorizing the
holders thereof to draw drafts upon it or its corre-
spondents at sight or on time , not exceeding one
year , and may also accept drafts or bills of exchange

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 47
drawn upon it, having not more than six months '
sight to run , growing out of transactions involving
the importation and exportation of goods , and any
bank or trust company may discount acceptances
which are based upon the importation or exporta-
tion of goods and which have a maturity at time of
discount of not more than three months and are
endorsed by at least one other bank or trust com-
pany, but no bank or trust company shall accept
such drafts or bills of exchange to an amount equal
at any time in the aggregate to more than one-half of
its paid up and unimpaired capital stock and surplus ,
except by authority of the banking commissioner
under such general regulations as said commissioner
may prescribe, and in no event to an amount exceed-
ing the capital stock and surplus of such bank or
trust company ; and such regulations shall apply to
all banks or trust companies alike , regardless of the
amount of capital stock and surplus . In addition
to the powers heretofore conferred upon and now
possessed by banks , trust companies , and combined
banks and trust companies doing business under the
laws of this State , they are hereby authorized and
empowered , subject to charter limitations , to pledge
such portions of their assets as may be required as
collateral security for the deposit and/or deposits
of public funds deposited with them , or any of them ,
by or under the authority of the United States , the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , or any county , munici-
pality , public school district , or other taxing district
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or by any
tax collected or other person having the legal
custody or control of any such public fund or funds .
§ 2. All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith ,
to the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .
3. On account of the financial conditions gen-
erally and of the difficulty in obtaining security and

48 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
protection for the deposit of public funds , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this act shall become
effective immediately upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Approved March 11 , 1932 .

pose of paying all salaries , printing , stamps , supplies and

partment , all the fees realized from examinations of the

repealed .

for the use of the Department of Banking for the
purpose of paying the salaries , printing , stamps ,
supplies , and other expenses of operation and main-
tenance of said Banking Department , all the fees
heretofore or hereafter realized from examination
of sundry banks and building and loan associations
as now provided by law .
$§ 3. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
§ 4. Due to the closing and suspension of a large
number of Banks in the Commonwealth thereby

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 49
increasing the labors of the Banking Department
and requiring more assistance , an emergency is

monwealth of Kentucky and approval by the Gover-
