If any issuer shall refuse to permit an examina-
tion to be made by the commissioner , it shall be
proper ground for cancellation of registration .
If any commissioner shall deem it necessary he
may enter an order suspending the right to sell
securities pending an investigation ; provided , that
the order shall state the commissioner's grounds
for taking such action .
Notice of the entry of such order shall be given
personally or by telephone , telegraph or mail to the
issuer and every registered dealer who shall have
notified the commissioner of an intention to sell such
security .
34. Registration of Dealers and Salesmen . No
dealer or salesman shall engage in business in this
state as such dealer or salesman or sell any securi-
ties including securities exempted in section twenty-
five of this act , except in transactions exempt under
section twenty -six of this act , unless he has been
registered as a dealer or salesman in the office of

96 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the commissioner pursuant to the provisions of this
section .
Every dealer subject to the provisions of this Act
before engaging in business in this state shall file
in the office of the commissioner an application for
registration in writing in such form as the commis-
sioner may prescribe , duly verified by oath , which
shall state the principal office of the applicant ,
wherever situated , and the location of the principal
office and all branch offices in this state , if any , the
name or style of doing business , the names , residence
and business address of all persons interested in the
business as principals , copartners , officers and
directors , specifying as to each his capacity and title ,
the general plan and character of business and
length of time the dealer has been engaged in busi-
ness . The commissioner may also require such addi-
tional information as toto applicant's previous
history , record and association , as he may deem
necessary to establish the good repute in business
of the applicant .
Every such dealer shall file with his application
an irrevocable written consent to the service of proc-
ess upon the Banking and Securities Commissioner
in actions against such dealer in manner and form as
hereinabove provided in section thirty -two.
Every such dealer shall also file with his applica-
tion a bond in the sum of five thousand dollars
($5,000 ) running to the Commonwealth of Kentucky
in such form as the commissioner may designate ,
such bond to be conditioned upon the faithful com-
pliance with the provisions of this act by said dealer
and by all salesmen registered by him . Such bond
shall be executed as surety by a surety company
having a net worth of not less than $1,000,000 and
authorized to do business in this state . Provided ,
however , that upon the bond required of any bank ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 97
trust company , real estate mortgage company or
title company organized and doing business under
the laws of this state or any national bank situated
therein , its capital stock shall be taken and con-
sidered as the only security required for the faith-
ful performance of its duties . If the commissioner
shall find that the applicant is of good repute and
has complied with the provisions of this section , in-
cluding the payment of the fee hereinafter pro-
vided , he shall register such applicant as a dealer .
Provided that banks , trust companies and mortgage
or other companies that may become dealers under
this act may , when required to execute as dealers
the bond required therein, execute said bond without
surety , when by action of the board of directors the
capital stock is authorized to be pledged as surety
and the capital stock of banks , trust and mortgage
companies is hereby authorized to be so pledged ;
and when so pledged no other surety shall be re-
quired .
Upon the written application of a registered
dealer and general satisfactory showing as to good
character and the payment of the proper fee the
commissioner shall register as salesman of such
dealer such natural persons as the dealer may re-
quest . No dealer or salesman shall sell or offer to
sell any security the sale of which has not been
authorized by the commissioner and the commis-
sioner shall deliver to each dealer and salesman a
license prepared and printed in such form as he
shall prescribe .
The names and addresses of all persons approved
for registration as dealers or salesmen and
all
orders with respect thereto shall be recorded
in
a
Register
ofDealers and Salesmen
, kept
inthe office
ofthe commissioner
, which shall be open
topublic
inspection
. Every registration under this section
Acts -
4

98 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
shall expire on the 31st day of December in each
year , but new registrations for the succeeding year
shall be issued upon written application and upon
payment of the fee as hereinafter provided withoutfiling of further statements or furnishing any
further information unless specifically required by
the commissioner . Applications for renewals must
be made not less than thirty ( 30 ) nor more than
sixty ( 60 ) days before the first day of the ensuing
year , otherwise they shall be treated as original
applications . The fee for such registration shall be
$50.00 and for each annual renewal shall be $25.00 in
the case of dealers and $ 5.00 in the case of salesmen ,
for registration and $5.00 for each annual renewal .
Changes in registration occasioned by changes in
the personnel or a partnership or in the principals ,
copartners , officers , directors , of any dealer may be
made from time to time by written application set-
ting forth the facts with respect to such change .
Every registered dealer who intends to offer any
security of any issue , registered or to be registered ,
shall notify the commissioner in writing of his inten-
tion so to do . The notice shall contain the name of
the dealer and shall state the name of the security
to be offered for sale and whenever a dealer shall
have prepared such notice and shall have forwarded
the same by registered mail , postage prepaid and
properly addressed to the commissioner , such
dealer , as to the contents of such notice and filing
thereof, shall be deemed to have complied with the
requirements of this paragraph . Any issuer of
security required to be registered under the pro-
visions of this act , selling such securities except in
exempt transactions as defined in section twenty -six
hereof, shall be deemed a dealer within the meaning
of this section thirty -four and required to comply
with the provisions hereof .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 99
$ 35 . Revocation of Dealers ' and Salesmen's
Licenses . Registration under section thirty-four
may be refused or any registration granted may be
canceled by the commissioner if after reasonable
notice and a hearing the commissioner determines
that such applicant or registrant so registered :
(1 ) Has violated any of the provisons of this act
or any regulation made hereunder ; or
( 2 ) Has made a material false statement in the
application for registration ; or
(3 ) Has been guilty of a fraudulent or deceitful
act in connection with any sale of securities , or has
been or is engaged or is about to engage in making
fictitious or pretended sales or purchases of any of
such securities or has been or is engaged or is about
to engage in any practice or sale of securities which
is fraudulent or deceitful or would work an imposi-
tion on the purchaser , or be in violation of the law ;
or
(4 ) Is of bad business repute or has demon-
strated his unworthiness to transact the business of
dealer or salesman .
Pending the hearing the commissioner shall have
the power to order the suspension of such dealer's
or salesman's license ; provided , such order shall
state the cause for such suspension .
In the event the commissioner determines to re-
fuse or cancel a registration as hereinabove pro-
vided he shall enter a final order herein with his
findings on the Register of Dealers and Salesmen ,
and shall notify the applicant or licensee of his final
order by registered mail .
It shall be sufficient cause for refusal or cancel-
lation of registration in case of a partnership or
corporation or any unincorporated association , if
any member of a partnership or any officer or
director of the corporation or association has been

100 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
guilty of any act or omission which would be cause
for refusing or cancelling the registration of an indi-
vidual dealer or salesman .
$ 36. Burden of Proof . It will not be necessary
to negative any of the exemptions or classifications
in this act provided in any complaint , action , infor-
mation , indictment or any other writ or proceedings
laid or brought under this act and the burden of
proof of any such exemption shall be upon the party
claiming the benefit of such exemption or classifica-
tion .
§ 37. Laws That This Act Shall Not Affect .
Nothing in this act shall be construed to relieve
corporations from making reports now or hereafter
required by law to be made to the secretary of state
or any other state officer or paying the fees now or
hereafter to be paid by corporations . This act shall
not be construed to repeal any law now in force
regulating the organization of corporations in this
state or the admission of any foreign corporation ;
but the provisions of this act shall be construed to
be additional to any provisions regulating the
organization of a corporation under the laws of this
state or the admission of a foreign corporation to
do business in this state .
$ 38. Certificate of Commissioner -Admissibility
in Evidence . In any prosecution , action , suit or pro-
ceeding based upon or arising out of or under the
provisions of this act a certificate duly signed by the
commissioner or his deputy showing compliance or
noncompliance with the provisions of this act re-
specting the security in question or respecting com-
pliance or noncompliance with the provisions of this
act by any issurer , dealer or agent or salesman shall
constitute prima facie evidence of such compliance
or noncompliance with the provisions of this act , as
the case may be , and shall also be admissible in evi-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 101
dence in any action at law or equity to enforce the
provisions of this act .
§ 39. Certified Copies of Statements and Docu-
ments-Admissibility . Copies of any statement and
documents filed in the office of the commissioner and
of any records of the commissioner certified to by
the commissioner or his deputy shall be admissible
in any prosecution , action , suit or proceeding based
upon or arising out of or under the provisions of
this act to the same effect as the original of such
statement , document or record would be if actually
produced .
$ 40. Escrow Agreement . If the statement con-
taining information as to securities , as provided for
in section thirty of this act , shall disclose that any
such securities shall have been or shall be intended
to be issued for any patent right , copyright , trade-
mark, process , lease , formulae or good will , or for
promotion fees or expenses or for other intangible
assets , the amount and nature thereof shall be fully
set forth and the commissioner may require that
such securities so issued in payment of such patent
right , copyright , trade mark , process , lease ,
formulae or good will , or for promotion fees or ex-
penses , or for other intangible assets shall be de-
livered in escrow to the commissioner under an
escrow agreement that the owner of such securities
shall not be entitled to withdraw such securities
from escrow until all other stockholders who have
paid for their stock in cash shall have been paid for
two years a dividend or dividends aggregating not
less than six per cent , shown to the satisfaction of
said commissioner to have been actually earned on
the investment in any common stock so held , and in
case of dissolution or insolvency during the time
such securities are held in escrow, that the owners
of such securities shall not participate in the assets

102 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
until after the owners of all other securities shall
have been paid in full .
§ 41. Fraudulent or Deceitful Sales- Imposi-
tions . Whenever it shall appear to the commis-
sioner , either upon complaint or otherwise , that in
the issuance , sale , promotion , negotiation , advertise-
ment or distribution of any securities within this
state including any security exempted under the pro-
visions of section twenty -five , or in any transaction
exempted under the provisions of section twenty-
six, any person , as defined in this act , or person
acting for him shall have employed , or employs , or
is about to employ any device , scheme or artifice to
defraud , deceive or work an imposition or for
obtaining money or property by means of any false
pretense , representation or promise , or that any
such person shall have made , makes or attempts to
make in this state fictitious or pretended purchases
or sales of securities or shall have engaged in or
engages in or is about to engage in any practice or
course of business relating to the purchase or sale
of securities which is fraudulent or deceitful or
would work an imposition or be in violation of law
and which has operated or which would operate as
a fraud or imposition upon the purchaser, any one
or all of which devices , schemes , artifices , fictitious
or pretended purchases or sales of securities , prac-
tices , transactions and courses of business which
are hereby declared to be and are hereinafter re-
ferred to as fraudulent or deceitful practices , or
practices that would work an imposition , the com-
missioner shall investigate , and whenever he shall
believe from evidence satisfactory to him that any
such person has engaged in, is engaged or about
to engage in any of the practices or transactions
heretofore referred to as and declared to be fraudu-
lent or deceitful or that would work an imposition ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 103
he shall in addition to any other remedies bring an
action through the attorney general in the Franklin
circuit court or through the commonwealth or
county attorney in any county where the trans-
actions or practices have been or are about to be
engaged in, in the name and on behalf of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky against such person and
any other person or persons concerned in or in any
way participating in or about to participate in such
transactions or practices to enjoin such person , and
such other person or persons from continuing such
transactions or practices or engaging therein or
doing any act or acts in furtherance thereof . In
said action a judgment may be entered awarding
such injunction as may be proper .
§ 42. Remedies . Every sale or contract for the
sale made in violation of any of the provisions of
this act shall be voidable at the election of the pur-
chaser and the person making such sale or contract
for sale and every director , officer or agent of or for
such seller who shall have participated or aided in
any way in making such sale shall be jointly and
severally liable to such purchaser in any action at
law in any court of competent jurisdiction in the
county where the contract was made or in which the
plaintiff resides , upon tender to the seller of the
securities sold or of the contract made for the full
amount paid by such purchaser .
Provided , that no action shall be brought for the
recovery of the purchase price , after two years from
the date of such sale or contract for sale ; and pro-
vided , further , that no purchaser otherwise entitled
shall claim or have the benefit of this section who
shall have refused or failed within a reasonable time
to accept the voluntary offer of the seller to take
back the security in question and to refund the full
amount paid by such purchaser , together with

104 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
interest or such amount for the period from the date
of payment by such purchaser down to the date of
repayment , such interest to be computed :
(a) In case such securities consist of interest
bearing obligations at the same rate as provided in
such obligations ; and
(b) In case such securities consist of other than
interest bearing obligations at the rate of six per
centum per annum ; less , in every case , the amount
of any income from said securities that may have
been received by such purchaser .
§ 43. Appeals . An appeal may be taken by any
person interested and affected thereby from any
final order of the commissioner to the Franklin cir-
cuit court by serving upon the commissioner within
twenty days after the date of the entry of such order
a written notice of such appeal stating the grounds
upon which a reversal of such final order is sought ;
a demand in writing for a certified transcript of the
record and of all papers on file in his office affecting
or relating to such order . He shall also as herein-
after directed execute a bond in the penal sum of
five hundred dollars to the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky with sufficient surety , to be approved by the
clerk of the court conditioned upon the faithful
prosecution of such appeal to final judgment , and
the payment of all costs that shall be adjudged
against the applicant . Thereupon the commissioner
shall within ten days make , certify and deliver to
the appellant upon the payment of the fee therefor
such a transcript ; and the appellant shall within five
days thereafter execute the appeal bond and file the
record and a copy of the notice of appeal with the
clerk of the said court , which said notice of appeal
shall stand as appellant's complaint and thereupon
said cause shall be entered on the trial calendar of
said court for trial . The court shall only hear and
1

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 105
consider such documentary and other evidence as
was heard and considered by the commissioner and
that is contained in the transcript upon which the
appeal is taken . If the order of the commissioner
shall be reversed said court shall by its judgment
specifically direct said commissioner as to his
further action in the matter , including the making
and entering of any order or orders in connection
therewith , and the conditions , limitations or restric-
tions to be therein contained , provided that the com-
missioner shall not thereby be barred from there-
after revoking or altering such order for any proper
cause which may thereafter accrue or be discovered .
If said order shall be affirmed , said appellant shall
not be barred after thirty days from filing a new
application , provided such application is not other-
wise barred or limited . Such appeal from the find-
ing of the commissioner shall not in any wise
suspend the operation of the order appealed from
during the pendency of such appeal unless so
ordered by the court . An appeal may be taken from
the judgment of said circuit court on any such
appeal to the Court of Appeals on the same terms
and conditions as an appeal is taken in civil actions .
Each and every final ruling of said commissioner in
the granting or refusing to grant a license or permit
to transact business in this state as well as the
suspension or revoking of any such license or per-
mit theretofore granted or issued , or the refusal of
said commissioner to make investigation as to re-
voke any such license or permit on complaint as
herein provided , shall be subject to review in a suit
or other proper proceeding in the Franklin circuit
court by any person , firm or corporation feeling
aggrieved, and an appeal shall be granted by said
circuit court to the Court of Appeals from any judg-
ment it may render on motion of the losing party

106 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
and in the same manner other appeals are prose-
cuted in similar cases.
$ 44 . Powers of Commissioner -Rules and
Regulations . The commissioner shall prescribe the
forms of application and the forms of all other
blanks , documents and records to be used and kept
in connection with the administration hereof and in
addition shall establish and promulgate such rules
and regulations concerning the procedure and prac-
tice of applicants appearing before him as in his
discretion may seem expedient and essential to the
satisfactory enforcement and administration of this
law.
$ 45. Power to Examine and Administer Oaths-
Expenses . The commissioner may within his dis-
cretion when and as soon as may be after an
application has been filed with the department make
a careful inspection and examination of and con-
cerning the application , the personnel of the appli-
cant , issuing person , its books , records , files ,
accounts , affairs and properties owned in fee or con-
trolled or covered by leases , options , or other forms
of contract , wherever same or any part of same may
be found , situated or located , and of such other mat-
ters as the commissioner may deem necessary . The
expense of making such inspection and examination
shall be borne by the applicant who when requested
by the commissioner shall make a cash deposit with
the commissioner sufficient to pay the expert inspec-
tor or accountant $25.00 per day and his actual ex-
penses and this right of examination and inspection
shall be inherent and continuing in the commis-
sioner and may be exercised from time to time by
him as deemed necessary within his discretion .
§ 46. Examination Upon Camplaint-When and
How Made-Expense . Should it at any time upon
information , complaint or otherwise , appear to the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 107
commissioner that any of the terms , provisions or
purposes of any order , permit , certificate or license
theretofore applied for and granted to any person
by him , has been or is being violated , and without
regard to the time expired since the original grant-
ing of said order , permit , certificate or license , he
shall within his discretion have the inherent and
continuing right , privilege and authority to require ,
make or conduct , at the expense of the person ex-
amined , inspected or investigated , at any and all
times and at any and all places , any and all such
examinations , inspections or investigations in and
of the business , affairs , accounts , books records ,
properties and personnel of any such person or per-
sons he in his discretion may deem necessary , essen-
tial or expedient for the purpose of determining
such fact or facts . For the purpose of defraying the
necessary expenses in making and conducting such
examination , inspection or investigation and where
a specific sworn written complaint coupled with a
written request asking the commissioner to make
such examination , inspection or investigation , has
been filed in the office of the commissioner , and
should the commissioner in his discretion deem said
complaint and request to be meritorious and suffi-
cient , he may as a condition precedent to making
such examination , inspection or investigation de-
mand and receive from the requesting complainant ,
a cash deposit in an amount sufficient to cover the
necessary expense of making such examination , in-
spection or investigation .
§ 47. Misleading Advertisements -Liabilities . In
addition to the liability now imposed by law , any
person or company that, by written or printed cir-
cular , prospectus , statement or advertisement of any
kind , shall offer for subscription or purchase any
security ; or receive the profits accruing from the

108 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
disposal of securities so advertised , shall be liable
to any person who , on the faith of such advertise-
ment or document , acquires such security, for the
loss or damage sustained by him by reason of any
false or misleading statement contained therein , un-
less such person or company shall establish that he
or it had no knowledge or notice of the publication
of such advertisement prior to the transaction com-
plained of, or had just and reasonable grounds to
believe the statement thereof to be true . Wherever
any corporation shall be so liable , the officers and
directors thereof shall also be , under like limita-
tions , jointly and severally liable . Any such officer
or director , upon payment of a judgment so obtained
against him , shall be subrogated to the rights of the
plaintiff against such corporation and shall have the
right of contribution for the payment of such judg-
ment under like limitations , against any of his fel-
low officers or directors . Lack of reasonable dili-
gence to ascertain the fact of such publication or the
falsity or misleading nature of any statement
therein contained , shall be deemed to be knowledge
of such publication and of the falsity or misleading
nature of any untrue statement thereof. Any action
brought against such officer or director , based upon
the liability hereby imposed shall be brought
within two years after the acquisition of the security
by any person so damaged or after payment of the
judgment for which contribution is sought .
$ 48 . Advertisement of Securities . It shall be
unlawful hereafter , except as to exempt securities ,
as provided in section twenty-five , and securities
that may be sold and offered for sale on notification
as provided in section six :
( 1 ) To advertise in this state , through or by
means of any prospectus , circular , price list , letter ,
order blank, newspaper , periodical or otherwise ; or
הו

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 109
( 2 ) To circulate or publish any newspaper
periodical or either written or printed matter in
which any advertisement in this section specified
shall appear ; or
(3) To circulate any prospectus , price list , order
blanks or other matter for the purpose of inducing
or securing any subscription to or sale of any
security or securities , not exempted under any of
the provisions of this act, and not sold or to be sold
in one of the transactions exempted under the pro-
visions of this act , unless and until the requirements
of this act have been fully complied with and such
advertising matter has been filed with and approved
by the commissioner .
§ 49. Certain information and Records Open to
Inspection by Public . All information received by
the commissioner shall be kept open to public in-
spection at all reasonable hours, and the commis-
sioner shall supply to any applicant upon payment
of fee therefor copies of any papers or record with
the commissioner , but the commissioner shall have
power and authority to place in a separate file , not
open to the public except on his special order , any
information which he deems in justice to the person
filing the same should not be made public . An ex-
emplification of the record under the hand of the
commissioner , or of his deputy , shall be sufficient
evidence of any record made or entered by or on file
with the commissioner . A certificate under the hand
of the commissioner or his deputy , showing that the
securities in question have not been recorded in the
register of qualified securities , shall constitute
prima facie evidence that such securities have not
been qualified for sale pursuant to the provisions of
this act , and shall be admissible in evidence in any
proceeding , either civil or criminal , instituted under
any of the laws or statutes of this state .

110 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 50. Warnings to Public . The commissioner
may when he deems it of public interest so to do
issue and give warnings in such manner as he thinks
necessary to the public concerning securities being
sold in this state .
§ 51. Fees Paid Into State Treasury- Expenses
of Administration . All fees herein provided for
shall be collected by the commissioner and shall be
paid over to the State Treasurer to go into the
general fund , except such fees as are paid directly
to the expert inspector , auditor or accountant by
the person examined which may be done when the
commissioner first approves expert inspector , audi-
tor or accountant and permits the person examined
to pay the fees directly to him . All expenses of the
administration of this act , including salaries , clerk
hire , postage , printing , stationery and the like shall
be paid out of available funds in the treasury on the
auditor's warrant for the payment of bills itemized
and certified to as correct by the commissioner .
$ 52 . Commission for Sale of Securities . It shall
be unlawful for any person, firm , corporation or
association to pay or agree to pay, directly or in-
directly , in consideration for the sale of any of the
securities included in this act a commission or fee
greater than twenty ( 20 ) per cent .
$ 53 . Construction of Act . No trick , device ,
subterfuge or pretense shall be allowed to evade the
operation or defeat the purpose of this law , and its
provisions shall be liberally construed in order that
the purpose and intention of the act to protect the
poblic from fraud , deceit , and imposition in the sale
of the securities referred to in this act , may be car-
ried out.
54. Penalty for Refusal to Obey Order of Com-
missioner . Any person who shall refuse to make
answer to any competent and material question pro-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 111
pounded to him by the commissioner , or his deputy ,
shall upon conviction in any court of competent
jurisdiction , be punished by a fine not to exceed five
hundred ( $500 ) dollars , or by confinement in the
county jail for not more than ninety days , or by both
such fine and imprisonment .
55. Penalty For False Statements . Any per-
son who , under oath makes a false answer to any
competent and material question on any hearing
before the commissioner , special inspection , exami-
nation or investigation , shall be guilty of false
swearing, and upon conviction shall be punished as
prescribed in section 1174 of the Kentucky Statutes .
§ 56. Penalty for Filing False Statement with
Commissioner . Any Person who shall make or file ,
or cause to be made or filed with the commissioner
under or in compliance with any of the provisions
of this act , any statement , document , or other paper
sworn to by him and which is false in any material
respect or matter , shall be deemed guilty of false
swearing and upon conviction thereof shall be
punished as provided for in section 1174 of the Ken-
tucky Statutes .
§ 57. Penalty for Sale of Securities of Insolvent
Issuer . Any issuer or agent , salesman , dealer or
broker of or for any issuer knowing such issuer to
be insolvent , who shall offer to sell any securities
issued by such issuer, or who shall make any sale
of any such securities of and for such issuer , shall
be deemed guilty of a felony , and upon conviction
thereof in any court of competent jurisdiction , shall
be punished by confinement in the penitentiary for
not less than one nor more than ten years . For the
purpose of this section an issuer shall be deemed
insolvent whenever the agregate of his or its prop-
erty shall not , at a fair valuation , be sufficient in
amount to pay his or its debts .

112 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 58. Penalty for Violations of this Act . Any
person who willfully does , or aids or advises in doing
any act that is not included in and punished by sec-
tions fifty -four , fifty -five , fifty -six and fifty - seven
of this act , and that is prohibited or made unlawful
by any of the provisions of this act , shall be guilty
of a felony , and upon conviction thereof, shall be
imprisoned in the penitentiary for not less than one
nor more than seven years .
§ 59. Constitutionality . Should the court de-
clare any section or clause of this act unconstitu-
tional , then such decisions shall affect only the sec-
tion or clause so declared to be unconstitutional and
shall not affect any other section or clause of this
act .
Statutes Repealed .
§ 60. That the following sections of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , be and the same
are hereby repealed , to -wit , Sections 165a - 1 to and
including 165a -22 , and Section 883e -1 to and includ-
ing 883e -36 , and all other sections of said statute and
all acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith .
§ 61. All funds in the state treasury to the credit
of the banking department and to the Kentucky
Securities Department are hereby transferred to the
Banking and Securities Department of Kentucky ,
and the state treasurer is hereby directed to make
such transfer upon the approval of this act by the
Governor .
Emergency .
$ 62 . Because this act repeals all existing laws
pertaining to the banking department of the Com-
monwealth and all existing laws pertaining to the
Kentucky securities department of the Common-
wealth and abolishes said departments and creates
a new department , combining the duties of the two
former departments into one department , an emer-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 113
gency is declared to exist , and this act shall take
effect when enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky into a law and
approved by the Governor .
Approved March 24 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 18 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact section 165a -17 , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to insolvent banks ,
powers and duties of Commissioner in respect to assets of
said bank and fixing amount of bonds for Commissioner ,
deputy commissioner , State Bank Examiner or receiver of
such banks , and providing for distribution of assets thereof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 165a - 17 of Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 edition , be repealed , amended and re-enacted
so that when amended and re - enacted said section
shall read as follows :
" 165a -17 Insolvent bank ; powers and duty of com-
missioner in respect to ; assets , how applied .-The
banking commissioner may , under his hand and
official seal , appoint one or more special deputy
banking commissioners , as agent or agents , to assist
him in the duty of liquidating and distributing the
assets of any insolvent bank, the certificates of
appointment to be filed in the office of the banking
commissioner , and a certified copy in the office of the
clerk of the Circuit court in the county in which the
office of such insolvent bank is located .
The banking commissioner may, from time to
time , authorize a special deputy commissioner to
perform such duties connected with the liquidation
and distribution , as the banking commissioner may
deem proper . The banking commissioner may em-
ploy such counsel , and procure such expert assist-

114 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ants and advice as may be necessary in the liquida-
tion and distribution of the assets of such bank, and
may retain such officers or employees of such bank
as he may deem necessary . He shall require from a
special deputy commissioner such surety for the
faithful discharge of his duties as he may deem
proper , provided , however , it shall not be for less
than 10 % of the assets of the bank when such assets
amount to one million dollars or more and not less
than 15 % of the assets of such bank when such
assets amount to five hundred thousand or more and
less than one million dollars , and not for less than
25 % of the assets of such bank when such assets
amount to less than five hundred thousand dollars .
Whenever, under the provisions of this act , the
banking commissioner in person or by a deputy or
by a state bank examiner , shall take charge of any
bank , or the court shall appoint a receiver as in this
act is provided , such person shall be allowed for his
services not exceeding the sum of two dollars per
hour for not exceeding ten hours per day for the
time actually engaged by him in labor in the liquida-
tion of the bank . If such person be a special deputy ,
appointed by the commissioner , or a receiver ,
appointed by the court , such allowance shall be paid
to him as his compensation , and if such person shall
be the banking commissioner or his deputy or a
state bank examiner , the amount so allowed as com-
pensation shall be paid into the state treasury ; and
the commissioner shall fix the compensation of the
counsel and any special assistants subject to review
by the court, and all such expenses shall be paid out
of the bank so liquidated .
Whenever the banking commissioner , in person ,
or by deputy or by state bank examiner , shall take
charge of any bank under the provisions of this act ,
he shall , within thirty days thereafter , file in the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 115
office of the clerk of the circuit court of the county
in which the bank is located a detailed statement in
the form prescribed in section 165a -10 of this act ,
of the assets and liabilities of such bank , and there-
after upon the first day of the term of each succeed-
ing session of the circuit court of such county , and
every sixty days in counties having circuit courts of
continuous sessions , he shall file a report of his acts
and doings in the administration of such bank since
his last report ; and any person, firm , or corporation
may , by petition addressed to the circuit court , have
any act of such commissioner reviewed by the court,
in the same manner and with the same rights and
powers as would have attached had such commis-
sioner been a receiver appointed by the court .
The banking commissioner shall cause notice to be
given by advertisement in such newspapers as such
bank's semiannual reports are published , weekly for
three consecutive months , calling on all persons who
may have claims against such insolvent bank to pre-
sent the same to the banking commissioner , and
make legal proof thereof, at a place and within a
time not more than six months after the last date of
publication , to be therein specified . He shall mail a
copy of such notice to all persons whose names
appear as creditors upon the books of such bank . If
the commissioner doubts the justice and validity of
any claim, he may reject the same and serve written
notice of such rejection upon the claimant , either by
mail or personally . An affidavit of the service of
such notice shall be prima facie evidence thereof and
shall be filed with the banking commissioner .
An action upon a claim so rejected must be brought
within six months after such notice . Claims pre-
sented after the notification of the time fixed in the
notice to creditors , shall be entitled to share ratably
in the distribution to the extent of the assets in the

116 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
hands of the banking commissioner , equitably
thereto .
The assets of any bank in liquidation under this
act shall be applied ( a ) to the payment of costs and
charges of liquidation , and preferred claims ; ( b )
to satisfaction of secured claims to the extent of
such security ; ( c ) to the satisfaction of all other
debts , including unsatisfied balances of secured
claims , ratably and without preference to the
amount allowed at the time of distribution , and ( d )
the residue , after all liabilities are paid shall be dis-
tributed for the benefit of the stockholders ratably ,
or may , on their request , be turned over to them or
their agents for settlement .
Whenever the banking commissioner in person or
by a deputy or by a state bank examiner shall have
charge of liquidating any bank, or a receiver
appointed by any court shall have charge of liqui-
dating the affairs of any bank as in this act is pro-
vided, such persons shall make a distribution of
such assets of said bank to the general creditors
thereof at such times as in his opinion is prudent ,
but in all events shall make the distribution to the
general creditors of said bank at such times as he
may have in his possession , in cash or cash items , an
amount equal to 10 % of the assets of such bank .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 19 .
AN ACT providing for the reopening and reorganization of
closed Banks and Trust Companies or combined Banks and
Trust Companies in this Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The word Banking Commissioner as used in
this Act shall be construed to mean the Banking

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 117
Commissioner of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and any other officer exercising the power or
authority now exercised by the Banking Commis-
sioner of this Commonwealth .
§ 2. That whenever any Bank or Trust Company
or combined bank and trust company organized
under the laws of this Commonwealth shall for any
reason be suspended or closed , or is now suspended
or closed , if not less than twenty-five of the deposi-
tors therein , with deposits in excess of twenty-five
dollars each , shall notify the " Banking Commis-
sioner" of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , in writ-
ing that they desire to attempt to reorganize , or
otherwise reopen or consolidate such bank or trust
company , or combined bank and trust company with
some other banking institution , a reasonable time ,
shall be given by the " Banking Commissioner " of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , during which,
liquidation of such institution shall not proceed ex-
cept in such matters as may in the opinion of the
Banking Commissioner be necessarybe necessary for the
preservation of the assets of said institution ; and
thereupon the depositors shall proceed without delay
with the perfection of a plan and articles of agree-
ment, outlining in general the proposed plan , of con-
solidation or reopening , which proposed plan must
be signed by deposit creditors representing seventy-
five per cent of the amount of all deposits in such
Bank or Trust Company or combined Bank and
Trust Company ; exclusive of deposits of less than
twenty -five dollars each . All depositors shall be
held to be subject to and bound by the terms of such
agreement to the same extent as though they had
joined in the execution thereof , and in case of the
restoration of said Bank or Trust Company or com-
bined Bank and Trust Company to solvency and the
reopening thereof , their claims shall be treated in

118 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
all respects as if they had been parties to the making
thereof, and had affixed their signatures to the same .
If at any time , in the opinion of the " Banking Com-
missioner" of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
reasonable progress is not being made in the at-
tempted reorganization , the grant of time to deposi-
tors may be withdrawn and the " Banking Commis-
sioner" may proceed with liquidation of the institu-
tion . When deposit creditors representing seventy-
five per cent of the deposits in amount as aforesaid
have joined in such agreement the same shall be pre-
sented to the " Banking Commissioner ' of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , with a full report of what
has been done in adjusting the affairs of the Bank
or Trust Company , or combined Bank and Trust
Company , in anticipation of reopening ; and the said
"Banking Commissioner " may thereupon require
any further or additional things to be done , as in his
opinion, will be necessary to place the Bank or Trust
Company or combined Bank and Trust Company in
position to open and function as a going concern ,
and may then grant such reasonable time as seems
necessary to place the Bank or Trust Company , or
combined Bank and Trust Company in such posi-
tion , and when the requirements of the “ Banking
Commissioner " of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
have been complied with , said Bank or Trust Com-
pany or combined Bank and Trust Company shall
reopen and become in all things a going Bank or
Trust Company or combined Bank and Trust Com-
pany, subject to all provisions of law and regula-
tions of the banking department ; provided , how-
ever , that if at any time , in the opinion of the
" Banking Commissioner " of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , the interests of the creditors of said
institution are being jeopardized by delay , he may
withdraw all grants or extension of time and pro-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 119
ceed with liquidation of the institution . The pro-
visions of this Act shall be severable , and if any of
its provisions or portions are held to be unconstitu-
tional , the decision of a court so holding shall not
effect the validity of the Act as a whole , or any of
the remaining provisions or portions . Because of
the stress now existing in business generally and for
the assistance of banks recently closed , an emer-
gency is declared to exist , and this Act shall become
effective upon its passage and approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 20.
AN ACT authorizing the funding of certain indebtedness of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , which is evidenced by outstand-
ing state warrants , by the issuing of funding bonds , and
acknowledging and confirming the validity of such warrants
and the indebtedness evidenced thereby , and waiving all and
singular defenses , if any , of the Commonwealth in relation
thereto , and prescribing the powers and duties of the State
Budget Commission in the premises , and providing for a
sinking fund for the payment of such bonds and the interest
thereon .
WHEREAS , There are now outstanding state
warrants of the Commonwealth of Kentucky begin-
ning with Warrant Number A 49 , dated July 5 , 1927 ,
and including all unpaid interest bearing warrants
issued since said, date , excluding warrants against
the State Road Fund , which were duly and regularly
issued by the Auditor of Public Accounts and
stamped " interest bearing " by the State Treasurer ,
each of which was issued in pursuance of an appro-
priation duly and regularly made by the General
Assembly of Kentucky in anticipation of revenues at
the time duly and regularly assessed , levied and in
the process of collection , which warrants were
issued and delivered to officers , employees , and

120 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
others for valid legal claims of like amount against
the Commonwealth for services , materials and/or
supplies for the ordinary and necessary operation
of governmental functions of the Commonwealth
within the legislative , executive , and judicial depart-
ments , and the penal , charitable, and educational
institutions of the Commonwealth , and which war-
rants are now outstanding and the indebtedness evi-
denced thereby unpaid , together with the interest
accrued thereon at five ( 5 % ) per cent per annum
from the respective dates of stamping thereof , less
interest payments which have been made thereon ;
and
WHEREAS , There is no money in the state
treasury , after reserving a sum sufficient to pay the
necessary operating expenses of the Commonwealth
for the remainder of the current fiscal year , to pay
the indebtedness evidenced by said warrants , or
any part thereof ; and
WHEREAS , Property values have greatly depre-
ciated , thereby reducing the revenues and antici-
pated revenues of the Commonwealth ; and
WHEREAS , It is deemed by the General Assem-
bly to be for the best interest of the Commonwealth
that its budgets be balanced , and that the current
revenues of each fiscal year applied to the pay-
ment of current expenses of the fiscal year ; and
WHEREAS , Some of said warrants have been
issued and outstanding for approximately 5 years ;
and
WHEREAS , It is deemed by the General Assem-
bly to be necessary and advisable that the indebted-
ness of the Commonwealth evidenced by said war-
rants be funded by the issuance of bonds of the
Commownealth of Kentucky maturing serially over
a period of twenty years , in order that said indebted-
ness may be paid without unduly burdening the tax-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 121
payers or impairing the credit of the Common-
wealth and without unnecessarily limiting the
ability of the Commonwealth to maintain its ordi-
nary and necessary governmental functions , and so
as to make it possible and practicable that hereafter
all expenses and obligations of the Commonwealth
may be paid from current funds during the fiscal
year in which created , thereby obtaining services ,
materials , and supplies for the ordinary and neces-
sary governmental functions of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky on the most favorable terms . Now ,
therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Negotiable coupon bonds of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky hereby are authorized to be
issued by the State Budget Commission in a sum
sufficient when sold as hereinafter provided to fund
the above described indebtedness by payment of
said warrants and accrued interest thereon to the
time of such payment , which bonds shall bear
interest at not more than five ( 5 % ) per cent per
annum , and shall be sold by the State Budget Com-
mission which hereby is empowered to make a con-
tract or contracts for the sale thereof and to sell the
same as, in its judgment, may be deemed for the
best interest of the Commonwealth , but not at a
price lower than that on which the interest yield as
computed from standard tables of bond values will
equal five ( 5 % ) per cent per annum . Such bonds
shall be issued in such denomination or denomina-
tions and with such provisions , if any, for the regis-
tration of the principal and/or interest thereof and
shall be payable at such place or places , all as may
be determined by the State Budget Commission ,
which shall prescribe the form of the bonds and the

122 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
manner of execution thereof . None of said bonds
shall mature later than July 1 , 1952 , and not more
than two million ( $2,000,000.00 ) dollars in principal
amount thereof shall mature in any one of the years
1948 to 1952 , and not more than one million ( $1,000 , -
000.00 ) dollars in principal amount thereof shall
mature in any one year prior to the year 1948. The
proceeds of the sale or sales of such bonds shall be
paid into the state treasury into a special fund and
held in trust for the sole purpose of paying the in-
debtedness evidenced by said warrants .
2. There shall be levied and appropriated in
each fiscal year while any of said bonds shall be
outstanding a tax in an amount sufficient to pay the
interest and principal which will become due in such
fiscal year upon said bonds , which tax may be in-
cluded in the levy of taxes for general purposes , but
an amount sufficient to pay the interest and princi-
pal of said bonds as the same shall become due shall
be set aside annually into a special sinking fund for
the sole purpose of paying such principal and
interest .
§ 3. The State Budget Commission hereby is
vested with full power to do all things deemed neces-
sary or proper by it for the issuance of bonds and
the funding of warrant indebtedness as herein pro-
vided , subject only to any applicable limitations of
the Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§ 4. All and singular of the warrants identified in
Section 1 herein hereby are confirmed and acknowl-
edged by the General Assembly to be valid and to
evidence valid debts of this Commonwealth , and the
acts of the Auditor of Public Accounts in issuing
said warrants and the acts of the State Treasurer in
stamping them as interest bearing hereby are rati-
fied and approved and the indebtedness of the Com-
monwealth evidenced thereby hereby is confirmed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 123
and acknowledged , and all and singular defenses , if
any , of the Commonwealth in relation thereto hereby
are waived .
§ 5. If any clause , sentence , paragraph , section ,
or part of this act shall for any reason be adjudged
by any court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid.
or unconstitutional , such a judgment shall not affect ,
impair , or invalidate the remainder of this act , but
shall be confined in its operation to the clause ,
sentence , paragraph , section , or part thereof directly
involved in the controversy in which such judgment
shall have been rendered ; and it hereby is declared
to be the intention of the General Assembly to enact
each clause , sentence , paragraph , and section
separately , and it is further declared that if any
clause , sentence , paragraph , section , or part of the
act be held invalid , the General Assembly neverthe-
less would have enacted the remainder of this act .
$ 6. For the reasons set forth in the preambles
hereof and for the further reason that there is now
no provision of law to enable persons holding state
warrants to obtain payment thereof an emergency
hereby is declared to exist and this act shall take
effect immediately upon its passage and apropval by
the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 21 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the first class , with the assent
of the voters , to issue and sell at an increased interest rate
and/or at less than their par value any bonds theretofore
voted .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The legislative body of any city of the first
class may by ordinance submit to the voters thereof

124 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
as one proposition the question whether all or any
of the unissued bonds of any issue of bonds hereto-
fore or theretofore voted at an election as required
by Section 157 of the Constitution shall be issued
bearing a rate of interest higher than the rate to
which the voters assented at such election , but not
exceeding 52 % per annum , and/or whether all or
any of such unissued bonds shall be sold at less than
their par value , but not less than a price which rep-
resents a 52 % net interest yield as computed from
standard tables of bond values . The powers herein
granted are continuing powers and may be exer-
cised from time to time as to any bonds then remain-
ing unissued .
§ 2. Any ordinance submitting an increase of
interest rate to the voters as herein authorized shall
provide for the collection of an annual tax sufficient
to pay the additional interest , if any , upon the bonds .
$ 3.§ 3. Authority is hereby given to issue all or any
of such bonds at the interest rate and/or the price
submitted to the voters at an election herein author-
ized , if two -thirds of the voters voting at the elec-
tion held for that purpose shall assent thereto .
§ 4. Elections in any city of the first class herein
authorized shall be called , noticed , held and can-
vassed in the same manner and under the same con-
ditions as bond elections therein .
§ 5. The authority herein granted shall be addi-
tional and supplemental to all other powers con-
ferred upon such cities including powers conferred
by acts passed at the present session of the General
Assembly. Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 22.
AN ACT relating to suits or actions by and against a County ,
City , Municipality , and other Taxing Districts , to enjoin the
issuance of bonds thereof, and forbidding the issuance thereof,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 125
except on the approval of a court of competent jurisdiction ,
prescribing the practice and fixing the burden of proof .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. It shall be unlawful for any Fiscal Court ,
City Council , Board of Trustees of town and other
Taxing Districts , to issue and offer to sell any bond ,
or obligation thereof, until the issuance thereof has
been approved by a court of competent jurisdiction ,
declaring the same are based upon and to cover an
indebtedness thereof , within the constitutional limi-
tation of the indebtedness governing the creation
of such indebtedness , and any bond sold , assigned ,
transferred without the approval of such court , in
advance of its issuance , shall be null and void , and
the same shall not thereafter , directly or indirectly
assumed or paid by such county , city, municipality ,
or other taxing district .
§ 2. In every action instituted for the purpose
provided for in section one hereof, no bond or obliga-
tion of any county , city, municipality or other tax-
ing district shall be approved by the court until and
unless it is alleged and proven by such county , city ,
municipality or other taxing district that the
indebtedness thereof and for which the bond
is intended to evidence , was created and was
within the constitutional limitation of the in-
debtedness thereof , and unless and until the
county , city, municipality or other taxing district ,
shall in apropriate pleading set forth each and every
item of indebtedness , created and existing or unpaid
and owing by the county , city , municipality or tax-
ing district during the period of time in which the
indebtedness was created and for which the pro-
posed bond or bonds is intended to cover . And if
it shall appear in such suit or action that the county ,

126 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
city or municipality or taxing district , that the
officials in office at the time of the creation of such
indebtedness have not had due regard for the
finances of the county , city , municipality or other
taxing district , during the time in which said in-
debtedness was created , then the court shall not
have the right and power to approve such bond
issue . On all disputed issues , in such action or suit ,
the burden of proof shall be on the county , city ,
municipality or taxing district .
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with , are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 23 .
AN ACT providing that all cities , counties and taxing districts
in the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall when issuing bonds
for any purpose whatsoever make provision for the payment
of a certain portion of said bonds annually in addition to
the interest on said bonds so that when the final maturity
of said bonds is reached all of said bonds shall be paid off
and retired.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. All cities , counties , and taxing districts in
the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall when issuing
bonds for any purpose whatsoever make provision
that in addition to the payment of interest annually
or semiannually , make provision that a certain pro-
portion of said bonds shall fall due annually and
shall be called in and paid off, so that the final
maturity all bonds shall have been paid off and can-
celled .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 127
CHAPTER 24.
AN ACT relating to the fiscal administration of counties ; Pro-
viding for a uniform budget system in each county of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , for the administration of the
fiscal affairs of the counties ; Prescribing the duties of the
county judge , county court clerk , county treasurer, county
attorney and other officers with reference thereto ; creating
a county budget commission , and prescribing its duties ;
amending sections nine hundred and twenty-nine ( nine hun-
dred and twenty-nine ) , nine hundred and thirty -four ( nine
hundred and thirty -four ) of Kentucky Statutes , nineteen hun-
dred and thirty , Carroll's Edition , as amended by Chapter one
hundred and fifty -three of the Acts of nineteen hundred and
twenty, Session of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , nine hundred and thirty -five (nine hun-
dred and thirty -five ) , and eighteen hundred and thirty -eight
(eighteen hundred and thirty -eight ) , Kentucky Statutes , nine-
teen hundred and thirty , Carroll's Edition ; and repealing sec-
tions nine hundred and thirty -eight (nine hundred and thirty .
eight ) Kentucky Statutes , nineteen hundred and thirty , Car-
roll's Edition , and section eighteen hundred and forty a
(eighteen hundred and forty a ) , Kentucky Statutes , nineteen
hundred and thirty , Carroll's Edition , as amended by section
one of chapter Sixty -seven of Acts of nineteen hundred and
sixteen , Session of General Assembly of Commonwealth of
Kentucky ; Providing for the compensation of county treas-
urer, and the designating by the fiscal court of depositories
for county funds and the fixing of interest rates to be charged
by the county ; and Providing for penalties for the violation
of any of the Provisions of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . The fiscal affairs of each county of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky shall be administered by the
fiscal court under a uniform budget system . The
county budget shall provide for all the funds to be
expended by the county from current revenue for
each fiscal year . All funds of the county provided
by bond issues shall be appropriated and accounted
for under the provisions of this Act .

128 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 2. There is hereby created in each county of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky a county budget
commission . The county budget commission shall
be composed of the county judge , who by virtue of
his office shall be chairman of the commission , and
two members to be appointed by the county judge ,
by an order entered on the county court order book .
The county treasurer shall by virtue of his office be
the secretary of said commission . The appointive
members of the county budget commission shall be
at least thirty years of age, citizens , residents and
taxpayers of the county . The budget commis-
sioners first appointed under this Act shall hold
their respective offices until the first Monday in
January , nineteen hundred and thirty-four , and
their successors shall be appointed by the county
judge and shall hold their respective offices for a
term of four years from and after the first Monday
in January, nineteen hundred and thirty -four , and
until their successors shall have been appointed and
qualified . The appointive members of the county
budget commission shall take the oath of office as
required by law for other county officials before
entering upon the discharge of their official duties .
A vacancy on said commission by resignation , re-
moval from the county , or otherwise , shall be filled
by the county judge . The appointive members of
the county budget commission shall serve without
compensation ; and the county judge and county
treasurer shall not be allowed any additional com-
pensation for their services as members of said com-
mission ,
The county judge shall have the right to remove
from office an appointive member of the county
budget commission at any time for cause .
3. In the classification of county expenditures
each separate activity ofthe county for which

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 129
county funds from current revenue are to be ex-
pended by the fiscal court shall be construed to con-
stitute a separate budget unit . If any budget unit
includes more than one class of expenditures , the
said budget unit shall be subdivided into budget
funds so that the cost of each separate class of ex-
penditures of the county can be ascertained . The
funds set apart in the county budget for a budget
unit , or any subdivision thereof, shall be construed
to constitute a separate budget fund , and shall be
appropriated and accounted for separately .
It shall be the duty of the state budget commission
to furnish to each county budget commission the
form of a properly drawn county budget provided
for under the provisions of this act ; and the form of
the county budget so furnished each of the county
budget commissioners shall be uniform . The budget
commission of the state is given the power and
authority to approve all county budgets as to form
and arrangement of budget unit , and all subdivi-
sions thereof . This authority is vested in the state
budget commission for the sole and exclusive pur-
pose of establishing and maintaining in each county
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky a uniform budget
system , so that the costs of each separate activity
of county government can be ascertained for the in-
formation of the people of the state .
The county budget shall have a fund to be known
as the " General Expense Fund " out of which the
emergency expenses of the county can be paid . If
the county has a bonded indebtedness , the county
budget shall have a fund to be known as the " Sink-
ing Fund " out of which the bonded indebtedness ,
including interest thereon , shall be paid . The
county budget shall have a fund to be known as the
"Interest Fund " for the payment of the interest on
all interest -bearing county warrants and notes .
Acts- 5

130 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 4. If any county has a floating indebtedness
evidenced by county warrants or contracts of the
county , the county budget shall provide a fund to be
known as the " Sinking Fund to Pay Floating In-
debtedness ' , and said indebtedness shall be paid
off and discharged from said fund .
5. The county budget commission in the
preparation of the county budget shall use the form
of the county budget furnished to them by the state
budget commission , and the county budget commis-
sion shall have no power or authority to make any
changes in the form of the county budget so
furnished to them by state budget commission or the
arrangements of any budget units or subdivisions
thereof .
The county budget commission may adjourn from
time to time until the county budget has been com-
pleted , and may hold such hearings as they may
deem necessary in the preparation of the county
budget . Their preparation of the county budget ,
however , being limited to proposing to the fiscal
court the sums of money to be expended by the
county under the county budget for the ensuing
fiscal year .
The county budget commission shall also , as a
part of their duties , prepare for the fiscal court an
itemized county tax levy to raise each separate
budget fund provided for in the county budget pre-
pared by them , which shall be filed with the Clerk of
the County Court at the time the proposed county
budget is filed .
The county budget commission shall also prepare
and file at the time provided for filing the proposed
county budget an itemized tax levy, a written state-
ment explaining their reasons for proposing the
adoption of the budget funds provided in the county
budget , together with all data and records con-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 131
sidered by them showing the resources of the county
and its needs in the preparation of the county
budget , for the information of the fiscal court in the
consideration of the county budget and county levy .
The county budget commission shall file the pro-
posed county budget and itemized county levy with
the clerk of the county court not less than fifteen
nor more than ten days before the time fixed for
making the county levy and adopting a county
budget .
§ 6. The county court clerk shall immediately
upon the filing in his office of the proposed county
budget by the county budget commission transmit
an attested copy thereof to the state budget commis-
sion for its approval as to form , and if the proposed
county budget be approved in form by the state
budget commission it shall so advise the county
court clerk that the proposed budget is in proper
form ; and if upon an examination of the proposed
county budget the state budget commission finds
that the same is not in the proper form , it shall
arrange the county budget as to form and transmit
same to the county court clerk of the county from
which the county budget was received .
$ 7. The fiscal court of the county shall meet at
the time fixed for the adoption of the county levy
and county budget . The fiscal court shall consider
the county budget and itemized county levy pro-
posed by the county budget commission . At this
meeting the fiscal court shall hold such hearings as it
may deem proper in determining the the financial
resources of the county and its needs , and may
require the members of the county budget commis-
sion to appear before them for explanation of
budget funds provided in the proposed county
budget, and it shall be the duty of the members of
the county budget commission if called upon by the

132 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
fiscal court to make such explanation of budget
funds and the reasons for providing for the same
and give the fiscal court all the information they
have as to the financial resources of the county and
the reason for the expenditure by the county of the
funds provided in the proposed county budget .
The fiscal court shall also hear protests or sug-
gestions any taxpayer of the county may desire to
make with reference to the county budget . The
fiscal court shall adopt a county budget providing
a budget fund for each separate activity of the
county .
The fiscal court may adopt the county budget as
proposed by the county budget commission or they
may add to or deduct from the budget funds pro-
vided for therein in their discretion , and may elimi-
nate a budget fund or funds , or they may provide
for other budget funds to provide for other activi-
ties of the county not included in the proposed
county budget . The fiscal court shall have no power
or authority to make any change in the form of the
budget or any budget unit or subdivision thereof .
The county budget adopted by the fiscal court shall
be in the form as approved by the state budget com-
mission.
The county budget as adopted by the fiscal court
shall provide for all funds to be expended by the
county from the current revenue for the fiscal year
for which the county budget is adopted , and any
appropriations by the fiscal court in excess of any
budget fund shall be null and void , and shall be con-
strued to be a violation of the provisions of this act
by each member of the fiscal court so voting for said
illegal appropriation .
$ 8. The fiscal court in allowing claims shall
designate in the order making the appropriation th
budget fund from which the claim is to be paid , and 1

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 133
shall by said order direct the clerk of the county
court to issue a warrant for same to the person to
whom the appropriation is made , payable out of the
budget fund named in the order of appropriation .
§ 9. The fiscal court shall have power and
authority to transfer money from one budget fund
to another. A copy of the order making said trans-
fer shall be certified by the county court clerk to the
county treasurer and he shall make the proper
adjustments on his budget ledger making the trans-
fer of budget funds as provided for in said order .
Provided , however , this provision shall not apply
to sinking funds or special funds raised for a spe-
cific purpose until the obligation or purposes for
which said funds were raised have been satisfied .
At the close of each fiscal year the fiscal court shall
use any surplus funds remaining in any budget fund ,
except in sinking or special funds , to pay deficits in
any budget fund or funds .
All deficits remaining in any budget fund , after
the surplus has been applied thereto , shall be car-
ried over into the next fiscal year and provided for
in the county budget for the next fiscal year . The
deficits referred to in this section shall be construed
to be a deficit in a budget fund or funds caused by
the failure of current revenue to raise the budget
fund or funds in which said deficit may occur .
10. The fiscal court shall in its discretion enter
orders making appropriations out of the budget
fund set apart to pay interest on interest-bearing
county warrants and notes as may be required to
meet interest charges against county obligations as
they accrue . Said orders of appropriation shall
direct the clerk of the county court to issue county
warrants to pay interest as it accrues on interest-
bearing county warrants and notes on the certifi-
cate of the county treasurer setting forth the

134 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
amount of interest to be paid on said obligation of
the county .
§ 11. The county court clerk of each county of
the Commonwealth shall keep in his office a record
book of the fiscal court to be known as the " Fiscal
Court Claim Book . '""
All persons , firms or corporations having claims
against the county, except claims for interest or
principal on bonded indebtedness of the county ,
expenses incurred in holding regular or primary
elections , including pay of election officers , salaries
and claims of county officials , per diem of members
of the fiscal court and claims growing out of written
contracts of the county and claims under force
account work , contracted by and performed under
the direction and supervision of the county road
engineer , as provided by law , after due advertise-
ment for bids on any work , and no bids have been
received or all bids have been rejected by the fiscal
court , shall make proof of their said claims against
the county , attaching thereto or incorporating
therein an itemized statement of the claim, and
setting forth the consideration the county has re-
ceived and the manner in which the county is liable
for the claim . Said proof of claim shall be signed
and sworn to by the person making same , or his or
its duly authorized agent . The claim properly
proven shall be filed with the county court clerk,
and the clerk shall endorse thereon the date of filing
and sign said endorsement . The county court clerk
shall thereupon list the claim on the " Fiscal Court
Claim Book ," giving the name of claimant , date of
filing and total amount of said claim, and properly
index the same . All claims shall be numbered by
the clerk consecutively in the order in which they are
filed in his office , and the number of the claim shall
be listed as a part thereof,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 135
It shall be the duty of the county attorney to
attend all meetings of the fiscal court and to file
exceptions to any and all claims as presented which
are not valid obligations of the county .
§ 12. The county treasurer shall by virtue of his
office be the exclusive custodian of all county funds
from whatsoever source they may be received . He
shall also be the exclusive checking agent of the
county and no bank or trust company in which
county funds may be deposited shall honor a check
on county funds unless the same is signed by the
county treasurer in his official capacity . All county
funds shall be deposited in the bank in the name of
the county , subject to be checked out by the county
treasurer .
§ 13. That section nine hundred and thirty-five
(nine hundred and thirty -five ) , Kentucky Statutes ,
nineteen hundred and thirty , Carroll's Edition , be
and the same is hereby amended and said section as
amended shall read as follows :
The books of the county treasurer shall at all
times be open to inspection of the fiscal court or any
member thereof , and shall be public records and
open to inspection at all reasonable times by any
taxpayer of the county .
The county treasurer of each county shall keep
a set of books of accounts of the financial trans-
actions of the county to be provided for under the
provisions of the uniform accounting law of this
state providing for uniform accounting in state and
county government .
The county treasurer shall keep as a part of his
record a book to be known as the " Budget Ledger . "
The county treasurer shall immediately after the
final adoption of the county budget by the fiscal
court for the fiscal year credit on the budget ledger
to be kept by him each budget fund provided for in

136 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the county budget for that fiscal year , and he shall
account for each budget fund separately .
The county treasurer shall on the first day of
each regular and special meeting of the fiscal court,
with the exception of the first meeting thereof , in
each fiscal year , file with said fiscal court a financial
statement showing the balance in each budget fund
as appears from his budget ledger . This financial
statement shall be signed by the county treasurer in
his official capacity , and said statement shall be
recorded by the county court clerk upon the fiscal
order book as a part of the proceedings of that term .
§ 14. The county clerk shall immediately after
the adjournment of any regular or special term of
the fiscal court issue county warrants to cover all
appropriations made by the fiscal court to the per-
sons named in said appropriations . The county
clerk shall designate in the face of each county
warrant the budget fund from which the warrant
is payable . At the time the county clerk issues
county warrants on appropriations made by the
fiscal court to pay claims which have been listed in
the "Fiscal Court Claim Book " he shall enter on
the margin of the claim where the same is listed the
date of the county warrant , serial number , to whom
issued , and amount thereof in payment of the claim .
A county warrant shall not be construed to have
been legally issued until the warrant has been
countersigned by the county treasurer as provided
for in this act . After the county clerk has finished
issuing warrants as provided for in this section he
shall immediately deliver the county warrant book
to the county treasurer .
No county warrants shall be issued without a
serial number and all serial numbers must be con-
secutive .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 137
§ 15. The county treasurer shall, upon the de-
livery to him by the county court clerk of the county
warrant book , verify each county warrant issued by
the county clerk by checking the county warrant
with the claim and apropriations , and if found cor-
rect he shall countersign all warrants within the
budget fund from which they are payable . If the
county treasurer upon an examination of his budget
ledger finds that the balance in any budget fund is
insufficient to pay any warrant or warrants issued
against said budget fund he shall refuse to counter-
sign all such warrants , and shall endorse on the face
of each warrant issued by the county clerk in excess
of any budget fund out of which it is payable the
following : " Void for want of funds , " and sign said
endorsement in his official capacity . The county
treasurer shall enter the amount of each warrant
countersigned by him as a charge on his budget
ledger against the budget fund from which it is pay-
able . In making said entry he shall enter the date
of the warrant , serial number , to whom payable and
the amount thereof . He shall keep a daily balance
of all budget funds on his budget in a column on
said budget ledger provided for the purpose of keep-
ing a daily balance .
The signature of the county treasurer on a county
warrant shall be construed to be his certificate that
the amount of the warrant is within the budget fund
out of which it is payable for the current year , and
that the amount of said warrant has been charged
on his budget ledger to said budget fund .
§ 16. The county treasurer shall be liable on his
official bond for the amount of any county warrant
countersigned by him under the provisions of this
Act in excess of the budget fund out of which said
warrant is payable , and any county warrant so

138 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
issued and illegally countersigned by the county
treasurer shall be null and void as to the county .
§ 17. The county treasurer shall issue his check
in payment of all county warrants when presented
for payment , if he has the money on hand belonging
to the budget fund out of which the county warrants
so presented are payable .
If the county treasurer shall have insufficient
funds on hand belonging to any budget fund out of
which county warrants are payable , at the time the
county warrant or warrants are presented for pay-
ment , the county treasurer shall fill in the blanks in
the following words and figures , which shall be
printed on all county warrants in the proper place ,
to-wit : " Due and payable on or before the
of

nineteen hundred, 9
per cent per annum from the
