Subject to
All interest -bearing county warrants shall be
made payable at the office of the county treasurer
or a county depository in the discretion of the county
treasurer . All interest -bearing county warrants
shall bear interest at not in excess of six per cent
(six per cent ) per annum from date said warrant is
made interest-bearing until paid . Provided , that
the county treasurer shall not make county war-
rants interest -bearing and negotiable in the aggre-
gate as to any budget fund in excess of ninety
(ninety per cent ) of the amount of any budget fund
of the county . Any county warrant made interest-
bearing and negotiable by filling in the blanks as
provided in this section by the county treasurer in
excess of ninety (ninety per cent ) of the amount of
the budget fund or funds out of which said interest-
bearing warrant or warrants are payable shall be

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 139
null and void as to the county , and the county treas-
urer shall be liable for the excess on his official bond .
The due date of each interest -bearing county war-
rant shall be fixed by the county treasurer from his
knowledge of the accrual of current revenues for
the fiscal year to pay said warrant , and if to be paid
from funds derived from other sources , from his
knowledge when said funds may be expected to be
received by the county .
§ 18. The county treasurer shall keep in his
office a record book to be known as the " Interest-
bearing Warrant Record , " which shall be provided
for under the provisions of the uniform accounting
law of the state . The county treasurer shall enter
all interest -bearing county warrants upon this
record before they are delivered to the persons en-
titled thereto , and shall be entered upon said record
under a heading which shows the budget fund from
which the county warrants so entered are payable .
The record shall show the date of each county war-
rant so entered , serial number , amount , and to
whom payable , and the due date of the warrant . As
interest -bearing warrants are paid by the county
treasurer , each county warrant so paid shall be
stamped " Paid " thereon with a stamp that cuts
holes through the warrant in stamping the word
"Paid" thereon ; and the county treasurer shall
thereupon enter on said record the date of payment
of the warrant , to whom paid and the amount paid .
He shall stamp " Paid " county warrants paid by
him that have not been made interest -bearing , the
same as interest -bearing county warrant . All paid
county warrants shall be filed as a part of the record
in his office .
$ 19. All county warrants issued under the pro-
visions of this Act shall be subject to call for pay-
ment by the county treasurer when he has sufficient

140 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
funds on hand in any budget fund to pay outstand-
ing county warrants issued and charged to that
budget fund or funds . The county treasurer shall
make a call for payment of interest-bearing county
warrants by serial number in the order in which
they were issued and the call for interest-bearing
warrants , in the aggregate , shall not exceed the
amount of money on hand in the budget funds from
which the warrants are payable . The call of the
county treasurer shall be by giving the holders of
the warrants written notice of the call or by notice
published in a newspaper in the county once a week
for a period of two weeks . No interest -bearing
county warrant , so called for payment , shall bear
interest after the expiration of ten days after publi-
cation of said note , or if said written notice has been
given to the holder or holders of said warrants .
$ 20 . The county treasurer shall keep in his office
a record book to be known as the " Cash Ledger " .
This record book of the office of the county treasurer
shall be provided for under the provisions of the
uniform accounting law of the state . The county
treasurer shall upon receiving county revenues
from the sheriff or tax collector , or from other
sources , distribute the money so received to the
proper budget funds on his cash ledger, according
to the terms of the budget and the itemized county
levy . All checks drawn by the county treasurer in
payment of county obligations shall be charged on
the cash ledger by the county treasurer to the
proper budget fund and a daily balance of cash kept
in pencil of each budget fund in a column on said
cash ledger providing for said daily balance .
§ 21. The fiscal court of any county of this Com-
monwealth shall have power and authority to antici-
pate the revenues of the county for the current fiscal
year and borrow money upon the faith thereof, to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 141
meet the current expenses of the county as they
accrue , or to pay off and discharge interest-bearing
county warrants becoming due before sufficient
revenues have accrued to pay off and discharge said
interest-bearing warrants . If the fiscal court of any
county elects to borrow money in anticipation of
current revenues for the fiscal year under the pro-
visions of this section , the fiscal court shall enter an
order or orders upon the order book of said court
authorizing the county judge and county treasurer
to negotiate said loan or loans , at a rate of interest
not in excess of six per cent (six per cent ) per
annum , and shall authorize the county judge to
execute a note or notes of the county in his official
capacity , for the purpose of borrowing said sum or
sums which is authorized by the order or orders of
the fiscal court . The note or notes executed by the
county shall be negotiable and shall designate the
budget fund from which the note or notes are to be
paid . Said note or notes so issued may be renewed
by the county , if there is insufficient money in the
budget fund from which the note or notes are pay-
able , when they become due and payable . The
county may issue notes against one or more or all
budget funds provided in the county budget in the
discretion of the fiscal court, in anticipation of
revenue for the current year . All notes so issued
by the county shall be countersigned by the
county treasurer and shall be charged on the
budget ledger by the county treasurer against the
budget fund from which they are payable and shall
be charged in a column on said budget ledger other
than a column in which warrants or contracts of the
county are charged . All notes of the county issued
under this section in excess of the budget fund from
which they are payable shall be null and void . The
signature of the county treasurer on a note issued

142 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
under this section shall be to be his certificate that
the amount of the note is within the budget fund
from which it is payable for the current year, and
that the amount of the note has been charged
against the budget fund out of which it is payable .
All notes issued under the provisions of this section
shall be made payable to bearer .
Notes issued under the provisions of this section
shall not be in the aggregate in excess of ninety
(ninety per cent ) per cent of the amount of the
budget fund out of which they are payable , and the
county treasurer shall be liable on his official bond
for the amount of the excess of any note of the
county countersigned by him in excess of ninety
(ninety per cent ) per cent of the budget fund out of
which said note is payable . Any note issued in
excess of ninety (ninety per cent ) per cent of any
budget fund of the county shall be null and void as
to the county .
§ 22. All contracts of the county entered into by
the fiscal court for the expenditure of county funds
provided for in the county budget , shall contain a
provision that the contract shall not be deemed an
obligation of the county to pay the sums to be ex-
pended under the contract until the contract has
been submitted to the county treasurer so that he
can attach his certificate thereto as provided for
under the provisions of this act . Upon the submis-
sion of a contract of the county to the county treas-
urer he shall determine from his budget ledger
whether the sums of money to be expended under
the contract are within the budget fund out of which
they are payable . If the county treasurer finds
from an examination of his budget ledger that the
sum to be expended under the contract is within the
budget fund for the current year out of which it is
payable , then he shall enter the date of the contract ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 143
the name of the contractor and the amount to be
expended thereunder as a charge on his budget
ledger against the budget fund out of which the
money to be expended under the contract is payable
and shall thereupon attach his certificate to said
contract certifying that the sum to be expended
under the contract is within the budget fund for the
year out of which it is payable , and that the amount
to be expended under the contract has been charged
on his budget ledger against said budget fund .
If the budget fund out of which the sum to be
expended under the contract be insufficient to pay
said sum to be expended under the contract , the
county treasurer shall refuse to make said certifi-
cate, and shall write across the face of the contract
the following words : " Void for want of funds " , and
sign said endorsement in his official capacity.
§ 23. All contracts of the county entered into by
the fiscal court for the expenditure of county funds
provided for in the county budget based on a unit
cost price fixed in the contract shall contain the
estimated cost of the work . Such contracts shall be
certified by the county treasurer under the provi-
sions of section twenty-two of this act . All such
contracts shall in addition to the provisions required
in section twenty-two of this act contain a further
provision that the obligation of the county to expend
money under the contract shall not be construed to
be in excess of the estimated cost set out in the face
of the contract . All such contracts shall contain a
further optional provision , providing , that in the
event the estimated cost of the work is consumed
by computing costs of the work under the unit cost
price fixed in the contract before the work has been
completed , the county has the option to require the
contractor to enter into a new contract with it to
complete the work at the same unit cost price fixed

144 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
by the original contract and under the same terms
and conditions of the original contract . Contracts
of the county for over runs on work shall be ex-
ecuted under the provisions of this Act .
All contracts of the county provided for under
sections twenty-two and twenty-three of this Act
shall be null and void unless the certificate of the
county treasurer is attached thereto certifying that
the sums to be expended are within the budget fund
out of which same is payable .
The county treasurer shall be liable on his official
bond for the amount of any contract certified by him
in excess of the budget fund from which it is pay-
able .
$ 24. All books , receipts , blanks , records , county
warrant book , and forms , required to be used by the
county treasurer , county court clerk , or other officials
in the administration of fiscal affairs of the county
under the provisions of this Act , shall be formulated
under the provisions of the uniform account law of
the Commonwealth , and shall be provided for the
officials of the county required to adminster the
fiscal affairs of the county under this act , and shall
be uniform in each county of the Commonwealth .
It shall be the duty of the state inspector and
examiner to install in each county of the Common-
wealth a uniform system of accounting and book-
keeping for the administration of the fiscal affairs
of the county under the provisions of this act .
§ 25. That sections nine hundred and thirty - eight
(nine hundred and thirty -eight ) and eighteen hun-
dred and forty- a ( eighteen hundred and forty-a ) ,
Kentucky Statutes , nineteen hundred and thirty ,
Carroll's Edition , as amended by Section one of
chapter sixty - seven of the Acts of nineteen hundred
and sixteen Session of the General Assembly of

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 145
Kentucky , be and each of said Sections are hereby
repealed .
§ 26. That section nine hundred and twenty -nine
(nine hundred and twenty -nine ) , Kentucky Statutes ,
nineteen hundred and thirty , Carroll's Edition , as
amended by chapter one hundred and fifty-three
(one hundred and fifty-three ) of the Acts of nine-
teen hundred and twenty Session of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , be
and the same is amended , and as amended shall
read as follows :
The county treasurer shall be appointed by the
fiscal court of the county at the regular April term ,
one thousand nine hundred and thirteen ( one
thousand nine hundred and thirteen ) of said court
held for the county , and at said April Term of said
court every four years thereafter , and the county
treasurer so elected shall hold his office for a term
of four years , and until his successor is elected and
qualified . A majority of all the members of said
fiscal court shall be necessary to select a county
treasurer . If for any reason the county treasurer
is not selected at the April term as provided for
herein , it shall be the duty of the county judge to
call said fiscal court to meet immediately after the
adjournment of said term on a day to be fixed by
order entered on the order book of the court and
said meeting shall be held within two weeks after
the adjournment of said April term. No person
shall be appointed county treasurer unless at the
time of his appointment he is a citizen of Kentucky ,
has attained the age of twenty-five years , and has
been for two years next preceding his appointment
a resident of the county for which he is appointed .
Before entering upon the duties of his office as such
county treasurer , he shall take the oath of office
required of other county officials according to law

.146 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
before the fiscal court , and execute the bond as
required by law for the faithful performance of his
duties as county treasurer . The fiscal court may
remove said county treasurer at any time for neglect
of official duty, incompetency or dishonesty . In case
of a vacancy in said office , from any cause whatever ,
it shall be the duty, of the county judge to call the
members of the fiscal court together , to hold a
special term of said court to fill said vacancy .
In case of the failure of the county judge to per-
form any of the duties required of him in this sec-
tion he shall be deemed guilty of a violation of the
provisions of this Act .
§ 27. That section nine hundred and thirty-four
(nine hundred and thirty -four ) , Kentucky Statutes ,
nineteen hundred and thirty, Carroll's Edition , be
and the same is hereby amended and as amended
shall read as follows :
The county treasurer shall receive for his services
the compensation fixed by the fiscal court and said
compensation shall be payable monthly . Provided ,
however , that ( in counties ) containing a population
of two hundred thousand or more the salary of the
county treasurer shall be thirty-six hundred (thirty-
six hundred dollars ) dollars per annum to be
allowed by the fiscal court ; and provided , further ,
that in counties containing cities of the second and
third class the salary of the county treasurer shall
not be more than twenty-four hundred (twenty-
four hundred dollars ) Dollars per annum , and pro-
vided further , that in all other counties of the Com-
monwealth the salary of the county treasurer shall
not be more than two thousand (two thousand
dollars ) dollars per annum . All such salaries of
the county treasurer as fixed by the fiscal court
under the provisions of this section shall be allowed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 147
by the fiscal court and paid monthly out of county
funds .
§ 28. The county treasurer shall, on or before the
first day of April after the close of each fiscal year ,
make off a financial statement as of December
thirty -first of the preceding year, showing in detail
receipts and disbursements of county funds , and in
said statement account for each budget fund sepa-
rately . The county treasurer shall file a copy of
said statement with the county court clerk of the
county and said statement shall be preserved as a
part of the records of the county . The county
treasurer shall also transmit a copy of said financial
statement to the state inspector and examiner of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§ 29. That section eighteen hundred and thirty-
eight ( eighteen hundred and thirty - eight ) , Ken-
tucky Statutes , nineteen hundred and thirty, Car-
roll's Edition, be and the same is amended and as
amended shall read as follows :
The fiscal court shall be a court of record and
shall hold two regular terms in each year , the first
regular term to be held on the first Tuesday of
April, and the second regular term to be held on the
first Tuesday of October . The county judge shall
have power to call a special term of the fiscal court
for the transaction of any business of which the
court has jurisdiction . Whenever the necessity
exists for a special term , and when the county judge
is unable to act , the special term may be called by
a majority of the members of the court . The county
judge of the county court of any county may, by an
order of record, fix a different date for the com-
mencement of said terms ; provided , however , that
one of said terms shall be held in October . The
fiscal court shall remain in session at its regular
term until the business of the court is completed .

148 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 30. It shall be the duty of the fiscal court of
each county of the state at its first meeting after this
act takes effect to enter an order or orders on the
fiscal court order book designating one or more
banks or trust companies as county depositories for
county funds , and the county treasurer shall be re-
quired to deposit all county funds in the deposi-
tories designated by order of the fiscal court . The
fiscal court shall enter into an agreement with said
depository or depositories , so designated , for the
payment of interest on county deposits , on daily
balances , and said interest rates shall not be less
than two per cent , on daily balances . The deposi-
tory shall credit interest to the county deposits
every four months and shall send deposit slips to the
county treasurer showing said credit .
The fiscal court shall require all county deposi-
tories to execute to the county good and sufficient
bond to pay over and account for all county funds
deposited in said depository , which bond shall be
taken before the court , signed and acknowledged as
required by law , or the depository may place col-
lateral security in lieu of said bond , if the fiscal
court is satisfied with the collateral offered in lieu
of personal or fidelity bond . All bonds shall be
recorded upon the fiscal court order book and
properly indexed .
$ 31 . Any officer or officers wilfully violating any
of the provisions of this act shall be deemed guilty
of a misdemeanor and subject to indictment , and
upon conviction , be fined in any sum not less than
fifty ( fifty dollars ) dollars , nor more than two hun-
dred (two hundred dollars ) dollars for each offense .
$ 32 . The duties required of the county court
clerk under the provisions of this act shall be per-
formed by the clerk of the fiscal court in counties
containing cities of the first class .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 149
33. If any section , provision , paragraph or
clause of this act shall be declared unconstitutional ,
the invalidity of such section , provision , paragraph
or clause shall not affect the remaining sections ,
provisions , paragraphs or clauses of this Act ; and
it is declared to be the legislative intent to enact
each clause , paragraph , provision and section of this
act separately.
§ 34. This act shall not become effective until
the first day of January , nineteen hundred and
thirty -three .
§ 35. All acts and parts of acts in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

GOVERNOR'S MESSAGE
It is my purpose to have the State live within its income .
Section 171 of our Constitution , among other things , provides :
"The General Assembly shall provide by law an annual
tax, which with other resources , shall be sufficient to defray
the estimated expenses of the Commonwealth for each
fiscal year ."
I requested that the General Assembly do this by some
method , but it failed to grant my request or make provision by
an annual tax sufficient to permit the Commonwealth to stay
within its income for the next two fiscal years .
In view of this situation, there is only one course left open
to me for the accomplishment of my desire and the fulfillment
of the law . That course is , the reduction of the expense of the
State by veto of certain appropriations and parts of appropria
tions made to various departments of the Commonwealth in
House Bill 378. Thus , as near as possible , require the Common-
wealth to live within its income , prevent the creation of debt ,
and save the taxpayers of the Commonwealth from further
burdens ..

the fiscal years ending June 30 , 1933 , and June 30 , 1934 .

250 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 26.
AN ACT to repeal Sections 2007k-1 to and including Section
2007k-10 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , relating to
the Kentucky Geological Survey , and enacting in lieu thereof
a new Act creating the Bureau of Mineral and Topographical
Survey ; defining its rights , powers and duties ; transferring
the property of the Kentucky Geological Survey to said Bureau ,
providing for the removal of said property ; and repealing all
laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Sections 2007k-1 , 2007k-2 , 2007k -3 ,
2007k-4, 2007k-5 , 2007k- 6 , 2007k -7 , 2007k - 8 , 2007k - 9 ,
2007k- 10 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition ,
relating to the Kentucky Geological Survey be and
the same are hereby repealed .
§ 2. That the Bureau of Mineral and Topo-
graphical Survey of Kentucky is hereby created and
established with a single executive officer , who shall
be designated as the Director and State Geologist ;
that the offices of said Bureau shall be at the Uni-
versity of Kentucky , Lexington , Kentucky ; that the
geological collections , records , maps , reports and
accumulated property of the former Kentucky Geo-
logical Surveys and the collections of the State
Museum shall be maintained at the offices set aside
for the Bureau at the University of Kentucky , in
Lexington , Kentucky .
§ 3. The Director and State Geologist shall be
the Professor of Geology at the University of Ken-
tucky and shall receive no salary or other compensa-
tion for the services he may render in carrying out
the provisions of this Act , except he shall be paid
fifteen dollars ( $ 15.00 ) per day during July and
August of each year . He and his assistants shall be
paid their necessary traveling expenses when on

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 251
official business for the Bureau of Mineral and Topo-
graphical Survey .
That while holding such position , the said Direc-
tor shall not act as agent nor geologist for any per-
son, corporation or association engaged in the
business of developing the mineral resources of this
State , nor shall he be financially interested in such
business , nor shall any one connected with the
Bureau so act or be interested .
§4. The Director shall supervise , outline and edit
the work of his assistants and undertake such geo-
logical field work as his time will permit and per-
form such other duties as may properly pertain to
his position . He shall have the supervision of the
work of the geological survey.
He shall make a report biennially to the Governor
on January 1st , at the convening of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , show-
ing the activities accomplished by the Bureau of
Mineral and Topographical Survey .
Under the direction and control of the Director
the corps of the Survey shall proceed to make care-
ful geological , mineralogical , chemical , physical soil ,
and economic surveys of the State ; enter upon
record an accurate statement of the extent of water
power and water courses , to locate coal , oil , metallif-
erous deposits , clays , ores , building stones , etc. , to
report characteristics and compositions of soils and
minerals ; to collect , analyze , classify and label speci-
men of rocks , fossils , ores , coals , oils , gases , min-
erals , soils and other natural resources of the State ;
to cause specimen of the above to be correctly
labeled and preserved in the museum of the Uni-
versity of Kentucky , at Lexington , Kentucky .
The Director shall be the curator of the mineralog-
ical and other collections of the Bureau of Mineral
and Topographical Survey , in the custody of the

252 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
University of Kentucky . As such curator of said
museum , he shall arrange for the proper presenta-
tion and display of such mineralogical collections to
the general public .
�
§ 5. The Director is authorized to employ com-
petent assistants to carry out the purposes of this
Act and such stenographic and clerical assistance
as is needed .
All traveling and field expenses of the Director
and his assistants , and all salaries and other ex-
penses of said Bureau shall be itemized each month
and shall be approved by the Board of Trustees of
the University of Kentucky , in writing , before the
Auditor of Public Accounts shall be authorized to
draw a warrant or warrants in payment thereof .
§ 6. The Director of the Bureau of Mineral and
Topographical Survey may enter into cooperative
arrangements for geological , topographical , soil
and such other surveys as are properly within the
scope of the Bureau herein created , with the State
Exepriment Station , United States Geological Sur-
vey , the United States Department of Agriculture ;
and other organizations of Governmental Depart-
ments , provided that in each case agreed upon the
organization or department shall furnish an amount
of money equal to that allotted for such work by this
Bureau ; and provided further that such co -opera-
tive agreements shall be made and carried out so
that in all things they will prove advantageous to
the State of Kentucky . No co -operative agreement
shall be made or entered into until approved by the
Governor .
In the event it should at any time prove that such
co-operative work is not being conducted to the best
advantage to the State of Kentucky , the Director is
hereby auhorized to withdraw from such co - opera-
tion when so advised by the Governor .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 253
§ 7. It shall be the duty of the Commissioner of
Public Printing to supply the Bureau herein created
all necessary stationery, accessory printing , blank
forms , maps , etc. , in the same manner as State offices
are supplied, upon the signed requisition of the
Director .
The geological and other reports of the Bureau
shall be reviewed , edited and approved for printing
by the Director and paid for out of the funds of the
Bureau . All printing caused to be done by the
Bureau shall be paid for out of such funds as may
be appropriated for the use of the Bureau . All
monies received for such maps and reports , together
with any other monies earned or received by said
Bureau shall be paid into the State Treasury and
credited to the general funds of the Bureau . Such
Bulletins , reports and maps prepared by said Bu-
reau shall be sold at a nominal charge of the approx-
imate cost thereof to the State , plus postage , the
price thereof to be determined by the Director , pro-
vided single copies shall be furnished to State
executive and administrative officers when called
for, free of cost .
§ 8. That for the purpose of carrying into effect
this Act there is hereby appropriated such a sum
annually as may be set apart in any budget law
enacted or hereafter enacted by the General Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and approved
by the Governor .
All funds heretofore appropriated or on hand to
the credit of the former Kentucky Geological Sur-
veys , unexpended , shall be and are hereby trans-
ferred to the Bureau of Mineral and Topographical
Survey . The State Treasurer is directed to make
such transfer upon the enactment of this Act by the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and its approval by the Governor .

254 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 9. That all maps , reports and property of
every nature and kind now in possession of the
former Geological Surveys at Frankfort , Kentucky ,
are hereby transferred and assigned to the Bureau
of Mineral and Topographical Survey at the Uni-
versity of Kentucky at Lexington , Kentucky , and
said Bureau is hereby directed to move and transfer
such property to the University of Kentucky at Lex-
ington , Kentucky , as soon as this Act is approved by
the Governor . The Commonwealth of Kentucky shall
pay from its general fund the necessary expense for
such transfer upon proper bills approved by the
Board of Trustees of the University of Kentucky
being presented to the Auditor of Public Accounts .
§ 10. It is the intention of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to create a new
Department or Bureau hereby , to be known as the
Bureau of Mineral and Topographical Survey and
to transfer to said Bureau all funds , property ,
rights , duties , and powers of the former Kentucky
Geological Surveys and to abolish the Kentucky
Geological Survey .
§ 11. Whereas , it is to the best interests of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that the former Ken-
tucky Geological Surveys be abolished and the Bu-
reau of Mineral and Topographical Surveys be
created and become operative at once and by so
doing a large sum of money will be saved , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this Act shall become
a law and be effective upon its passage by the Gen-
eral Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and approval by the Governor .
§ 12. All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 255
CHAPTER 27 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 98a -7 of the Kentucky
Statutes (Carroll's 1922 Edition ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the ..
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 98a -7 of the Kentucky Statutes
(Carroll's 1922 Edition ) , be amended and re -enacted
so that the same , when amended and re - enacted ,
shall read as follows , to -wit :
Section 98a -7. Written Application to Clerk ;
Fee ; Time Between Additional Examinations ; Al-
lowance to Board . Each person who applies for a
license to prcatice law in this state under any rule
adopted by said court shall file a written application
with the Clerk of the Court , on such form as may
'be adopted by the court , and shall pay to said clerk
a fee of Ten Dollars ($10.00 ) , and in case of the
failure of such applicant to secure his license , he
shall at the time of any subsequent application pay
a fee of Five Dollars ( $ 5.00 ) ; but no one shall be
permitted to file another application within six
months after his last application . All fees collected
as aforesaid shall be held by said clerk and paid out
by him only on the order of said Court of Appeals ;
all fees so paid to him . The said Court of Appeals
and said clerk shall be liable on his official bond for
shall , each year , make reasonable allowance to each
of said examiners for his services , for necessary
clerical assistance and for his necessary expenses
while engaged in the performance of his duties , the
same to be paid , in quarterly installments , out of the
said fund arising from the fees so paid in , and from
no other source .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

256 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 28 .
AN ACT creating a County Civil Service Board in counties of
this Commonwealth in which there is located a city of the
first class ; defining the powers and duties of said Board ;
providing for the expenses thereof ; repealing Sections 3783
and 3784 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , compiled and edited
by the Baldwin Law Book Company , 1930 Edition , insofar as
said sections relate to counties having a city of the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby created in each county of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky having a city of
the first class a County Civil Service Board con-
sisting of four members with powers and duties
coextensive with the county . The members of such
boards shall be appointed by the duly elected , qual-
ified and acting sheriff of the county , two of whom
shall at all times be members and supporters of , and
affiliated with , the political party receiving the high-
est number of votes , and two of them shall be sup-
porters and members of , and affiliated with , the
political party receiving the next highest number of
votes , at the last preceding general election pre-
vious to their appointments . The sheriff shall select
and appoint the members of the board from lists.
of four names each previously submitted to him
for that purpose by the chairman of city and county
executive committees of such respective political
parties . The members of County Civil Service
Boards shall be at least thirty years of age, citi-
zens and continuous residents and legal voters in
said city for not less than three years next pre-
ceding their appointments . No person holding any
civil office shall be eligible to membership on any
County Civil Service Board . The sheriff shall ap-
point the first members of said board on or before
May 1 , 1932 , and all members appointed on any
443

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 257
such boards shall hold office until their successors
are appointed and qualified . One member of said
board shall be appointed for a term of one year , one
member of the opposite political party shall be
appointed for a term of two years, one member ,
who is of the same political party as the member
appointed for a term of one year, shall be appointed.
for a term of three years, and one member of the
same political party as the member appointed for a
term of two years shall be appointed for a term of
four years ; and upon the termination of the respec-
tive terms , their successors shall be appointed and
qualified for a term of four years each . Any
vacancies occurring within the membership of said
board shall be immediately filled , for the unexpired
term , by the then qualified and acting sheriff , in the
same manner as the members of the first board were
selected and appointed .
The fiscal courts of said counties shall fix the sal-
ary and compensation of said members at a sum not
less than six hundred dollars nor more than twelve
hundred dollars per annum , payable monthly , and
the salary and compensation of any member shall
not be changed during his term of office .
Before entering upon the discharge of his duties ,
each member of said board shall execute and deliver
a surety bond to the county judge of said county ,
in an amount to be fixed by the fiscal court of said
county , not exceeding one thousand dollars .
In the event of a tie vote on any matter that may
come before any such board , the sheriff of said
county shall become an ex -officio member of said
board only for the purpose of casting the decid-
ing vote upon said question .
The member of the board appointed for the one-
year term shall be chairman for the first year , and
Acts-9

258 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
thereafter the member whose term next expires .
shall be chairman for the last year of his term .
The board shall meet regularly at nine o'clock
A. M. on the first and third Tuesdays of each month ,
at a place convenient and suitable , to be designated ,
provided and equipped by the fiscal court of the
county . Adjourned meetings may be held as deter-
mined by the board . Special meetings of the board
may be called by the chairman or by any two mem-
bers , by delivering reasonable written notice to each
member .
A quorum of said board shall consist of not less
than three members for the transaction of business
that may come before it.
§ 2. Each County Civil Service Board may em-
ploy a secretary, and a chief examiner , but the
same person may perform the duties of both offices ,
and other examiners and clerical and other assist-
ants as in its judgment may be necessary for the
efficient administration of this act, and shall deter-
mine , by order of record , the terms of office , duties
and services to be performed , and the compensation
of all persons so employed .
All clerical employees of the County Civil Service
Board shall be selected and appointed from lists
furnished the said Board by the chairman of the
county and city executive committees , respectively ,
of the political parties designated herein , in such
manner that one-half of the employees shall at all
times be members and supporters of, and affiliated
with , the political party receiving the highest num-
ber of political votes , and one-half of whom shall at
all times be members and supporters of, and affi-
liated with , the political party receiving the next
highest number of political votes , at the last pre-
ceding general election previous to their appoint-
ments . All patrols shall be selected and appointed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 259
from lists previously submitted to the Board for
that purpose by the chairman of the county and
city executive committee of such respective political
parties . These lists shall include only those names
appearing upon the eligibility lists as provided for
in this Act . The patrols so selected and appointed
from the lists so furnished the Board shall be equal-
ly divided so that one-half of the patrols shall
at all times be members and supporters of , and
affiliated with , the political party receiving the high-
est number of political votes and one -half of said
patrols shall be members and supporters of, and
affiliated with , the political party receiving the next
highest number of political votes at the next pre-
ceding general election previous to their appoint-
ments .
The fiscal court of said county upon recommen-
dation of the County Civil Service Board , shall
make an annual levy and appropriation sufficient to
cover all salaries and necessary expenses of said
board and its employees ; provided , that the ex-
penses for the year 1932 shall be paid out of the
present county police fund for the year 1932 .
§ 3. Each County Civil Service Board shall have
the power and it shall be its duty to select and ap-
point county patrols and designate a chief and as-
sistant chief of county patrol from eligibility lists
as hereinafter provided for . The county patrols
shall be discreet , sober citizens and residents of
said county , not less than twenty-one years of age,
and shall subscribe and take an oath in the county
court for the faithful impartial performance of his
duties , and execute a bond , with good surety, in a
sum to be fixed by the board. All patrols shall have
jurisdiction co -extensive with the county , and shall
possess all the common law and statutory powers
of deputy sheriffs except for the purpose of civil

260 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
process . The board , by order of record , shall pre-
scribe the number of hours in each month each
patrol shall be on duty in his district .
§ 4. The County Civil Service Boards may , once
in every year , divide their counties into as many
district as the public peace and good order of
society may require, and appoint in each district a
company of patrols to consist of a captain and
patrols necessary for efficient service in maintaining
peace and good order .
Each board may , by order of record , remove , sus-
pend or reduce in grade any officer or employee in
the county partol service for any cause which will
promote the efficiency of the service ; provided a
written statement of the reasons therefor be first
furnished such officer or employee , and he is allowed
a reasonable time for answering such reasons , in
writing , which answer , if he so requests , shall be
made a part of the records of the board. After a
full and impartial hearing, in public , of the charges
preferred against such officer or employee , with the
examination of all witnesses offered to testify con-
cerning the charges so preferred , the board shall
enter an order of record of its action in said cause
and furnish to the said officer or employee a copy
thereof . In all hearings the chairman of the board
shall have the power to administer oaths to wit-
nesses in giving testimony therein . The said board
shall have power, through its chairman or secretary ,
to issue subpoenaes to compel the attendance of
witnesses upon any proceeding authorized by law
or for the infraction of any of its rules or regu-
lations .
§ 5. The County Civil Service Boards shall have
the power to fix the salary and compensation of
county patrols , chief and assistant chief of patrols .
The county patrols shall be paid by order of the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 261
fiscal court out of the levy provided therefor a sal-
ary not less than fifteen hundred nor more than
eighteen hundred dollars per annum , payable in
equal monthly installments . The chief and assistant
chief of patrols shall each be paid by order of the
fiscal court, out of the county levy provided for
county patrol service , a salary of twenty - one hun-
dred dollars per annum , payable in equal monthly
installments . Each patrol shall present a verified
claim to the County Civil Service Board for serv-
ices rendered , specifying the number of hours en-
gaged , upon which the board shall fix the amount
to be paid by the fiscal court .
$ 6 . The County Civil Service Boards , upon peti-
tion of at least ten citizens , residents and taxpayers
in any magisterial district , may appoint in such
magisterial district not exceeding three special
patrols and to continue in service not exceeding
twelve months , unless sooner removed for cause by
the board . None but discreet and sober citizens , at
least twenty -one years of age , shall be appointed
such special patrol , and they shall take oath in the
county court for the faithful and efficient perform-
ance of their duties and shall give bond therefor
with good surety in the sum of one thousand dol-
lars , and when so appointed shall have jurisidiction
co -extensive with the whole county . The compensa-
tion of said special patrol shall be paid by the per-
sons who petition for their appointment .
§ 7. The County Civil Service Boards as pro-
vided for herein may , in the case of need , appoint
special patrols to do special duty at any time and
place within the county , and on such terms as it may
deem proper and not in conflict with the civil service
provisions of this act . These special patrols shall
be governed by such rules and regulations as said
board may provide , and they shall have all the

262 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
powers of regular patrols . The hours of service
and the compensation of such special patrols shall
be fixed by order of record of the board, not to ex-
ceed the compensation of regular patrols , and the
compensation shall be paid in the same manner as
provided for regular patrols .
8. The chief of county patrol shall have ex-
clusive direction and control of the county patrol
force subject to the rules , regulations and orders of
the County Civil Service Board , except in times of
peril , riot, extensive conflagration , disorders or the
apprehension thereof, the chief of the county patrol
shall be subordinate to the board and under their
direct supervision and orders . The county patrol
shall visit all suspected places or places of unlawful
assemblage within their respective districts and
such other duties as may be prescribed by the board .
9. The County Civil Service Boards , shall
after published notice and hearing , make , promul-
gate and when necessary amend rules and regula-
tions for the appointment , promotion , transfer , sus-
pension , reinstatement and removal of the personnel
of county patrol and for the qualification of men for
appointment , promotion and transfer , and shall
report its proceedings , rules and regulations to the
fiscal court .
§ 10. Such rules and regulations shall , among
other things , provide :
(a) For standardization and qualification of all
positions . All salaries shall be uniform for like
service in each grade and the same shall be standard-
ized and classified by the board .
(b) For open and competitive test , ascertaining
the relative fitness of all applicants for appointment
to said county patrol service , and all tests shall be
practical and related only to matters which fairly
measure the relative fitness of the candidates to dis-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 263
charge the duties of the positions to which they seek
appointment . Notice of such tests shall be given
not less than ten days in advance by public adver-
tisement in at least one newspaper of general circu-
lation in the county and by posting a notice on the
door of the county court in the county courthouse .
(c) For the creation of eligibility lists , upon
which shall be entered the names of successful candi-
dates in the order of their standing in examinations
and for the filling of places in said county patrol
from selections of not more than three candidates
graded highest on such eligibility list , which list
shall remain in force not longer than one year . In
the absence of an appropriate eligibility list , any
place may be filled temporarily by the County Civil
Service Board without examination for a period not
to exceed sixty days , during which time the County
Civil Service Board shall hold the necessary exami-
nations for the filling of the place permanently.
No person shall be appointed or employed under
any title not appropriate to the duties to be dis-
charged , and no person shall be transferred or
assigned to perform any duties of any position
subject to competitive tests equivalent to that
required for the position to be filled . Each list of
eligibles with the respective grades shall be open to
public inspection .
Any person appointed from the eligibility list and
laid off for lack of work or lack of appropriation
shall be placed at the head of the eligibility list for
reappointment for a period of eligibility as provided
by the rules of the board.
(d ) For a period of probation not exceeding
six months before an appointment or employment
is made permanent .

264 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
( e ) For reinstatement on the eligibility list of
persons who, without fault or delinquence , are
separated from the service .
(f) For promotion from the lower grades to the
higher , based upon records of efficiency and seniority
to be furnished by the captains of the county patrol
districts .
§ 10. Within six months after the County Civil
Service Board has promulgated rules herein pro-
vided for , all county patrols then members of the
force shall attend the examination prescribed , and
during said period and thereafter said examinations
shall be open to all candidates , and all persons
taking such examinations shall be graded , classified
and their eligibility for service or continued service
determined by the County Civil Service Board as
herein provided , and all appointments , except as
otherwise provided , shall be made by the County
Civil Service Board from the eligibility lists so
created . All persons appointed from such eligibil-
ity lists shall be known as in the classified service .
§ 11. No person in the county patrol service ,
shall directly or indirectly , give , solicit or receive ,
or be in any manner concerned in giving , soliciting
or receiving any assessment , subscription or contri-
bution for any political party or for any purpose
whatever . No person connected with said service
shall orally , or by letter , solicit or in any manner be
concerned in soliciting -any assessment , subscription
or contribution for any political party or for any
purpose from any person holding a position in the
patrol service . No person shall use or promise to
use his influence or official authority to secure any
appointment or prospect of any appointment to any
position within the patrol service as a reward or
return for personal or political service . No person

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 265
appointed or about to be appointed or an applicant
for an appointment to any position in the patrol
service shall sign or execute a resignation dated or
undated in advance of such appointment . No per-
son in the classified service shall be suspended ,
demoted or promoted , or in any way changed in
official rank or compensation for soliciting or receiv-
ing any contribution of money , service or any other
thing of value for any political purpose . No person
shall participate , directly or indirectly , in preparing
any political assessment , subscription , or contribu-
tion , with the intent that the same shall be sent or
presented to or collected from any person in any
branch of the patrol service , and no person shall
knowingly send or present , directly or indirectly , in
person or by a letter , any political assessment , sub-
scription , or contribution to or request payment by
any person in any branch of the patrol service .
No person in any branch of the patrol service shall
act as an officer of any political organization or be a
member thereof, or take any active part in any polit-
ical campaign or serve as a member of any com-
mittee of any political organization or circulate or
seek signatures to any petition provided for any
primary or general election , or act as a worker at
the polls in favor of or opposed to any candidate for
election or nomination to any public office , whether
federal, state , county or municipal .
§ 12. No question in any examination held under
the provisions of this act shall relate to any political
or religious opinions , affiliations or service , and no
appointment , transfer , promotion , demotion , in-
crease or reduction in compensation , suspension or
removal , shall be affected or influenced by such opin-
ions or affiliations .

266 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 13. Any person who shall violate any of the
provisions of this act shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor and shall upon conviction be punished by a
fine of not less than fifty dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars , or by imprisonment of six months ,
or both so fined and imprisoned , and be barred from
future employment or service under said board ; and
if such person be an applicant for examination for
patrol service , he shall be excluded from such exami-
nation for one year thereafter , and if the name of
such person be on an eligibility list, his name shall
be removed therefrom .
§ 14. The County Civil Service Board shall fix
a minimum standard of conduct and efficiency in the
service , and whenever it shall appear to the board
that the efficiency and eligibility of any employe in
the service has fallen below the minimum of such
standard of conduct or efficiency , he shall be sum-
moned before the board to show cause why he should
not be removed , and if, upon hearing , no satisfactory
reason shall be given to the board, he shall be
removed , suspended or reduced in rank by the board
as it shall determine , and said hearing shall be had
in the manner as hereinbefore provided .
15. The County Civil Service Boards created
herein shall provide a manual of instruction for the
use of persons who wish to acquire a knowledge of
the rules and regulations of said board and the
duties of the members of the county patrols of said
counties .
§ 16. No member of any County Civil Service
Board , officer , employe or patrol shall receive or
accept any money or compensation , not provided by
law , for any service or neglect of duty, in addition
to his salary or compensation provided for herein .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 267
17. The provisions of this act are severable
and if any of its provisions or any penalty provided
by this act shall be held unconstitutional , such pro-
visions or the particular penalties held to be invalid
may be rejected without affecting the remainder of
the act or such parts of the penalties as are not held
to be invalid , and the decisions of the courts shall not
affect or impair any of the remaining provisions of
this act or any of the penalties anywhere in this act
provided . It is hereby declared as a legislative
intent that this act would have been adopted had
such unconstitutional provisions or such invalid
penalties not been included herein .
§ 18. That Sections 3783 and 3784 of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , compiled and edited by the Bald-
win Law Book Company , 1930 edition , insofar as
they relate to counties having a city of the first class ,
be and the same are hereby repealed .
$ 19. Whereas it is necessary to organize , estab-
lish and create the office of County Civil Service
Board in counties containing cities of the first class
and make all necessary preparations for the enforce-
ment of laws and maintenance of peace and good
order in society in said counties , and emergency is
declared to exist , and this act shall become effective
from and after its passage and approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 29 .
AN ACT amending Section 41141-1 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4114i - 1 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , be and it is hereby amended so that
said section as amended shall read as follows :

268 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
There shall be a State Tax Commission composed
of three Commissioners . The members of said Com-
mission shall be electors of the State , free - holders ,
and not less than thirty years of age.
All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith to
the extent of such conflict are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 30 .
AN ACT to repeal sections 2 , 3, 4 , 5, 6, and 7 of chapter 156 of
Acts of the General Assembly of 1930 , and enacting in lieu
thereof an Act creating a commission of nine members , each
to serve four years , to be appointed by the Governor , and
confirmed by the Senate , to be known as "The State Highway
Commission of Kentucky " ; providing for the qualifications ,
terms of office and appointment of its personnel ; defining its
powers and duties , both general and particular ; providing for
the compensation and expenses thereof , including the salaries ,
compensation and expenses of its officers and employees ;
dividing the Commonwealth into road districts and describing
the same ; defining the duties of the Attorney General and
of the County Attorneys in relation thereto ; accepting the
provisions of the Statutes of the United States , now or which
may hereafter be enacted in aid of the State in the construc-
tion and maintenance of rural post roads and other purposes ;
vesting in said commission the power to appropriate from
the State Road Fund such sums as may be necessary to
cooperate with the United States under any Federal Statute
which is now or which may hereafter be enacted , and authoriz-
ing the State Treasurer to receive such money as may be
paid to the Commonwealth of Kentucky by the United States
in aid of the construction and maintenance of roads within
this Commonwealth ; and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That sections two , three, four , five , six and
seven of Chapter one hundred and fifty-six of the
Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine
hundred and thirty , be and the same hereby are

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 269
repealed , amended and re -enacted , so that the same
when so amended and re -enacted shall read as fol-
lows :
There shall be, and hereby is created a commis-
sion to be known as " The State Highway Commis-
sion of Kentucky , " which shall be composed of nine
( 9) members to be appointed by the Governor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . The Common-
wealth of Kentucky hereby is divided into nine ( 9 )
Road Districts . The first road district shall be com-
posed of the following named counties : Ballard ,
Caldwell , Carlisle , Fulton , Graves , Livingston ,
Lyon , Marshall , McCracken , Trigg , Calloway , Hick-
man and Crittenden ,
The second road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Christian , Daviess , Han-
cock , Henderson, Hopkins , McLean , Union , and
Webster .
The third road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Allen, Barren , Butler ,
Edmonson, Logan , Muhlenberg, Simpson , Todd ,
Warren , Cumberland , Monroe , and Metcalfe .
The fourth road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Jefferson , Breckinridge ,
Bullitt , Grayson , Green , Hardin , Hart, Larue ,
Marion , Meade , Nelson , Ohio , Taylor , and Washing-
ton.
The fifth road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Boone , Campbell , Carroll ,
Gallatin , Grant , Kenton , Pendleton , Trimble ,
Henry , Owen , and Oldham ,
The sixth district shall be composed of the follow-
ing named counties : Clark , Estill , Fayette , Franklin ,
Lee , Scott, Woodford , Adair , Anderson , Boyle ,
Casey , Garrard , Jessamine , Madison , Mercer ,
Shelby , Spencer , and Lincoln .

270 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The seventh road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Bath , Bracken, Elliott ,
Fleming , Harrison , Lewis , Mason , Menifee , Mont-
gomery , Bourbon , Nicholas , Robertson , Rowan ,
Powell , and Wolfé .
The eighth road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Harlan , Perry , Knott,
Pike , Floyd , Magoffin , Martin , Johnson , Lawrence ,
Carter , Boyd , Greenup , Letcher , Morgan , and
Breathitt .
The ninth road district shall be composed of the
following named counties : Bell , Clay , Clinton , Knox,
Laurel , Leslie , McCreary , Pulaski , Rockcastle ,
Wayne , Whitley , Owsley , Jackson and Russell .
On or immediately after the effective date of this
Act the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
shall appoint a member of the State Highway Com-
mission from each of the nine ( 9 ) foregoing road
districts , each member so appointed to serve until
February 1 , 1936. Any vacancy in the commission
which may occur by death , resignation or otherwise
shall be filled by the Governor for the unexpired
term . Each of the said appointments , except
appointments to fill a vacancy or vacancies
occurring when the Senate is not in session ,
shall be confirmed by the Senate . The said
commission shall , immediately after its appoint-
ment and qualification , or as soon thereafter as
practicable , meet and organize by selecting a chair-
man and secretary thereof from among its members
to serve for a term of four years . Five members of
the commission shall constitute a quorum for the
transaction of its business . Each and every mem-
ber of said commission shall be a citizen and resi-
dent of Kentucky and of the district for which he is
appointed , and shall be not less than thirty years of
age, a representative citizen , and be well informed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 271
as to the needs and requirements of the State with
reference to roads , highways and bridges . No per-
son while in the employ of or while holding any
official relation to any person , firm or corporation
selling or furnishing or proposing to sell or furnish
any materials entering into the construction , recon-
struction , repair or maintenance of any road , bridge
or highway of this state , or any part thereof , or who
is pecuniarily interested therein as a stockholder , or
otherwise , shall be a member of the commission ; nor
shall the commission appoint any person or persons
as engineers , superintendents or foremen sustaining
such relation to such persons , firm or corporation ;
nor shall such engineers , superintendents or fore-
men , or any other employees of the commission be
come in any way connected with or interested in any
such person , firm or corporation while in the employ
of the said commission ; nor shall any of said com-
missioners or any engineer , superintendent , foreman
or employee appointed by the commission be a candi-
date for or hold any public office than that of com-
missioner , or of employment under said commission ,
or be a member of any political committee either
while acting as such commissioner or while holding
employment thereunder. In case any of said com-
missioners or its servants or employees shall be
a candidate for or hold any public office , or shall be
a member of any political committee , his office as
a member of the Highway Commission or position
as employee , as the case may be , shall be vacated
ipso facto . Each of said commissioners before
entering upon the discharge of his duties shall
qualify by taking and subscribing to the constitu-
tional oath , and an oath that he is not at the time
of entering on the discharge of his duties , and will
not thereafter , while a member of said commission ,
be in the employ of or hold any official relation to

272 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
any person, firm or corporation selling or furnishing
or proposing to sell or furnish any materials enter-
ing into the construction , reconstruction , repair or
maintenance of any road , bridge or highway of this
state , or any part thereof , and is not and will not
become pecuniarily interested therein as a stock-
holder , or otherwise , during his term, and is not
and will not, during his term of office be pecuniarily
interested , directly or indirectly , in any person , firm ,
or corporation engaged in the business of building ,
constructing, reconstructing, or maintaining public
roads , bridges or highways ; or in furnishing , solicit-
ing , or executing bonds for any member or employee
of the commission , or for any contractor dealing or
about to deal with the commission . Before acting
as a member of said commission a record of such
oath shall be entered on the minutes of the commis-
sion , and said oath shall be filed in the office of the
Secretary of State .
§ 2. Said commission shall have its principal
office in the city of Frankfort , Franklin County ,
Kentucky . Provided , however , that the commission
may , for the purpose of accommodating the people
of Kentucky , hold its sittings in any part of the
commonwealth ; this provision having for its pur-
pose the right to hold its meetings in various parts
of the state so that those who are interested in roads
or bridges in different parts of the state may have
the commission come to them instead of the commis-
sion having them to travel great distances to the
city of Frankfort , it being well known that there are
many person in the state who wish to appear before
the commission , but are not financially able to bear
the expense of such trips .
§3. The commission shall provide a book in which
shall be recorded all the minutes and orders of said
commission at each meeting , which minutes and
A

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 273
orders, after having been read to or by the com-
mission while in session and approved by the com-
mission, shall be signed by the chairman and secre-
tary thereof . Such minutes and orders shall be re-
corded in a book wherein the leaves are permanently
fixed and bound and no loose -leaf book shall be used
for this purpose.
4. The commission shall meet at least once
every two weeks , the time of such meeting to be
fixed by the commission , but the dates of such meet-
ings may be changed at the will of the commission .
The chairman of the commission or any five mem-
bers thereof may call an extra or special meeting of
the commission ; but such call shall be in writing and
shall be so far in advance of the call meeting to
enable each and every member of the commission to
be present at the meeting , and such call of an extra
or special meeting shall be entered on the minute
book as a matter of record . Such notice if given by
telegram shall be treated as written notice .
§ 5. It shall be the duty of the commission to
make a careful investigation of all problems relating
to the construction and maintenance of roads and
bridges in the state . The commission shall examine
all projects and ascertain the feasibility of all
routes ; and shall obtain information as to the proper
type or character of road for any project . It shall
examine all types of road materials which may be
used , or the use of which is contemplated , in the
construction , reconstruction , or maintenance of any
road or bridge to be constructed by the commission
or under its direction or supervision ; and the com-
mission shall , from time to time , examine and have
examined and audited all of its books , papers and
records ; and no invoice or bill to be paid out of the
road or bridge funds shall be approved by the com-
mission for payment or authorized by it to be paid
L#

274 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
unless and until each and every bill or invoice shall
have been carefully examined , separately , by the
commission while in session , for the purpose of
ascertaining whether any such bill or invoice is in
every respect a proper , just , equitable , and legiti-
mate charge against the road or bridge funds ; and
each member of the commission shall have the privi-
lege of calling before the commission any person
who may have knowledge or information respecting
any of said bills or invoices ; and , further , no salary
or compensation shall be paid or caused to be paid
by the commission to any person until such person
has been appointed by the commission and his salary
or compensation fixed by the commission . Provided ,
however , that common labor may be employed and
paid for by the commission under such rules and
regulations as the commission may adopt therefor ;
provided , further , that the chairman of the commis-
sion may , in order to meet an emergency , make
necessary appointments , which appointments shall
terminate at the next succeeding meeting of the com-
mission unless confirmed by the commission .
§ 6. Subject to the immediate foregoing provi-
sions the commission is empowered and authorized
to employe such engineers , draftsmen , bookkeepers
and such other assistants and employees as it may
deem necessary to efficiently and economically dis-
charge the duties imposed upon it by law , and it is
authorized and empowered to fix the salary or com-
pensation of each person so employed , which salary
or compensation shall be paid out of the state treas-
ury upon certification by the commission to the
Auditor of Public Accounts , and charged to the
State Road Fund , except as otherwise authorized ,
but no salary or compensation paid to any employee
by the Highway Commission shall exceed five
thousand dollars per year. It may remove

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 275
any employee when , in its judgment, the good of the
state so requires . The commission shall , from time
to time , assign each of its employees to such work
as it may deem best . The chairman shall be the
chief executive officer of the commission ; and , in
addition to the authority of that position , he is
specifically authorized in cases deemed to be those
of emergency or to prevent fraud or the appear-
ances of fraud , to suspend or change the assignment
of any employee .
If any person having a contract with the commis-
sion shall fail or refuse to carry out said contract ,
then the commission hereby is authorized to cancel
such contract and relet same without advertisement ;
provided it can obtain a qualified contractor to per-
form such work at or less than the price at which the
contract was let in the first instance ; otherwise the
work shall be advertised as required by law .
In event it is ascertained by the commission that
the cost of any contract or contracts , or cost of con-
tinued work on any contract or contracts will exceed
the revenues of the then fiscal year , the commission
hereby is given the power and authority to cancel
any such contract or contracts , or any portion
thereof, as may be deemed necessary to certainly
keep expenditures within the revenues of the fiscal
period . If such authority should be exercised , then
the commission is authorized and empowered to
enter into a contract or contracts for the completion
of any such work at any time funds are available
without advertising such work ; provided it can
obtain a qualified contractor to assume such work
at the price at which the contract was let in the first
instance ; otherwise the contract shall be advertised
as required by law .
No contractor shall deviate from the contract , the
provisions , plans or specifications upon which a con-

276 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tract has been awarded without first having obtained
written authority from the State Highway Commis-
sion so to do ; and , if done without such written
authority , neither the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
nor the State Highway Commission , nor the Auditor
of Public Accounts , nor the State Treasurer , shall
pay or be liable for any work or material not fully
provided for in the original contract .
The commission shall make a report to the
General Assembly at the beginning of each of its
sessions , giving in detail all information necessary
to fully advise the members thereof of the work
done since the next preceding report , and particu-
larly showing in detail all moneys expended and the
purpose for which spent .
Each member of the commission shall be paid
twenty dollars for each day upon which he may
render service as a member of the commission , and
days engaged in traveling on the business of the
commission shall be considered as days on which he
is rendering service as a member of the commission ;
provided , however , that the compensation of no
member of the commission , except the chairman ,
shall exceed three thousand dollars in any one year .
The annual salary of the chairman shall be ( $5 ,-
000.00 ) five thousand dollars . In addition to the com-
pensation for each and every member of the commis-
sion shall be allowed his actual and necessary travel-
ing expenses , including hotel bills , while engaged in
the performance of his duties under this Act ; pro-
vided , further that each employee of the commission
shall be allowed , in addition to his salary or com-
pensation , his actual and necessary traveling ex-
penses when traveling under the direction of the
commission and while engaged in the discharge of
his duties ; said expenses to be approved by the com-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 277
mission and certified to the Auditor of Public
Accounts for payment .
The commission may cause any road to be con-
structed or reconstructed by what is generally
known as " force account " when no satisfactory bid
is received for the construction or reconstruction of
such road, after the necessary steps have been taken
as provided by law to obtain bids for such work ;
and may , in like manner and under like limitations
do any part of the work of construction or recon-
struction of any road by " force account . " The ex-
pression " force account , " is hereby defined to be
work done by employees of the Highway Commis-
sion instead of through contractors , and for this
purpose the commission may employ such assistants
and laborers as may be necessary to economically
carry on said work .
The per- diem and expense account of the commis-
sioners and employees shall be paid out of the State
Treasury and charged to the State Road Fund when
presented and approved by the commission , or when
approved by the Attorney General ; all annual
salaries shall be paid monthly in equal installments ;
all compensation for those who are employed by the
hour or day shall be paid twice a month . Said
annual salaries and said compensations shall be
paid out of the State Treasury upon the certifica-
tions of the commission to the Auditor of Public
Accounts and charged to the State Road Fund , ex-
cept as otherwise authorized .
The commission , in its discretion , may require
members of the commission and any of the em-
ployees of the commission to execute such bonds as
the commission may deem necessary . The premium
on all of said bonds shall be paid as other expenses
of the Highway Department are paid .

278 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Upon the order of the commission the Auditor of
Public Accounts shall draw his warrant in a sum
not to exceed twenty-five thousand dollars at any
one time , payable to the commission , which shall be
paid by the State Treasurer to the commission , and
by the commission placed in a fund to be known as
the " Labor Expense Fund , " the same to be dis-
bursed and paid out from time to time as the com-
mission may direct, for the performance of labor
incident to the work of the department on roads
under charge of the commission .
It shall be the duty of the Attorney General and
of his assistants and of the county attorneys of the
several counties to render to the commission , with-
out additional compensation , such legal services as
it shall require of them in the discharge of its duties
under the provisions of this act . Provided , how-
ever , that no county attorney can be required to
render such service except in his county .
In addition to the duties imposed upon it in the
preceding sections , the commission may cause to be
published and distributed from time to time
bulletins containing useful information touching
only the construction and maintenance of roads and
matters pertinent thereto .
The commission shall cause to be made all neces-
sary survey in the establishment and construction
of the system of roads now or hereafter designated
as such , and shall cause to be made and provided
all necessary maps , prints , plans , and specifications
of all work to be done on said roads , or any of them ;
and shall select the route , or any part of the route ,
between the points designated , and may deviate
from existing or proposed routes when necessity
for such deviation is found ; it shall establish the
width and grades of all roads , and provide suitable
and adequate systems of drainage ; it shall deter-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 279
mine the type or character of all roads which shall
be constructed in whole or in part between desig-
nated points, and shall cause to be prepared esti-
mates of costs , advertisements for bids , contracts
for construction or maintenance and all necessary
forms in connection therewith ; it shall have full
power within the limitations in this act , to designate
roads and sections of roads for construction . After
surveys , plans , specifications , and estimates have
been completed for any road , or any part or section
of any road , and the type and character of such road
has been determined , it shall advertise in news-
papers published in or out of this commonwealth for
and receive bids as provided in this act ; it shall
prescribe rules and regulations for the care and
maintenance of roads after they have been con-
structed ; it shall have full power to stop or regu-
late all traffic or travel over any road in process of
construction or improvement and until same is
ready to be opened to the public and for that pur-
pose it shall have power to designate other roads
for the accommodation of all travel and traffic until
the road under construction is entirely completed
and it shall have power to establish temporary roads
to accommodate travel and traffic until the road
under construction is finally completed and may
make arrangements with the landowners for such
necessary temporary rights of way for establish-
ment of temporary roads , and it may contract and
pay for same out of the road fund by certifying the
cost thereof to the Auditor of Public Accounts ; such
temporary roads shall be designated and opened for
travel by the commission at least two weeks before
work is begun on the road proposed to be con-
structed or improved . The commission is em-
powered and authorized to advertise for bids and
may enter into contracts for the purchase of and to

280 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
purchase any and all materials and supplies of any
kind or character necessary to be used in the con-
struction or maintenance of roads and highways
constructed or maintained under this law or laws
hereafter enacted or other laws now in force , and
may pay for such materials when delivered at the
destination specified in the contract . For the faith-
ful performance of any contract entered into by the
Commission and any contractor , the Commission
may require bond or bonds , or secure same by any
means they in their discretion may deem advisable
or expedient . The Commission hereby is author-
ized and empowered to enter into contracts and
agreements with the United States Government re-
lating to the survey, construction and maintenance
of roads under the provisions of the act of Congress ,
approved July 11 , 1916 , entitled : " An Act to pro-
vide that the United States shall aid the states in
the construction of rural post roads , and other pur-
poses , " which act will be found in the United States
Statutes at Large , page 355 , or any act amendatory
thereof , or any act of Congress having in view the
same , or substantially the same object , which may
hereafter be enacted , and submit such plan or pro-
gram of construction and maintenance as may be
required by the Secretary of Agriculture of the
United States , or any other officer who may be
designated by Congress , and do all other things
necessary to fully carry out the co -operation con-
templated , or that may hereafter be contemplated
and provided for by said act or any future act ; the
Commission shall have the power, and it hereby is
authorized at its discretion to set apart and appro-
priate from the State Road Fund an amount equal
to any sum or sums that have been or may be
allotted to the Commonwealth of Kentucky by the
United States Government , or any sum or sums that

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 281
may be necessary to carry on co -operative work with
the United States Government under existing or
future laws and plans , and there hereby is appro-
priated from the State Road Rund sufficient funds
to enable the said Commission to pay the Common-
wealth's portion for the construction and mainte-
nance of rural post roads , or other federal aid roads ,
as provided for in the present , or any future federal
aid act ; the Commission hereby is directed and
authorized to prepare or cause to be prepared , the
necessary plans and specifications and to enter into
contracts for and to supervise the construction of
federal aid roads in accordance with the rules and
regulations of the Secretary of Agriculture of the
United States . The State Treasurer hereby is
authorized to receive such money or moneys as may
be due under the provisions of the present or any
future federal aid act , and the same shall be paid
out by him upon warrants of the Auditor of Public
Accounts , which warrants shall be issued only in
payment of claims certified to him by the Commis-
sion . The Commission shall have the power and
authority at any time to add to the primary system
of highways in this State , any and all roads , proj-
ects or sections thereof as may be designated or
approved by the Federal Government as state or
national highways , and may adopt as part of such
system any federal routes .
In addition to the foregoing enumerated powers
and duties , it shall be the duty of the Commission
to perform any and all other duties imposed upon it
by this or any other act , and particularly shall it be
its duty to do and cause to be done all things neces-
sary to construct and maintain the system of pri-
