mary roads now or hereafter designated as such and
where there is no provision of law covering any
necessary thing to be done in the construction and

282 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
maintenance of said roads , it shall do or cause to be
done such necessary things in the way and manner
best calculated , in its judgment, to accomplish the
necessary objects , and it is intended that the pro-
visions contained in this act shall not be treated as
restrictions on its powers , save as expressly stated
or clearly implied , that is to say , it is intended that
it may do all things necessary in the construction or
maintenance of said roads except where the power
is by this or another statute , or by the Constitution ,
expressly or by clear implication denied or re-
stricted . Wherever and whenever the word " Com-
mission " occurs herein it shall mean " The State
Highway Commission of Kentucky . "
The Commission shall succeed to and be vested
with all the rights , powers and duties now vested in
or imposed upon the State Highway Engineer for
the State Highway Commission by any and all other
statutes now in effect , and it shall be the agent of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky in carrying out all con-
tracts now in effect which have been executed by the
State Highway Commission as an agent of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .
If any provision of this act be held to be invalid ,
or unconstitutional , such fact shall not affect any
other provision of this act , it being the intention of
the General Assembly in enacting this act to enact
each provision separately ; and if any provision or
exception contained in any provision of this act shall
be held invalid , or unconstitutional, such fact shall
not affect the remaining portion of this act , it being
the intention of the General Assembly to enact each
provision of this act and each proviso and each ex-
ception thereto , separately .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 283
Whereas , it is very urgent and a public necessity
to have a new Highway Commission to construct
and maintain the primary system of public high-
ways and bridges in this Commonwealth , and in
order that no delay happen an emergency is hereby
declared to exist, and this act shall become effective
from and after its passage and approval .
Approved February 3 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 31 .
AN ACT to establish an employment service ; providing for
cooperative agreement with county , municipal , school and
Federal government agencies ; prescribing the duties and
authority of the chief officer of the Department of Labor to
carry out the objects of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in order to provide an employment
service for men , women and minors seeking employ-
ment ; for employers seeking employees ; to stabilize
employment ; to cooperate with counties , municipali-
ties , school and the United State Employment
Service ; for the extension of vocational guidance to
minors and aid to the physically handicapped , there
is hereby established in the Department of Labor a
" Kentucky Employment Service ," and
§ 2. It shall be the duty of the chief officer of the
Department of Labor and he is hereby empowered
subject to the advice and consent to the Commis-
sioner of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics :
(a) to establish and conduct employment offices in
the State where such offices are deemed advisable ;
(b) to ascertain and make known the opportuni-
ties for employment ;

284 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(c ) to create and maintain such divisions of the
employment service as will best serve the public
welfare ;
(d ) to cooperate with existing State and Federal
agencies in extending vocational guidance to minors
seeking employment ;
(e ) to render especial assistance to physically
handicapped persons seeking employment , to help
them become self supporting and aid in the support
of their dependents ;
(f) to cooperate with county agricultural agents
and farmers ' agencies to ascertain needs of labor
and mobilization and distribution of labor among
the agricultural classes ;
(g ) to devise and adopt the most efficient means
to avoid unemployment ;
(h) to establish relations with employers and
organizations for the purpose of supplying demands
for labor , for the stabilizing and prevention of unem-
ployment ;
(i ) to make public through newspapers and other
media information as to employment available ;
(j ) to investigate economic developments , the
extent and causes of unemployment and remedial
projects advanced by agencies , private or govern-
mental , for the purpose of preparing for the General
Assembly facts which may make further legislation
desirable ;
(k) to collect , collate and publish statistical and
other information from all sources considered per-
tinent to the work within the employment service
jurisdiction and shall use this data in the publication
and dissemination of changing social and economic
conditions that might adversely affect employers ,
employees and the general public ;

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 285
(1) to correlate the functions of the employment
service with private and public agencies such as the
United States Employment Service , the United
States Bureau of Labor Statistics , the Red Cross ,
the Rehabilitation Service , War Veteran organiza-
tions , county and Municipal welfare agencies and
such other organizations of similar function and
intent in order that the Kentucky Employment
Service shall be utilized as a central base or focal
point as a survey agency , alleviation and distribu-
tion point for the relief of distress among workers
and the public in times of economic depression or an
emergency ;
(m ) to enter into agreement with the governing
authorities of any county or municipality in the
State for such period of time as may be deemed
advisable for the purpose of establishing and main-
taining local employment offices ;
(n ) to enter into agreement with the governing
authorities of any school or school district for the
extension of vocational guidance to minors ;
(o ) to enter into agreement with the United
States Employment service , or such bureau of the
United States Department of Labor designated or
may hereafter be authorized by the United States
Congress for the purpose of securing financial and
other aid from the United States government for the
establishment and maintenance of a public employ-
ment service in Kentucky ;
(p ) to appoint advisory committees to assist in
conducting an efficient employment service ;
(q ) to accept and solicit monies for the Kentucky
Employment Service from private sources not
incompatible to the welfare and efficiency of the pro-
visions of this Act and the Kentucky Employment
Service , and all such monies collected shall be

286 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
accounted for to the State Auditor and credited to
the account and use of said employment service .
§ 3. No fee , charge or other perquisite shall be
accepted or exacted for any service rendered appli-
cant -worker or employer by said employment
service .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 32 .
AN ACT to assist disabled ex -service men and women of the
World War and veterans of other wars in obtaining from the
Federal Government the relief authorized by Acts of Congress ,
and continuing in existence for that purpose the Kentucky
Disabled Ex-Service Men's Board and appropriating funds
therefor ; providing maintenance of an office and stenographic
help to carry on the work of said Board .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service
Men's Board , created by Act approved February 27 ,
1922 , and recreated by Acts of the General Assem-
bly approved March 21 , 1924 , February 24 , 1926 ,
February 16 , 1928 , and March 11 , 1930 , be continued
in existence for an additional period of two ( 2 )
years for the purpose of assisting ex-service men
and women of the World War and their dependents ,
and veterans of other wars and their dependents .
who are residents of Kentucky , and who are or may
be entitled to aid from the United States Govern-
ment pursuant to federal legislation for the benefit
of veterans and their dependents , in preparing and
presenting their said claims to the proper federal
agencies and authorities and in securing and expedit-
ing action thereon ; to relieve and prevent congestion
and delay in the proper consideration , rating and
final adjudication of said claims and to, as far as

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 287
possible , cause every disabled veteran and the
dependents of every disabled veteran to receive
promptly and regularly the maximum relief and
benefits to which they are entitled under the federal
law .
§ 2. Said Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's
Board shall, during the time for which its existence
is extended by Section 1 of this Act , be composed of
three ( 3 ) members : One of whom shall be the Lieu-
tenant -Governor of Kentucky , ex-officio , another of
whom shall be the State Commander of the American
Legion , Department of Kentucky , ex-officio , and the
third member shall be elected annually by these two
members . The members of the Board shall serve
without salary or compensation , but they shall be
paid their actual and necessary traveling expenses
while in the discharge of their official duties .
3. The words , " veterans " and " ex- service
men and women ," as used in this Act, include any
individual who served on active duty in the army ,
navy or marine corps of the United States and who
has received an honorable discharge from such
service .
§ 4. Said Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's
Board shall elect a Secretary and such assitants as
may be found necessary whose duties shall be to pre-
pare in proper form , present and prosecute the
claims of disabled ex-service men and women and
their dependents with the United States Veterans '
Bureau and United States Bureau of Pensions ,
Veterans Administration and other federal agencies ,
and to secure and expedite action on said claims ; to
keep in contact with the United States Veterans
Administration so that the status of any claims may
be known at any time ; to continue a survey of the
State to make certain that no disabled man or his

288 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
7
dependents are neglected , and to perform such other
duties as the Board may direct . The secretary and
such assistants as may be authorized shall be paid a
salary fixed by the Board and shall be paid actual
and necessary traveling expenses while engaged in
the performance of duties as the Board may direct .
§ 5. For the purpose of carrying out the pro-
visions of this Act , there is hereby appropriated out
of State funds , not otherwise appropriated , to the
Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's Board , the
sum of twenty thousand ( $20,000.00 ) dollars per
year for two ( 2 ) years . The Board shall keep an
accurate and detailed account of all receipts and dis-
bursements , and its books shall be subject to exami-
nation and audit by the State Inspector and
Examiner . Any part of the fund unused at the
expiration of the period for which the Board is
created shall be turned back to the general State
fund .
§ 6. The Kentucky Disabled Ex - Service Men's
Board shall have the power to expend such reason-
able sums as may be necessary to employ clerical
and stenographic assistants , who shall be paid their
actual and necessary traveling expenses while en-
gaged in the performance of their duties and to
establish and maintain offices .
§ 7. The Kentucky Disabled Ex -Service Men's
Board shall cease to exist on July 1 , 1934 .
8. All laws and parts of laws inconsistent
herewith are hereby repealed .
9. There being many thousands of disabled ex-
service men who have not received the benefits of
federal legislation to which they are entitled because
of lack of knowledge of the federal law as amended
and the United States Veterans Administration
regulations and procedure , and other remedial

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 289
causes , and it being considered that if this Board
ceases to function , the disabled ex - service men will
not receive the information , assistance , and benefits
to which they are entitled , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect from
and after its passage and approval as required by
law .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 33 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 199A of Carroll's ( 1930 )
Kentucky Statutes and being Chapter 143 of the Acts of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , enacted
at the 1906 Session thereof, and entitled , "An Act to regulate
Burial Associations , corporations and companies doing busi-
ness in the State of Kentucky ," as amended by " Chapter 72
of the 1908 Acts of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky ," entitled , " An Act to amend Section 2
of an Act entitled , 'An Act to regulate Burial Associations ,
corporations and companies doing business in the State of
Kentucky ,' which Act became a law, March 24 , 1906 , with-
out the approval of the Governor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter 143 of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , enacted
at the 1906 Session thereof , as amended by Chapter
72 of the 1908 Acts , be amended and re -enacted so
that the same when so re -enacted shall read as fol-
lows :
Section 1. The term " Burial Associations "
within the meaning of this Act, shall mean and
include all persons , individuals , firms , partnerships ,
co -operative and other kind of societies , corporations
and organizations of every kind , which , in the con-
sideration of the payment of dues , fees , assessments
or premiums , shall issue any kind of agreement ,
Acts- 10

290 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
policy, contract , bond , assurance or guarantee , or
have in effect any by-law under which the insured is
to be provided , in case of death , with a funeral or
funeral expenses .
Section 2. It shall be unlawful for any associa-
tion as herein defined , to issue or cause to be issued ,
any agreement , policy , contract , bond , assurance or
guarantee , or have any by-laws which shall provide
for the payment of any funeral benefit in merchan-
dise or services , but all payments of benefits there-
under must be made in money .
Section 3. Every Burial Association , as herein
described shall , before commencing such business , or
further continuing therein , execute articles of incor-
poration as now required by law , and file same in
the office of the Insurance Commissioner of this
State ; but before same shall be filed , it shall be
referred to , and examined by the Attorney General ,
and if found by him to be conformable to the require-
ments of law and not inconsistent with the laws of
this State , he shall certify accordingly and return
same with his certificates of such conformity , to the
Insurance Commissioner , who shall cause said arti-
cles , with the certificate of the Attorney General , to
be recorded in a book to be kept for that purpose ,
and shall deliver to such associations certified copies
of papers so filed and recorded in his office , and all
certificates of the Attorney General , together with
license of said Associations , to engage in the busi-
ness proposed in said declaration , upon such certi-
fied copy and license being filed in the office of the
clerk of the county wherein the association is to be
located , said corporators and those who may become
associated with them , or their successors , shall con-
stitute a body , politic or corporate, and lawfully
entitled to commence business .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 291
Section 4. Each Burial Association , as defined in
this Act , shall deposit with the Treasurer of the
Commonwealth , securities to the amount of not less
than One Hundred Thousand ($100,000.00 ) Dollars ,
to be held by the said Treasurer for the benefit of
the policy holders of the Burial Association making
such deposit . Such securities shall be of the nature
and kind in which domestic life insurance companies
are allowed by law to invest their capital , but such
associations shall have , at all times , on approval of
the Insurance Commissioner , the right to exchange
any part of such securities for other of like amount
or character .
When any Burial Association shall discontinue
business or when for any cause a dissolution shall be
decreed , or when for sixty ( 60 ) days any judgment
shall remain unsatisfied , a Circuit Judge in any
county wherein said Burial Association has trans-
acted business , may appoint a receiver to distribute
its assets among its policy holders for any persons
having claims against said association , the same to
be applied first , on accrued or natural claims , certif-
icates or policies ; second , on claims of any other
kind or character ; third , in the payment to members
or policy holders of all dues paid in by them , and if
a balance remains after payment of the above named
claims , then such sum is to be returned to said Burial
Association .
Section 5. Such Burial Association , desiring to
do business in this State , shall file with the Insur-
ance Commissioner like power of attorney as is now
required of Insurance companies , designating the
Insurance Commissioner as proper person upon
whom process may be served .
Section 6. All such Burial Associations doing
business contemplated by this Act, shall pay an

292 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
annual license tax of one hundred ( $ 100.00 ) dollars
to the State of Kentucky ; such tax to be paid
through the insurance department , as license tax is
now paid by insurance companies ; and in addition
thereto such associations shall pay the same fees as
assessment life insurance companies are now
required to pay to organize in this State .
Section 7. All such Burial Associations shall , on
or before the first day of March in each year , make
and file with the Insurance Commissioner report of
the affairs and operations during the year ending
December thirty-first immediately preceding . Such
reports shall be upon blank forms furnished by the
commissioner and shall be verified under oath by the
authorized officers of such associations , and report
shall be published , or substance thereof, in the
annual report of the Insurance Commissioner , and
shall report the number of outstanding policies ,
bonds and certificates , also the number of matured
policies , bonds and certificates , and shall also report
to said commissioner all other information connected
with their business as said commissioner may
require . Said commissioner shall have the right to
make any examination of the books or affairs of such
associations as is now given by law to the Insurance
Commissioner in the examination of insurance com-
panies in this State .
Section 8. If upon examination of such associa-
tions by the commissioner or any person designated
by him to make such examination , it shall appear
that the liabilities of said association exceed its
resources , and it cannot in a reasonable time , not
more than three months from the date of the original
default, pay its accrued indebtedness in full , he shall
report the facts to the Attorney General , who shall,
upon the commissioner's report , apply to the Judge

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 293
of the Franklin Circuit Court , or to the Judge of the
Circuit Court of the county wherein such association
is located , for an order closing the business of the
association , and appointing a receiver for the distri-
bution of its assets among creditors . No such final
order shall be made until the association shall have
ten days ' notice of the application and an opportu-
nity to be heard . And upon hearing the matter , the
court shall have power to make any order which the
interest of the association and the public may
require .
Section 9. All policies , certificates or bonds here-
after issued to persons within the Commonwealth ,
by Associations transacting business under this law ,
which policies or certificates contain any reference
to the application of the insured or the Constitution ,
by-laws or other rules of the asssociation , either as
forming a part of the policy or contract between the
parties thereto or having any bearing on said con-
tract , shall contain or have attached to said policy
or certificate a correct copy of the application as
signed by the applicant , and the portions of the con-
stitution , by-laws or other rules referred to, and
unless so attached and accompany the policy , no such
application , constitution , by-laws or other rules
shall be received as evidence in any contro-
versy between the parties to or interested in said.
policy or certificate , and shall not be considered a
part of the policy or contract between such parties .
Section 10. Any Burial Association as herein de-
fined , now in business or hereafter organized , failing
to comply with the provisions of this Act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction shall
be fined not less than one hundred ( $ 100.00 ) dollars
nor more than five hundred ( $ 500.00 ) dollars , and
each day they shall so engage in business without

294 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
complying with the provisions of this Act , shall be a
separate offense , and the issuance of any agreement ,
policy , contract, bond , assurance or guarantee , or
the enforcement of any by-law in violation of the
terms of this Act , shall also be a separate offense ,
provided , however , that this Act shall not apply to
fraternal societies , or fraternal beneficiary societies ,
nor to assessment , life and accident insurance com-
panies or associations existing or operating under
or by virtue of any statute of this State , nor to any
society which pays sick or disability benefits and
which limits its membership to a particular class of
persons or to the employees of a designated person ,
firm or corporation .
Approved February 18 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 34.
AN ACT to amend an act in relation to the Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners and to regulate the practice of
Dentistry in the State of Kentucky . Passed March , 1912 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
CONTINUANCE OF BOARD AND MANNER OF
APPOINTMENT
§ 1. That the Kentucky State Board of Dental
Examiners heretofore created be continued , to con-
sist of five practicing dentists , whose duty it shall be
to carry out the purposes and enforce the provisions
of this Act as hereafter specified . The members of
said Board shall be appointed by the Governor from
a list of three names , to be recommended by the
Kentucky State Dental Association , at its annual
meeting , and at the time of their appointment upon
said board must have been actual residents and
legally licensed practicing dentists of this state for

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 295
a period of five years or more immediately preced-
ing their appointment : Provided , however , That
no person shall be eligible to appointment to said
board who is in any way connected with or interested
in any dental college or dental department of any
institution of learning or dental supply business .
The term for which the members of said board shall
hold office shall be five years , provided the present
members of the board in office at the time of the pas-
sage of this act shall continue in office until their
respective terms have expired and until their suc-
cessors are appointed and qualified . All vacancies
in said board shall be filled by the Governor from
said list .
OFFICERS , MEETINGS , RECORDS , ETC.
§ 2. Said Board of Dental Examiners shall
choose one of its members President , and one Secre-
tary -Treasurer , thereof, at an annual meeting on
the first Tuesday in June of each year . Said board
may meet oftener if necessary in the discretion of
the board at such places as it may deem proper , for
the examination of applicants who desire to practice
dentistry in this state , and for the transaction of any
other business that may come before it. Said board
shall keep a record book in which shall be registered
the names , addresses , license numbers , of all persons
legally entitled to practice dentistry in this state . A
majority of the members of said board shall at all
times constitute a quorum for the transaction of
business , and the proceedings of said board shall be
recorded in a minute book open at all reasonable
times to public inspection . The Secretary- Treas-
urer shall execute to the said board bond with
approved security for the faithful performance of
his duties .

296 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
LICENSES AND REGISTRATION AND HOW GRANTED
§ 3. No person shall practice dentistry in this
state or attempt to do so after the passage of this
Act without first applying for and obtaining a license
for such purpose from the said Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners , and registering such
license as herein provided , and this provision applies
to all persons whether they have heretofore prac-
ticed dentistry or not in this state , except such per-
sons as have been heretofore licensed and registered .
Application shall be made to the said board in writ-
ing for license and shall in every instance be accom-
panied by the examination fee of Forty Dollars ,
( $40.00 ) , which sum is authorized to be charged for
each examination by said board . The applicant must
be of good moral character , at least twenty -one ( 21 )
years of age at the time of mking the application ,
and the application of each person seeking a
license must be accompanied by satisfactory
evidence to said board that the applicant so
applying is a graduate of and has a diploma
from the faculty of a reputable dental col-
lege , school , or dental department of a reputable
university , rated as A or B by the Dental Educa-
tional Council of America and should the Dental
Educational Council of American cease to exist the
board of examiners shall rate dental colleges , dental
schools , or dental departments of a university .
Examinations must be clinical and written and of
such a character as to satisfactorily test the quali-
fications of the applicant to practice dentistry , and
the board may, in its discretion , refuse to grant
license to any person they find guilty of cheating ,
deception , or fraud during such examination .
Examination papers and clinical examination results

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 297
where possible shall be retained by the secretary of
the board for a period of five ( 5 ) years .
REGISTERING LICENSE WITH COUNTY CLERK
§ 4. Every person licensed to practice dentistry
in this state by the said Kentucky State Board of
Dental Examiners , as herein provided shall , before
beginning the practice of dentistry , cause said license
to be registered with the county clerk of each county
in which such person desires to engage in the prac-
tice of dentistry by appearing before such clerk and
filing his affidavit , showing that he has been
examined and licensed as herein provided , and the
county clerk of each county before whom such per-
sons appear shall attest the filing of the license cer-
tificate in duplicate on blanks furnished to the
licensee by the board , one copy to be retained by the
licensee and the other copy to be forwarded to the
secretary of the board by the licensee , and the county
clerk of each county is authorized to receive a regis-
tration fee of fifty cents (50c ) for each registration .
DISPLAY OF LICENSE AND CERTIFICATE OF REGISTRATION
§ 5. The license to practice dentistry herein pro-
vided, and the certificate of registration , ( together
with the annual renewal certificate ) shall at all times
be displayed in a conspicuous place in his or her
office wherein he or she shall practice the profession
of dentistry , and if there are more dentists than one
practicing or employed , in any dental office , the pro-
prietor of such office shall post and display or cause
to be posted or displayed in like manner the name ,
license , and registration certificate of each dentist
so practicing and so employed therein . Any person
practicing dentistry within the meaning of this act ,
who shall fail to so post and display or cause to be

298 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
posted or displayed , the name , license , and registra-
tion certificate of himself and any person practicing
or employed to practice as a dentist in his or her
dental office , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and
upon conviction thereof they shall be subject to a
fine of not less than twenty-five ($ 25.00 ) dollars nor
more than ($50.00 ) fifty dollars for the first offense ,
and for every subsequent offense he or she shall be
subjected to a fine of not less than one hundred
($100.00 ) dollars nor more than two hundred and
fifty ( $ 250.00 ) dollars , or by imprisonment for a
period of not less than sixty days , or by both such
fine and imprisonment , and he or she shall , whenever
requested , exhibit such license to members of the
Board of Dental Examiners or its authorized agent .
REVOCATION OF LICENSE
6. The State Board of Dental Examiners may
refuse to issue licenses or may suspend or revoke
the same for any of the following causes :
1. The presentation to the board of any diploma ,
license or certificate illegally or fraudulently
obtained , or one obtained from an institution which
is not reputable , or an unrecognized or irregular
institution or state board , or the practice of any
fraud or deception . Any person who shall assist
any applicant to cheat or deceive the examining
board or any member thereof or who shall give or
attempt to give assistance to any applicant in the
course of his or her examination or assist any
applicant to fraudulently obtain any license to which
said applicant is not entitled shall upon conviction
thereof be adjudged guilty of a misdemeanor .
2. The commission of a criminal operation or
conviction of a felony involving moral turpitude , or
chronic or persistent inebrity or addiction to drugs ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 299
or if the person holding such license shall advertise
to practice dentistry without causing pain , or shall
in any other manner advertise with a view of de-
ceiving or defrauding the public , or advertise to use
any drug , nostrum, patent or other proprietary
drug or medicine be guilty of any conduct likely
to deceive or defraud the public , or which disquali-
fies the applicant to practice with safety to the peo-
ple , or who employs directly or indirectly any un-
registered or unlicensed person to practice dentis-
try in his or her office , or be guilty of splitting a
professional fee . In all proceedings for a suspen-
sion or revocation of license , the holder thereof shall
be given thirty ( 30 days ) days ' notice to prepare
for a hearing , and he shall be heard in person or by
counsel or by both . The President and Secretary of
the State Board of Dental Examiners shall have the
power to administer oaths , in the hearing of all
matters arising in the course of their duties , and in
such trials as are herein referred to the State Board
of Dental Examiners may take oral or written proof
for or against the complainant , as it may deem will
best present the facts . The Board of Dental Exam-
iners shall have the right and power to issue sub-
poenas and thereby compel the attendance of per-
sons before it for the purpose of examination into
any facts or conditions which may be properly pend-
ing before said board for its action . In all of re-
fusal to issue license , suspension or revocation , the
applicant or holder may appeal to the Governor of
the state within thirty ( 30 ) days after such action
by the State Board of Dental Examiners .
FAILURE TO REGISTER LICENSE
§ 7. Any failure , neglect or refusal on the part
of any person obtaining a license to practice den-
tistry from the State Board of Dental Examiners ,

300 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
to register said license with the county clerk of
some county in this state within ninety ( 90 )
days from the date of the issual of such license , on
removal to another county within the State , to
cause his license , to be registered in the same man-
ner as above provided , shall work a forfeiture of
such license , and no license when once forfeited shall
be restored except upon payment to the said board
of the sum of twenty -five ( $ 25.00 ) dollars for such
neglect , failure , or refusal to register such license .
EXAMINATION FEES AND COMPENSATION TO
MEMBERS OF THE BOARD .
§ 8. In order to provide the means for carrying
out and enforcing the provisions of this Act , the
said board shall charge each person applying for an
examination for a license to practice dentistry in
this state an examination fee of Forty ( $40.00 )
Dollars , and in addition thereto , a fee of Five
( $5.00 ) Dollars for every license and Five ( $ 5.00 )
Dollars for every duplicate license issued by said
board, and out of the funds coming into possession
of the said board under the provisions of this Act ,
the members of said board shall each receive as com-
pensation the sum of Five ( $5.00 ) Dollars for
each day actually engaged in duties of the office ,
and all necessary expenses incurred in attending
the meetings of said board ; provided that the Sec-
retary of the board shall be allowed a reasonable
salary to be fixed by the board and no per diem :
all expenses shall be paid from the fees and penal-
ties received and recovered by the board under the
provisions of this Act ; provided that no part of
said expenses shall be paid out of the State Treas-
ury. All money received in excess of said per diem
allowances and other expenses , herein provided ,
shall be held by the Secretary-Treasurer of said

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 301
board as a special fund for meeting the other ex-
penses of said board , and for such use as the said
board may deem necessary in the enforcement of
this Act , and said board shall make an annual report
of its proceedings to the Governor on or before the
31st day of December each year , showing all money
received and disbursed by it pursuant of this Act .
RENEWAL CERTIFICATE OF LICENSE
9. On or before the 31st day of December each
year, every dentist legally licensed to practice
dentistry in this state shall transmit to the Secretary
of the said Board of Dental Examiners his signature
and address , together with the fee of Three ( $ 3.00 )
Dollars and the number of his or her resignation
certificate , and receive therefore a renewal license
certificate . Provided that the license shall not be
renewed , if the holder thereof has not been in active
practice for five (5 ) years immediately preceding .
Any license granted by said board shall be cancelled
and annulled if the holder thereof fails to secure the
renewal certificate herein provided for by March
1st of each year , provided any license thus cancelled
may be restored by the board upon the payment of
fee of Twenty -Five ( $25.00 ) Dollars if paid within
one year after such cancellation .
PENALTY FOR FRAUD IN OBTAINING CERTIFICATE
OF REGISTRATION .
§ 10. Any person filing or attempting to file as
his own the diploma or license of another , or a
forged affidavit of identification or qualification ,
shall be deemed guilty of a felony and be punish-
able upon conviction by imprisonment in the peni-
tentiary of not less than one ( 1 year ) nor more than
five (5) years .

302 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
DENTISTRY DEFINED .
§ 11. Any person shall be regarded as practic-
ing dentistry , within the meaning of this act , who
shall diagnose or profess to diagnose , X- Ray , or
treat or profess to treat , any of the diseases or
lesions of the oral cavity , teeth , gums , maxillary
bones ( or their dependent tissues ) , or extract teeth ,
or shall prepare or fill cavities in human teeth , cor-
rect malposition of teeth or jaws or supply artificial
teeth as substitutes for natural teeth , or administer
anesthetics , general or local , or any other practice
included in the curricula of recognized dental col-
leges , or who shall use the word " Dentist , " Dental
surgeon , the letters D. D. S. , or other letters or titles
in connection with his or her name or in any other
manner represent themselves as being engaged in
the practice of dentistry . Provided , that nothing
in this Act shall be so construed as to prevent regu-
larly licensed physicians and surgeons from extract-
ing teeth or treating any diseases coming within
the province of the practice of medicine .
EXEMPTION FROM JURY SERVICE .
$ 12. All dentists of this state shall be exempt
from service as jurors in any of the courts of this
state .
SIGNATURES , SEAL , ETC. , ON LICENSE .
§ 13. All licenses issued by said board shall bear
a serial number , the full name of the applicant , the
date of the issue , the seal of the board, and be
signed by a majority of the members and attested
by its President and Secretary.
UNLAWFUL TO PRACTICE UNDER NAME OF A COMPANY .
14. It shall be unlawful for any person or per-
sons to practice or offer to practice dentistry or

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 303
dental surgery under the name of any company ,
association , or corporation , and every person or
persons practicing or offering to practice dentistry
or dental surgery , shall practice under his or her
own respective name or names only, and shall not
use his name in connection with that of another
dentist .
DENTAL RECIPROCITY .
$ 15. Said board may in its discretion issue a
license to practice dentistry without examination
other than clinical to a legal and ethical practitioner
of dentistry who removes to Kentucky from another
state or territory of the United States , whose stand-
ard of requirement is equal of that of Kentucky ,
and in which he or she conducted a legal and ethical
practice of dentistry for at least five ( 5 years ) years
immediately preceding his or her removal , provided
such applicant shall present a certificate from the
dental board of a like board of the state or terri-
tory from which he or she removes , certifying that
he or she is a legal , competent and ethical dentist ,
and of good moral character : and provided further ,
that such certificate is presented to the Kentucky
State Board of Dental Examiners within six ( 6 )
months from the date of its issue , and that the
board of such other state or territory shall permit
in like manner by law the recognition of licenses
issued by the Kentucky State Board of Dental
Examiners when presented to such other board by
legal practitioners of dentistry from this state who
may wish to remove to or practice in such other
state or territory .
RECIPROCITY LEAVING STATE .
§ 16. Any one who is a legal , ethical and compe-
tent practitioner of dentistry in the state of Ken-

304 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tucky , and of good moral character , and known to
the Board of Dental Examiners as such , who shall
desire to change his or her residence to another state
or territory , or foreign country, shall upon appli-
cation to the said Board of Dental Examiners of
this state , receive a special certificate over the sig-
nature of the president and secretary of said board
and bearing its seal , which shall attest the facts
above mentioned in section 15 (fifteen ) and give the
data upon which he or she was registered and
licensed .
RECIPROCITY FEES .
17. The fees for issuing a license to a legal
practitioner from another state , as provided in sec-
tion 15 ( fifteen ) , shall be sixty ( $ 60.00 ) dollars ,
and the fee for issuing a certificate to the legal
practitioner in this state as provided in section 16 ,
shall be five ( $5.00 ) dollars , and in each case the fee
shall be paid in cash before the license or certificate
respectively shall be issued .
PENALTY FOR PRACTICING WITHOUT LICENSE OR
CERTIFICATE .
§ 18. Any person who shall practice or attempt
to practice dentistry in this state without having
been licensed and registered for that purpose , as
herein provided , or who shall violate any of the pro-
visions of this Act , for which no specific penalty
has been provided herein , shall be subject to fine of
not less than one hundred ( $ 100.00 ) dollars nor
more than five hundred ( $500.00 ) dollars for the
first offense . Each offense shall constitute a prac-
ticing of dentistry within the meaning of this Act ,
and each day that a person shall hold himself out
as practicing dentistry within the meaning of this

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 305
act ; and each day that a person shall hold himself
out as practicing in any name except his own shall
be deemed a separate offense . Any person who
shall be guilty of a second offense shall be deemed
guilty of a felony and upon conviction shall be
punished by a fine of one hundred ( $ 100.00 ) dollars
nor more than five hundred ( $ 500.00 ) dollars or im-
prisonment of six ( 6 ) months or both . The open-
ing of an office for the purpose of practicing
dentistry , or to announce to the public in any way
a readiness to do any act defined herein as being
dentistry , shall be deemed to engage in the practice
of dentistry , within the meaning of this act .
DISPOSITION OF FEES .
19. All fees , imposed and collected under the
provisions of this Act , shall be paid to the Secre-
tary - Treasurer of the Kentucky State Board of
Dental Examiners for the use of said Board .
DRUGGISTS MAY FILL DENTISTS ' PRESCRIPTIONS .
§ 20. Legally licensed druggists of this state
may fill prescriptions of legally licensed dentists of
this state for any drug necessary to the practice of
dentistry . Dentists may sign death certificates the
same as physicians , when necessary in the line of
their profession .
§ 21. This Act shall not prevent students from
performing dental operations under the supervision
of competent instructors within the dental school ,
college , or dental department of a university recog-
nized by the Kentucky State Board of Dental
Examiners : Provided , the board at its discretion
may authorize the students of any dental college ,
dental school , or dental department of a university
to practice dentistry , in any of the state or munici-

306 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
pal institutions or public schools or under the board
of health , or public clinic, or charitable institution ;
provided they are at all times under the direct super-
vision of a licensed dentist in Kentucky , who is an
instructor of the institution at which the students
are studying .
The provisions of this act are severable , and if
any of its provisions shall be held unconstitutional
the decisions of courts shall not affect or impair any
of the remaining provisions of this act . It is hereby
declared as a legislative intent that this act would
have been adopted had not such unconstitutional
provisions been included therein .
REPEALING FORMER DENTAL LAWS .
§ 22. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
this Act are hereby repealed .
Approved March 3 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 35 .
AN ACT to amend and Re-enact Section One Thousand Five
Hundred and Ninety -Nine a-Eleven of the Kentucky Statutes
compiled by the Honorable John D. Carroll .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one thousand five hundred and
ninety-nine a -eleven of the Kentucky Statutes com-
piled by the Honorable John D. Carroll , be amended
and re enacted , so that said Section as amended and
re-enacted shall read as follows :
The state board of embalming may, upon suffi-
cient proof of the violation of any of the provisions
of this act, or of the violation by an embalmer or
undertaker of any rule or regulation prescribed by
the said board for the preparation , embalming ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 307
shipping , or burial of any dead human body or
management of funerals , or any embalmer or under-
taker who shall procure , or assist another in pro-
curing registration either as an embalmer or under-
taker for himself or another , by making or causing
to be made any false representations as to the char-
acter or qualifications of any such applicant , or any
embalmer or undertaker who shall be convicted of
a felony , or who is proven to be and addict to habit-
forming drugs , or who is a habitual drunkard , re-
voke the license of the person so offending, after
due notice to the offender and a full and fair hear-
ing of the charges against him .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 36 .
AN ACT repealing Section 2739f -4 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , including all and singular sub -sections thereof ,
being all of Chapter 26 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1922 ; and enacting in lieu thereof an act creating a com-
mission of five members to be known as "My Old Kentucky
Home Commission ," fixing the qualifications and terms of
office of the members of said Commission , and prescribing the
powers and duties of said Commission ; and declaring an
emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 2739f -4 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , including all and sigular sub-
sections thereof , being Chapter 26 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1922 , be and the same
hereby is repealed .
§ 2. There hereby is created a commission to be
known as " My Old Kentucky Home Commission . "
The said Commission shall be composed of the Gov-
ernor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and four
members to be appointed by the Governor and con,

308 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
firmed by the Senate , each of whom shall be a resi-
dent of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and must
have resided in the Commonwealth of Kentucky for
at least ten years next preceeding his or her appoint-
ment , and must be reasonably conversant with the
history of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . The
Governor as a member of said Commission , shall be
entitled to a vote only in cases of tie vote among the
other members . The members of the said Com-
mission shall be appointed by the Governor
immediately after the effective date of this act . The
term of office of each member shall be four years
from the date of appointment and qualification .
Each and every member of said Commission shall
be eligible for re -appointment . The position as
members of said Commission shall not be construed
as incompatible with any other official position or
office within this Commonwealth . The members of
said Commission shall serve without compensation ;
and a majority thereof shall constitute a quorum
for the transaction of business . The Commission
shall meet as soon as practicable after appointment ,
qualification and confirmation ; and shall organize
by electing one of its members as vice -chairman ,
and another as secretary . The Commission shall
also elect a treasurer . The Governor shall be chair-
man .
§ 3. The Commission is hereby empowered and
authorized to employ such assistants and employees
as it may deem necessary to efficiently and eco-
nomically discharge the duties imposed upon it by
law ; and it is empowered and authorized to fix the
salary and compensation of those so employed , with
salary or compensation shall be paid out of the
State Treasury upon certification by the Commis-
sion to the Auditor of Public Accounts , and charged
to the appropriation which may be made by the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 309
General Assembly for the use and benefit of the
Commission .
§ 4. All monies received by the Commission , no
matter from what source , shall be deposited with
the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and shall be paid out only on proper certification
made by the Commission to the Auditor of Public
Accounts .
§ 5. The Commission shall have the right to re-
ceive donations and gifts for the improvement and/
or maintenance of the property ; and shall have
the power to institute , promote and/or advance an
" Old Kentucky Home Memorial Association , " and
provide for both life and annual membership therein ,
and fix the fees for such membership ; but the fee
for a life membership in such an association shall
be $ 25.00 and the fee for an annual membership in
such an association shall be $ 5.00 . All sums re-
ceived as a result of the payment of membership
fees in such association , shall be used by the Com-
mission for the improvement and maintenance of
the said memorial , all vouchers to be paid through
the office of the Auditor of Public Accounts and
Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . All
gate receipts shall be handled in a like manner . No
member of such " Old Kentucky Home Memorial
Association " shall have any vote or voice in the
management of the affairs of the Commission , but
the names of all members of such association shall
be kept upon an official register at " My Old Ken-
tucky Home . " The Governor is hereby authorized
and empowered to appoint an Executive Commit-
tee , and such other committees as he may deem best .
§ 6. The regular meeting place of the Commis-
sion shall be at "My Old Kentucky Home " near
Bardstown , Kentucky ; but the Commission may
hold other meetings in the State as may be deemed

310 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY .
best . This Commission shall hold regular meetings
at least every two months , and special or called
meetings may be held when such a call is made either
by the Governor or by three members of the Com-
mission .
§ 7. The treasurer shall be required to execute a
bond in a sum of not less than Five Thousand
( $5,000.00 ) Dollars , the bond to be approved by the
Commission and the premiums on said bond shall
be paid out of any funds at the disposal of the Com-
mission . The treasurer , at the end of each month ,
shall deliver to each member of the Commission and
to the Auditor of Public Accounts , and the State
Treasurer , a written , itemized statement showing all
receipts and disbursements of funds .
$§ 8. Any vacancy in said Commission , resulting
from death , resignation or other cause , shall be
filled for the unexpired term thus vacated , by ap-
pointment to be made by the Governor .
§ 9. Nothing in this act shall be construed as
revoking or withdrawing the acceptance of title to
the estate known as " Federal Hill , " together with
all of the buildings and appurtenances thereon , in-
cluding the mansion house , which estate is more
particularly described in a deed of conveyance made
on the 22nd day of March , 1921 , by and between
Mrs. Madge Frost , party of the first part , and the
members of the old Kentucky Home Commission ,
parties of the second part ; which deed is of record
in the office of the Clerk of the County Court of Nel-
son County in Deed Book No. 88 at page 580 ; which
acceptance of title is expressly made by the General
Assembly by Section 1 , Chapter 26 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1922 .
10. The Commission created and provided for
in this act shall succeed to all the rights , powers ,
privileges and duties of the Commission appointed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 311
and provided for pursuant to the provisions of
Chapter 26 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1922 , and shall succeed to and be vested with , as
herein set out , the title to all the property , money ,
subscriptions , donations and endowments of said
former Commission .
§ 11. If any section , clause , sentence , paragraph
or provision of this act shall be held invalid or
unconstitutional , such fact shall not affect the re-
mainder of the act .
$ 12 . Inasmuch as there is criticism as to the
economical propriety of the expenditure of some of
the funds of the present Commission , the Commis-
sion appointed under this act shall immediately
take over the possession and control of said prop-
erty ; and because of that condition , an emergency
hereby is declared to exist and this act shall take
effect immediately upon its passage , approval by the
Governor and the qualification of the commissioners
herein provided for .
Approved February 19 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 37 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 2622 of the Kentucky
Statutes complied by the Honorable John D. Carroll .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2622 of the Kentucky Statutes
compiled by the Honorable John D. Carroll , be
amended and re-enacted so that said section as
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
2622. The Kentucky board of pharmacy shall
meet in the month of January of each year , and
organize by the election of a president , secretary

312 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
and treasurer , who shall be elected for a term of
one year , and shall perform the duties prescribed by
the Board . The president and treasurer shall be
elected from the members of the board , but the sec-
retary need not be a member of the board . It shall
be the duty of the said board to examine all appli-
cants for registration , to grant certificates of regis-
tration to such persons as may be entitled to the
same under the provisions of this act ; to report
annually to the Governor and to the Kentucky
pharmaceutical association upon the conditions of
pharmacy in the Commonwealth , which report shall
also furnish a record of the proceedings of said
board for the year , and also the names of all per-
sons duly registered under this act , and shall include
an account of all moneys received and expended by
the board . The board shall keep a book of registra-
tion in which shall be entered the name and place of
business of all persons registered under this act .
The said board shall hold meetings for the examina-
tion of applicants for registration , and the trans-
action of such other business as shall pertain to its
duties at least semi -annually on the second Tuesday
of January and July of each year , in such cities as
the Board may select , and shall continue in session
from day to day until its business is disposed of .
The records of said board, or a copy of any part
thereof, certified by the secretary to be a true copy ,
attested by the seal of the board , shall be accepted
as competent evidence in all courts of this common-
wealth . The said board shall have power to make
by-laws for the proper execution of its duties under
this act . Three members of said board shall consti-
tute a quorum .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 313
CHAPTER 38 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Sections two hundred and six-
teen a-one ; two hundred and sixteen a-seven ; two hundred
and sixteen aa-sixteen ; two hundred and sixteen aa -twenty-
five ; two hundred and sixteen aa-twenty-seven ; two hundred
and sixteen aa -twenty -eight , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
nineteen hundred and thirty Edition ; and to repeal Sections
two hundred and sixteen aa-four ; two hundred and sixteen aa-
five ; two hundred and sixteen aa-six , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , nineteen hundred and thirty Edition , relating to the
charitable , penal and eleemosynary institutions ; creating the
Department of Public Welfare , and providing its duties and
transfering and vesting in the Department of Public Welfare
all of the powers and authority now vested by law in the
Board of Charities and Correction , and declaring an emer-
gency , and repealing all laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with to the extent of such conflict ; and fixing the compensa-
tion of the Commissioners , Board and Chairman of the Board .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One . That Section two hundred and sixteen a-
one of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes (Baldwin's Re-
vision of nineteen hundred and thirty ) being Sec-
tion one of Chapter seven of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of nineteen hundred and twenty , be
and the same is hereby repealed , amended and re-
enacted so that the same when so amended and
re -enacted shall read as follows :
There shall be , and there hereby is created a
Board to be known as " Department of Public Wel-
fare " which shall be composed of five ( five ) mem-
bers , all of whom shall be residents of Kentucky
and not less than thirty (thirty) years of age . The
members of said board shall be appointed by the
Governor , by and with the consent of the Senate ;
one ( one ) for a period of one ( one ) year ; one ( one)
for a period of two ( two ) years ; one ( one ) for a
period of three ( three ) years ; and two ( two ) for a

314- COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
period of four ( four ) years from the date of ap-
pointment , unless sooner removed for cause , and
thereafter all appointments , except to fill vacancies ,
caused by death , resignation or removal , shall be
for a full term of four ( four ) years .
Each member of the board, except the chairman ,
shall receive an annual salary of Two Thousand
(Two Thousand dollars ) dollars per year, to be paid
monthly out of the State Treasury , and the chair-
man shall receive an annual salary of Five Thou-
sand ( Five Thousand dollars ) dollars per year , to
be paid monthly out of the State Treasury .
The chairman and members of said board shall
also be paid their actual and necessary expenses
while in the discharge of their official duties . All
of the power and authority vested in the State
Board of Charities and Corrections by Chapter six-
teen , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , nineteen hundred
and thirty Edition , is hereby transferred to and
vested in the Department of Public Welfare . The
powers and duties vested in the Commissioner of
Institutions by Section three thousand nine hundred
and ninety-two , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , nine-
teen hundred and thirty , edition , are hereby trans-
ferred to and vested in the chairman of the board
of the Department of Public Welfare .
Two . That Section two hundred and sixteen
a- seven of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ( Baldwin's
Revision of nineteen hundred and thirty ) , being
Section seven of Chapter seven of the Acts of the
General Assembly of nineteen hundred and twenty ,
be and the same is hereby repealed , amended and
re-enacted , so that the same when so amended and
re-enacted shall read as follows :
The board shall meet at least once a month and
at other times on the call of the chairman . The
Governor shall annually designate and appoint one

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 315
of the members of said board as chairman , and the
said chairman shall be required to give all of his
time to the business and affairs of the board , and
he shall reside in Frankfort , Kentucky . He shall
have general charge , subject to the supervision and
control of the board of the institutions of the state ,
which are under the jurisdiction of said board , and
shall perform such duties and exercise such power
as the board may, from time to time , direct .
The board shall adopt By -laws and regulations for
the transaction of its business and the manage-
ment of its affairs and for the management of the
institutions under its charge , as it may consider ex-
pedient and as are not inconsistent , with law . It
shall keep a record of all its proceedings and of all
receipts and disbursements made under its manage-
ment , which records shall be open at all times to
inspection by the Governor or a committee of the
Legislature to examine or inspect the same .
Four . That Section two hundred and sixteen
aa -sixteen of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes (Bald-
win's Revision of nineteen hundred and thirty ) ,
being Section sixteen of Chapter sixteen of the Acts
of the General Assembly of nineteen hundred and
twenty-eight , be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended and re-enacted , so that the same when so
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
The board shall provide for such officers and em-
ployees for each institution and for such clerical
and other assistants for itself as it may deem nec-
essary , except that the board may not dispense with
a superintendent and the receiver for each institu-
tion .
Five . That Section two hundred and sixteen
aa-twenty -five of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ( Bald-
win's Revision of nineteen hundred and thirty ) ,
being Section twenty -five of Chapter sixteen of the

316 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Acts of the General Assembly of nineteen hundred
and twenty -eight , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed , amended and re -enacted , so that the same
when so amended and re - enacted shall read as fol-
lows :
Appropriations made for the Department of Pub-
lic Welfare shall be payable by warrant drawn by
the Auditor . The Board may draw upon the Audi-
tor of Public accounts monthly for such sums of
money as are necessary to run the institutions under
its control , including the salaries due all members
of the board , officers and employees . The order
upon the Auditor shall specify the purpose for
which the expenditure was made and the amount
and the persons to whom paid . The order shall be
signed and approved by the chairman of the board
and shall not be issued except by order of the board
entered of record , specifying the amount and the
purpose for which the expenditure was made and to
whom . When such order is received by the Auditor
he shall draw his warrant on the State Treasury in
favor of the person specified in said order and the
amount directed to be paid , and said warrant must
specify for what purpose the money is to be used
and the fund from which said money is paid , and
the State Treasurer shall , out of the money in the
State Treasury , appropriated to the Department of
Public Welfare , pay said warrants . Said orders
and warrants shall be carefully preserved and shall
become vouchers for the amounts paid to be speci-

CHAPTER 39 .
AN ACT to amend Section 7 of Chapter 35 , Acts of the General
Assembly of 1914 ( Section 415B-7 , Ky. Stats . 1922-30 ) relating
to Pensions for Confederate soldiers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 7 of Chapter 35 , Acts of the
General Assembly of 1914 , the same being Section
415B -7 , Ky . Stats . 1922-30 , be and the same is now
amended and re -enacted and when amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
That when a person to whom a pension has been
granted shall die , the amount due such pensioner
at the date of his death shall be paid to an adminis-
trator who shall be appointed by the County Judge
of the county where the pensioner resided at the
time of his death , and also to such administrator the
amount of funeral and burial expenses to the extent
of not more than one hundred dollars in each case .
The Commissioner of Pensions shall before the next
succeeding date for the payment of pensions , fur-
nish to said administrator a voucher for the amount
due the pensioner at the time of his death , which
shall be filled out and returned to said Commis-
sioner , together with a properly verified claim of the

320 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
undertaker for the funeral and burial expenses of
the pensioner , which same shall be approved by the
County Judge of the County in which said pensioner
resided , or died or was buried , all of which shall by
the Commissioner be filed with the Auditor of Pub-
lic Accounts , who shall issue his warrant to the ad-
ministrator for the unpaid portion of the pension ,
and a warrant to the claimant for the funeral and
burial expenses , or in the latter case , upon proper
and satisfactory proof of the fact , the warrant may
be issued to any one paying such funeral or burial
expenses .
The administrator shall distribute the pension
money to such persons as are legally entitled
thereto .
In case of the death of a pensioner who at the
time of his death there is due no unpaid pension
money , then the funeral and burial expenses , shall
be paid as is above provided .
The sums paid for funeral and burial expenses
shall be paid out of any fund now or hereafter ap-
propriated for Confederate pensions .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws and Acts in con-
flict herewith are now repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

page 521 , and said Section 6 of the 1928 re -enacted Act is

Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 321
§ 1. That Section 1 of Chapter 150 , Acts of 1928 ,
page 521 , now section 415b -6 of the 1930 Edition of
Kentucky Statutes be amended and re -enacted so
that when so re -enacted it will read as follows :
That any Confederate veteran who has been an
actual bona fide resident of this State continuously
since January 1 , 1907 , and who did actual military
service in the Confederate army from the date of
his enlistment till the close of the war or until he
was honorably discharged as an officer or private
soldier or sailor in the military and naval service
of the Confederate States of America in the war of
1861-65 , or the widow of such officer or private
soldier or sailor to whom she was married prior to
January 1 , 1900 , and who was living with him as
his lawful wife at the time of his death and who has
not re-married again since the death of her vet-
eran husband , but if re -married , again becomes a
widow by the death of or divorce from her husband ,
shall be paid out of the state Treasury the sum of
twenty dollars per month upon proof of the fact
according to forms and regulations prescribed by
this Act and conditions therein ; Provided , that any
officer or private soldier or sailor who was pre-
vented from so serving until the close of the war , or
until he was honorably discharged by reason of
wounds or disease contracted in line of duty , which
rendered him unable to perform further military
service , shall be entitled to the benefits of this Act
as though he had served until the close of the war or
until honorably discharged ; and provided , further ,
that any officer or private soldier or sailor who
deserted or was dishonorably discharged , shall not
be entitled to the benefits of this Act unless he be
re -enlisted or returned to the service in the Con-
federate army and served until the close of the war
or until he was honorably discharged or was pre-

vented from so serving by wounds received or dis-
ease contracted in line of duty which rendered him
unable to perform further military service .
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 41 .
AN ACT to give authority to the Board of Charities and Cor-
rections or its successor to employ Chriopractors in the
Charitable and Penal Institutions , to fix salaries and prescribe
duties , for same .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That the Board of Charities and
Corrections or its successor be and it is hereby au-
thorized to employ regularly licensed and registered
chiropractors to treat and care for patients in the
Charitable and Penal institutions of this State ; and
any and all chiropractors so employed in any of the
state charitable and penal institutions shall receive
a salary equal to doctors of other schools of healing
who are employed in such institutions .
Section Two . The Board may employ or author-
ize the employment and prescribe the duties of any
chiropractor or chiropractors in any of the institu-
tions under its control .
Section Three . All acts or parts of acts in con-
flict with this act are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 44.
AN ACT relating to the advancement of credit against labor
performed or to be performed .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person , firm or corporation engaged in
any trade or business , either directly or indirectly ,
may issue , sell , give or deliver , upon the request
of any employee of such person , firm or corporation ,
to such employee as a medium of credit, in payment
for labor performed or for labor to be performed ,
non -transferable scrip , tokens , drafts , orders or
coupons , payable and redeemable in merchandise
only by the employee to whom issued ; provided ,
that the issuance of any such non -transferable scrip ,
tokens , drafts , orders or coupons by any such per-
son , firm or corporation , shall be construed , taken
and held in all courts and places to be a promise to
pay, by the person , firm or corporation issuing
same , to the employee to whom issued , in lawful
money of the United States or check , upon demand
and surrender by said employee of such scrip or any
unused portion thereof, at such regular settlement
day or pay day when the same would have been due
in cash had not the said order, scrip or token been
issued .
Any person, firm or corporation failing and refus-
ing to pay the employee to whom is issued such non-
transferable scrip , tokens , drafts , orders or coupons ,
or any unused portion of the same, for which labor
has been performed on their regular settlement day

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 327
or pay day, shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon convcition shall be punished by a fine of
not less than twenty nor more than fifty dollars ,
and for a second or a subsequent offense shall be
fined not less than twenty-five nor more than one
hundred dollars .
All acts or parts of acts inconsistent with this act
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

the following :
"and a memorandum of such dissolution , signed
by an authorized officer or agent of the corporation ,
and attested by the Clerk or his Deputy , shall be
made on the margin of the record in the office of
the Clerk of County Court , where its articles of

shall send written notice thereof, signed by its
authorized officer or agent and a copy of the action
of the corporation authorizing such dissolution ,
together with one copy of said newspaper notice as

330 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
solved except from the time of its compliance with

So that Section 561 as amended will read as fol-
lows :
" 561. Termination of Existence of Corporation
-How Effected . Any corporation organized under
this chapter may, by the consent in writing of
the owners of the majority of its shares of stock ,
unless otherwise provided in the articles of incor-
poration , or amendments thereto , close its business
and wind up its affairs ; and when any corporation
expires by the terms of the articles of incorpora-
tion, or by the voluntary act of its stockholders ,
it may thereafter continue to act for the purpose of
closing up its business , but for no other purpose ;
and it shall be the duty of the officers to settle up
its affairs and business as speedily as possible ; and
they shall cause notice to be published , for at least
once a week for four consecutive weeks , in some
newspaper printed and published in the county , if
any, of the fact that it is closing up its business ;
and a memorandum of such dissolution , signed by
an authorized officer or agent of the corporation ,
and attested by the Clerk or his Deputy , shall be
made on the margin of the record in the office of the
Clerk of the County Court , where its articles of
incorporation . are recorded ; and the corporation
shall send written notice thereof , signed by its
authorized officer or agent , and a copy of the action
of the corporation authorizing such dissolution , to-
gether with one copy of said newspaper notice
as published , to the Secretary of State , who shall
file same , and note such dissolution on the records ,
in his office ; and no corporation shall be deemed to
be dissolved except from the time of its compliance

with the provisions of this Section . For each attes-
tation so made by the Clerk of the County Court ,
he shall receive twenty-five cents ; and all debts and
demands against the corporation shall be paid in
full before the officers receive anything ."
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 49 .
AN ACT to require a public utility company to pay interest at
rate of six ( 6) per cent annually on amounts they exact as
'deposits as security for the payment of gas , electric and
water accounts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That public utilities , such as gas , electric
and water companies shall be required to pay
holders of certificates of deposits six ( 6 ) per cent
annually on amounts exacted from patrons for gas ,
electric and water accounts .
§ 2. Failure to comply with the above section
shall subject the Utility Company violating said
provision to indictment and prosecution and upon
conviction to a fine of not less than One Hundred
($ 100.00 ) Dollars for each offense .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 50.
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Section Thirty , Chapter One
Hundred Seventy -One , Acts General Assembly, 1893 , being
Section Five Hundred Sixty -Seven of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1922 and 1930 , relating to private corporations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 30 , Chapter 171 Acts of General
Assembly of 1893 , being section 567 , Kentucky Stat-
utes , edition of 1922 and 1930 , be , and the same is

332 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
hereby repealed , and in lieu thereof the following
enacted so said section when re-enacted will read
as follows :
Section 567. Business Corporation May Engage
in ; What Land May be Held .- No corporation shall
engage in business other than that expressly author-
ized by its articles of incorporation or amendments
thereto ; nor shall any corporation , directly or in-
directly , engage in or carry on in any way the busi-
ness of banking, or insurance of any kind , unless it
has become organized under the laws relating to
banking and insurance ; nor shall any corporation
hold or own any real estate , except such as may be
necessary and proper for carrying on its legitimate
business , for a longer period than five years under
penalty of escheat . Any action to escheat real estate
held contrary to the provisions of this section shall
be brought in the name of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the circuit court of the county in which
said real estate , or a part thereof, is situated . Be-
fore any such action may be instituted , the Attorney
General, or the Auditor of Public Accounts , shall
give written notice to the corporation holding the
title to said real estate , that , unless said property
is disposed of within two years after receipt of said
written notice , an action will be commenced to for-
feit said real estate and no such action shall be
brought or prosecuted before the expiration of said
period . Said notice shall contain a brief description
of said real estate , and shall be served as provided
by Section Six Hundred Twenty -Eight of Civil
Code of Practice . The serving of the notice , as
herein provided , shall in no way invalidate or impair
the corporation's title to said real estate , nor
abridge its right to convey the same before the
institution of such action .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

BILL to provide an appeal from the Board of Equalization cities

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 2 of Chapter 144 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1930 be and the same
hereby is amended and re- enacted so that the same
shall read as follows : It shall be the duty of the
Secretary of State to record in a book kept in his
office for that purpose all such deeds , and in another
book similarly kept , all leases , and to file , safely keep
and have accessible the originals of all such records ,
judgments , deeds , maps , and abstracts of title , as
well as the book in which such deeds are recorded .
It shall be the duty of the Secretary of State to index
and cross index such original papers and record
books , and to index the matters therein concerned
or recorded according to the county wherein the
property is located and also according to the use or
department of the state government using such prop-
erty . Such records and indexes shall be public
records , but shall not be moved from the office of the
Secretary of State , and whenever any of such
records shall be necessary as evidence , a true copy
thereof, certified to by the Secretary of State , shall
be used in lieu of the original record or paper .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

336 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 55.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact section 75 of Carroll's Kentucky
Criminal Code of Practice , Baldwin's 1932 revision , same be-
ing Chapter 44 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1924
as amended by Chapter 30 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1927 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 75 of Carroll's Kentucky
Criminal Code of Practice , Baldwin's 1932 revision ,
and being Chapter 44 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1924 as amended by Chapter 30 of the
Acts of the General Assembly of 1926 be amended
and re- enacted so that when so re-enacted it will
read :
"In all cases , in the circuit court , when the defend-
ant shall have been convicted of a felony, except in
cases where the jury has inflicted the death penalty
or life imprisonment ; and except cases where the
defendant has been convicted of robbery or burglary ,
or having in possession burglarious tools , as
denounced and punished by section 1159 of the 1930
edition of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's
edition ; and except convictions for robbery of bank,
or safe , as denounced by section 1159a of the same
edition of the Kentucky Statutes ; and except convic-
tions for assault with intent to rob , as denounced by
section 1160 of the same Statutes ; and except con-
victions for kidnaping and holding a person for
ransom, as denounced by section 1157a of the same
Statutes ; and except convictions for voluntary man-
slaughter where the punishment exceeds confinement
in the penitentiary for five years , he shall be per-
mitted to execute bond pending his appeal to the
Court of Appeals .
" The substance of such bond shall be that he will
render himself amenable to the orders and process

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 337
of the courts , and if his case is affirmed he will sur-
render himself to the sheriff of the county of his con-
viction within thirty days after the affirmance has
become final , to be taken by such sheriff to prison
as provided in the judgment of conviction or if his
case shall be reversed , he will appear in the next
term of the circuit court in which he was convicted ,
which does not begin within less than ten days of
the reversal of the judgment of the circuit court ,
again to answer said charge .
" The bond shall be for a penal sum sufficient to
secure the defendant's appearance or his surrender
to the court in execution of the judgment if it should
be affirmed , and it may be executed before the circuit
court or the trial judge at any time before the
record is filed in the Court of Appeals , and after that
time it may be executed before the Clerk of the Court
of Appeals , and in all cases where a bond is allow-
able hereunder it shall be the duty of the trial judge
to fix the sum for which it may be given by an order
made a part of the judgment, and which amount so
fixed shall govern the authority taking the bond as
herein provided for . In either case the bond may
be adjudged to be forfeited by order of the circuit
court for non -compliance with its terms by the
defendant ."
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
