CHAPTER 58.
AN ACT to fix and regulate the time for calling the docket of
felony cases and fixing the date for the trial thereof and
excepting therefrom that felony indictments may be tried at
the term the indictments are returned .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Hereafter the Judges of all the Circuit
Courts of this Commonwealth shall on the last day
of each term call the docket of all felony cases in
which the defendant or defendants are before the
court and to be tried at and during the next succeed-
ing term and fix date of trial for said cases . Pro-
vided however , felony indictments may be tried at
the term the indictments are returned .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 59 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section Two of Section six hun-
dred and six of Carroll's Kentucky Civil Code of Practice .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

340 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
$ 1. That section two of section six hundred and
six of Carroll's Kentucky Civil Code of Practice be ,
and the same hereby is , amended and re -enacted so
that the same , when amended and re -enacted , shall
read as follows , to -wit :
Two (Infant , lunatic or dead person - when party
may testify against ) . Subject to the provisions of
subsection seven of this section , no person shall
testify for himself concerning any verbal statement
of , or any transaction with , or any act done or
omitted to be done by an infant under fourteen
years of age, or by one who is of unsound mind or
dead when the testimony is offered to be given ex-
cept for the purpose , and to the extent , of affecting
one who is living , and who , when over fourteen
years of age and of sound mind, heard such state-
ment , or was present when such transaction took
place , or when such act was done or omitted , and
except " in actions for personal injury , death or
damage to property by negligence or tortious acts ,
unless a. (Infant-provision as to . ) The infant or
his guardian shall have testified against such per-
son , with reference to such statement , transaction or
act , or, b . (Person of unsound mind . ) The person
of unsound mind shall, when of sound mind , have
testified against such person , with reference thereto ,
or c . ( Decedent-provision as to . ) The decedent ,
or a representative of, or someone interested in , his
estate , shall have testified against such person , with
reference thereto , or d . ( Agent of decedent or per-
son of unsound mind . ) An agent of the decedent
or person of unsound mind, with reference to such
act or transaction , shall have testified against such
person , with reference thereto , or be living when
such person offers to testify , with reference thereto .
2. Owing to the fact that injustice results from
the application of the law as it now stands , and

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 341
there are numerous suits pending in the courts of
this Commonwealth in which such application will
result in grave injustice , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this act shall take effect and
be in force from and after its passage and approval
as required by law .
Approved March 11 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 60 .
AN ACT to amend section 191 of the Criminal Code of Practice
relating to the formation of the jury and providing for a
thirteenth juror in certain cases .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 191 of Carroll's Criminal Code of
Practice , Baldwin's 1927 edition, be amended and
re -enacted to read as follows :
When an issue of fact in a criminal prosecution is
about to be tried , the clerk shall draw , in the manner
directed by the Kentucky Statutes , the names of
twelve jurors , who , if not challenged by the parties ,
nor excused by the court , shall compose the trial
jury ; provided , that, at the sound discretion of the
court, in all capital or other felony cases, a
thirteenth juror may be selected and qualified and
throughout the trial shall be treated as one of the
jury , provided however that said thirteenth juror
shall take no part in the deliberations or verdict of
the jury except when one of the twelve has been
excused by the court upon some reasonable ground .
The said thirteenth juror shall be selected and
qualified as now provided by law for the other
jurors , save that no additional peremptory chal-
lenges shall be allowed on account of the selection
and qualification of such thirteenth juror .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

342 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 61 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 971-3 , 971-4 , 971-10 ,
971-12 and 971-13 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's
Revision of 1930 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 971-3 , 971-4 , 971-10 , 971-12 and
971-13 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's
Revision of 1930 , be and the same are hereby
amended and re -enacted so that when so amended
and re -enacted they shall read as follows :
Section 971-3 . When from any cause the judge of
any circuit court of continuous session or division
thereof , being in attendance , can not properly pre-
side in an action , proceeding or prosecution pending
in said court, or if either party shall file with the
clerk of the court his affidavit that the judge will not
afford him a fair and impartial trial , or will not
impartially decide an application for a change of
venue , the parties by agreement , may elect one of
the members of the bar of said court to hear and
try the case , proceeding or application ; and if any
of the parties to said action , proceeding or prosecu-
tion are non -resident defendants , who have not
entered their appearance , nor have been summoned ,
or are infant defendants , the attorney appointed to
defend for such non -residents , or guardian ad litem
for such infants , may agree with the other parties
to such action , proceeding or prosecution , upon a
member of the bar having all the qualifications of a
circuit judge , to try such action , proceeding or
prosecution . However , if the parties can not agree
upon a member of the bar to act as judge , as above
stated , or if no judge of said court or division
thereof is available , the clerk of the court shall at
once certify that fact to the Chief Justice of the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 343
Court of Appeals of Kentucky , who shall designate
as circuit judge a member of the local or state bar
having the qualifications of a circuit judge to try
such action , proceeding or prosecution , and the
judge so designated shall have all of the powers of
the regular judge of the court over which the mem-
ber of the bar is designated by the Chief Justice to
preside in such action , proceeding or prosecution ,
and shall receive for his services the compensation
hereinafter provided .
§ 2. Section 971-4 . When from any cause the
judge of any circuit court of other than courts of
continuous session fails to attend , or is unable for
any reason to hold the court , or if the parties litigant
can not agree upon an attorney to act as special
judge as provided in Section 971-6 of this act , the
clerk of said court shall at once notify the Chief
Justice of the Court of Appeals of Kentucky , who
shall, if no regular circuit judge is available , desig-
nate as circuit judge a member of the local or state
bar . having the qualifications of a circuit judge , to
hold said court during the time when the regular
judge shall fail to attend , or shall be , as above
stated , unable to preside over said court, and the
judge so designated shall hold said court, and shall
have all the powers of the regular judge of the court
over which he is designated by the Chief Justice .
The Chief Justice , in his discretion , in designating
as provided in this section , a special judge to try
any action, proceeding or prosecution in which the
regular circuit judge can not properly preside or in
which either party shall have filed with the clerk of
the court his affidavit that the judge will not afford
him a fair and impartial trial , or will not impartially
decide an application for a change of venue , may
designate such special judge to preside over the
trial of such action , proceeding or prosecution for

344 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
•
such time or purpose as the Chief Justice may desig-
nate or may , in his discretion , designate such special
judge to preside in the trial of such action , proceed-
ing or prosecution until its final determination on
such trial . Special judges designated by the Chief
Justice as provided for in this section shall receive
for their services the compensation hereinafter pro-
vided.
§3 . Section 971-10 . The commissions of regular
circuit judges as special judges , the designation of
special judges by the Chief Justice , the reason of
such designation , and the fact that the requisite
oaths have been taken , must be entered upon the
order books of the courts over which such special
judges are designated to preside .
All claims for compensation and expenses of at-
torneys or members of the bar designated as special
judges , and as provided for in Sections 971-5 and
971-7 of this act , shall be allowed and paid as pro-
vided by law for the payment of other claims against
the Commonwealth , but before such claims shall be
allowed by the auditor , the amounts thereof , the
number of days ' services rendered on the bench , to-
gether with a statement of expenses , shall be entered
upon the order book of the court over which such
special judge is designated to preside , and the same
shall be presented to and approved by the Chief
Justice of the Court of Appeals .
§ 4. Section 971-12 . It shall be the duty of every
regular circuit judge to call a special term of his
court , when at the close of any regular term , as
many as ten cases remain on the docket of his court
undisposed of and not called for trial , nor under
submission , and upon the failure of said regular
circuit judge so to do , and upon manifestation of
that fact by any of the parties litigant in such cases
or by counsel of record, by motion and notice in

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 345
writing to the Chief Justice of the Court of Appeals ,
the said Chief Justice is hereby empowered , in his
discretion , to call a special term of such court , for
the purpose of trying such cases or hearing any
motion or pleading in relation thereto , and to desig-
nate any regular circuit judge, not otherwise en-
gaged , to preside over such special term , or if no
regular circuit judge is available , the Chief Justice
may designate any member of the bar of this state
having all the qualifications of a regular circuit
judge to preside over such special term .
The order of the Chief Justice , calling a special
term of any circuit court as provided in this section ,
shall specify the court in which said special term
shall be held , when the same is to commence , and the
style of each case to be tried , or in which any motion ,
pleading, order or judgment is to be filed , made or
entered at said special term , but if all the cases on
the docket are to be before the court at such special
terms , a statement to that effect will be sufficient
without copying the entire docket in such notice .
Whenever a special term shall be ordered by the
Chief Justice of the Court of Appeals , as provided
in this section it shall be the duty of the moving
party to serve a copy of such order upon all parties
litigant affected thereby or upon counsel of record
in each case specified in the said order , or in lieu of
such notice , the Chief Justice may , in his discretion ,
direct that notice of such special term shall be given
by posting a copy of said order on the door of the
court house where such special term is to be held ,
within a reasonable time previous thereto .
§ 5. Section 971-13 . In each county of each
judicial district , except counties having circuit
courts of continuous session , there shall be held
each year the number of terms of the circuit court
provided for by law, and the term in any district

346 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
may be extended if the business requires so that it
does not interfere with any other term in the dis-
trict ; and whenever it is necessary to tranact the
business , a special term may be held in any county ,
either by an order entered of record at the last pre-
ceding regular term in the county or by notice
signed by the judge and posted at the court house
door of the county for ten days before the special
term is held . The order or notice shall specify the
day when the special term is to commence , and shall
give the style of each case to be tried , or in which
any motion, order or judgment is to be made or
entered at the said special term , and no other case
shall be tried , or motion , or order , or judgment
entered therein, unless by agreement of parties , but
if all the cases on the docket are to be before the
court at such special term , a statement to that effect
will be sufficient without copying the entire docket
in such notice . Grand juries shall be summoned and
criminal and penal causes shall be heard at but three
terms in each year in any county , to be fixed by
order of court, unless in an emergency the court may
otherwise direct ; and the grand or petit jurors may
be summoned for any special term by direction of
the judge ; provided , however , that whenever , in the
opinion of the regular judge of any circuit court,
there is such a congestion of the civil or criminal
docket in any such court as that it cannot be dis-
posed of by the court at its regular terms , as now
provided by law , or at any special term which may
be called and held as provided by this section then
and in every such case it shall be lawful for the
regular circuit judge or such court, by order entered
of record, at a regular term of such court , to call a
special term of said court to be held while a court is ,
or may be , in session in the same or some other
county in the district . Every such order calling

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 347
such special term shall specify the day when the
special term of the court shall commence , and the
time it shall continue , and shall give the style of
each case to be tried , or in which any motion, order
or judgment may be made or entered at such special
term , and no other case shall be tried , or motion
made , or order or judgment entered therein in any
case, even by agreement of parties , except upon
direction of the Chief Justice as provided by Sec-
tion 971-12 of this act, butif all the cases on the
docket are to be before the court at such special
term, a statement to that effect be sufficient without
copying the entire docket in such notice . The grand
jury and petit jury for the trial of criminal or ordi-
nary actions at such special term , shall be drawn at
the term at which such special term is called in the
manner and from as provided by law for drawing
of grand and petit jurors for the regular terms of
such court . Whenever the regular judge of such
court can not attend at any special term called under
this section , then a copy of the order calling such
term shall be forwarded by the clerk of such court
to the Chief Justice of the Court of Appeals , whose
duty it shall be to designate some other regular
circuit judge not then engaged in the trial of cases ,
or if no regular circuit judge be available at said
time , it shall be the duty of the Chief Justice to
designate some member of the bar of this state hav-
ing all the qualifications of a regular circuit judge
to hold the special term of such court, provided for
in this section . The special judge so designated
shall have all the powers of the regular judge of said
court .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

348 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 62.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 362 of the Criminal Code
of Practice in relation to the appellate jurisdiction of circuit
courts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 362 of the Criminal Code of
Practice be and the same hereby is amended and
re-enacted by adding thereto the following , to -wit :
Provided , also , that an appeal shall lie from the
Juvenile Session of the County Court to the Circuit
Court from all orders and judgments whereby any
infant , or other person , shall be restrained of his
liberty , or placed in the custody of any institution ,
or fined , or punished , in any manner , whether in a
criminal or quasi -criminal , civil , or other proceed-
ing , and said appeal shall be prosecuted in the man-
ner provided by this chapter , except that the tran-
script when filed in the office of the Clerk of the Cir-
cuit Court shall be accompanied by a motion for an
appeal, supported by an affidavit of merits , signed
by the appellant , or if he be an infant , by his parent
or guardian , or if such infant have no parent or
guardian , then by his next friend , which affidavit
shall state the nature of the controversy involved ;
that is to say , whether one of law or fact , and if one
of fact , the nature and extent of the facts in contro-
versy , so as to enable the Circuit Court or the pre-
siding judge thereof to determine whether an appeal
shall be allowed or denied ; so that when said section
is so amended and re -enacted the same shall read as
follows :
If a judgment against a defendant on a trial before
a county judge , or in a justice's court , or in a city or
police court , unless otherwise provided in the stat-
utes creating or regulating it , be for imprisonment

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 349
or for a fine of twenty dollars or more , he shall have
the right of appeal to the circuit court of the county
in which the judgment is rendered :
Provided , also , that an appeal shall lie from the
Juvenile Session of the County Court to the Circuit
Court from all orders and judgments whereby any
infant , or other person , shall be restrained of his
liberty , or placed in the custody of any institution ,
or fined , or punished , in any manner , whether in a
criminal or quasi -criminal , civil , or other proceed-
ing , and said appeal shall be prosecuted in the man-
ner provided by this chapter , except that the tran-
script when filed in the office of the Clerk of the Cir-
cuit Court shall be accompanied by a motion for an
appeal , supported by an affidavit of merits , signed
by the appellant , or if he be an infant , by his parent
or guardian , or if such infant have parent or
guardian , then by his next friend , which affidavit
shall state the nature of the controversy involved ;
that is to say , whether one of law or fact , and if one
of fact , the nature and extent of the facts in con-
troversy , so as to enable the Circuit Court or the
presiding judge thereof to determine whether an
appeal shall be allowed or denied .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 63.
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Sections 280 and 281 of Chap-
ter 8 of the Criminal Code of Practice relating to bills of
exceptions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 280 of the Criminal Code of

350 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Practice be and the same is amended and re-enacted
and as amended and re- enacted shall read as follows :
Upon the trial of criminal or penal prosecutions ,
either party may except to any decision of the court
by which the substantial rights of such party are
prejudiced .
§ 2. That section 281 of the Criminal Code of
Practice be and the same is amended and re-enacted
and as amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
The decision of the court upon challenges , and for
cause , or upon motions to set aside the indictment,
shall be subject to exception .
§ 3. All acts and parts of acts in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 64.
AN ACT relating to juries and amending and re -enacting Sec-
tions 2249 , 2251 and 2260 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930
edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 2249 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 edition , be and the same hereby is
amended and re -enacted and as amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
A grand jury shall be summoned at three regular
terms of circuit court , unless otherwise ordered by
the judge , by an order entered of record . In such
courts and branches thereof , having continuous ses-
sion , as are authorized by article 1 hereof to draw
and empanel a grand jury , it shall be done on the
first Monday of each month that such court is in
session , unless otherwise ordered by the judge .
§ 2. That section 2251 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 edition , be and the same hereby is

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 351
amended and re-enacted and as amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
Grand jurors shall be paid for their services each
three ( $3.00 ) dollars per day, and shall not remain
in session longer than six days at any regular term
of court, but the court in its discretion may if the
business before the grand jury requires it extend
the time of the grand jury not more than three days
longer, by an order showing the necessity for such
extension entered on the order book of the court .
The court may in its discretion in case of an emer-
gency call not more than three special sessions of the
grand jury for not more than three days at a time.
The court in calling a special grand jury shall set
forth in an order entered on the order book of the
court the reason for calling a special session of the
grand jury .
§ 3. That section 2260 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 edition , be and the same hereby is
amended and re -enacted and as amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
Jurors in circuit court shall receive three ( $3.00 )
dollars per day, and when kept together overnight
in custody of the sheriff , shall receive one ($1.00 )
dollar additional for each night they are kept to-
gether ; and in courts inferior to the circuits , fifty
( 50c ) cents for each case . In the circuit court a
jury fee of four ( $4.00 ) dollars shall be taxed as
costs against the unsuccessful party ; and in other
courts the cost of the jury shall be paid by the party
demanding the jury , and shall be taxed as costs
against the unsuccessful party ; but in such courts ,
if the same juror serves on more than two juries in
one day, he shall not be allowed any thing for such
excess .

352 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 4. All acts and parts of acts in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 66.
AN ACT to provide for the appointment of a curator for person
absent and not known to have been living for a year or more ;
and to prescribe the duties of such curator.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. When any person , resident of this Common-
wealth , or owning property lying or found in this
Commonwealth , has been absent from his last known
place of residence for a period of one year , and is
not known to have been living during said time , the
County Court of any County in which a personal
representative could be appointed for such person's
estate if deceased , may appoint a curator to collect
and preserve the estate of said absent person . The
Court shall take bond with good surety , from the
person appointed , for the full and faithful perform-
ance of the trust confided to him . An appeal from
an order of appointment may be taken as in the case
of an administrator , but this appeal shall not sus-
pend the powers of the curator .
§ 2. Before any curator shall be appointed for
an absent person , a written application requesting
the appointment shall be filed with the Clerk of the
County Court . Said application shall be verified
and shall recite the fact of such absence , the last
known address of such absent person , and the pres-
ence of property within this Commmonwealth , or
other ground requiring the appointment of such
Acts- 12

354 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
curator for the protection of the property rights of
such absent person . The application may be made
by any relative , creditor or debtor of the absent
person , or by any person in possession of or inter-
ested in any property or rights of said absent per-
son .
§ 3. Upon the filing with the Clerk of the County
Court of any such application , the Clerk shall send
by United States registered mail , a letter addressed
to such absent person at the last known address as
given in the application , stating that such applica-
tion has been filed, and the Clerk shall insert in a
newspaper having general circulation in said county ,
once each week for four consecutive weeks , a notice
to the effect that such application for the appoint-
ment of a curator has been made .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the curator to collect
and safely keep the estate of the decedent , and to
make and return to Court , within three months , a
full and complete inventory of the same . It shall be
his further duty to deliver up the estate , including
the income therefrom , when required , to the absent
person upon his return , or to the executor or admin-
istrator , when appointed . He may pay debts , com-
mence and maintain actions , and be sued as such ,
and he may invest or reinvest any funds coming into
his hands , and may sell such perishable and other
goods as the County Court or the Circuit Court may
order to be sold , and shall be allowed a reasonable
compensation for his services , in accordance with
Kentucky Statutes , section 3883 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 67.
AN ACT granting members of Fraternal Benefit Societies the
right to designate certain beneficiaries to receive the payment

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 355
of death benefits ; exempting certain societies from the pro-
visions of the Act ; and amending Sections 681c -6 and 681c -29
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ; and repealing said sections
and all laws and parts of laws in conflict therewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 681c -6 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes be amended so as to read as follows :
Section 681c -6, Beneficiaries.-The payment of
death benefits shall be confined to husband , wife ,
relative by blood to the fourth degree , father -in -law ,
mother -in-law, son -in-law , daughter -in -law , step-
father , step -mother , step -children , children by legal
adoption , orphans homes or homes for the aged
maintained by any Society or Association , or to a
person or persons dependent upon the member ; pro-
vided , that if after the issuance of the original certif-
icate the member shall become dependent upon an
incorporated charitable institution , he shall have
the privilege , with the consent of the society , to
make such institution his beneficiary . Within the
above restrictions each member shall have the right
to designate his beneficiary , and from time to time
have the same changed in accordance with the laws ,
rules or regulations of the society , and no bene-
ficiary shall have or obtain any vested interest in
the said benefit until the same has become due and
payable upon the death of the said member , pro-
vided , that any society may, by its laws , limit the
scope of beneficiaries within the above classes .
§ 2. That Section 681c -29 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes be amended so as to read as follows :
Section 681c -29 , Exemption of Certain Societies .
--Nothing contained in this Act shall be so construed
as to effect or apply to grand or subordinate lodges
of Masons, Odd Fellows , Knights of Pythias ( exclu-

356 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
sive of the insurance department of the Supreme
Lodge Knights of Pythias ) and the Junior Order
United American Mechanics ( exclusive of the Bene-
ficiary Degree or insurance branch of the National
Council Junior Order United American Mechanics ) ,
or to similar orders , societies or associations now
doing business in this state which provide benefits
exclusively through local or subordinate lodges , or
to orders , societies or associations which limit their
admission to membership to any one hazardous
occupation , nor to an association of local lodges of a
society now doing business in this state which pro-
vides death benefits not exceeding three hundred dol-
lars , nor to any contracts of re -insurance business on
such plan in this state , nor to domestic societies
which limit their membership to the employees of a
particular city or town , designated firm , business
house or corporation , nor to domestic lodges , orders ,
or associations of a purely religious , charitable and
benevolent description which do not provide for a
death benefit of more than one hundred dollars , or
for disability benefit of more than one hundred and
fifty dollars to one person in any one year . The
insurance commissioner may whenever he deems
proper make an examination of any such society or
organization ( domestic or foreign ) , operating in this
state to enable him to determine whether such
society or organization is exempt from the provi-
sions of this Act , and if found to not be so exempt
the expenses of such examination shall be paid by
the society or organization examined upon state-
ment furnished by him.
Any fraternal benefit society heretofore organized
and incorporated and operated within the definition
set forth in 681c -1 , 681 c-2 , 681c -3 of this Act , pro-
viding for benefits in case of death or disability

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 357
resulting solely from accident , but which does not
obligate itself to pay death or sick benefits , may be
licensed under the provisions of this Act and shall
have all the privileges and shall be subject to all the
provisions of this Act except that the provisions of
this Act requiring medical examinations , valuations
of benefit certificates and that the certificates shall
specify the amount of benefit shall not apply to such
society .
3. That said sections 681c -6 and 681c - 29 of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes be and they are hereby
repealed .
§ 4. That all acts or parts of acts inconsistent
with or in conflict with this act are hereby repealed .
§ 5. That this Act shall be in force from and
after ninety days after the adjournment of this ses-
sion of the Legislature .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 68.
AN ACT to repeal and re-enact Section Four thousand three
hundred ninety-nine a-Sub -Section Ten of Baldwin's nineteen
hundred and Twenty -eight Supplement to Carroll's nineteen
hundred and Twenty -two Edition , Kentucky Statutes , relating
to public schools , and being Section Ten of an Act , entitled ,
"An Act creating a county board of education , prescribing its
duties , fixing the tenure of office and compensation of its mem-
bers ; providing for the election of a county superintendent by
said board ; fixing his qualifications , compensation and term
of office ; and prescribing his duties and fixing his bond ; pro-
viding for the election of an attendance officer by the county
board of education ; providing for the levy of tax for common
school purposes in each county ; prescribing the duties of the
county tax commissioner , the county court clerk , with ref-
erence to the assessment of the property subject to said tax
and prescribing the duties of the county board of education
and the fiscal court levying said tax and prescribing the duties
of the sheriff in collecting said taxes and making his settle-
ment to the fiscal court ; providing the manner of paying his

358 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
commission for collecting same ; prescribing the duties of the
fiscal court with reference to making settlement with the
sheriff for said tax ; providing for the employment of teachers ;
providing for the appointment of district trustees and pre-
scribing their duties ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section four thousand three hundred ninety-
nine -a , sub -section ten , of Baldwin's nineteen hun-
dred and twenty-eight Supplement to Carroll's
nineteen hundred and twenty -two Edition , Kentucky
Statutes be , and the same is now repealed and re-
enacted so that said sub -section ten when re -enacted
shall read as follows : At the regular November
election , held on the first Tuesday after the first
Monday in November , nineteen hundred and thirty-
three , and every four years thereafter , there shall
be elected in each county in this state a county
superintendent of public schools , who shall assume
office on the first Monday in January , nineteen hun-
dred and thirty -four and hold said office for a period
of four years , and until his successor has been
elected and qualified . No person shall be eligible to
the office of superintendent of county schools who
does not hold a proper certificate for scholarship ,
administration and supervision as provided by law ;
provided , however , that all county superintendents
of schools at the time this act takes effect shall con-
tinue to serve to the end of the term for which they
were appointed and qualified , unless sooner re-
moved as now provided by law . Said superintend-
ent of county schools shall be eligible for re - elec-
tion , provided , however , that the voters in cities of
the first , second , third and fourth class shall not be
eligible to vote at the election herein provided for .
The superintendent of county schools shall devote
his time to the public schools and shall receive such

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 359
compensation as the fiscal court of the county may
direct , to be paid from the county school funds , in
equal monthly installments . In case of vacancy in
the office of the superintendent of county schools
from any cause whatever , said vacancy shall be filled
as provided by Section one thousand five hundred
twenty-six, Carroll's Nineteen hundred and twenty-
two Edition , Kentucky Statutes . The county super-
intendent of schools as executive officer of the
county board of education shall see that the laws
relating to the schools , the by -laws , rules and regula-
tions of the state board of education , and regula-
tions and policies of the county board of education
are carried into effect .
The superintendent of county schools shall recom-
mend for condemnation school buildings which are
unsanitary and unfit for use . He shall also recom-
mend the repairs to be made on school buildings , the
purchase of school sites and grounds and the sale
of same and make all such other recommendations
as he deems necessary or advisable . He shall pre-
pare or cause to be prepared , at the direction of the
county board of education all plans and specifica-
tions for any and all school buildings to be erected
or remodeled in his county .
The superintendent of county schools shall ap-
prove in writing all contracts , of any kind , entered
into by the county board of education and no con-
tract entered into by the county board of education
without the written approval of the county superin-
tendent of schools shall be valid . The county
superintendent of schools shall be the representative
of the state superintendent of public instruction in
all examinations for teacher's certificates , con-
ducted within the county and shall perform such
other duties connected therewith as may be required
by law .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

360 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 69 ,
AN ACT to amend an act entitled " An Act to provide for the
distraining for graded common school taxes in graded com-
mon school districts other than those in cities of the first ,
second , third or fourth class ," being Chapter 59 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1928 , approved March 17 , 1928 , and
to provide additional remedies for the collection of school
taxes in said graded common school districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an act entitled " An Act to provide for
the distraining for graded common school taxes in
graded common school districts , other than those in
cities of the first , second , third or fourth class , '
being Chapter 59 of the Acts of the General Assem-
bly of 1928 , approved March 17 , 1928 , be and the
same is hereby amended , so that when amended and
re-enacted , it shall read as follows :
That in all graded common school districts other
than those in cities of the first , second , third or
fourth class , the tax collector of the graded com-
mon school taxes therein , shall have the power and
it shall be his duties , after said taxes become de-
linquent to distrain sufficient personal property of
each taxpayer of the said district , who has not paid
his graded common school taxes , due the said dis-
trict from the said taxpayer . Said officers shall sell
for cash the property so distrained , or so much
thereof , as may be necessary to satisfy the said
taxes , for which said distraint was made and cost
of advertising said sale in the same manner as prop-
erty is sold under execution . That in addition to
said remedy and the remedies now provided by law,
all taxes levied and assessed for said graded com-
mon school purposes , together with any percentage
imposed for delinquency and the cost of collection ,
shall constitute a lien on the property , or real estate ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 361
so assessed and said graded common school dis-
tricts , shall have the right and power to enforce said
lien by a suit in any court having jurisdiction
thereof to foreclose such liens .
§ 2. Whereas, there is no adequate remedy for
collecting taxes in said graded common school dis-
tricts , an emergency is declared to exist and this act
shall take effect and be in full force and effect , from
and after its pasage and approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 70.
AN ACT providing for the issuance of college elementary cer-
tificates ; repealing Sub -section two of Section four thousand
five hundred one -seven and Sub -section one of Section four
thousand six hundred thirty -six b-two , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , Baldwin's Revision of one thousand nine hundred thirty
and Sub -section one of Section two of chapter thirty -seven ,
Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. A college elementary certificate , valid for
two years , for teaching in any elementary school of
the State , shall be issued by the Superintendent of
Public Instruction to any applicant who has filed in
his name a transcript certified by the officials of the
College of Education , University of Kentucky , or
the State Teachers Colleges , or any other standard
college , showing that he has earned in residence at
least thirty-two semester hours of prescribed and
elective standard college credit ; at least six of the
thirty -two semester hours standard college credit
shall have been earned in teachers professional
courses . This certificate shall be eligible for re-
newal , before or after its expiration , of two years

362 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
each upon filing an official transcript showing that
the holder has earned at least sixteen semester
hours of prescribed and elective standard college
credit since the issuance of said certificate or since
its last renewal , at least eight of which semester
hours shall have been earned in residence in the
institution certifying the transcript .
§ 2. Sub -section two of section four thousand
five hundred one - seven and sub -section one of four
thousand six hundred thirty -six b-two , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Revision of one
thousand nine hundred thirty, and sub- section one
of section two of chapter thirty - seven , Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty , are hereby repealed .
$ 3. This Act shall become effective and
operative as of September first , one thousand nine
hundred and thirty -two .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 71 .
AN ACT to provide for the appointment of a County Superin-
tendent of Schools in Counties containing cities of the first
or second class , and describing his term of office , qualifica-
tions , duties and salary, and repealing all laws or parts of
laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
The County Board of Education of each County
containing cities of the first or second class shall
appoint , on the first Monday in January , 1933 , a
County Superintendent of Schools for a term of not
more than four years next succeeding his appoint-
ment , and he shall hold office until his successor has
been appointed and qualified . No person thus
appointed shall be eligible to the office who does not

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 363
hold a proper certificate in scholarship , administra-
tion and supervision , as provided by law , provided
that all County Superintendents of Schools in
counties containing cities of the first or second class
holding office at the time that this Act shall take
effect shall continue to serve to the end of the term
for which they were originally appointed and until
their successors are appointed and qualified , unless
removed as provided by this Act , and he shall be
eligible for reappointment .
The County Superintendent of Schools shall de-
vote his entire time to the public school business and
shall receive such compensation as the County
Board of Education shall direct to be paid from the
county school funds in monthly installments , pro-
vided that no County Superintendent of Schools
appointed to office under the provisions of this sec-
tion shall be paid a salary of less than twelve hun-
dred dollars ( $ 1,200.00 ) per year . In case of
vacancy due to any cause the County Board of Edu-
cation shall fill the vacancy and the appointment
shall be for the unexpired term , and until his suc-
cessor shall have been appointed and qualified .
The County Superintendent of Schools as execu-
tive officer of the County Board of Education , shall
see that the laws relating to the schools , the by-laws,
rules and regulations of the State Board of Educa-
tion , and regulations and policies of the County
Board of Education are carried into effect .
The County Superintendent of Schools shall
recommend for condemnation school buildings
which are unsanitary and unfit for use . He shall
recommend the repairs , the purchase of grounds ,
school sites and grounds , the sale of the same , and
shall cause to be prepared all plans and specifica-
tions for remodeling of old buildings , provided that
the plans and specifications for any and all build-

364 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ings to be erected in said county shall be submitted
to the State Superintendent of Public Instruction or
his agent for approval .
The County Superintendent shall approve in
writing all contracts of any kind , entered into by
the County Board of Education , and no contract
entered into by the County Board of Education
without the written approval of the County Superin-
tendent of Schools shall be valid .
The County Superintendent of Schools shall be
the representative of the State Superintendent of
Public Instruction in all examinations for teachers '
certificates , conducted within the county , and shall
perform such duties connected therewith as may be
required by law.
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
hereby are repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 72 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Section 4526b -5, Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1930 edition , relating to high school pupils
attending the high school that is most convenient to them and
providing for the payment of tuition therefor , so that said
Section , when repealed and re -enacted , shall read :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That where an approved high school already
exists that any high school pupil shall have the
privilege of attending the school in the county which
is most convenient or the most conveninent school
within the county wherein the pupil resides ; and
that the county in which he resides shall pay the
tuition of said pupil at the same rate as fixed for
other high school pupils in said county .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 365
)
CHAPTER 73.
AN ACT providing for the certification of public school admin-
istrators .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That each Superintendent of Schools in this Com-
monwealth shall hold a standard certificate in ad-
ministration and supervision as provided for by law .
Such certificate shall be issued to any applicant who
files evidence of having had at least four years ex-
perience in administration and supervision in the
public schools or colleges of this Commonwealth ,
and who has satisfied the requirements for the issu-
ance of a standard certificate for high school teach-
ing and in addition thereto files standard college
credits for six semester hours in public school ad-
ministration and supervision . Such certificate shall
be issued for a term of four school years and shall
qualify the holder for any position in public school
administration and supervision in the Common-
wealth and shall be eligible for renewal on the
presentation of evidence that the holder has earned
twelve additional semester hours of standard college
credit in educational subjects and satisfactory evi-
dence of three years of successful administrative
experience .
All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 74 .
AN ACT providing for a treasurer or depository of school funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

366 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
$ 1. Each board of trustees or board of educa-
tion in this Commonwealth shall elect a treasurer or
depository, or both for a term of two school years
unless otherwise provided by law .
§ 2. Before the duties of treasurer or depository
are entered upon , the person or corporation selected
to serve in such capacity shall execute and file with
the board a bond in an amount to be determined by
the local board and approved by the State Board
of Education . Such bond shall guarantee the faith-
ful performance of duties by the treasurer or de-
pository , and shall guarantee the safety of all funds
in possession of such treasurer or depository belong-
ing to the board of education . The bond shall be
guaranteed by personal surety or a surety company
authorized to do business in this Commonwealth ,
and shall be made payable to the Commonwealth for
the benefit of the respective board of trustees or
board of education . The bond shall provide that the
treasurer or depository of the respective board will
faithfully perform the duties of the office , receive
all moneys to which the board is entitled by the
Constitution of the State or by the statutes , or which
may in any way come into its possession , hold such
moneys as a separate fund in his or its hands , and
pay all such moneys to such person and in such man-
ner as the board may direct , and that he or it will
keep full and complete accounts in such manner and
make such reports thereon as the local board of
trustees , or board of education , or the State Board
of Education may require ; that he or it will pre-
serve all records relating to the transaction and
duties of the office and turn to his or its successor
all public school funds in hand and all accounts and
records after due and proper audit by a competent
outside agent if required by the local board .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 367
§ 3. Such board shall give at least ten days '
notice of its meeting to select a treasurer or deposi-
tory , and shall receive sealed bids from persons or
corporations authorized to act in such capacity , and
the person or corporation filing the best bid shall be
selected .
§ 4. The forms for the bids and bonds referred
to in this Act shall be prepared by the Superintend-
ent of Public Instruction and approved by the
State Board of Education .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 75 .
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Section 4475a - 6, Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes 1930 Edition , being an act relative to com-
bining two or more graded school districts and combining with
graded school districts containing cities of the fifth class
adjacent territory from county districts so that , when repealed
and re -enacted , said Section shall read as follows :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
By concurrent majority action of the boards of
trustees of two or more adjacent graded common
school districts , and districts may be combined into
one graded common school district , the members of
the two or more boards constituting the board of
trustees of such combined district for the terms for
which they were respectively elected , subject to the
regular election of members as provided by law,
and provided further that the board of trustees of a
graded common school district embracing a city of
the fifth class and the county board of education of
the county in which such graded school district is
located , may by concurrent action of the two boards
combine with the graded school district embracing
a city of the fifth class , adjacent territory and pupils

368 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
from the county district , provided a majority of the
legal voters of the adjacent territory proposed to
be added to the graded school district , petition
either the graded school board of such district , or
the county board of education of the county embrac-
ing the graded school district , to be made a part of
the graded school district .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 76 .
AN ACT guaranteeing to all children in this Commonwealth
school privilege for the legal term of school .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
It shall be the duty of the Board of Education of
every county in this Commonwealth receiving reve-
nue from public taxation to provide at least the
minimum legal term of school for all pupil children
residing within the geographic limits of such dis-
trict . Such schools shall be maintained within
reasonable walking distance of such pupils of ele-
mentary grade , or transportation shall be provided
for them to such school or schools .
.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 77 .
AN ACT setting out the qualifications of members of boards of
education in the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
A person to be eligible to membership on a board
of education must have attained the age of twenty-
four years , must have been a citizen of the United
States for at least three years preceding his election

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 369
and must be a resident of the district for which he is
elected . He must have completed the eighth grade
in the common school and must not hold or discharge
the duties of any office , deputyship or agency under
the city or county of his residence ; except that any
member of such board or any existing board shall
be eligible for re -election . No person shall be eligi-
ble to this office who at the time of his election is
directly or indirectly interested in the sale to the
board of books , stationery , or other property . If,
at any time after the election of any member of such
board he shall become interested in any such con-
tract with or claims against the board , or if he
shall after election become a candidate for any
office or agency or for the nomination thereto ,
the holding and the discharging of the duties of
which would have rendered him ineligible before
election , or if he shall move his residence from
the district for which he was chosen of ( or )
if he shall do or incur anything which would
have rendered him ineligible for re -election , his
office shall without further action be vacant , and
it shall be filled as now provided by law ; provided
that no member of such board shall vote regarding
the appointment or employment in any capacity of
any person related to , such member as father ,
mother , brother , sister , husband , wife , son or daugh-
ter, nephew or niece . Board members in office at
the time this Act becomes operative may be re-
elected regardless of the educational qualifications
set out in this Act .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 78 .
AN ACT providing for the creation of an educational commis-
sion , prescribing its powers and duties and the duties of per-

370 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
sons in connection with it , and fixing penalties for failure to
perform same , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The Kentucky Educational Commission
shall consist of the Superintendent of Public In-
struction and eight appointed members . The Gover-
nor of this Commonwealth is authorized to appoint
these members , not more than half of whom shall be
persons actively engaged in educational work .
§ 2. It shall be the duty of this Commission to
direct a study of public education in Kentucky and
report its findings to the Governor and the General
Assembly of this Commonwealth at the opening of
its next regular session , with recommendations of
such measures and such revision of our school code
as may be found necessary for increasing the effi-
ciency and equalizing the benefits of public education
throughout the Commonwealth .
3. The Superintendent of Public Instruction
shall be the chairman of the Commission, and it
shall on his nomination and recommendation appoint
such assistants and incur such expenses as may be
necessary ; and the Superintendent of Public In-
struction shall pay all the expenses of the Commis-
sion and its assistants from any funds that may
become available for such purpose .
§ 4. The members of the Commission shall
receive no pay for their services , but be reimbursed
for expenses authorized by the Commission and
actually incurred in the discharge of duties imposed
by this Act , the evidence of such expenses to be sub-
mitted as provided in section 359a - 1 , Kentucky Stat-
utes .
§ 5. The commission and the persons working
under its direction shall be accorded free access to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 371
all public records . Any person who willfully with-
holds public records or information within his pos-
session or obstructs the work of the commission in
any way shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon
conviction shall be fined in any sum not less than
one hundred ( $ 100 ) dollars .
§ 6. Because of the urgent need of a general
revision of the common school laws of Kentucky
and a reorganization of the State's program of
public education , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist , and this Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval .
Approved March 28 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 79 .
AN ACT to amend Section 4426b - 1 and 4426b -3, Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , and providing for the election of
sub -district school trustees and prescribing the duties of the
county superintendent of schools and the county board of
education .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4426b - 1 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 edition , being Section 1 of Chapter
37 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1922 ,
relating to the election of subdistrict school trustees ,
be and the same hereby is amended by repealing
said section in its entirety, and enacting in lieu
thereof the following so that said section as amended
shall read as follows :
On and after the passage of this Act three sub-
district trustees in county rural school districts
shall be elected by the qualified voters of each sub-
district , one of whom shall serve for a period of one
year, one for a period of two years , and one for a
period of three years . The election shall be held

372 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
as provided for in section two of this act and the
three persons receiving the highest number of votes
shall be elected subdistrict trustees for a term of one
year , two years , and three years , each of whom shall
hold office until his successor is appointed and quali-
fied ; and said three persons so receiving the highest
number of votes shall determine by lot the respective
term for which each shall serve , and a record thereof
shall be filed with the county superintendent of
schools of the county in which such election is held .
No persons shall be eligible to hold the office of sub-
district trustee who is not a qualified elector in the
sub -district and who cannot read and write a legible
hand . At each succeeding regular election one sub-
district school trustee shall be elected for a term of
three years , and he shall hold office until his succes-
sor is elected and qualified . Any vacancy or
vacancies that may occur in the trusteeship of any
school subdistrict shall, upon the recommendation
of the county superintendent , be filled by appoint-
ment by the County Board of Education , and the
trustee thus appointed shall hold office until the next
regular election for sub -district trustees . A major-
ity of said trustees shall control in the performance
of the duties imposed by law as now provided . One
of their number hold the oldest commission shall
preside as Chairman at any of their meetings .
§ 2. That section 4426b - 3 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended and as amended shall read as follows :
The first election to be held under this Act shall
be held on the first Saturday in July , 1932 , and an
election shall be held on said day each year there-
after for the elections of sub-district trustees . The
sub -district trustees then elected shall qualify and
enter upon the discharge of the duties of their

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 373
respective offices as soon as the vote can be examined
and canvassed and certified by the County Board of
Education . The election shall be held at the school
house in the sub -district between the hours of one
o'clock P. M. and four o'clock P. M. and shall be viva
voce . The said election shall be conducted by two
election officers , appointed by the County Board of
Education , possessing all of the qualifications of
electors in said sub -district and able to read and to
write a legible hand . One of said officers shall act
as clerk of the election and the other as Judge of
said election . The clerk of the election shall enter
the names of all candidates for sub-district trustee
on the poll sheets furnished and provided by the
County Superintendent of Schools for said purpose
and keep a record thereon of the name of each voter
and for who he voted at said election . The poll
sheet shall be properly certified by the election
officers and returned over to the county superintend-
ent within five ( five ) days after the election " Pro-
vided , however , that wherever the word ' sub-
district ' is used in this act it shall be construed to
include all schools and school districts under the
control of the County Board of Education of the
various counties of this State , including consolidated
school district . "
§ 3. All acts and parts of acts , laws and parts of
laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 80 .
AN ACT relating to schools and changing boundary line of
Graded Common School Districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

374 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 1. If at any time it becomes desirable to extend
the existing boundary of a graded common school
district , a majority of the legal voters within the
boundary of the territory sought to be annexed ,
after having first procured the written consent and
approval of the County Board of Education , may
file with the board of trustees of the said graded
common school district , a written petition , signed
by said majority of legal voters within such pro-
posed extended boundary , requesting said board of
trustees of the graded common school district to
order the extension in conformity to the petition .
The petition so filed shall set out the names of each
and every legal voter in the boundary sought to be
added , giving the number of pupil children in said
boundary, together with a definite boundary ( not
necessarily by metes and bounds ) of the boundary to
be annexed territory .
§ 2. If it appears that the petition so filed com-
plies with the foregoing requirements , it shall at
once be the duty of the board of trustees of such
graded common school district to enter an order an-
nexing the boundary described in said petition , and
declaring same to be a portion of their graded com-
mon school district , provided , however , that if within
ten days after the entry of such order , a majority of
the legal voters in said original graded common
school district , file , in writing a protest against such
extension , then the said order shall be set aside and
held for naught , otherwise to remain in full force
and effect , provided , however , no change whatever
shall be made under this law without the written
consent and approval of the County Board of Edu-
cation .
§ 3. Since former laws with relation to the exten-
sion of graded common school districts were , by

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 375
oversight , repealed by the General Assembly in
1922 , and there has been no re-enactment of same ,
and because of the further fact that in many parts
of the Commonwealth , the right to extend graded
common school districts would result in better edu-
cational facilities , and in order that such changes
where desirable and ordered , may be made for the
school year 1932-3 , an emergency is declared , by the
General Assembly , to now exist , and this enactment
shall be and become effective upon its passage and
approval .
§ 4. All laws and acts in conflict with this enact-
ment are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 81 .
AN ACT to amend , revise and re -enact Section 1 of an Act
entitled "An Act to amend , revise and re -enact Section 1 of an
act entitled An Act to amend an act entitled 'An Act defining
boundaries for school districts , embracing cities of the fourth
class and providing systems of schools in such districts and
creating boards of education for such districts , providing for
the election thereof, defining their powers and duties and
repealing all laws in conflict therewith ,' being Chapter 61 of
the 1924 Acts of the Kentucky General Assembly ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1 of an Act of the General
Assembly of Kentucky , approved March 28 , 1924
and published as Chapter 61 of the Acts of 1924 ,
entitled " An Act to amend , revise , and re- enact Sec-
tion 1 of an act entitled ' An Act to amend , an act
entitled , ' An Act defining boundaries for school dis-
tricts , embracing cities of the fourth class and pro-
viding systems of schools in such districts and creat-
ing boards of education for such districts , providing

376 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
for the election thereof, defining their powers and
duties and repealing all laws in conflict therewith , "
be and the same is hereby amended , revised and re-
enacted so that same when so amended , revised and
re -enacted , will read as follows , to -wit :
Section 19. Before making any tax levy it shall
be the duty of said Board of Education to make a
careful estimate of the amount of money necessary
to be raised (over and above the amount due or to
become due it from the State as provided in Section
21 of the Act to which this is an amendment ) for
purposes as follows :
(a ) For maintaining the schools .
(b ) For repair and improvement of buildings .
(c) For sinking fund purposes .
(d ) For paying incidental expenses and any
other lawful claims against the school district .
Said estimate shall be entered in full upon the
minute book of the Board . The failure to make such
estimate , however , shall not invalidate the levy .
At least thirty days prior to time prescribed in
the Charter of cities of the fourth class for the tax
levy to be made for city purposes , it shall be the
duty of said Board of Education , in districts where
the boundary thereof is identical with the boundary
of a city of the fourth class , to levy an ad valorem
tax for school purposes upon all property of every
kind in the city assessed or subject to taxation for
city purposes . Said tax shall be at such rate as will
produce in that year the sum estimated by the Board
of Education as necessary to be raised for the afore-
said purposes . The Board shall also levy a poll tax
on each person assessed for poll tax by said city,
but the ad valorem taxes for all school purposes ex-
clusive of sinking fund shall not exceed $ 1.50 on
each $ 100.00 of assessed value and the poll tax shall

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 377
not exceed $ 2.00 for each poll . The assessment
made for the city shall also be the assessment for
the school district . Said levy shall be certified by
the Board of Education to the General Council or
Board of Commissioners of the city and said
General Council or Board of Commissioners shall
cause the taxes accruing thereunder to be added to
and included in the regular tax bills rendered by the
city for city purposes and shall cause said tax to be
collected by the same officers and in the same man-
ner and at the same time as taxes are collected for
city purposes .
At least thirty days prior to the time when the
Fiscal Court of the county is required to make its
levy for county purposes , it shall be the duty of the
Board of Education in districts where the boundary
includes a city of the fourth class together with
other territory , to levy an ad valorem tax for school
purposes upon all property of every kind in the dis-
trict assessed for county purposes . Said tax shall
be at such rate as will produce in that year the sum
estimated by the Board of Education as necessary to
be raised for school purposes in the district . The
Board shall also levy a poll tax on each person
assessed for poll tax by the county within the dis-
trict but the ad valorem tax (for all school purposes
exclusive of sinking fund ) shall not exceed $ 1.50 on
each $ 100.00 of assessed value and the poll shall not
exceed $ 2.00 for each poll . The assessment made
for the county shall be the assessment for each
school district . Said levy shall be certified by the
Board of Education to the Fiscal Court of the county .
and said Fiscal Court shall require the county clerk
to make out the tax bills for the ad valorem and poll
taxes accruing thereunder in the stub book to be
devised and furnished by said Board of Education
for that purpose , and said clerk shall sign and turn

378 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
them over to city tax collector for collection , taking
his receipt therefor in duplicate , showing the num-
ber of bills so delivered to said collector and the
aggregate amount thereof . One copy of said re-
ceipt shall be retained by said county clerk and one
copy certified to said Board of Education . For
making out said tax bills the clerk shall be allowed
the sum of seven (7c ) cents for each tax bill so made
out for the said collector , to be paid by the Board of
Education . Said Board of Education shall then
cause the same to be collected by said city collector
in the same manner and at the same time as taxes
are collected for city purposes .
All powers and duties conferred upon and re-
quired of officers in collecting taxes for city purposes
and for county purposes are hereby conferred upon
and required of them in collecting taxes levied by the
Board of Education . The Board of Education shall
pay the collector for collecting the taxes a reason-
able compensation not exceeding one and one -half
per cent on the amount collected .
The officer collecting the school tax and the sure-
ties upon his bond shall severally be liable under
his official bond or bonds for any failure in the per-
formance of his duties . Within ten days after the
close of each calendar month the collecting officer of
said city shall promptly pay over to the treasurer
of the Board of Education all sums collected by him
during the preceding month less his commissions
and upon failure to do so the Board of Education
shall have the right to institute suit against him
and his bondsmen upon his official bond for all sums
collected and unpaid by him .
The limitation hereinbefore prescribed upon the
rate of tax that may be levied for school purposes
shall not apply where a bonded indebtedness has
been incurred for public school purposes and when

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 379
such indebtedness has been incurred there may be
levied in addition to the tax heretofore authorized
an ad valorem tax sufficient to pay interest on said
indebtedness and also to constitute a sinking fund
for the payment of the principal thereof . The pro-
vision hereof shall apply to any bonded indebted-
ness whether heretofore or hereafter created .
The failure of the Board of Education by in-
advertence or otherwise to make a levy within the
time prescribed shall not invalidate any levy made
thereafter ."
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 82 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 1460 of the 1930 edition
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1460 of the 1930 edition of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes be amended and re-
enacted so that when done the section will read as
follows :
" The county clerks of the several counties shall
cause the names of all candidates of their respective
jurisdictions , where nominations for any office speci-
fied in the ballot have been duly made and not with-
drawn , in accordance herewith , to be printed on one
ballot all nominations of any party or group of
petitioners , as designated by them in their certifi-
cate or petition ; or if none be designated , under
some suitable title and device . If the same device
for designating candidates be selected by two par-
ties or groups of petitioners it shall be given to the
one which first selected it, and the clerk shall select
a suitable device for the other . The arrangement
of the ballot shall in general conform, as nearly as

380 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
practicable , to the plan hereinafter given, and the
devices named and lists of the candidates of the
various parties shall be printed in parallel columns ,
in such order as the Secretary of State may direct ,
precedence , however , being given to the party which
polled the higher number of votes for the head of
the ticket in the last preceding election . The device
of each party shall be placed at the head of the list of
candidates of the party . The device shall not be
enclosed in a square , but immediately under the
device shall be placed a circle of one inch in diame-
ter . Immediately under it shall be placed the name
or title of the party ticket , and immediately under
the name or title the list of candidates of the party ,
the name of each candidate having immediately on
its right a small square large enough to contain the
cross -mark by which the voter is required to desig-
nate his vote . Underneath the name of each candi-
date shall be left a blank space large enough to con-
tain a written name , and the general arrangements
shall conform as nearly as possible to the following :
(Insert cut contained in Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes .)
Name of voter
Consecutive number
Residence
Party emblems in here
(Continuing in like manner as to all candidates to
be voted for at such elections .)
Name of voter
Consecutive number
The secondary stub shall be on the end of the bal-
lot opposite to that of the several party devices .
"On the back shall be printed ' official ballot , ' the
date of the election and facsimile of the signature of
the clerk , who has caused the ballot to be printed .
There shall also be printed on the back of the bal-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 381
lot a blank line followed by the word 'judge , ' and
after the ballot has been detached from the stub in
the book of ballots and before the ballot is delivered
to the voter to be voted one of the judges shall sign
his name on such blank line and no ballot not so
signed by one of the judges shall be counted by the
canvassing board whose duty it is to certify the
results of the election . Should and elector desire
to vote for each and every candidate of one party
he shall make a cross -mark ( X ) in the circle under
the device of said party and the vote shall be
counted for all candidates under said device : Pro-
vided , however , if a cross -mark (X) be made in the
circle under the party device and a cross -mark ( X )
be also made after one or more candidates of a dif-
ferent party , or parties , the vote shall be counted
for the candidates so marked , and not for the candi-
dates for the same offices of the party so marked ,
but the vote shall be counted for the other candi-
dates of said party .
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 83 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 1482 of the Baldwin's
1931 Supplement to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , and being
Section 4 of Chapter 49 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky at its 1930 session .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 1482 of Baldwin's 1931 Supplement
to Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , and which is Sec-
tion 4 of Chapter 49 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky at its

382 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
1930 session be amended and re- enacted so that
when so amended it will read as follows :
"Section 4. When the hour of four P. M. has ar-
rived the officers shall announce the same at the
door of the voting room and close the polls . They
shall then go and take charge of the ballot box , at
the booth , and in the presence of each other shall
place the lock on the small vent lid and securely
lock it , and return the key to the county clerk . Said
election officers shall then stamp upon all unused
stubs and ballots , with rubber stamp or by writing
on the stub and the ballot attached thereto , the word
' unused ' and place said stub or ballot book and
all other utensils or election material into a linen
envelope , to be provided by the county clerk for that
purpose , and shall seal with sealing wax across the
seal of the envelope and place the county election
seal upon said wax, so as to make the letters plainly
visible in three different places on said envelope .
The officers of election shall immediately deliver
the ballot box , stub book and the envelope contain-
ing the unused ballots to the county clerk . The
judge and the sheriff of election , of opposite faith ,
shall forthwith convey said ballot box , stub book
and envelope to the county clerk's office , taking his
receipt therefor . Said officers shall see that no per-
son other than themselves has access to , or custody
of , said ballot box , and they shall each remain in
the presence of the other , until said box and en-
velope is delivered to the county clerk .
" And any violation by them of any duty imposed
upon them by this section with reference to such
conveyance shall render them guilty of a misde-
meanor and upon conviction thereof they shall be
punished by a fine of not less than $500.00 , nor more
than $1,000.00 or imprisonment in the county jail
for not less than sixty nor more than one hundred

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 383
days , or both so fined and imprisoned within the dis-
cretion of the jury trying the case . Said officers
shall also upon delivery to the county court clerk
of the ballot box and the poll books , certificates and
the envelope containing the spoiled , mutilated and
questioned ballots and the keys to the ballot box ,
together with the county election seal , each make an
affidavit and file it with the county court clerk stat-
ing in substance that neither they nor anyone else
took from the ballot box while in their custody any
ballot or put into it any ballot , or in any manner
whatever tampered with the contents of the ballot
box and that it and its contents as well as all of the
articles delivered by them to the county court clerk
were and are in the same identical condition as
when taken charge of by them at the closing of the
polls , and for any false statement made in such
affidavit the one so making it shall be subject to in-
dictment and prosecution for false swearing and
punished accordingly .
" The ballot box shall remain locked , from the
time it leaves the county clerk's office and is re-
turned to said office , and until the county election
commissioners shall unlock the same for the purpose
of counting the ballots as herein provided .
" The county court clerk , upon receiving said bal-
lot box and envelope from the election officers shall
receipt for the same and shall place said ballot boxes
and envelopes in a secure and substantial place and
shall keep a sufficient guard over said boxes and
envelopes in a careful and painstaking manner until
all the ballots have been counted by the election
commissioners and those acting withthem . Pro-
vided , that the chairman of each of the two domi-
nant political parties , shall have the right , and
each candidate to be voted for , at any election , may
designate one or more persons to assist the county

384 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
clerk and other guards , to guard the safety of the
ballots in said clerk's office . The county clerk shall
assist the county election commission , and shall
act as its clerk, in the counting and certifying of
the ballots and the results of said election , for which
he shall receive compensation to be allowed by the
fiscal court .
" The county election commission , for the pur-
pose of counting the ballots and issuing certificates
and making reports , shall meet on the first week-
day after any primary or general election at 10
o'clock on said day in the office of the county clerk,
and proceed as speedily as practical , to count the
ballots of every precinct in such county and at the
finish of each and every precinct they shall
announce the result at the door of the clerk's office
and they shall fill out the blank in the back of the
stub book provided for that purpose and each com-
missioner shall sign the same on a blank provided
for that purpose.
"The election commission is authorized to employ
at county expense , such clerical help as said com-
mission may deem necessary to make said count ,
and shall remain continuously in session , until each
and every precinct has been counted and the result
ascertained , Sundays and legal holidays excepted .
There shall be provided by the county clerk a large
sheet upon which to tabulate the vote of the whole
county, district or municipality , so that each candi-
date can ascertain the number of votes in each pre-
cinct and the total number in the election unit , cast
for him. If any ballots are found mutilated , de-
faced , marked or spoiled or otherwise questionable
so that they are of doubtful integrity , the commis-
sion shall put all such ballots in a sealed envolepe
after writing on them and each of them , whether
they have been counted , and for whom .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 385
"In any general or primary election held in this
state or any county , district or municipality , the
same procedure shall prevail and the county election
