commission shall count and certify the result in the
manner herein provided .
" The said election commission shall issue to suc-
cessful candidates for office or nominations , their
certificates of election or nominations in all county ,
magisterial districts or any subdivision of any
county and shall certify the result of the election
in their said county , the Secretary of State in all
elections or nominations , to be made for state and
district officers and nominations .
" In all general elections the governing authority
of each political party , and in all primary elections ,
each group of candidates for an office , may desig-
nate to the county election commission , one repre-
sentative each to meet with the county election com-
mission, as herein provided , and it shall be the duty
of said commission to permit said representative
to assist in , and witness the count of the ballot in
all the precincts of such county and each and all of
said commissioners and said representatives , so des-
ignated , shall have equal and full opportunity to
inspect and count said ballots , and it shall be unlaw-
ful for any county election commission or a major-
ity thereof , to refuse to permit said representative
to exercise full and free action in witnessing the
count of said ballots and any interference with , or
refusal , on the part of said election commission , or
either of them , to permit said representatives , either
of a political part , or of a candidate , or group of
candidates , to have a free and full opportunity to
witness and observe the counting of said ballots ,
shall be guilty of a high misdemeanor , and upon con-
viction thereof shall be fined not less than one thou-
sand dollars nor more than two thousand dollars or
Acts- 13

386 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
imprisoned in the county jail not less than six
months nor more than twelve months in the discre-
tion of the jury .
" The county election commission after fifteen
days , provided no contest is filed in any race against
any candidate , shall return the keys to said ballot
boxes to the county court clerk, but if any such con-
test , either of nominations or elections , has been
filed , said commissioners shall hold said keys sub-
ject to the orders of the court trying such contest .
" This act , shall govern both the conducting , hold-
ing , counting of ballots , and certifying of returns , in
general and primary elections .
" Nothing herein contained shall be construed to
prevent the election commissioners in counties
having cities of the first , second and third class from
employing extra tabulators , who under their super-
vision and responsibility may expedite the counting
of the ballots."
§ 2. All acts or parts of acts in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 84.
AN ACT relating to registration and other means of identifica-
tion of voters for elections in counties of the Commonwealth
containing a city of the first class ; providing the revenue to
defray the expenses of such registrations ; repealing Chap-
ter 48 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1930 relating
to and governing registration of voters for elections in cities
of the first class to the extent that same relates to the reg-
istration of voters in cities of the first class for the general
November elction of 1932 and thereafter .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
1. That in so far and to the extent that Chap-
ter 48 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1930 ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 387
approved February 26 , 1930 , relates to and governs
the registration of voters in cities of the first class ,
said act and the provisions thereof are hereby re-
pealed to the extent that same relates to and gov-
erns the registration of voters for the November
election of 1932 and thereafter .
2. Every citizen of the United States , not
otherwise disqualified by the laws of this Common-
wealth, who is twenty -one years of age, or who will
become twenty- one years of age on or before the
next regular election to be held in November of any
year , or on or before the date of any special election ,
who will have been a resident of the state of Ken-
tucky one year , and the county six months , and of
the precinct, in cities or towns of the first class in
such county , in which he or she offers to vote , sixty
days next preceding such election and who has reg-
istered as provided by this act , and who is other-
wise qualified, shall be entitled to vote for all offices
elective by the people and on all measures or ques-
tions submitted for determination by the voters at
any regular or special election .
§ 3. There is hereby created , in such county
having a city of the first class in the state of Ken-
tucky, a Board of Registration Commissioners des-
ignated as " The Board of Registration Commis-
sioners of the County of
99
The administration of registration for elections in
such county shall be centralized under the supervi-
sion and control of said board , as herein provided ,
in a single office .
4. Said board shall be bi -partisan and consist
of four members to be appointed by the Board of
Election Commissioners of such county in the man-
ner herein provided . They shall be citizens and
electors of the county from which they are ap-
pointed , and not less than twenty-five years old .

388 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The first members of said board shall be appointed
as soon as this act becomes effective and they shall
hold office until June 15 , 1936 , or until their succes-
sors are appointed and qualified . In 1936 , and every
fourth year thereafter , members of said board shall
be appointed in the same manner and at the time.
herein provided , whose term of office shall be for
four years from the 15th day of June , of such year,
and until their successors are appointed and quali-
fied . Members of said board shall be eligible for
reappointment and to succeed themselves . The
chairman of the county and city executive commit-
tees of the two political parties having representa-
tion on the county board of election commissioners ,
in the county in which such city of the first class is
situated , may immediately upon this act becoming
effective and every fourth year thereafter , desig-
nate in writing a list of four names to the
county board of election commissioners and from
these names , if any are so designated , the
members of said Board or Registration Com-
missioners shall , within five days , be appointed
from said lists as follows : Two from each list so
that said board shall be composed of an equal num-
ber of members from the said two political parties ,
represented as hereinabove stated . If no lists are
submitted to said county board of election commis-
sioners , then said members shall be so selected and
appointed so that two shall be of one such political
party and two of the other such political party . It
shall be the duty of the county board of election
commissioners to determine the qualifications of
said members before appointment . The county
board of election commissioners shall have the
power to remove any member of said board who is
or shall become disqualified under the provisions
of this act . No removal shall be made at any time

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 389
without cause , and the grounds shall be reduced to
writing by said county board of election commis-
sioners . Said commissioners , or any employee of
said board , may be removed by the written request
properly certified to show that said action was
legally taken , of the county and city executive com-
mittee of the party responsible for the appointment
of said member or employee . If a vacancy shall
occur by removal or otherwise , it shall be filled by
the county board of election commissioners , or by
said Board of Registration Commissioners , accord-
ing to who made the original appointment , from a
supplemental list of names , furnished in like man-
ner as above provided , and in conformity with the
provisions of this Act , and the member so appointed
shall be of the same political party as his predeces-
sor . If there shall be two or more contending
county and city executive committees of the same
political party in the county , then that executive
committee which is recognized by the State Central
Committee of such party , by the written certificate
of the chairman thereof, shall be recognized by the
County Board of Election Commissioners in making
the appointment of members of the Board of Regis-
tration Commissioners . Resignation from said
board shall be in writing , directed to the county
board and filed with the secretary thereof . The
acceptance of any office , either elective or appoin-
tive , or becoming a candidate for any elective office ,
by any member of said board or employee , during
his or her term of office shall, ipso facto , vacate said
office . No members of the county and city executive
committee of either party shall be eligible to mem-
bership on the said Board of Registration Commis-
sioners , nor to any clerical or other position under
said board .

390 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
5. The Board of Registration Commissioners
of such county shall establish , maintain and be fur-
nished offices in the county courthouse or County
Armory of the county in which such city is situated .
Said officers of said board shall be maintained per-
manently .
Said board is hereby authorized to organize and
equip its offices in such manner as it may deem nec-
essary and consistent with the provisions of this
Act. The expense of supplies for and of organizing ,
equipping and maintaining said . offices , shall be
paid out of the County Treasury . Said Board is
hereby further authorized to appoint two chief
clerks and two competent stenographers . The two
chief clerks and stenographers shall hold office as
herein provided , but shall be removable by the
Board of Registration Commissioners for cause ,
and the grounds and action taken thereon by the
Board shall be reduced to writing by said Board
and made a part of its record . The County and
City Executive Committees of the two political par-
ties having representation on the County Board of
Election Commissioners , may each, immediately
upon the appointment of the first members of said
Board of Registration Commissioners , as herein
provided , and not later than ten days thereafter ,
designate in writing a list of not more than two
names for clerks and two names for stenographers
to the Board of Registration Commissioners and
from these names , if they are so designated , the two
chief clerks and two stenographers of said Board
shall , within two days , be appointed from said lists
as follows :
One chief clerk and one stenographer from each
of said lists . If no such lists are submitted to said
Board , then the chief clerks and stenographers of
said Board shall be so selected and appointed as

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 391
that one clerk and stenographer shall be of one
political party and the other clerk and stenographer
of the other political party . If said Board of Reg-
istration Commissioners can not agree upon the per-
son or persons to be appointed as chief clerks of
said Board , and a tie -vote results , then the deciding
vote shall be cast by the County Board of Election
Commissioners . The Board of Registration Com-
missioners is hereby authorized to employ such
other deputy clerks or employees from time to time
and for such periods of time as , in the opinion of
said Board , may be necessary for the proper con-
duct of the business of said offices . The four Regis-
tration Commissioners , the two Chief Clerks and
the two stenographers shall constitute the perma-
nent force of Registration employees . They shall
devote their entire time , during office hours , to the
work of registration and shall not engage in any
other work . The Commissioners shall each receive
an annual salary of two thousand four hundred
dollars and the chief clerks and stenographers shall
each receive an annual salary of one thousand eight
hundred dollars . Additional or extra deputy clerks
or employees shall be paid such compensation as
may be fixed by the Board of Registration Com-
missioners , not to exceed compensation at the rate
of one hundred and fifty dollars per month per per-
son . The salaries and compensation of said chief
clerks , stenographers , deputy clerks and employees
shall be paid by the county in which said city is sit-
uated and in the same manner and at the same time
that county officers are paid .
§ 6. All employees under this Act shall be se-
lected in equal numbers from lists submitted by
the City and County Executive Committees of each
political party having representation on the County
Board of Election Commissioners . All appointments

392 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
from said lists shall be made by the Board in the
order in which the names appear thereon .
If a majority of said Board can not agree upon
any question which arises in connection with the
administration of its office , not otherwise provided
for , and which is within the jurisdiction of said
Board , then the chief clerks of said Board shall be
entitled to vote in the following manner : On the
first disagreement the chief clerk of the Democratic
party shall break the tie and on the next tie vote the
chief clerk of the Republican party shall break the
tie, and thereafter the two chief clerks shall alter-
nate in voting to break the tie vote .
7. There shall be conducted in every county
having a city of the first class , under the supervi-
sion of the Board of Registration Commissioners for
said County , the following registrations , which shall
be permanent , which means that the registration of
any voter is continued so long as he or she resides
at the registered address . First : A general initial
registration in each precinct on the days and in the
manner herein provided , which shall form the basis
of the records to be established and kept at the
offices of said Board as provided herein . There-
after there shall be no further general registration
in the precincts in such city except as is otherwise
provided herein . Second : After said general
initial registration all further registration must be
made at the offices of the Board of Registration
Commissioners in said county in the manner herein
provided .
§ 8. The general initial registration herein pro-
vided for shall be held and conducted in the manner
herein provided in each precinct in each county
containing a city of the first class on the 11th and
12th days of July , 1932 .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 393
§ 9. During and at said initial registration and
thereafter , individual registration records shall be
made and preserved by or under the supervision
of the Board of Registration Commissioners in
counties having a city of the first class as herein
provided , for each qualified voter in said counties
in the form of loose -leaf pages . Said registration
records shall be maintained in triplicate . The orig-
inal records and one copy thereof shall be filed in
loose -leaf binders by precincts and shall constitute
the precinct registers , to be used as herein pro-
vided ; the other copy of said records shall be filed
in a loose -leaf binder or binders , without regard to
precincts , in alphabetical order for the entire
county , to be used as herein provided .
$ 10. It shall be the duty of the Board of Regis-
tration Commissioners , constituted and appointed
as herein provided , of counties having a city of the
first class , not later than the 5th day of July , 1932 ,
to appoint for each precinct in said county two
judges , one clerk and one sheriff of registration , to
act as registration officers in such precinct , at the
general initial registration herein provided for , who
shall be residents of said county and who shall
serve and act as such for the days during which
said general initial registration , herein provided
for, shall be held in the year 1932 .
The County and City Executive Committees in
such cities , of the two political parties having rep-
resentation on the County Board of Election Com-
missioners shall each, on or before the 1st day of
July , 1932 , designate in writing for each of said
precincts a list of not less than four names to the
Board of Registration Commissioners , and from
these names , if any are so designated , the officers
of registration shall , within five days , be appointed
from said lists as follows : One judge at each place

394 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of registration shall be selected from each of said
lists and in like manner the sheriff shall be chosen
from one of said lists and the clerk from the other
and sheriffs clerks for said precincts shall be
appointed alternately beginning with the first pre-
cinct in the first ward and so on through the last
precinct in the last ward and in every election pre-
cinct in the county , so that each of such political
parties shall have in each city of the first class and
in every election precinct in the county having a
first class city as near an equal number of sheriffs.
and clerks as possible . If no lists are submitted to
said Board of Registration Commissioners , then
the officers of said initial registration shall be so
selected and appointed by said Board as that one
of the judges at each place of registration shall be
of one such political party and the other judge of
the other such political party , and there shall be a
like difference at each registration place between
the sheriff and clerk of registration . No person
shall be eligible as an officer of registration who has
been convicted of a felony or is under indictment
therefor , or who is not sober , temperate , discreet
and of good demeanor , or who has anything of value
wagered on the result of the next election , or who
is a candidate to be voted for at the next election ,
and who is not capable of reading the Constitution
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in English and
of writing a plain hand . It shall be the duty of the
Board of Registration Commissioners to determine
the qualification of said registration officers before
appointment . The Board of Registration Commis-
sioners shall have the power to remove all registra-
tion officers who are disqualified under the provi-
sions of this act , but no such removal shall be made
at any time without cause , and the grounds shall be
reduced to writing by said board and made a part

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 395
of its records . If a vacancy shall occur by removal
or otherwise , it shall be filled (filed ) by said board
from the list already submitted , if any , from which
the officer was selected , and in conformity with the
provisions of this act . The Board of Registration
Commissioners shall give due notice of said appoint-
ment of registration officers to the sheriff of the
county in which, said city is located , who shall
within five days next after said appointment , give
each officer of registration written notice of his ap-
pointment . If there shall be two or more contend-
ing executive committees of the same political party
in the county , then that executive committee which
is recognized by the State Central Committee of
such party , by written certificate of the chairman
thereof , shall be recognized by said Board of Reg-
istration Commissioners in making the appointment
of registration officers .
Should the Board of Registration Commissioners
fail to appoint such officers of registration or if any
of such officers fail to attend for thirty minutes
after the time for commencing the said general
initial registration , or refuses to act , the officer in
attendance representing the same political party of
the absentee shall appoint a suitable person to act in
his stead for said registration ; or if both represen-
tatives of the same political party are absent , the
qualified voters present affiliating with the party of
said two absentees shall select , viva voce , suitable
persons to act in their stead . Such officers of regis-
tration shall , before entering upon the duties of
their office , take an oath faithfully to discharge their
duties as such officers before some person authorized
to administer an oath , or if such person can not be
present it may be administered by the clerk of the
registration , who shall in turn be sworn by one of
the judges of registration .

396 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 11. The fact that a person has been appointed
and is an election officer shall not disqualify such
person from being appointed or as acting as a reg-
istration officer at the initial registration herein pro-
vided for .
12. The registration officer for the initial reg-
istration herein provided for in each election pre-
cinct of said county having a city of the first class
in this Commonwealth , shall, on July 11 , 1932 , pro-
ceed to register the qualified voters in each precinct ,
as provided herein , and such registration officers
shall, beginning on said day , sit for two consecutive
days between the hours of six A. M. and seven P. M.
for the purpose of registering the qualified voters
in each precinct, as herein provided .
§ 13. Each political party having representation
on the County Board of Election Commissioners
shall have the right , for , at and during said initial
registration , to appoint one challenger in each pre-
cinct by certifying the name of such challenger to
the officers to permit such challenger to remain in
the place where said registration is being conducted
for the purpose of challenging the right of any per-
son to register .
§ 14. Officers of registration at said general
initial registration shall have the same power to
preserve order at the place of registration as is
exercised by sheriffs of election at polls . Said officers
shall register every citizen of the United States in
his or her respective precinct in such county who
shall apply to be registered at the time and place
and in the manner required by law , who is legally
qualified to vote at the next election after such reg-
istration . Every person so applying to be registered
shall be required to sign in triplicate his or her
first , middle and last name in full , in his or her own
handwriting , upon loose -leaf registration record

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 397
forms to be used in his or her respective precinct ,
and which signature shall be attested by the clerk
and sheriff of said precinct , and there shall also be
entered on said original loose -leaf registration
record form , in proper places provided therefor , the
name of such person applying , address of such per-
son , the color or race , and sex of such person , his
or her apparent weight and apparent height , his or
her party affiliation , his or her occupation , if any ,
at the time of such registration , the birth -place and
date of birth or apparent age of such person ;
length of time in state , county and precinct ; if nat-
uralized ; when and how, and other means of identi-
fication , and the date of such registration . The
original loose -leaf record forms shall also contain
blank spaces sufficient for six years of comparative
signatures of voters which must be made on original
registration record before ballot is issued voter .
Where voter swears he is unable to write the word
" sworn" should be written in blank for said year's
election by clerk of precinct .
In the event any person applying to register shall
state that he or she can not write his or her name ,
as above provided , the clerk shall swear such person
and the officers of registration shall make such in-
vestigation of the case as they deem necessary , and
after such oath has been taken and such investiga-
tion made , and if it shall be determined that such
person is entitled to register, the clerk shall sign
the loose -leaf registration record form for such per-
son , and such person shall sign said record by mak .
ing his or her mark thereon , and the clerk shall
attest such mark and note on said record that said
person's name was signed at the request of the per-
son so registered and upon oath and testimony that
such person could not write .

398 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
If any officer of registration , or any challenger ,
entertain any doubt as to whether or not any person
offering for registration is entitled to such registra-
tion , or if any one's right to register is challenged ,
citizens may be called in , not exceeding three in
number , who shall be examined touching the quali-
fications of such person or persons who offer to
register . In making registration the clerk shall act
as the recording officer , and the judges shall decide
all questions relating to the qualifications of persons
offering to be registered , except that, in case of a
difference of opinion between the judges , the clerk
shall have the deciding vote .
It shall be the duty of the sheriff of each county
in which there is a city of the first class to furnish
a suitable and convenient place in each precinct for
such initial registration .
It shall be the duty of the officers of said general
initial registration in each precinct to return the
individual registration records in the form of loose-
leaf pages , which have been signed as herein pro-
vided , and all unsigned pages , blanks , forms and all
paraphernalia to the office of the Board of Registra-
tion Commissioners of the county in which the said
initial registration is held , not later than two days
after the holding of said initial registration , and
said board shall immediately cause to be made four
exact copies of the records of each person who reg-
istered at said general initial registration . For the
purpose of making the said copies of said records
of said general initial registration , the Board of
Registration Commissioners is hereby authorized to
employ such necessary additional temporary extra
employees and help , in addition to the clerks and
employees provided for in Section 5 of this act , in
order that the records of said board and the work
herein specified shall be completed within thirty

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 399
days after initial registration . Such additional
temporary extra employees shall be paid in the same
manner as is provided in Section 5 of this act for
the payment of the clerks and employees thereby
authorized . Said board shall preserve the original
record of each registrant , signed by him or her , and
two of said copies thereof , in its office , arranged as
herein provided . Said board shall then furnish one
copy of said records aforesaid to each of the city
executive committees of the two political parties
having representation on the County Board of Elec-
tion Commissioners .
The chief clerks and employees in said office shall
file , arrange and group the original signed loose-
leaf registration records or pages , and one set of
signed copies thereof , according to precincts , to be
known as precinct registers . Said precinct registers
shall be arranged in order by streets and numbers
in each respective precinct , except in precincts out-
side the corporate limits of said city of the first
class , the said two registration records shall be
arranged in alphabetical order according to the last
names of said voters . Where more than a single
voter is registered at one and the same address said
loose -leaf registration record of said voters at said
address shall , in said precinct registers , be arranged
in alphabetical order under said address according
to the last names of said voters . The other set of
signed copies of said loose -leaf registration records ,
retained by said board , shall be filed and arranged
without regard to precincts in alphabetical order for
the entire county according to the last name of
said voters .
Upon each primary , special or general election ,
the precinct registers , containing the original loose-
leaf registration records , signed by the voters , after
having first been securely locked in the loose -leaf

400 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
binders provided therefor , shall be turned over by
the board to the regularly appointed clerks of elec-
tion of each respective precinct for their use in
holding said election . Said clerks shall sign receipts
for said precinct registers and within two days
after such election or primary said clerks shall
return said precinct registers to the office of said
board and receive a receipt therefor .
Before a ballot shall be issued to any such per-
son, applying therefor and writing his or her name
on the said original registration record , as provided
for herein, it shall be the duty of the judges of the
election in that precinct to compare the signature
of every such person , as soon as he or she has so
written his or her name , as herein provided , with
the signature of said person on the loose -leaf regis-
tration record in the precinct register . The challeng-
ers of the respective political parties present shall
also have the right to compare said signatures at the
same time said comparison is made by said judges
of the election . If the signature does not cor-
respond with and is not the same as the signature
on the original registration record in that precinct
register , a ballot shall not be issued to the person
so applying . In case of a difference of opinion.
between the judges over said signature , the sheriff
of the election shall have the deciding vote . And if
a ballot is issued to such person in violation hereof,
the election officer or officers issuing such ballot or
consenting thereto and the person receiving same ,
shall severally be guilty of a felony and such offense
shall be punishable by a fine of not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than one thousand dol-
lars for each offense or imprisonment in a state
penitentiary for not less than one year nor more
than five years , or both such fine and imprisonment .
And if any election officer wrongfully and wilfully

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 401
refuses to issue a ballot to a person entitled to same ,
he shall be guilty of a felony and such offense shall
be punishable by a fine of not less than one hundred
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars for each
offense , or imprisonment in the penitentiary of not
less than one year nor more than five years , or both
such fine and imprisonment .
In the event any person applying to vote at any
election shall state that he or she can not write his
or her name as above provided , the clerk in that pre-
cinct shall immediately swear such person as to his
or her name and his or her inability to write his or
her name and thereupon he and the challengers of
the respective political parties shall examine the
loose -leaf registration record in the precinct regis-
ter , and if it be shown by said record that the person
of that same name signed his or her name on the
loose -leaf registration record in his or her own
handwriting a ballot shall not be issued to the per-
son so applying . And if a ballot is issued to such
person in violation thereof the election officer or
officers issuing such ballot or consenting thereto and
the person receiving same, shall severally be guilty
of a crime and such offense shall be punishable by a
fine of not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than one thousand dollars for each offense , or im-
prisonment in a state penitentiary for not less than
one year nor more than five years , or both such
fine and imprisonment . And if a ballot is wrong-
fully and wilfully refused to a person entitled to
vote , the election officer or officers so refusing such
ballot or any election officer or officers consenting
to said refusal shall severally be guilty of a felony
and such offense shall be punishable by a fine of not
less than one hundred dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars for each offense , or imprisonment
in the penitentiary for not less than one year nor

402 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
more than five years , or both such fine and imprison-
ment .
§ 15. The Board of Registration Commissioners
and the Chairman of the County and City Executive
Committee , in said county of each of the two
political parties having representation on the
County Board of Election Commissioners shall
constitute and compose a board to prepare
proper loose -leaf registration blanks , forms and
loose -leaf binders for the registration of voters .
as herein provided . It shall be the duty of
said committeemen and board to have said
loose -leaf registration blanks , forms and binders
properly prepared on or before the 5th day of July ,
1932 , by which date the necessary blanks , forms and
binders shall be in the hands of the Board of Reg-
istration Commissioners of each county having a
city of the first class in the State for distribution to
the officers of the initial registration in each precinct
of said county as herein provided . Said loose -leaf
registration blanks , forms and binders shall be
printed , prepared and furnished as other books are
now printed and prepared for the Commonwealth .
Said blanks , forms and binders shall be paid for by
each county out of the county treasury , as is now
provided by law for the payment of any and all
other blanks , forms and books furnished each county
by the State of Kentucky . The forms of the loose-
leaf registration record for each voter shall be 4½
inches x 7½ inches , and in forms as follows :
