§ 16. Each officer of registration for said gen-
eral initial registration shall receive as compensa-
tion the sum of five dollars per day for each day he
or she is required to register voters , to be paid out
of the county treasury .
§ 17. After a person has once registered in the
precinct in said county or city in which he or she
resides , either at the initial registration or there-
after , as herein provided , it shall not be necessary
for such person again to register except as herein
provided . If and when said person moves from the
precinct in which he or she is registered into a dif-
ferent precinct, such person , before he or she shall
be permitted to vote in any election , shall have his
or her registration record canceled and register in
new precinct in the manner herein provided .
§ 18. Only the persons who are duly and prop-
erly registered , as herein provided , more than four-
teen days preceding any primary election in each
year shall be permitted to participate in the vote
at such primary election . During the period com-
mencing on the 14th day preceding any primary
election in any year , and lasting through the day on
which such primary election is held , any person
legally qualified to register but who has theretofore
failed to register , or who desires to change his or
her registration , may register or change his or her
registration in the manner herein provided ; but
such person shall not be entitled to participate in or
vote at such primary election in that year . It shall

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 405
be the duty of the Board of Registration Commis-
sioners to register such persons , or to change their
registration records , who apply during such period .
But said Board shall not place the registration
records of such persons in the respective precinct
registers until after the primary election in that
year has been held , provided the provisions of this
Act and the registrations made thereunder shall not
apply to nor affect the qualification of any voter in
any primary or special election held previous to the
regular November election of 1932. The registra-
tion records now in force in cities of the first class
in the State of Kentucky shall be used in the regu-
lar August primary of 1932 or in any special elec-
tion that may be called , and the special primary
registration for said August primary of 1932 shall
be held as now provided by law to supplement said
present registration records , and those voters resid-
ing outside of a city of the first class in said coun-
ties shall be required to register under the provi-
sions of this Act for qualification to vote in the gen-
eral November election of 1932 and thereafter .
§ 19. After the general initial registration in
1932 has been held , as herein provided for, no per-
son shall be permitted to register until the Board
of Registration Commissioners opens its offices as
herein provided . Commencing August 1 , 1932 , the
Board of Registration Commissioners of such
county shall open its offices for the purpose of con-
ducting and making all further and future registra-
tions in such county of such qualified voters of such
county as have heretofore failed to register in the
precinct in which they live or who have moved from
the precinct in which they had registered into a
different precinct in said county . Said office shall
be maintained and kept open every day in each year
between the hours of 8:30 A. M. and 5:00 P. M. ex-

406 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
cepting Sundays and legal holidays , and excepting
the period of 60 days each year commencing 55 days
preceding any election held in November of any
year . Provided , however , that said offices may be
kept open until 10:00 P. M. on two days out of each
month , to be fixed by the Board , except during the
said 55 -day period when such offices are closed as
herein provided . Said offices may be closed at 1:00
o'clock P. M. on Saturdays if and when such closing
is authorized by said board . Commencing on the
55th day preceding the November election in each
year and for a period of 60 days thereafter , no per-
son who has not theretofore registered shall be per-
mitted to register or transfer to any other precinct
in such county during such period . For the pur-
pose of registering persons not theretofore reg-
istered , said offices during said 60 day period shall
be deemed closed . Except during the hours and
periods in each year when said offices shall be closed ,
as herein provided , any person , qualified to register
in said county containing a city of the first class ,
who has not theretofore registered , may , upon going
in person to the Board of Registration Commis-
sioners in said county , register in the manner herein
provided . Said registration shall be made in the
same general manner provided herein for registra-
tion at the initial registration , and under such rules
as the Board of Registration Commissioners shall
adopt , consistent with the provisions of this Act .
The same number of copies of said registrations
shall be made , preserved , and disposed of , in the
same manner as provided in section 14 ofthis Act .
At any time during the year, excepting said 60 -day
period , any person who is registered in said county
and who has moved from the precinct in which he or
she is registered to a different precinct in said
county , may , upon going in person to said office ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 407
have his or her prior registration changed in the
manner herein provided .
It shall be the duty of any person who is
registered under this Act and who moves thereafter
from the precinct in which he or she is registered to
a different precinct in said county , to go in person ,
as soon as possible after such removal , to the office
of the Board of Registration Commissioners and
have former registration record canceled and then
register from present address . And if such removal
or removals took place more than 60 days prior to
the next election , and said person at said time is
otherwise qualified to register , it shall be the duty
of said Board to cancel former registration and
have applicant register from present address .
$ 20 . Whenever and wherever the boundaries of
any precinct are changed by law , and such change
places a registered voter of any old precinct in a new
or different precinct, it shall be the duty of the
Board of Registration Commissioners and its
officers and employees to transfer the individual
registration record of such voters in said old pre-
cinct to the proper new precinct register in which
such voters are placed by said change of boundaries .
And said transfer shall be made without any applica-
tion therefor by such registered voters . Such
change of precinct and ward numbers and the date
thereof shall be noted in the individual registration
records of such voters , and a notice shall be mailed
by said board to such voters advising him or her of
such transfer .
§ 21. Any registered voter may, at any time dur-
ing office hours of the Board of Registration Com-
missioners , change his or her party affiliation by
appearing in person before the officers and employ-
ees of said board at its offices and making applica-
tion that such change be made . It shall then be the

408 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
duty of the board to cancel the former registration
record and have applicant register in order to
change party affiliation .
§ 22. Any person who shall disturb , intimidate ,
abuse or by force or other unlawful means hinder ,
delay or disturb or attempt to hinder , delay or dis-
turb , any officer of registration , while performing
his or her official duties , shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor , and upon conviction shall be fined not less
than ten dollars nor more than one hundred dollars .
And any person who shall intimidate , abuse , or by
force or threat attempt to intimidate or prevent any
lawful registrant from registering shall be guilty of
a misdemeanor and upon conviction shall be fined
not less than ten dollars nor more than fifty dollars ,
or confined in jail for not less than ten days nor
more than thirty days .
23. If and when any person is denied proper
registration by the registration officers at the initial
registration or by the officials or employees in the
office of the Board of Registration Commissioners ,
or if the registration record of any person is altered
in any way, or transferred , such person or either of
the executive committees , may appeal to said Board
of Registration Commissioners for a hearing and
correction in respect to said registration alteration
or transfer . The hearing of such appeal shall be
conducted in the manner and under such rules and
regulations as may be adopted by said board and
not inconsistent with the provisions of this act . No
such appeal , in respect to any action on the part of
said registration officials , may be filed later than
thirty days prior to any election . The board shall
proceed to hear such appeals as soon after same are
filed as possible , but not later than ten days there-
after , and in each case it shall make and file its
written order disposing of same . If upon such

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 409
appeal registration , or correction , is denied by said
board then and in that event such person , so denied ,
or either of the executive committee , shall have the
right , not later than five days after said final order
is filed , to appeal such order of said board to the
Election Commissioners of the county in which such
city is located by filing with the clerk of such Board
of Election Commissioners a petition of appeal .
Such appeal shall be heard by said Board of Elec-
tion Commissioners de novo . Upon the filing of
such petition it shall be the duty of said clerk to
cause to be issued immediately a notice thereof to
the Board of Registration Commissioners and place
same in the hands of the sheriff of such county who
shall within twenty-four hours serve said notice on
one of the chief clerks . It shall be the duty of said
chief clerk immediately to mail a notice of said
appeal to each of the County Executive Committees
of said county , of the political parties represented
on the County Board of Election Commissioners .
Said cases shall be docketed for a hearing on the
seventh day after same are filed with the clerk , or
upon the order of said Board of Election Commis-
sioners , may be heard on an earlier date , in which
case the clerk shall give notice of such order by mail
to the petitioner and the Board of Registration
Commissioners . Appeals may be prosecuted from
the decisions of the Board of Election Commis-
sioners to the circuit clerk in the time and manner
provided for civil appeals .
$ 24. Any officer of registration who shall wil-
fully fail to perform his or her duty , as set forth in
this act , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon
conviction be fined not less than ten dollars nor more
than one hundred dollars .
§ 25. The following means and methods shall be
used to purge the registration records , herein pro-

410 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
vided for, and keep them corrected up to date ,
to-wit :
( 1 ) The Board of Registration Commissioners
shall preserve the original records of all registra-
tion records which are canceled , for whatever
reason , in a separate permanent file , and they shall
be filed and arranged in alphabetical order for the
entire county according to the last name of the per-
son registered thereon .
""Said file shall be designated " The Canceled
Registration Register . ' When a registration
record is canceled , the original record and the two
copies thereof shall be marked in large letters in
red " canceled ," and there also shall be noted
thereon in red ink the reason for such cancellation .
The original of such record shall be preserved as
above provided . The board shall on the first day of
each week transmit one set of the canceled copies
of the signed registration records canceled during
the preceding week to each of the County Executive
Committees , of such county , of the political parties
represented on the County Board of Election Com-
missioners .
( 2 ) It shall be the duty of the office and officers
of the Bureau of Vital Statistics of the State Board
of Health , located in any city of the first class , to
furnish , on Monday of each week , to the Board of
Registration Commissioners of said county , an
official and certified report containing a complete list
of the registration of deaths occuring in said county
during the week immediately preceding thereto ;
likewise , it shall be the duty of the clerk of the
circuit court of such county to furnish on Monday
of each week to the board an official and certified
report containing the names and addresses of all
persons who have during the week immediately pre-
ceding been adjudged insane or convicted of treason

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 411
or felony or bribery in any election , and the offense
of which each person was convicted ; likewise , it
shall be the duty of the clerk of the county court of
such county to furnish on Monday of each week to
the board an official and certified report containing
the names and addresses of persons whose names
have been changed by marriage , or otherwise , as
evidenced by the record , during the week im-
mediately preceding thereto . Said lists shall be
used by the Board of Registration Commissioners
to purge the registration records .
Immediately upon receipt of such lists of deaths ,
adjudications of insanity , convictions and changes
of names , it shall be the duty of the board , for the
purpose of avoiding mistakes , to mail a notice to any
person whose name is included in said lists , and who
is registered as herein provided , at the address
appearing on the registration record of such person,
stating that the registration record of such person
will , unless protest is filed within five days from and
including the date of such notice , be canceled and
the reason therefor . The failure to file a protest
within said time , shall cause the registration record
of such person to be canceled . If a protest is filed ,
the board shall investigate the case and shall correct
any mistake in the registration records concerning
such person which such investigation discloses . It
shall be the duty of the board on Monday of each
week to cause to be removed from the registration
records , maintained in triplicate as herein provided ,
the registration record of any person to whom
notice was mailed , and no protest filed , as herein pro-
vided , and to cancel said registration records and
to preserve and dispose of the original and copies
of said registration records as provided in the pre-
ceding subsection No. (1 ) .

412 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(3) Whenever any elector , who is registered ,
moves out of the precinct , in which he is registered ,
to a different precinct in said county , it shall be his
or her duty to go in person to the office of the Board
of Registration Commissioners , and make and sign
an application to have his or her registration record
changed and transferred to the proper precinct as
herein provided . Said application and the means
of identifying the applicant shall be made in the
same manner and form provided for original regis-
tration , and it shall be the duty of said board to
keep a record of all changes , and to furnish the
county and city executive committees of said county
of the two political parties having representation on
the County Board of Election Commissioners with
lists thereof in the same manner as hereinbefore
provided .
(4 ) If any elector , who is duly and properly
registered , fails to vote at any election within a
period of two years , as shown by the voting record
of such person on his or her registration record , the
registration record of such person shall be can-
celed . Provided , however , the elector whose regis-
tration is about to be canceled for this reason shall
be notified through the mails of that fact and he or
she shall be permitted to reinstate his or her regis-
tration by mailing to the registration office , within
five days after notice , a signed request for reinstate-
ment . Cancellation of this character shall be han-
dled in the same manner as other cancellations are
made under the provisions of this act . If an elector
is denied such reinstatement such a person may
appeal in the same manner prescribed in Section 23
of this act .
It shall be the duty of the board to cause to be
made a house to house canvass in each precinct of
said county in each year , for the purpose of check-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 413
ing the registration records . Said canvass shall be
made jointly by two investigators in each precinct .
One of said investigators in each precinct shall be
appointed by the board from lists of three names
per precinct to be submitted in writing by one of the
county executive committees , and one in each pre-
cinct from lists of three names per precinct to be
submittted in writing by the county executive
committee of the opposite political party . Said
lists shall be submitted ten days in advance of
the date specified herein for the commencement of
said canvass ; and in the event either committee fails
to submit a list or lists within said time , then the
board shall appoint the investigators to represent
the party failing to furnish such list or lists , in its
own discretion , from persons registered as members
of that party . The board shall have the right , in its
discretion , to remove any investigator and to ap-
point a substitute from the lists submitted as above
provided, or in the event no lists have been sub-
mitted or all the names thereon have been exhausted ,
to appoint any person registered as a member of the
same political party as the investigator who is re-
moved . All appointments from said lists shall be
made by the board in the order in which the names
appear thereon.
Said canvass shall be made during the first week
of the period of 60 days next preceding the general
election held in the year 1932 and each year there-
after .
The board shall prepare and furnish each investi-
gator with a list of the names and addresses of the
registered voters in the precinct which he is to in-
vestigate , arranged in convenient order to facilitate
the investigation .
The investigators appointed as aforesaid for each
precinct shall make a writen report to the board , not

414 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
later than the last day of the said one week period ,
giving the names and addresses of all persons
registered in said precinct who are not legally en-
titled to vote at the election to be held in such year .
Where the report of the investigators in any pre-
cinct agree that any person registered in said pre-
cinct is not legally entitled to vote at the election to
be held in such year , the board shall make such can-
cellations in the registration records in its posses-
sion as may be necessary to make said records cor-
respond with the reports made by said investigators
and correct up to date ; provided that no such change
shall be made until after notice has been mailed to
the person affected , at the address appearing upon
his registration record on file in the office of the
board, stating that it is the intention of the board
to cancel his registration unless protest is filed
within five days , nor until after the expiration of
said five day period ; and in the event protest is filed ,
no such change shall be made until after the protest-
ant has been given a reasonable opportunity to be
heard.
The investigators hereinabove provided for shall
be paid by the board such compensation , not exceed-
ing five dollars per investigator per precinct , as the
board shall determine .
Protests filed under the provisions of this sub-
section shall be heard by the board as expeditiously
as possible and in such manner and at such times
as herein provided ; and the final actions of said
board in these cases must be filed not later than the
day following the date of said written orders . Said
appeals shall be taken and disposed of in the same
manner as provided for appeals and hearings
thereof in Section 23 of this act . The board shall
on or before ten days prior to any November elec-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 415
tion, furnish each county and city executive commit-
tee of said county , of the two political parties repre-
sented on the County Board of Election Commis-
sioners , with a copy of its records of all changes ,
transfers and cancellations so made under the pro-
visions of this subsection in the registration records
of said office . No further change or cancellation
shall be made in registration records until after the
election , except to correct clerical errors , all of
which clerical corrections shall be reported daily to
each of the executive committees .
(5 ) Any citizen of such county and each of the
county and city executive committees shall have the
right to challenge the registration of the registered
voters . Such challenge shall contain the name and
address of the registered voter , the grounds of the
challenge and the signature of the challenger and
shall be filed not later than four weeks prior to any
election . Such challenger shall be heard and appro-
priate action thereon taken by the Board of Regis-
tration Commissioners at such time as it may fix,
after notice has been served the challenged voter .
Said notice to the challenged voters shall direct them
to appear within three days and show cause why
their names should not be canceled and shall be
served by an employee of the board, to be employed
and paid in the same manner as herein before pro-
vided for extra help , by delivering or offering to
deliver a copy of said notice to said voter in person ,
or , if said person can not be found at his place of
residence as shown by his registered record , by leav-
ing a copy thereof , with a person over the age of
sixteen years , residing in the same family with said.
voter ; or if no such person be there , by affixing such
copy to the front door of such place of residence ,
and the person who serves such notice must make
an endorsement in the notice stating when and how

416 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
it was served ; and , if a copy of it be not delivered to
the person to whom it is directed , the endorsement
must state the fact authorizing the manner of
service pursued , and if the person who serves the
notice be an officer his endorsement must show his
official position ; if he be not an officer , he must make
an affidavit that his endrosement is true , that he is
over sixteen years of age, and that he is not a party
to , nor interested in the proceding . Such endorse-
ment of an officer and such endorsement and affidavit
of one who is not an officer shall be proof of service .
Such officer or person authorized to serve the notice
shall make a return within three days after the re-
ceipt of such notice . The failure of an elector ,
against whom a challenge has been served , as herein
provided , to appear shall be prima facie evidence
that he or she is not entitled to be registered and
shall cause his or her registration record to be can-
celed . Appeals from the action of said board may
be taken in the manner provided in Section 23 of
this act .
§ 26. Any voter who changes his or her name by
marriage or otherwise shall be required to register
anew . The old registration record of such voter
shall be marked " Canceled
changed to
name
" and the new name
inserted in the blank space therefor ; and same shall
than be placed and filed in the " canceled registra-
tion register , " and the copies thereof disposed of ,
as herein provided .
27. In addition to the registration records
proper , herein provided for, the office of the Board
of Registration Commissioners shall maintain ade-
quate and systematic records to be adopted by said
board, covering the following subjects : ( 1 ) person-
nel of all employees , permanent , temporary and
field ; ( 2 ) fraud cases , protests and appeals , includ-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 417
ing reports , investigations, hearings , trials and dis-
position ; ( 3 ) official actions of the office , or board ,
including complaints and petitions received ; ( 4 ) de-
tailed statistics of registration in voting ; and ( 5 )
financial accounts .
§ 28. It shall be the duty of the Board of Regis-
tration Commissioners and its officers and employ-
ees to keep the registration records , herein pro-
vided for, safely in its office and not to suffer same
to be taken therefrom , except that the precinct
registers , containing the original of the registration
records of the voters in the respective precincts ,
may be taken from said office for the purposes pro-
vided for herein or on the order of any court or the
County Board of Election Commissioners for the
purpose of using same in any trial or proceeding .
The board shall permit any citizen , at such time or
times as will not interfere with the proper conduct
of its office , to copy any registration record without
fee or charge . Copies of registration records made
and furnished by said board shall be paid for by the
person ordering and receiving same at the rate of
twenty-five cents per copy for each registration
record so copied . Fees so collected by said board
shall be paid by said board into the county treasury
each week .
§ 29. In the event the original of any individual
registration record is lost or destroyed by fire or
otherwise , or so mutilated , blotted , defaced or
otherwise in such condition as to render same diffi-
cult , troublesome or unsafe to use same longer , the
Board of Registration Commissioners may order a
new registration of voters in said precinct which
shall be conducted in the same manner as registra-
tion of voters is conducted in the precincts at the
initial registration as herein provided for ; or said
board may make new registration records of the
Acts- 14

418 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
said voters in such precinct in the manner herein
provided for the replacement of a single lost or
mutilated registration record .
$ 30 . Any member of the Board of Registration
Commissioners , the chief clerk and each of the em-
ployees , temporary or permanent , of said Board ,
shall , during their respective terms of office or em-
ployment , have authority to administer oath to any
person of whom information is sought touching any
matter pertaining to the administration of this Act ;
and furthermore any member of said Board , or any
of its said officers or employees , may issue and serve
subpoenaes for witnesses to appear and give testi-
mony in any investigation having for its purpose
inquiry into the registration of any person under
this Act . Any person who shall refuse to take the
oath herein provided and any person who shall fail
to attend in compliance with such subpoenae , served
by delivering a copy thereof to such person , and any
person having been sworn shall refuse to answer
any pertinent question put to such person, shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and fined not less
than five nor more than one hundred dollars , or im-
prisoned in the county jail not less than five nor
more than thirty days , or both so fined and im-
prisoned . Any person , having been sworn as herein
provided , who shall wilfully and knowingly give in
evidence that which is false , concerning any subject
or subjects pertaining to the registraton of any
person registered or seeking to register , shall be
deemed guilty of a felony and be confined in the
penitentiary not less than two nor more than five
years . The word " oath " as used in this act shall
include affirmation .
31. Any person who shall cause himself to be
registered in more than one election precinct, or
give a false number of his residence , or registered

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 419
more than once , in the same precinct , or who shall
cause himself to be registered , knowing that he is
not lawfully entitled to registration , or any person
who shall make a false oath as to his ability to read
or write , or any person who shall aid or abet in the
commission of any of said acts , shall be deemed
guilty of a felony , and shall be imprisoned in the
penitentiary not less than two nor more than five
years .
§ 32. Any officer of registration , or member ,
officer or employee of the Board of Registration
Commissioners , or election officer , or other person ,
who shall unlawfully alter any registration record ,
or add any name thereto , or who shall wilfully
secrete , suppress or destroy any such record or
records , or who shall make or aid in making any
false or fraudulent registration record , shall be
deemed guilty of a felony , and shall be confined in
the penitentiary not less than two years nor more
than five years , and shall forfeit any office he then
holds , and shall forever be disqualified from holding
office .
33. Any person who , by himself or in the aid
of others , shall forcibly break up , or attempt to
break up , a registration held as provided in this
act , or shall forcibly prevent or attempt of prevent ,
any person from approaching or entering a place of
registration , shall be fined not less than one hundred
dollars nor more than five hundred dollars , or im-
prisoned not less than six months nor more than
twelve months in the county jail , or both .
$ 34 . The officers of registration shall not re-
quire any person applying to register to state his or
her age , if he or she objects to answering such ques-
tion , except that each person applying to register
shall state that he or she is more than twenty - one

420 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
years of age, or will be at least twenty - one years of
age on the day of the next election .
$ 35. All supplies , required under this act , shall
be bought by competitive bids upon uniform specifi-
cations and contract shall be awarded to the lowest
bidder.
or§ 36. If any section , paragraph , clause
separable provision of this Act insofar as the same
relates to and requires the registration of voters in
the county or counties outside of the corporate
limits of cities of the first class , should be adjudged
to be unconstitutional , such decision shall not effect ,
or render unconstitutional , any other section , para-
graph , clause or separable provision of this Act in-
sofar as it relates to and requires the registration
or other means of identification of voters living
within the corporate limits of any city or cities of
the first class , it being the intention of the General
Assembly in enacting this Act to enact each section ,
paragraph , clause or provision separately .
$ 37 . The registration records now existing in
cities of the first class in the State of Kentucky are
hereby declared to be null and void and canceled .
Said registration records or books shall be de-
livered , by the officials in whose charge and posses-
sion said records now are , to the Board of Registra-
tion Commissioners of such counties , herein pro-
vided for and created . Said Board shall preserve
said old records , for purpose of reference and for
such other purposes as may be necessary , for a
period of not less than two years, after which said
old records may be destroyed , in the discretion of
the Board .
$ 38 . This act and each of its varous sections ,
paragraphs , clauses and provisions shall be liberally
construed in favor of the purposes of this Act .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 421
§ 39. All laws or parts of laws relating to the
registration of voters in cities of the first class in
this State , conflicting with the provisions of this
Act, are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 85.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 1550-17 , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's Edition , 1930 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section 1550-17 . Ballots and Ballot Boxes .
That Section 1550-17 be and the same is hereby
amended and re - enacted so that when said section
is re-enacted and shall read as follows :
There shall be a separate ballot for each political
party subject to this act , at the primary election
provided for herein . Such ballots shall be printed
in substantially the same manner as now provided
by law in case of regular elections , except that on
the back thereof shall be printed the words " Official
Primary Ballot , " and at the head thereof shall be
printed the words " Official Primary Ballot , "
together with proper party name and the party em-
blem . The party emblem in each case shall be the
same as that used at the last preceding regular elec-
tion , unless sixty days before the primary election
the proper party authority certify a different
emblem to the secretary of state , in which event he
shall certify the new emblem to the county clerks
as herein provided .
All the official ballots designed to be voted in the
primary nominating elections shall be printed in
black ink upon a good quality of white ballot paper.
The arrangement of each ballot shall be exactly the

422 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
same for each political party , and the size and the
printing shall be the same for each political party .
Duplicate impressions of the ballots for each polit-
ical party voted for at every primary election shall
be printed upon cheaper colored paper . These col-
ored ballots shall be used solely as sample ballots
for the information and convenience of voters , and
they shall not be voted or counted .
.
The ballots shall be printed so as to give each
elector a clear opportunity to designate his choice
of candidates for nomination by making with the
stencil a cross in the square after the name of each
candidate for whom he wishes to vote for nomina-
tion ; and on the ballot may be printed such words
as will aid the elector to do this , such as , " Vote
for one," "Vote for two , " and the like , to inform
the elector of the number of candidates for whom
he is entitled to vote for each office , and at the bot-
tom or outer end of each ballot shall be prepared a
secondary stub , separated from the body of the bal-
lot by a perforated line . Said secondary stub shall
be in respects as the like stub on the ballot used at
the general elections , and shall be used in like man-
ner for the same purpose.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 86.
AN ACT relating to elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Any person , being qualified , otherwise than
as to residence in the State and County , to vote in
any primary or general election held in this State or
any subdivision thereof , and who had theretofore
come into the State , or into the County in which

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 423
such election is held , from some other state , or into
the County from some other county , for the purpose
of attending any college or university located in this
state , shall be entitled to vote in such primary or
regular election upon complying with the following
conditions , and registering his intention to become
a resident of the State or County, as the case may
be in the following manner :
(a ) If the person offering to vote in such pri-
mary or regular election had theretofore been a res-
ident of some other state , such person shall have
filed with the County Court Clerk of the County in
which the election is held , one year before the day
of such election , a written , signed and dated state-
ment to the effect that such person , at the time
of the making of such statement , in good faith in-
tended then to be and become a resident of the Com-
monwealth .
(b ) If such person offering to vote in such pri-
mary or regular election had theretofore been a resi-
dent of this State , but a non -resident of the county
in which such person offers to vote , shall have filed
with the County Court Clerk of the county in which
such election is held , six months before the day of
said primary or general election a written , signed
and dated statement to the effect that such person
at the time of the making of such statement in good
faith intended then to be and become a resident of
such county .
§ 2. The County Court Clerk with whom such
statement is filed , shall lodge and safely keep such
statements , and shall list them alphabetically in a
book to be kept for the purpose , and shall give each
statement a serial number , which number shall be
noted on the alphabetical list , and which list together
with the statements shall constitute a public record .
When a clerk furnishes the ballots and other vot-

424 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ing paraphernalia to the election officers he shall
also furnish a copy of the list to the officers of elec-
tion in each precinct of the county .
$ 3. Any person not a resident of the State , or
of the county in which such college or university is
located , and who has come into the State or such
county for the purpose of attending such college
or university , and who shall vote in any primary or
general election without having first complied with
the foregoing section 1 of this Act , shall be deemed
to be guilty of a misdemeanor , and punished on con-
viction by a fine of not less than fifty nor more than
two hundred dollars , or imprisoned in jail for not
less than ten nor more than sixty days , or both so
fined and imprisoned within the limits fixed herein ,
and any clerk failing to comply with the duties
herein imposed shall, upon conviction , be punished
in like manner .
$ 4. For filing and recording each statement re-
quired hereunder , the County Court Clerk shall be
paid ten cents ( 10c ) ; and for each name furnished
to the officers of election in each precinct he shall
be allowed one cent ( 1c ) . This cost to be paid as
other election and registration expenses are now
paid .
§ 5. The provisions of this law shall not apply
to persons offering to vote in elections held in
cities where registration of voters is required .
"Except that students of colleges or universities
in such cities or towns shall be entitled to register
and vote upon expression of intention to establish
residence in such cities or towns . "
Approved March 8, 1932 .
CHAPTER 87 .
AN ACT to amend paragraph eleven ( 11 ) , Section 1938a , Chapter
57 , and paragraph twenty-four ( 24 ) , Section 1954c , Chapter

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 425
57a , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to
open season and bag limit on quail or bobwhite , and license
to hunt, and providing for statement of game birds and animals
killed , by adding thereto the language as hereinafter set out .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That paragraph 11 , Section 1938a , Chapter
57 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition 1930 , relat-
ing to bag limit on quail or bobwhite , be amended
by adding thereto at the end of the section as it now
stands , the following language :
" Provided that it shall be unlawful for any per-
son to kill , catch , or have in possession after same
have been caught or killed , in this state , a total ex-
ceeding in the aggregate seventy -five quail or bob-
white in any one year ."
So that said paragraph and section , when amended
and re -enacted will read as follows :
Section 1938a . Paragraph 11. Quail or Bob-
white ; Bag Limit . No person shall shoot , kill , or
have in possession , more than twelve ( 12 ) quail or
bobwhite in any one day , between the 15th day of
November of any year and the 1st day of January
of the succeeding year , both dates inclusive ; pro-
vided , however , that any person having hunted two
days in succession may have in possession a total of
not to exceed twenty -four ( 24 ) quail or bobwhite
killed by himself during such hunt ; provided that it
shall be unlawful for any person to kill , catch , or
have in possession after same have been caught or
killed , in this state , a total exceeding in the aggre-
gate seventy -five quail or bobwhite in any one year .
§ 2. That paragraph 24 , Section 1954c , Chapter
57a , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition 1930 , re-
lating to license to hunt , be amended by adding
thereto at the end of the section the following lan-
guage :
1
T

426 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
"Provided , that before any license to hunt shall
be issued under this section , the person applying
for such license shall file with the clerk of the county
court a statement , duly sworn to , showing the total
number of game birds and animals killed , or cap-
tured in this state , by the applicant during the last
preceding calendar year .
""
So that said paragraph and section when amended
shall read as follows :
Section 1954c . Paragraph 24. Resident License-
How Obtained . Any person who has been a bona
fide resident of this state for one year then past,
may procure a hunter's license for himself or her-
self by filing his or her affidavit with the county
clerk of the county in which he or she resides , stat-
ing his or her age , place of residence , post office
address , height , color , color of his or her eyes and
hair , and the fact whether he or she can write his
or her name , and by paying to the said county clerk
the sum of one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) which license shall
entitle him or her to hunt in all counties of this
state , but nothing herein contained shall be construed
as repealing the present law requiring every hunter
to obtain the written permission of the landowner
to hunt on his premises .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force
according to the provisions of the Constitution , and
all acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith are here-
by repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 88 .
AN ACT to amend Section 1938a -6, and Section 1938a -16 , of the
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the open
and closed season on game animals and birds , by striking out
certain words and inserting others therein so as to change the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL . ASSEMBLY 427
closed season on wild turkey , deer and elk by extending it ,
and providing a penalty for violation thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 1938a - 6 , of the Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , be amended by striking
out beginning with the word " imported " in the
sixth line of said section , the following words ,
"imported pheasant or Hungarian partridge , before
November 15 , 1932 , and on and after November 15 ,
1932 , no person shall kill more than two wild tur-
keys during the open season in one year , nor more
than two pheasants or Hungarian partridges in any
one day when it is lawful to kill them " , and insert-
ing in lieu thereof the following words , " Before the
15th day of November , 1934 , and on and after Nov-
ember 15th , 1934 , no person shall kill more than
two wild turkeys during the open season in one year ,
and no person shall catch or kill , or pursue with
such intent or have the same in his possession after
it has been caught or killed , any imported pheasant
or Hungarian partridge , before the 15th day of
November , 1932 , and on and after November 15th ,
1932 , no person shall kill more than two pheasants
or Hungarian partridges in any one day when it is
lawful to kill them " , so that said section when
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
1938a -6 . Wild turkey , imported pheasants or
Hungarian partridge , when unlawful to catch or kill .
No person shall catch or kill , or pursue with such
intent or have the same in his possession after it has
been caught or killed , any wild turkey , before the
15th day of November 1934 , and on and after Novem-
ber 15th , 1934 , no person shall kill more than two
wild turkeys during the open season in one year ,
and no person shall catch or kill , or pursue with

428 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
such intent or have the same in his possession after
it has been caught or killed , any imported pheas-
ant or Hungarian partridge , before the 15th day of
November , 1932 , and on and after November 15th ,
1932 , no person shall kill more than two pheasants
or Hungarian partridges in any one day when it is
lawful to kill them . It shall be lawful to kill im-
ported pheasants on November fifteenth , sixteenth ,
seventeenth , eighteenth and nineteenth and on these
days only each year , provided that no person shall
kill more than two cock birds each day .
§ 2. That Section 1938a -16 , of the Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , be amended by
striking out the word and figures " November 15 ,
1932 " , and inserting in lieu thereof the following
word and figures , " November 15th, 1934 " , so that
said section when amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows :
1938a -16 . Deer and elk , penalty for molesting .
No person shall in the State of Kentucky , before
November 15th , 1934 , hunt, pursue , chase , catch , kill ,
injure , or molest any deer or elk. Any person vio-
lating this section , or any part thereof , shall upon
conviction be fined not less than fifty dollars nor
more than one hundred dollars for each offense , and
on failure to pay such fine , shall be confined in the
county jail or work house or placed at labor on any
of the public works of the county in which he is con-
victed and fined for a length of time not exceeding
one day for every one and a half dollars of said fine .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 89 .
AN ACT relating to Game and Fish Commission , amending and
re-enacting sections 1 and 2 of Chapter 53 , and repealing
Section 3 of Chapter 53 of the Acts of the General Assembly
of 1930 .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 429
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 3 of Chapter 53 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1930 , being Section 1954c -5
of Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Supplement ,
be and the same is now repealed .
§ 2. That Section 1 of Chapter 53 of the Acts of
the General Assembly of 1930 , being Section 1954c - 1 ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Supplement , be
amended and re -enacted and when so amended and
re -enacted shall read as follows , to -wit :
Game and Fish Commission .-A game and fish
commission is hereby created ; said game and fish
commission shall have perpetual succession with
power to make and use a corporate seal and with
power to alter the same ; to adopt by -laws , rules and
regulations for the government of its members ,
agents and employees , provided such by -laws shall
not conflict with the constitution of Kentucky . Said
commission shall consist of five members , who shall
serve without compensation , except for necessary
expenses to be paid upon an itemized statement
thereof, duly audited by said commission , and shall
have an office in the capitol and be supplied with
suitable stationery , blanks , and postage for the
transaction of its business ; provided , however , that
the Chairman of said commission shall receive com-
pensation for his services , as fixed in the following
section .
§ 3. That section 2 of Chapter 53 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1930 , being section
1954c - 2 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Supple-
ment , be amended and re -enacted , and when so
amended and re-enacted shall read as follows , to-
wit :

430 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
On or before the first day of July , 1932 , the Gov-
ernor of the Commonwealth shall appoint five mem-
bers of the Fish and Game Commission , one of
whom shall serve for a term of four years , two of
whom shall serve for a term of three years , and two
of whom shall serve for a term of two years , and
thereafter as the terms of each member expires ,
the Governor shall appoint their successors , respec-
tively for a term of four years each , and whenever
a vacancy shall occur , by death , resignation or re-
moval , from office , or from the state , or otherwise
the Governor shall appoint his successor for the
remainder of the unexpired term . In making the
appointments the Governor shall not appoint more
than one member of the Commission from any one
county .
Of the first Commission appointed the Governor
shall designate the person who is to fill the office for
a term of four years as Chairman of the Commis-
sion , and such person shall hold the office of Chair-
man for a period of four years, unless he shall
vacate his office , or be removed from office or the
Chairmanship . Thereafter , upon appointment of
the succeeding Commission the Governor shall name
the Chairman of the Commission from among the
members who are appointed , and such person so
named shall be the Chairman until the expiration
of his term , unless such person should vacate said
office of Commission or should be removed from
office , or from the Chairmanship of said Commis-
sion .
Said Chairman shall perform all duties hereto-
fore , now or hereafter required of the Chairman ,
and in addition shall perform all the duties now or
heretofore required of the Executive Agent , and
shall devote all his time to the discharge of such

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 431
duties as are now or heretofore required of the Exec-
utive Agent , and it shall be his duty to use his best
efforts toward the enforcement of the game and
fish laws , and to carrying into effect its full pur-
poses , and he shall remain in the office of the Com-
mission at all reasonable times . Before entering
upon the discharge of his duties as such Chairman ,
he shall execute a bond to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the penal sum of $ 5,000.00 , with surety ,
said bond to be approved by the Governor ; and to
be conditioned on the faithful discharge of his duties
as Chairman of the Commission ; the premium, if
any , on said bond to be paid out of funds appropri-
ated to or belonging to the Commission ; and said
bond to be filed with the Secretary of State . The
Chairman shall be paid for his services hereunder
the sum of $2,500.00 annually payable monthly and
in the manner of the payment of other officers of the
Commonwealth , and shall also be allowed his ex-
penses as is above provided for other commis-
sioners .
Said Game and Fish Commission shall meet quar-
terly at its office , and as much oftener as may be
necessary . Special meetings of the commission may
be called by the chairman at any time , and also shall
be called by him on the written request by registered
mail of two or more members of the Commission
within five ( 5 ) days after receiving such written
request .
§ 4. Whenever and wherever the words " Execu-
tive Agent " are used in Chapter 53 of the Acts of
1930 , and wherever and whenever any duties are
imposed on the Executive Agent , the said words
"Executive Agent " shall be held to mean the Chair-
man of the Commission , and all and such duties and
powers shall be imposed upon and vested in said
Chairman .

432 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 5. All laws or parts of laws and all Acts in
conflict with this Act are now repealed , and Section
3 of Chapter 53 , Acts of 1930 , is specifically re-
pealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 90.
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact section 2043-12 , Supplement Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition , the same being section
12 of chapter 68 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1930
and entitled , “ An Act concerning the manner of commitment
of incompetent veterans of the World War who are beneficia-
ries of World War Veterans ' Act as amended ; and regulating
the appointment , defining the duties and governing the actions
of guardians and committees for beneficiaries of the World
War Veterans ' Act , as amended , and the World War Adjusted
Compensation Act , as amended ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 2043-12 , Supplement Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1930 edition , being section 12 of
chapter 68 of the Acts of the General Assembly ,
1930 , relating to the manner of commitment of
incompetent veterans of the World War who are
beneficiaries of the World War Veterans ' Act as
› amended ; and regulating the appointment , defining
the duties and governing the actions of guardians
and committees for beneficiaries of the World War
Veterans ' Act, as amended , and the World War
Adjusted Compensation Act , as amended , be and the
same is hereby repealed , amended and re - enacted ,
so that such section when thus repealed , amended
and re-enacted shall read as follows :
Section 12. Every guardian or committee shall
invest the funds of the estate in such manner or in
such securities , in which the committee or guardian

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 433
has no interest, as allowed by law or approved by
the court or in annuities .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Whereas, any delay in the privilege of so invest-
