Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 91 .
AN ACT amending section 4706 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 4706 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended so that said section as amended shall read
as follows : It shall be lawful for persons or cor-
porations holding funds in a fiduciary capacity for
loan or investment , to invest the same in bonds or
other interest bearing obligations of the Govern-
ment of the United States of America ; bonds or
State Warrants and other interest bearing obliga-
tions of the State of Kentucky ; securities authorized
by an Act of Congress entitled " The Federal Farm
Loan Act , " and approved July 17 , 1916 ; and real
estate , real estate mortgage notes , bonds and other
interest bearing or dividend paying securities which
would be regarded by prudent business men as safe
investments ; and to make loans with such securities
as collateral ; and a fiduciary shall account for all
interest or profit received .

434 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
•
Where securities originally received by a fiduci-
ary do not meet the requirements for the invest-
ment of trust funds as provided in this statute , or
where securities in which trust funds have been
properly invested thereafter become inelligible for
investment of trust funds as provided in this stat-
ute , then such inelligible securities shall be sold at
such time , and upon such terms and conditions , or
shall be held or otherwise handled in such manner ,
as prudent business men would , under the particu-
lar circumstances , consider proper ; and a fiduciary
shall be responsible for otherwise acting.
All acts and parts of acts in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Approved March 25 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 92 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 762a -20 , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the writing of insur-
ance through licensed agents within this Commonwealth , and
prescribing penalties for violation of the provisions of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 762a -20 of the Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby
is amended and re -enacted , and as amended and re-
enacted the same shall read as follows : No insur-
ance company shall write , place or cause to be writ-
ten or placed , any policy or contract for direct in-
surance upon any property in this Commonwealth ,
except through a duly licensed resident local agent
in this Commonwealth ; provided , however , the
words duly licensed resident local agent , as used in
this section , shall not be construed to mean or to
include , in any sense, any State or special agent or

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 435
travelling and/or salaried employee of any stock
fire insurance company doing business in this Com-
monwealth ; and provided further that this section
shall not apply to policies covering property in tran-
sit while in the custody of any common carrier , or
to rolling stock or other property of any common
carrier used and employed by it as a common car-
rier of freight or passengers ; nor to policies of
mutual insurance companies or inter -insurance as-
sociations on which no commissions are paid except
to a home office manager or attorney in fact .
§ 2. Section 762a -20a . Nothing in this act shall
apply to farm property .
§ 3. Section 762a -20b . None of the provisions
of this act shall apply to assessment or cooperative
associations or companies , mutual companies , recip-
rocal or inter -insurance exchanges , their attorneys
in fact , or their agents , servants or employees or
representatives .
§ 4. Section 762a -20c . Any person , firm , or cor-
poration violating any of the provisions of this sec-
tion shall upon conviction for such offense be fined
in a sum not less than five hundred ( $ 500.00 ) dollars
nor more than one thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) dollars for
each offense , and in the event that any such person ,
firm or corporation so offending shall be convicted
and fined as herein provided , the Insurance Com-
missioner shall suspend the license to do business
in this Commonwealth theretofore granted to any
such person , firm or corporation for a period of not
exceeding ninety days for the first offense , and upon
conviction and fine as herein provided for a second
offense , the Insurance Commissioner shall revoke
the license of any such person , firm or corporation ,
and no license to do an insurance business in this
Commonwealth shall thereafter be granted to any
such person , firm or corporation so offending for a

436 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
second time , for a period of twelve months . Pro-
vided , however , that any order of the Insurance
Commissioner suspending or revoking any license
pursuant to the provisions of this section shall be
subject to adequate court review upon the law or
equity side of the docket in any court of competent .
jurisdiction within this Commonwealth .
§ 5. It is hereby declared to be the intention of
the General Assembly in the enactment of this law
to regulate the writing of insurance within this Com-
monwealth by duly licensed resident local agents ,
excepting such othr agents , servants , or employees
as are specifically exempted herein from the opera-
tion of this act , but in so doing it is also specifically
declared to be the intention of the General Assembly
to enact each and every one of the sections herein.
contained separately and independently of each
other , and , therefore , in the event that any section
of this act should be declared to be void or uncon-
stitutional by any court of competent jurisdiction ,
such decision shall not affect the remainder of this
act nor any other section hereof.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 93 .
AN ACT authorizing cities of the Third class and Second Class
in Kentucky by ordinance to establish groups of its police or
fire departments to obtain the advantages of the group plan of
life insurance ; and to appropriate from the funds of the city
in aid thereof any sum annually, not exceeding fifty per cent
of the annual premiums on such policies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the legislative departments of cities of
the third class and second class in Kentucky shall
have authority , by ordinance , to form its employees

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 437
of the police or fire departments into a group , or
groups , for the purpose of their obtaining the ad-
vantages of the group plan of life insurance ; to aid
such employees as long as they continue under such
employment by paying from the funds of the city
any sum annually not exceeding fifty per cent . of
the annual premiums on such policies , and to con-
tract with the insurer on such other terms as may
be provided for in such ordinance .
Whereas , many of the police and fire departments
of cities of the third class in Kentucky are now with-
out life insurance and can only obtain such insurance
upon the group plan and are daily incurring grave
hazards to their lives , an emergency is hereby de-
clared to exist and this Act shall take effect from
and after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 94 .
AN ACT to amend chapter 146 of an Act of the General Assem-
bly passed at its 1920 session entitled " An Act relating to
free public libraries in cities and towns of the third , fourth ,
fifth and sixth classes of this Commonwealth ", the same being
sections 2741d -1 to and including 2741d -5 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Official edition 1930 ; by adding thereto sections pro-
viding that certain libraries in said cities and towns shall be
free public libraries , how they may become such , for their
support , for separate funds , separate libraries for the white
race and for the Negro race , for taxing separate property of
the two races and the division of funds , for issuing warrants
on claims , for appointment of the first board of trustees and
defining the construction to be applied to this amendment .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In cities and towns of the classes named
herein in which there is no free public library , but
in which a library building which cost at least ten

438 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
thousand dollars ( $ 10,000,00 ) in cities of the third ,
fourth and fifth classes , or at least five thousand dol-
lars ( $ 5,000.00 ) in towns of the sixth class , and is
reasonably of that value , has been or hereafter may
be erected , and fully paid for by voluntary dona-
tions made by citizens and business organizations
of said city or town and the vicinity thereof, not
including in any one donation any amount over two
per cent of the whole sum contributed , on a lot in
said city or town , the fee - simple title to which is in
a library association or corporation or trustees for
library purposes , and which lot is fully paid for and
free from incumbrance , and which library building
and lot with the emoluments thereof are devoted
wholly to library purposes , and into which library
building and library , all citizens of said city or town ,
or all white citzens thereof , or all colored citizens
thereof, as the case may be , have , and shall continue
to have , free and equal rights of admitttance into
said library building and free and equal rights and
privileges to the use of the library as named and
provided for in this Act, shall be entitled to and
shall have all of the aid , benefits , and applications
of the terms and provisions herein , and in said
chapter 146 ; provided , however , the two -tenths of
one mill on each one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) named in sec-
tion 1 of said chapter 146 as the least popular sub-
scription required , and the one mill on each one
dollar ( $ 1.00 ) named in said section and in section 3
thereof as the maximum limit of the tax levy author-
ized therein shall, for all separate class libraries
provided for in this section , be based or levied upon
each one -dollar ( $ 1.00 ) of all property which is sub-
ject to taxation for other municipal purposes in such
city or town owned by all persons of the race or
races , respectively , to whom is given free access to
said library building and library ; provided , the min-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 439
.
imum valuation of the library building erected or
that may be hereafter erected by voluntary dona-
tions , as herein provided , for the exclusive use of
the class or race known as the Negro or colored race ,
shall be four thousand dollars in cities of the third ,
fourth and fifth classes and two thousand ($2,000.00 )
in towns of the sixth class .
§ 2. If a library building herein provided for
has been or hereafter shall be erected from volun-
tary donations as herein named , for the separate
and exclusive use of each of said races in such city
or town and a library is or shall be maintained
therein according to the provisions of this amend-
ment for each separate race , the revenue derived
from the one-half of all fines , forfeitures , and costs
from the police court, appropriated in section 5 of
said chapter 146 shall , when paid to the treasurer of
the city or town, be credited to the library fund of
said separate library organizations , respectively , in
the proportion per capita as the total number of
citizens of such city or town belonging to each of the
respective races bears to the total population of the
city or town as shown by the last preceding census
made by the official authorities of the said city or
town . The revenues derived from the ad valorum
tax against property belonging to the respective
races shall be credited to the library fund for which
the levy was made , giving to each the amount col-
lected on property of each race , respectively , viz .:
credit to the library fund for the white race the
revenue received from property of the white race
and credit to the library fund for the Negro race the
revenue received from property of the Negro race .
Amounts received from ad valorum taxes on all
property and any and all other taxes received for
library purposes , shall be apportioned to the said
library funds in the way and manner that is now or

440 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
hereafter may be , provided by law for common or
graded school purposes .
§ 3. If in any of said cities or towns only one of
the library buildings named and provided for in
section 1 hereof has been or heerafter may be
erected and a library installed therein for the sep-
arate and exclusive use of only one of the said races
of said city or town , such free public library shall
receive the full amount of the fines , forfeitures and
costs appropriated in section 5 of said chapter 146 ,
and the full amount of taxes levied for library pur-
poses on all the other property of said city or town
subject to taxation for school purposes , except there
shall be no tax levied on any property of the race
who do not have free access to the said building and
library.
§ 4. Members of the first board of any public
library herein provided for shall be appointed imme-
diately after the tax is levied or subscriptions have
been made and approved by the mayor or chief exec-
utive-one for one year , one for two years , one
for three years and two for four years ; thereafter
the term of office shall be for four years and until
their successors are appointed and qualified .
$ 5. Each warrant issued on claims allowed for
such public libary shall specify the proper library
fund out of which it is payable if there are more
than one library fund .
§ 6. The trustees and managing authorities of
any library which they desire shall become a free
public library herein authorized or a majority of
the members of each thereof may file their petition
with the common council or governing authority of
such city or town or county requesting that such
library property be declared a free public library
as provided for in said chapter 146 ; but no such

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 441
library property shall be in any way affected by
this law until such petition is so filed .
The petition shall state the facts and conditions
existing as required herein to become a free public
library and shall be sworn to by one of the petition-
ers . The council or governing authority may require
additional proof of the statements in the petition .
An appeal may be taken from the decision of the
council or governing authorities by any of the peti-
tioners or any taxpayer of said city or town to the
Circuit Court of the county .
§ 7. The provisions of this act shall not in any
way apply to any free public library heretofore or
hereafter organized under said chapter 146 , except
those libraries named in section 1 of this amend-
ment which shall hereafter be organized ; but all
provisions of said chapter 146, not inconsistent with
this amendment , shall apply to all libraries named
in section 1 of this amendment .
§ 8. If any portion of any provision of this act
shall be adjudged to be unconstitutional such judg-
ment shall not affect any other portion and the re-
mainder of the act shall continue in force and effect .
If the provision in section 3 hereof for the use of
fines , forfeitures and costs in the police court shall
be adjudged to be unconstitutional , the library
therein named shall receive its proportional part of
said fines , forefeitures and costs as provided for in
section 2 hereof .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 95 .
AN ACT to provide the circuit courts of this State with copies
of the Statutes and Codes of Practice and Supplements thereto
and repealing Section 2432 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1930 Revision .

442 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That on a certificate from the circuit judge
of any circuit court of this State that such circuit
court is without a copy of the latest edition or
revision of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes and/or
Codes of Practice , the State Librarian be and he is
hereby required and directed to furnish to the cir-
cuit clerk of such circuit court a copy of the Statutes
or Codes so lacking for the use of the circuit judge
in such circuit court and shall , on such certification
of such circuit judge that such copy of the Statutes
and/or Codes of Practice have been lost , mutilated
or torn , furnish to such circuit clerk for the use of
the circuit judge in such circuit court a duplicate
copy of such book so lost, mutilated or torn , and
shall furnish to such circuit clerk for such circuit
judge from time to time any supplement or supple-
ments issued to such Statutes and/or Codes . In
courts of continuous session possessing more than
one judge , the Librarian shall furnish a copy of such
Statutes and/or Codes and any supplement thereto
to each judge of such circuit court on such certifica-
tion . The Librarian shall certify the cost of any
book or books so furnished hereunder to the Auditor
of Public Accounts who shall issue his warrant on
the Treasurer for the same , payable out of the gen-
eral fund .
§ 2. That Section 2432 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's 1930 Revision , be, and the same
is hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 96 .
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Sections 2711a -1 to 2711a -127 ,
inclusive and Sections 2711a -129 to 2711a -141 , inclusive , cf

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 443
Chapter 86 of Baldwin's 1930 Supplement to the Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition , same being an Act providing
for the organization , armament , discipline and government of
the militia and National Guard of Kentucky ; providing for a
Military Department of Kentucky ; providing for the proper
officer and other employees of said Department ; same being
intended to repeal and re -enact all of the present laws of Ken-
tucky relating to the militia and National Guard except Sec-
tion 2711a -128 of Baldwin's 1930 Supplement to the Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition , which section is specifically
exempted from the effect of the repeal and re -enactment herein
provided for .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections 2711a - 1 to 2711a -127 , inclusive , and
Sections 2711a -129 to 2711a -141 , inclusive , of Chap-
ter 86 of Baldwin's 1930 Supplement to the Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's 1922 Edition , be and the
same are hereby repealed and re - enacted so that
said laws when re - enacted shall read as follows :
§ 1. There is hereby created a department of
the State government to be known as the " Military
Department of Kentucky " , which department shall
have charge of and shall be responsible to the Gov-
ernor for the proper functioning of the National
Guard , the militia and all other military or naval
matters of the Commonwealth . The Adjutant Gen-
eral, who shall be appointed by the Governor , shall
be the executive officer of this Department , and shall
be responsible to the Governor for the proper car-
rying into effect of all laws , rules and regulations
of the United States Government and of the State
of Kentucky affecting the National Guard , the
organized militia or other military organizations
properly established by law .
§ 2. The Governor of the State , by virtue of his
office , shall be the Commander -in -Chief of the militia
of the State , except of such portions as may be at

444 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
times in the service of the United States ; but he
shall not command personally in the field unless
advised so to do by a resolution of the General
Assembly .
§ 3. The Staff of the Commander -in-Chief shall
consist of an administrative and a personal staff .
The administrative staff shall consist of an Adjutant
General of the grade of Brigadier General , and of
such other officers of the Kentucky National Guard
as may be detailed by the Governor , of the grade
prescribed by the War Department of the United
States for the administrative staff for the State of
Kentucky . The personal staff shall consist of not
more than four aides -de-camp , detailed from the
commissioned officers of the National Guard in
active service from those of the grades below that
of Colonel . Such detail shall not add to the grade
of the officer so appointed . The officers detailed as
aides -de-camp shall not be relieved from duty with
their respective organizations , but shall perform all
duties pertaining thereto , except when on duty as
aides -de-camp under orders of the Governor . The
staff shall be appointed by the Governor and shall
hold office during his pleasure or during the term
for which he was elected .
§ 4. The militia of the State shall consist of all
able -bodied male citizens , and all other able - bodied
males who have or shall have declared their inten-
tion to become citizens of the United States , between
the ages of 18 and 45 , who are resident of the State ,
and of such other persons as may upon their own
application be enlisted or commissioned therein pur-
suant to the provisions of the law , subject , however ,
to such exemptions as are now or may be hereafter
created by the laws of the United States . The militia
of this State shall be divided into two classes .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 445
(a) The organized militia to be known as the
Kentucky National Guard .
(b ) The reserve militia , consisting of all other
persons liable to military service .
§ 5. The officers , judicial and executive , of all
the departments of the State of Kentucky and of
the United States , without regard to age , shall be
exempt from duty in the militia , and all persons
who because of religious beliefs shall claim exemp-
tion from military service , when the conscientious
holding by such persons shall be established under
such regulations as the Governor shall prescribe ,
shall be exempt from military service in a combatant
capacity , but no person so exempted shall be exempt
from military service in any capacity that the Gov-
ernor shall declare to be non -combatant .
§ 6. When the Governor , by public proclama-
tion , so commands , it shall be the duty of each
County Clerk to enroll the names of all persons
liable to duty in the militia and by such times as
the said proclamation may prescribe , and to trans-
mit said rolls to the Adjutant General of the State .
When such service is required of the County
Clerk , he shall be allowed one cent ( 1c ) for each
name returned by him , to be paid out of the State
treasury , and if the County Clerk neglects or re-
fuses to render such service when so required , he
shall be subject to a fine of not more than five hun-
dred dollars ( $500.00 ) and not less than one hundred
dollars ($100.00 ) .
7. The officer making the enrollment shall , at
the time of making same , serve a notice of such en-
rollment upon each person enrolled by delivering
such notice to him or leaving it with some person of
suitable age and discretion at his place of residence .
All persons claiming exemption must , within
fifteen days after receiving such notice , file a written

446 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
statement of such exemption , verified by affidavit ,
in the office of the county clerk. Such clerk shall
thereupon , if such persons be exempted according
to law , mark the word " exempt " opposite his name ;
and the remainder of all thus enrolled and not found
thus to be exempt shall constitute the reserve militia
of the State , and such county clerk shall transmit
a copy of such corrected roll to the Adjutant Gen-
eral of the State . The commanding officer of each
organization in the National Guard and the heads of
each fire and police department in each city or town
shall , whenever an enrollment is ordered shall file
in the office of such county clerk a certified list of
the names of all persons in his command or depart-
ment .
8. The Governor shall have power , in case of
insurrection , invasion , tumult, riot or breach of the
peace , or imminent danger thereof , to order into the
active service of the State any part of the militia
that he may deem proper . When the militia of this
State , or a part thereof , is called forth under the
Constitution and laws of the United States , the Gov-
ernor shall order out for service the Kentucky
National Guard , or such part thereof as may be
necessary and , if the number available be insuffi-
cient , he shall order out the reserve militia or such
part as may be necessary .
§ 9. Whenever it shall be necessary to call out
any portion of the reserve militia for active duty in
case of insurrection , invasion , tumult , riot or breach
of the peace , or imminent danger thereof , or when
called forth for service under the Constitution and
laws of the United States , the Governor may call
for and accept as many volunteers as are required
for such service , or he may by public proclamation
summon all or any number of them to be asembled
by the sheriff at any point or points within the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 447
county or respective counties as may be designated
by the proclamation, and thereupon the sheriff , on
penalty of a fine of one thousand dollars ( $1,000.00 )
for refusal or neglect , shall summon the members
of the reserve militia within his county as he would
summon a civil posse , and under similar penalties
against those who fail to obey his summons . But
should the Governor's proclamation call for only a
part of the reserve militia of any county , the sheriff
may first call for volunteers therefor and if it
should appear to be inexpedient , he shall summon
the members of the reserve militia to be assembled
in their magisterial districts and there determine ,
under the supervision and control of a justice of the
peace , by lot , those who shall be selected to fill the
quota of that magisterial district . The reserve mili-
tia so called into service shall become subject to the
same laws and regulations as members of the Ken-
tucky National Guard .
$ 10 . The Governor shall immediately upon his
induction into office appoint an Adjutant General ,
who shall be not less than thirty years of age and
shall have been an officer in the federally recognized
Kentucky National Guard for a period of not less
than three years . The officer so selected as Adju-
tant General shall by Executive Order of the Gov-
ernor be ordered and placed on active duty, and in
the event said appointee does not already hold the
commission of Brigadier General of the Kentucky
National Guard , he shall be given temporary rank
as Brigadier General of the Kentucky National
Guard and shall be so commissioned by the Gov-
ernor . In the event that the commission is only
temporary, said commission shall automatically be
vacated when said appointee shall be relieved from
duty as Adjutant General or at the expiration of the
term of the Governor making the appointment . The

448 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
office of Adjutant General shall not be incompatible
with or serve to vacate the commission in the Ken-
tucky National Guard held by the officer appointed
Adjutant General at the time of his appointment ,
nor shall it be incompatible with any office to which
he might be promoted , nor shall it interfere with or
prevent his attending to the duties of the office
held at the time or to which he may be thereafter
appointed .
§ 11. The Adjutant General of the State shall
perform the duties prescribed for him by the laws
and regulations now or hereafter issued thereunder
by the United States or the State of Kentucky , now
or hereafter enacted . He shall direct and supervise
the functions and duties of the chiefs of staff depart-
ments . He shall supervise all troops and all depart-
ments , arms and branches of the Kentucky National
Guard . Such supervisory power covers primarily
duties pertaining to command , discipline , training
and recruiting of the National Guard , military op-
erations , distribution of troops , inspections , arma-
ment , military education and instruction and kindred
matters connected with the fiscal administration and
supply of the National Guard when not in the service
of the United States . The Adjutant General shall
(a) Superintend the preparation of all returns
and reports required by the United States from the
State .
(b ) He shall keep a register of all the officers
of the National Guard of the State , and keep in his
office all records and papers required to be kept
and filed therein and make a report to the Governor
for the annual period ending on June 30 of each
year of the operations and condition of the Adjutant
General's office , the State Arsenal and the National
Guard .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 449
13
(c ) He shall cause to be prepared and issued all
necessary blank books , forms and notices required
to carry into full effect the provisions of this law.
All such books and blanks shall remain the property
of the State .
(d ) He shall attend to the safe keeping and re-
pairing of the ordinance , arms , accoutrements ,
equipments , and all other military property belong-
ing to the State or issued to it by the United States .
All military property of the state , which after a
proper inspection shall be found unsuitable for use
of the State , shall under the direction of the Gov-
ernor be disposed of by the Adjutant General at
public auction or by inviting bids after suitable
advertisement of sale daily for ten days in at least
one newspaper published in the city or county
where the sale is to take place , or the same may be
sold at private sale when so ordered by the Gover-
nor , or may , with the approval of the Governor , be
turned over to any other department , board or com-
mission of the State government by which it can be
used . He shall from time to time render a just and
true account of the sales made by him to the Gover-
nor and shall turn the proceeds of same into the
General Fund of the State .
( e ) He shall keep a just and true account of all
expenses necessarily incurred , including pay of
officers and enlisted men , allowances to officers and
organizations , and of any other moneys required to
be disbursed by him and through his office , including
subsistence and transportation of the National
Guard and of all military property of the State and
of the United States .
(f) He shall issue such military property as the
Governor shall direct and under his direction make
purchases for that purpose . No military property
shall be issued to persons or organizations other
Acts- 15

450 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
than those belonging to the National Guard , except
to such portion of the reserve militia as may be
called out by the Governor . Purchases of property
not exceeding five hundred dollars ( $500.00 ) in value
and purchases from the Federal government shall
be made in such manner as the Adjutant General of
the state shall direct . If such purchase requires an
expenditure exceeding five hundred ( $500.00 ) dol-
lars and not exceeding oneone thousand dollars
($ 1,000.00 ) , the Adjutant General of the State shall
procure written proposals to furnish such property
from the lowest responsible bidder . If such purchase
shall require the expenditure of a sum exceeding
one thousand dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) , he shall publicly
advertise , for not less than ten days , for sealed pro-
posals for furnishing such property ; such proposals
to be publicly opened by the Adjutant General of
the state at the place , day and hour designated in
such advertisement . The Adjutant General shall , if
the Governor approves , make contract with the low-
est responsible bidder to furnish such property . All
proposals and contracts made under the authority
hereby conferred shall be filed in the office of the
Adjutant General of the state . The Adjutant Gen-
eral is authorized and directed , whenever in his
opinion it shall be to the interest of the state , to
require a party who shall agree or contract to
furnish such property to give bond to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in such sum and with such
surety as he shall direct, conditioned for the faith-
ful performance of such agreement or contract . In
case of default such bond shall be prosecuted by the
Attorney General and all moneys recovered shall be
paid by the Adjutant General of the state into the
general fund of the state . All property purchased
under the authroity hereby granted shall be in-
spected by an inspector or officer detailed for that

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 451
purpose , and no payment shall be made therefor
until it shall appear by the certificate of such officer
that such property is of the kind and quality speci-
fied in such agreement or contract . In case of in-
surrection , invasion , tumult , riot , breaches of the
peace or imminent danger thereof, the Governor
may , upon the recommendation of the Adjutant Gen-
eral , temporarily suspend the operation of this
paragraph and direct the adjutant General of the
state to purchase such military property as may be
required in open market . He shall report such
action , with the reasons therefor and a statement of
the property purchased and the price paid therefor
to the General Assembly at its next session .
(g) The Adjutant General shall establish and
maintain as part of his office a bureau of war
records in which all records pertaining to wars and
relics shall be kept . He shall be custodian of all
such records , relics , colors , standards and battle
flags now the property of the state , or in its posses-
sion , or which the state may hereafter procure or
become possessed of .
(h ) In the absence or inability to perform the
duties of the Adjutant General, he may designate
by special order an officer to perform those duties
temporarily or , if no officer is so designated , the
senior officer on duty in the Adjutant General's
office shall perform the duties prescribed for the
Adjutant General .
(i ) He shall organize units of the National
Guard at such places as the Governor shall desig-
nate and have the members therof mustered into
service under such regulations as the Governor shall
prescribe .
§ 12. All accounts and claims of every charac-
ter and description against the Military Department
shall be carefully audited by the Adjutant General ,

452 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
and all vouchers for expenditures shall first be au-
thenticated and approved by the Adjutant General
before same are allowed by the Auditor . In the
event the Adjutant General shall be absent from the
State for as much as fifteen days , or shall be other-
wise incapacitated for the same period of time to
properly audit and approve said vouchers , then said
vouchers may be allowed by the Auditor after same
have been properly audited and approved by an
officer serving in the Military Department , who has
been properly designated by Special Order to act
for and on behalf of the Adjutant General during his
absence or other incapacity .
§ 13. The Governor may also appoint , in his dis-
cretion , an Assistant Adjutant General from among
the officers of the Kentucky National Guard . Said
appointee at the time of his appointment shall be
at least thirty years of age and shall have had active
service as an officer on the active list of officers of
the Kentucky National Guard for not less than five
years next preceding the date of his appointment .
Said five years service shall be in at least the grade
of First Lieutenant . The officer so selected by the
Governor as Assistant Adjutant General shall by
Executive Order be placed upon active duty, and in
the event said appointee does not already hold the
commission of Major of the Kentucky National
Guard , he shall be given temporary rank as Major
of the Kentucky National Guard and shall be so
commissioned by the Governor . In the event the
commission is only temporary , said commission
shall automatically be vacated when said appointee
shall be relieved from duty as Assistant Adjutant
General or at the expiration of the term of the Gov-
ernor making the appointment . The office of Assist-
ant Adjutant General shall not be incompatible with
or serve to vacate the commission held by the officer
47775

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 453
appointed Assistant Adjutant General at the time
of his appointment nor one to which he might be
promoted , nor shall it interfere with or prevent his
attending to the duties of the office held at the time
or to which he may be promoted thereafter , and
shall in no wise be incompatible with the office held
or to which he may later be promoted .
14 . The Adjutant General by and with the
advice and approval of the Governor shall have the
power to appoint the officers and other personnel
necessary for the proper functioning of the Mili-
tary Department and he shall prescribe their duties .
The salaries of such officers and other employees
shall be fixed by the Adjutant General with the ap-
proval of the Governor .
$ 15. Officers and enlisted men employed in the
Adjutant General's office shall not be entitled to
additional pay from the State for active field service
nor for active duty , but shall be entitled to actual
cost of transportation and three dollars per diem
in lieu of subsistence and quarters , or to actual cost
of such subsistence and quarters when so provided
in the order under which such duty is performed
when serving under orders beyond the limits of the
City of Frankfort , except that the foregoing pro-
visions shall not prohibit an officer or enlisted man
from receiving pay from the United States for par-
ticipation in encampments or field service while in
the service of the State .
§ 16. The System of organization , armament ,
discipline and government of the militia shall be
prescribed from time to time in a code of regula-
tions to be promulgated by the Governor , subject at
all times to the laws and regulations of the United
States governing the organized militia or National
Guard, and to such general exceptions as may be
authorized by the Secretary of War.

454 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
17. The articles of war for the government of
the armies of the United States as now or hereafter
enacted shall be in force at all times as to the forces
of the State and regarded as a part of this law so
far as they are consistent with its provisions and
with the regulations now or hereinafter issued
thereunder . But no punishment under such rules
and articles which shall extend to the taking of life
shall , in any case , be inflicted except in time of actual
war , invasion , or insurrection , declared by procla-
mation of the Governor to exist , and then only after
the approval by the Governor of the sentence inflict-
ing such punishment . Imprisonment other than in
guard house shall be executed in jails , penitentia-
ries or prisons designated by the Governor or by the
Adjutant General or by the convening authority of
any court martial .
§ 18. The Attorney General of the State shall be
the legal advisor of the Governor and of the Adju-
tant General of the State .
19. The National Guard of the State shall con-
sist of general officers of the line , the several staff
corps and departments as prescribed in tables of or-
ganization of the United States Army for the
National Guard , the officers and enlisted men on the
retired list, reserve list and in the National Guard
Reserve , the organizations forming the National
Guard at this date , such others as may be organized
hereafter and such persons as are enlisted and com-
missioned therein . The Governor shall have power
to alter , divide , annex , consolidate , disband or reor-
ganize any organization , department or corps when-
ever in his judgment the efficiency of the State
forces will be thereby increased , and he shall have
power to change the organization of any organiza-
tion , department or corps so as to conform to any
organization, system of drill or instruction now or

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 455
hereafter prescribed by the laws of the United
States for the organization of the National Guard ,
and for that purpose the number of the officers and
non -commissioned officers of any grade in any or-
ganization , department or corps may be increased
or diminished and the grades of such officers and
non -commissioned officers may be altered to the
extent necessary to secure such conformity ; pro-
vided that no organization of the National Guard ,
members of which shall be entitled to and shall have
received compensation under the Act of Congress
approved June third , nineteen hundred and sixteen
as amended , shall be disbanded without the consent
of the President of the United States , nor without
such consent shall the commissioned or enlisted
strength of any such organization be reduced below
the minimum that shall be prescribed therefor by
the President ; and provided further , that the as-
signment of organizations of the Federally recog-
nized National Guard to divisions , brigades and
other tactical units shall be made in accordance with
the provisions of the Act of Congress approved
June third , nineteen hundred and sixteen , as
amended . The Governor shall have power to fix
and , from time to time , to alter the maximum and
minimum number of enlisted men which shall form
part of any organization , department or corps
within the limitations fixed by the Act of Congress
approved June third , nineteen hundred and sixteen ,
as amended . The aggregate forces of the National
Guard in time of peace , fully armed , uniformed and
equipped , shall not be less than twenty -five hundred
enlisted men .
$ 20. The Governor is authorized to prescribe
the organization of the National Guard to conform ,
as far as practicable , to that prescribed by the laws
and regulations of the United States now or here-

456 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
after in force . National Guard troops allotted to
the State under the Act of Congress approved June
third , nineteen hundred and sixteen , as amended ,
when organized under authority of the Governor ,
shall be maintained by the State and furished with
adequate and suitable armory accommodations ,
ranges and camp grounds .
$ 21 . No officer or enlisted man of the Kentucky
National Guard shall incur any expenses whatso-
ever to be paid by the State , except such as are
authorized by this Act, without first obtaining
authority therefor , provided that in extreme emer-
gencies the commanding officer of any organiation ,
unit or detachment of the Kentucky National Guard
may procure services and purchase such stores and
supplies as are absolutely required for the im-
mediate use and care of his command ; and a report
of his action , containing an itemized statement or
account of such services procured or stores and sup-
plies purchased , shall be made forthwith to the
Adjutant General . Such accounts , when the emer-
gency and correctness of same are certified to by the
Adjutant General , shall be paid out of the funds of
the State .
§ 22. It shall be the duty of the Governor , when-
ever he shall deem it necessary for the safety or
welfare of the Commonwealth , or when any actual
or threatened invasion , insurrection , domestic vio-
lence , or other danger to the public interest make it
necessary to employ military force in aid of the civil
power of the government , for the enforcement of
law or to preserve the peace and the security of the
rights and lives or property of the citizens to order
into active field service so much of the Kentucky
National Guard , or military force of the Common-
wealth , as he may deem necessary , and he may by

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 457
his order prescribe the duty to be performed by the
troops thus called into active field service . The
Governor may direct the commanding officer of such
military forces thus ordered into service to report
to any of the following named civil officers of the dis-
trict , county , city or town ; circuit judge ; county
judge ; mayor ; and such civil officers may direct the
specific object to be accomplished by such military
force , but the tactical direction and disposition of the
troops and the particular means to be employed to
accomplish the object , shall be left solely to the
officers of the National Guard . Any one of the civil
officers named above shall have authority in case of
breach of the peace, riots , mobs , great calamity,
resistance to the processes of the State , or imminent
danger thereof, or imminent danger of violence to
life or property , by written order to the command-
ing officer of any division , brigade , regiment , bat-
talion , company or other unit of the Kentucky
National Guard , order into active field service such
part of the National Guard to be specified in his
written order as such civil officer may deem neces-
sary to assist the civil authorities to enforce the law
and to prevent violence to persons or property ; pro-
vided , however , that such civil officer shall have
authority only in case of emergency , when in his
judgment it is impossible or impracticable to com-
municate with the Governor in time to prevent
threatened violation of law or damage to persons or
property . Such officer and the commanding officer
of the troops ordered into service by him shall , as
soon as passible , report said facts and circumstances
relating thereto to the Governor , and the Governor
shall take such action as he may deem necessary .
Troops shall not be relieved from active field duty
except by order of the Governor .

458 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
$ 23. All officers of the National Guard shall be
commissioned by the Governor at his discretion ;
but no one shall be commissioned unless the condi-
ditions set forth in the next two sections have been
complied with , and no one shall be recognized as an
officer unless he shall have been duly commissioned ,
and shall have taken the oath of office . Commis-
sions shall be issued to officers in the arm , corps or
departments of the service of which they are a part
and not in any particular regiment or unit . Com-
missioned officers shall rank in their grade according
to the date of their commission . Between officers
of the same grade and date of appointment or com-
mission, where there has been no previous commis-
sioned service , the relative rank shall be determined
by age, where there has been previous service in the
Army of the United States or in the Kentucky
National Guard , or the National Guard Reserve of
this State , such previous service shall count in the
order herein named . The day of the appointment
of an officer shall be expressed in his commission
and shall be considered as the date thereof. When
an officer is appointed or transferred from one office
or organization to another, without increase of
grade or loss of continuous service , he shall rank
in the grade according to the date of his original
commission , which shall be stated in his new com-
mission .
$ 24. Commissioned officers must be citizens of
the United States and of the age of twenty - one and
upwards . No person who has been expelled or dis-
honorably discharged from the military or naval
service of this State , or any other state , or of the
United States , shall be commissioned . No person
shall be commissioned unless he shall possess the
qualifications now or hereafter prescribed by the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 459
laws or regulations of the United States and the
additional requirements herein prescribed for the
particular office to which he is to be commissioned .
A general officer above the grade of brigadier gen-
eral , at the time of his appointment , must be an
officer in active service in the National Guard of
Kentucky of the grade of brigadier general or field
officer , and for seven successive years immediately
preceding his appointment , he must have been in
active service in said National Guard as a field
officer . A brigadier general , at the time of his
appointment , must be an officer in active service in
the Kentucky National Guard of the grade of field
officer for five successive years immediately preced-
ing his appointment . A colonel of a regiment , at
the time of his appointment , must either be an officer
in active service in the National Guard , and for
three successive years immediately preceding his
appointment , he must have been in active service in
said National Guard as a commissioned officer ; or ,
if not in active service at the time of appointment ,
he must have had prior service of at least six years
in said National Guard or in the Army of the United
States , or in both combined , as a commissioned
officer . A lieutenant colonel and major of the line ,
at the time of his appointment , must either be an
officer in active service and for two successive
years immediately preceding his appointment he
must have been in active service in said National
Guard as a commissioned officer or, if not in
active service at the time of his appointment ,
he must have had prior service of at least six
years in said National Guard or in the Army of the
United States , or in both combined , as a com-
missioned officer . Officers of the corps of engin-
eers , the signal corps , the adjutant general's de-
partment , the inspector general's department , the
�

460 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
quartermaster corps and the ordnance department
above the grade of captain must possess the same
requirements as officers of the line of the same
respective grades . Officers of all arms , corps and
departments of or below the grade of captain must
have served one year immediately preceding
appointment in said National Guard or , if not in
active service at the time of appointment , they must
have had at least one year's service in said National
Guard , or the Army, Navy or Reserve Corps of the
United States , or both combined . An officer of the
judge advocate general's department must be a
counselor - at -law of the highest court of this State
of at least ten years ' standing if of the grade of
colonel or lieutenant colonel , of at least five years '
standing if of the grade of major , of at least three
years ' standing if below the grade of major . An
officer of the medical corps must be a graduate of a
recognized school of medicine and of at least ten
years ' practice if of the grade of colonel or lieuten-
ant colonel ; of at least five years ' practice if of the
grade of major ; of at least three years ' practice if
of the grade of captain ; and of at least one year's
practice if of the grade of first lieutenant . An officer
of the signal corps must have a knowledge of signal-
ing , telegraphy , topography and map making . A
chaplain must be a regularly ordained minister of
some religious denomination .
Staff officers , including officers of the pay , inspec-
tion , subsistence and medical departments , hereafter
appointed shall have had previous military experi-
ence , and all officers shall hold their commissions
until they shall have reached the age of sixty -four
years , unless retired prior to that time by reason of
resignation , disability or for cause to be determined
by a court martial or efficiency board legally con-
vened for that purpose.

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 461
$ 25. Every officer commissioned in the National
Guard shall be examined as to his physical condi-
tion , knowledge of military affairs and general
knowledge and fitness for service , in the arm , corps
or department in which he shall be commissioned , in
accordance with the rules and regulations of the
United States Army and of the State of Kentucky .
$ 26 . Examinations for commissioned officers of
the National Guard may be conducted by boards
organized in accordance with the provisions of the
National Guard Act or any amendment thereof
which may hereafter be enacted by Congress .
Nominations for commissions in the National Guard
shall be initiated by the commanding officer of the
organization to which the candidate is to be as-
signed and referred to the Adjutant General for pre-
liminary determination of the eligibility and fitness
of the candidate to serve in the grade to which he is
nominated . The Adjutant General may constitute
and dissolve boards to pass upon the eligibility and
fitness of candidates .
27. Every officer duly commission shall, within
ten days after his commission is tendered to him , or
within ten days after he shall have been notified
personally or by mail that the same is held in readi-
ness for him by a superior officer , take and subscribe
to the following oath of office :
9“ I, do solemnly swear that I will
support and defend the Constitution of the United
States and the Constitution of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky against all enemies , foreign and domes-
tic ; that I will bear true faith and allegiance to the
same ; that I will obey the orders of the President
of the United States and of the Governor of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ; that I make this

462 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
obligation freely without any mental reservation
or purpose of evasion , and that I will well
and faithfully discharge the duties of the office
of in the National Guard of the United
States and of the Commonwealth of Kentucky upon
which I am about to enter , so help me God . "
Such oath shall be taken and subscribed before an
officer of the National Guard of the grade of general
or field officer , summary court , or any civil officer
authorized to administer oaths . In case of neglect
or refusal to take and subscribe to such oath within
the time mentioned , such commission shall be can-
celed by the Governor and a new appointment shall
be made to fill the vacancy .
§ 28. The National Guard Reserve shall consist
of such organizations , officers and enlisted men as
may be authorized and prescribed by the laws of the
United States and regulations issued thereunder .
In time of peace officers of the Kentucky National
Guard may , upon their own application , be trans-
ferred to the Kentucky National Guard Reserve
after completing three years commissioned service
in an active organization of the National Guard ,
including commissioned service in the United States
Army. Upon the disbandment or consolidation of
organizations of the National Guard , duly author-
ized , thereby rendering the commissioned officers or
any of them surplus , all such officers qualified for
active service whether they have had three years
active commissioned service or not , except those
transferred or assigned to active units , shall be
transferred in the grade in which they are commis-
sioned to the National Guard Reserve . Officers of
the National Guard Reserve can not become mem-
bers of the National Guard in time of peace except
by appointment . Officers of the Kentucky National

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 463
Guard , who have been Federally recognized under
the National Defense Act and who shall lose or have
had withdrawn by the Federal Government such
recognition , shall be transferred by the Adjutant
General to the National Guard Reserve , and such
transfer shall create a vacancy in the list of the
officers of the Kentucky National Guard , which
vacancy shall be filled by appointment or promotion
as prescribed in this law .
§ 29. A commissioned officer tendering his resig-
nation , if the Governor accepts it , shall receive an
honorable discharge , provided he shall not be under
arrest or to be returned to a military court for any
deficiency or delinquency . If the Governor accepts
the resignation of an officer , who at the time shall be
under arrest , under charges , or returned to a mili-
tary court for any offense , deficiency or delinquency ,
such officer shall then cease to be an officer of the
National Guard and shall receive a discharge in such
form as the Governor shall direct , but he shall con-
tinue to be subject to trial and the judgment and
punishment prescribed by such military court until
the proceedings before such court are finally dis-
posed of .
$30 . Any commissioned officer who has served as
a member of the National Guard for a period of ten
years , five of which has been as a commissioned
officer , may upon his own request , and shall upon
attaining the age of sixty-four years , be placed upon
the retired list , which shall be kept in the office of
the Adjutant General. Officers so retired shall
receive no compensation for their services , but shall
be permitted on all occasions of ceremony to wear
the uniform of the grade upon which retired . The
Governor may detail officers so retired upon duty
other than in the command of troops , and when so

464 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
detailed , they shall receive like pay and allowance
as officers on the active list detailed or employed
under like conditions .
The Governor may order any commissioned officer
before a medical board consisting of at least three
commissioned medical officers and , if the board
reports such officer to be physically unable to per-
form the duties of his office , the Governor shall retire
him .
Any officer retired under the provisions of this
section , who shall have served as a commissioned
officer on active duty in the Kentucky National
Guard , or in the Army , Navy or Marine Corps of the
United States during any war in which United
States troops were engaged , shall be placed on the
retired list in the grade next higher than that actual-
ly held by him on the date of his retirement .
§ 31. The Governor may , whenever he may deem
that the good of the service requires it, order any
commissioned officer before an efficiency board to
consist of three officers senior in rank to the officer
whose fitness for service is under examination , which
is hereby invested with the powers of the courts of
inquiry and courts martial , and such board shall
inquire into the moral character , capacity and gen-
eral fitness for the service of such commissioned
officer , and record and return the testimony taken
and a record of the proceedings . If the findings of
such board be unfavorable to such officer and be
approved by the Governor , he shall be discharged
from the service . No officer whose grade or pro-
motion would in any way be effected by the decision
of such board in any case before it , shall participate
in the examination or decision of the board in such
case . Failure to appear when ordered before a
board constituted under this section shall be suf-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 465
ficient grounds for a finding of such board that the
officer ordered to appear be discharged . Upon the
recommendation of the board , an officer discharged
under the provisions of this section may be placed
upon the retired list by the Governor where the dis-
charge was issued solely because of physical inabil-
ity to perform active service .
§ 32. The commission of any officer who absents
himself without leave for three months shall be
vacated , he shall be dropped from the rolls of the
National Guard , and shall be dismissed from the
service .
§ 33. Any able -bodied man of good character ,
resident of the State of Kentucky , who can read and
write the English language , who is between the ages
of eighteen and forty -five years , and who has passed
the required physical examination , which physical
examination shall be the same as that required for
enlistment in the United States Army , may be en-
listed or re -enlisted in the Kentucky National Guard
for a term of not less than three years . A man who
has been dishonorably discharged or discharged
without honor from the United States Army, United
States Navy , Marine Corps , the organized militia
of any other state , or from the organized militia of
this State , shall not be eligible for enlistment or
re-enlistment except he produce the written con-
sent to such enlistment or re -enlistment of the com-
manding officer of the organization from which he
was dishonorably discharged or discharged without
honor , and of the officer who approved such dis-
charge .
§ 34. Every person who enlists or re -enlists
shall sign and make oath to an enlistment paper .
Such oaths shall be taken and subscribed to before
an officer authorized to accept enlistments . A per-
son making a false oath as to any statement con-

466 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tained in such enlistment papers shall upon con-
viction be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and pun-
ished by a fine of not less than ten dollars ( $ 10.00 )
nor more than fifty dollars ($50.00 ) .
$ 35. Enlisted men may be transferred to or
from organizations , departments or corps or other-
wise as provided by regulations approved by the
Governor .
§ 36. Warrant officers shall be appointed and
warranted by the Governor in his discretion upon
the recommendation of the commanding officer of
the organization with which they are to serve . Non-
commissioned staff officers and non -commissioned
officers shall be warranted :
(a ) In brigades , in regiments , in battalions or
squadrons not part of a regiment and in similar or-
ganizations by the commanding officer thereof .
(b ) In the staff corps by the senior officer there-
of ; in the medical department , by the senior
medical officer , where such medical force or detach-
ment is not a part of a regiment , brigade or similar
organization .
( c ) In all cases not hereinbefore provided for by
the Adjutant General .
All non -commissioned officers shall be warranted
in the discretion of the officer issuing the warrant ,
upon the written recommendation of the officer
under whose immediate command they shall re-
spectively serve . When an examination is required
by Federal laws or regulations or by State regula-
tions , no enlisted man shall be warranted until he
shall have successfully passed such examination . In
all other cases warrants may be issued without ex-
amination . The officer warranting a non - commis-
sioned officer shall have power to reduce him to the
ranks .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 467
$ 37 . When an enlisted man of the National
Guard absents himself without leave and there is
reason to believe that he does not intend to return ,
he may be dropped from the rolls under such regu-
lations as may be prescribed .
§ 38. An enlisted man dropped from the rolls
may be restored to duty by the Governor and will
thereafter serve for such period as added to the
time served prior to his disertion will amount to the
full term for which he enlisted .
39. An enlisted man shall not be discharged
before the expiration of his term of service , except
(1 ) By order of the Governor ; ( 2 ) By sentence of
court martial , approved by the Governor . When an
enlisted man is discharged for expiration of service
his discharge shall take effect on the last day
thereof , i . e . , if enlisted on the second day of the
month , his term will expire on the first day of the
same month in the third year of his term of enlist-
ment . A soldier on his discharge from service will
be given a certificate of discharge , signed by his
commanding officer and approved by the Adjutant
General . The character given on a discharge will
be signed by the company or detachment command-
er , and great care will be taken that no injustice is
done the soldier . If the soldier's service has been
honest and faithful , he will be entitled to such char-
acter as will warrant his re - enlistment , that is , to
character , at least " good ". Where the company
commander deems the service not honest and faith-
ful, he shall, if practicable , so notify the soldier at
least thirty days prior to discharge , and shall at the
same time notify the Adjutant General , who will , if
requested in writing by the soldier, convene a board
of officers , three if practicable , to determine whether
the soldier's service has been honest and faithful .

468 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The finding of the board , when approved by the con-
vening authority , shall be final .
§ 40. An enlisted man may be discharged without
honor : ( a ) without trial , on account of fraudulent
enlistment ; ( b ) without trial , on account of having
been disqualified for service , physically or in char-
acter , through his own misconduct ; ( c ) on account
of imprisonment under sentence of civil courts ; ( d )
when the service has not been honest and faithful ;
( e ) when especially ordered by the Governor for
any other reason .
§ 41. A dishonorable discharge shall be given
only by sentence of a general court martial ap-
proved by the Governor .
§ 42. Upon satisfactory proof of the loss of a dis-
charge certificate or of its destruction without the
fault of the person entitled to it, the Adjutant
General may cause to be issued to such person a cer-
tificate of service , showing date of enlistment and
discharge from the military service and character
given on the original discharge certificate .
§ 43. Any soldier of the National Guard who
shall refuse or neglect to obey the orders of his
commanding officer , or of any officer or non com-
missioned officer , issued as provided in this act , or
who shall hinder , interfere with or prevent any
other soldier from performing any duty under such
order , shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor ,
and on conviction shall be punished by a fine not
exceeding one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) or by im-
prisonment in the county jail for not exceeding
thirty days , or by both such fine and imprisonment .
§ 44. Upon affidavit and complaint being filed
with any county judge , police judge or magistrate ,
by any officer of the Kentucky National Guard , any
soldier of the Kentucky National Guard who shall
fail or refuse to obey any lawful order of any officer

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 469
or non -commissioned officer placed over him , or
shall violate any law , rule or regulation governing
the Kentucky National Guards , or shall absent him-
self from any drill , assembly for instruction , en-
campment , inspection , or other duty or service which
he may have been ordered to attend , shall be deemed
guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction shall
be fined in any sum not exceeding fifteen dollars
( $15.00 ) , or imprisoned in the county jail not exceed-
ing fifteen days , or both so fined and imprisoned .
No appeal shall lie to any court from any conviction
under this section , but a conviction or acquittal in
any civil court shall bar a prosecution for the identi-
cal offense in any military court . The jurisdiction
of general courts martial and summary courts shall
not be affected by this law , further than to allow any
commissioned officer of the Kentucky National
Guard to begin any prosecution he may see
fit
against any enlisted man
inany of the civil courts
herein mentioned
.
§45. Except when
inthe service
ofthe United
States
, officers and enlisted men
, when employed
in
active field service of the State
,
asprovided and
defined
inthis act
, beginning with the day they as-
semble
attheir armories or other designated place
until the day they return thereto and have been
properly relieved
, inclusive

a

as

, shall receive pay
atthe

:

470 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
