and in addition thereto actual and neccessary cost
of transportation , when such transportation is not
furnished in kind . Officers shall be allowed a flat
per diem of three dollars ($ 3.00 ) per day in lieu of
quarters and subsistence , when such quarters and
subsistence are not furnished in kind . Enlisted
men shall receive subsistence in addition to the pay
and transportation provided above .
§ 46. For duty at all encampments ordered by
the Governor , except when authorized and paid by
the Federal Government , each commissioned officer
and enlisted man shall receive the pay and allow-
ances provided in this act , except that members of
rifle teams or other team of officers and men com-
peting in any competition for which they may vol-
unteer shall not receive pay as provided in this act ,
but may receive actual and necessary expenses , in-
cluding transportation and subsistence .
47. For service and attendance upon general
courts martial , courts of inquiry and boards ap-
pointed by the Governor as member , judge advocate ,
recorder or witness , or upon inspection or other
duty ordered by the Governor , officers and enlisted
men , when not engaged on duty for which pay is
provided , shall receive the same pay and allowances
as is provided for other active duty service .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 471
§ 48. General courts martial , courts of inquiry
and boards , convened by order of the Governor , may
require the attendance of civilian witnesses by the
issuance and service of subpoenas in the same man-
ner as is provided in the laws of this State for courts
of record. The same fees and allowances shall be
paid such witness as are provided in the laws of this
State for civil courts , and shall be paid by the Adju-
tant General out of State funds upon vouchers
therefor certified to by the president of the court or
board.
$ 49. ( a ) A member of the National Guard who
shall , when on duty or assembled therefor , in case of
riot , tumult , breach of peace, insurrection or inva-
sion or whenever ordered by the Governor , or called
in aid of the civil authorities , receive any injury or
incur or contract any disability or disease by reason
of such duty or assembly therefor , or who shall
without fault or neglect on his part be wounded or
disabled while performing any lawfully ordered
duty, which shall incapacitate him from pursuing
his usual business or occupation , shall receive the
pay provided by this law and actual necessary ex-
penses for care and medical attendance during the
period of such incapacity not exceeding ninety days
from the date of receiving the injury or of incur-
ring or contracting the disease or disability . Where
a claim is made under this section , the Adjutant
General may cause examinations of the claimant
to be made from time to time by a medical officer or
officers designated by him , and he may direct the
removal of the claimant to and his treatment in a
hospital designated by the Adjutant General , and
if the claimant refuse to permit such examination
or if he refuse to go to such hospital or to follow
the advice given or treatment prescribed for him

472 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
therein , he shall forfeit and be barred from all right
to any claim , pay or allowance under this section .
(b) The body of an officer or enlisted man , who
dies while in active field service or an active duty
while his command is absent from its home station ,
shall , under the direction of his commanding officer ,
be properly prepared for burial , enclosed in a suit-
able casket , and escorted to his late residence by
a proper escort ; provided , that the cost of embalm-
ing and preparing the body for burial and cost of
casket combined shall not exceed the sum on one
hundred dollars ($ 100.00 ) . The cost of such prep-
aration for burial and casket and escort shall be a
proper charge against the State and shall be paid
from the treasury on properly certified and ap-
proved vouchers .
§ 50. If an enlisted man wilfully, maliciously ,
purposely or through carelessness or neglect per-
mits any of the arms , utensils , clothing, bedding , or
other equipment issued to him , being public prop-
erty or belonging to the State or to the United
States , to become lost, damaged or in any other
manner unfit for its proper use , the commanding
officer of his company , troop , battery or other organ-
ization shall charge the value of such article or art-
icles against the pay of the enlisted man , and it shall
be collected on such forms and under such regula-
tions as may be prescribed .
§ 51. Payments under the preceding sections
shall be made on such forms as directed by and upon
voucher approved by the Adjutant General out of
moneys in the treasury , appropriated for that pur-
pose . The necessary commissary and quartermas-
ter stores and medical supplies , transportation for
troops in service , transportation and subsistence of
organizations of the National Guard representing
the State officially on occasions of ceremony within

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 473
or without the State shall be contracted for by the
proper departmental officers and paid for in like
manner .
$ 52 . The system of tactics and field exercises
for the Army of the United States shall be the sys- .
tem of tactics and field exercises for the National
Guard of this State .
§ 53. Each company , troop and battery and de-
tachment in the National Guard shall assemble for
drill and instruction , including indoor target prac-
tice , not less than forty-eight times each year and
shall in addition thereto participate in encamp-
ments , maneuvers or other exercises , including out-
door target practice , at least fifteen days in each
year , unless such company , troop , battery or detach-
ment shall have been excused from participation in
any part thereof by the Governor, provided that
credit for an assembly for drill shall not be given
unless the assembly shall equal or exceed a mini-
mum to be prescribed by the Governor , nor unless
the period of actual military duty and instruction
participated in by each officer and enlisted man at
each assembly at which he shall be credited as hav-
ing been present shall be of at least one and one-
half hours duration and the character of training
such as may be prescribed by the Governor .
$ 54. The Governor may order the National
Guard , or any portion thereof, to perform military
duty of every prescription and to participate in
small arms and gunnery practice and competition in
this State , or in any other state or territory , or the
District of Columbia , or in any fort , camp or reser-
vation of the United States therein , and he may
authorize the performance of militray duty or par-
ticipation in small arms or gunner competition by
any part of the National Guard anywhere without
the State or without the United States .

474 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
55. The National Guard shall be provided by
the State with such arms , equipment , colors , camp
and garrison equipage , books of instruction and of
record, and other supplies , as may be necessary for
the proper performance of the duty required by this
law ; and each command shall keep such property
in proper repair and in good condition . The
National Guard shall , as far as practicable , be
uniformed , armed and equipped with the same type
of uniforms , arms and equipment as are , or shall be ,
provided for the Regular Army , and no organiza-
tion of the National Guard shall be furnished by the
State with any other type of uniforms , arms , or
equipment than are , or may be , furnished by the
United States to the members of the Regular Army .
§ 56. Every commissioned officer shall provide
himself at his own expense , with the arms , uniforms
and equipment required to be worn by officers of the
Regular Army of the United States .
§ 57. Every officer and enlisted man to whom
public property has been issued shall be personally
responsible to the State for such property , and no
one shall be relieved from such responsibility , except
it be shown to the satisfaction of the Governor that
the loss or destruction of such property was un-
avoidable and in no way the fault of the person
responsible for same . In all other cases the value
of the property lost or destroyed in the amount
determined by a surveying officer or a board , as
herein provided , shall be charged against the per-
son at fault or with the concurrence of its command-
ing officer to the command to or for which it has
been issued , and , if not relieved from such charge
by the Governor , it shall be an indebtedness from
such person or command to the State .
§ 58. Any person who shall wear any uniform ,
or any device , strap knot , or insignia of any design

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 475
or character used as a designation of rank , grade or
office , such as by law or by general regulation is
prescribed for the use of the Kentucky National
Guard , except a member of the Army and Navy of
the United States , the National Guard of this or
any other state , members of associations wholly
composed of soldiers honorably discharged from
the service of the United States , or any state , and
the faculty and students of educational institutions ,
shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon
conviction shall be punished by a fine of not less
than ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) nor more than one hun-
dred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) at the discretion of the court
or jury .
§ 59. The military shall be , at all times and in
all cases , in strict subordination to the civil power.
§ 60. The officers and enlisted men of the Ken-
tucky National Guard shall be exempt from jury
service .
§ 61. Such officers as may be designated by the
Governor shall execute proper bond to the state of
Kentucky , in such sum as the Governor may pre-
scribe , and no officer shall be relieved of any bond
he may have executed , nor shall his surety be re-
lieved , nor shall his estate be effected in any way,
by resignation , discharge , change in official position ,
or death , until final settlement of all his accounts
has been made and a certficate of such settlement
issued by the Adjutant General. The Adjutant Gen-
eral shall , if possible , obtain a surety company bond
or bonds , running to the state of Kentucky , cover-
ing such officers as are designated to be bonded , and
the premium or premiums on said bond or bonds
shall be paid from the state military fund ; otherwise
such officer shall execute personal bond with good
and approved surety running to the state of Ken-
tucky. Should any officer fail to qualify by furnish-

476 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ing the prescribed bond within fifteen days after his
appointment , or to renew same within fifteen days
after the original bond shall expire, the office shall
be deemed vacant and shall be so declared to be
vacant .
§ 62. Every armory , drill hall , stable or parts
of buildings or grounds occupied or used by the
Kentucky National Guard for military purposes
shall , during the period of its occupancy for such
purposes , be under the control of the commanding
officer so using same , and any person who intrudes
or enters therein contrary to his orders , or who
molests or obstructs the troops , or any of them , so
occupying such premises , shall be deemed guilty of
a misdemeanor and shall , upon conviction , be pun-
ished by a fine of not exceeding one hundred dollars
( $100.00 ) ; and it shall be lawful for such officer to
arrest such person and turn him over to the civil
authorities for trial .
§ 63. All articles of military property furnished
for the use of the several organizations of the Ken-
tucky National Guard shall be kept in the armory
or storerooms of the command , except when in use
for authorized military purposes . No article of mil-
itary property shall be used for other than author-
ized military purposes .
$ 64. No officer or soldier shall wear , or use , or
have in his possession , except for authorized mili-
tary purposes , or by special permission of his com-
manding officer , any uniform or part thereof , or
any other article of military property belonging to
the state or the United States ; and any officer or
soldier violating any provision of this secion shall ,
upon conviction , be punished therefor by imprison-
ment in the county jail not exceeding thirty days ,
or by a fine not exceeding fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) , or
by both such fine and imprisonment .
774

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 4.77
§ 65. If any person shall receive , conceal , or aid
in concealing , or have or retain in his possession
with intent to convert to his own use or gain any
property , record , voucher or valuable thing what-
soever pertaining to the military establishment of
the state , and belonging to the state or the United
States , which has theretofore been embezzled , stolen
or purloined from the state by any other person ,
knowing the same to have been so embezzled , stolen
or purloined , such person shall be deemed guilty
of a felony , and on conviction shall be confined in
the penitentiary for not less than one nor more than
three years ; and such receiver may be tried either
before or after the conviction of the principal felony
but , if the party has been convicted , then the judg-
ment against him shall be conclusive in the prosecu-
tion against such receiver that the property of the
state or of the United States therein described has
been embezzled , solen or purloined .
§ 66. The clothes , arms , military outfits and ac-
coutrements furnished by the United States , through
the agency of the State , to any officer or soldier of
the Kentucky National Guard shall not be sold , bar-
tered , exchanged , pledged , loaned or given away ;
and no person , not a soldier or duly authorized of-
ficer of the State , who has possession of any such
clothes , arms , military outfits or accoutrements so
furnished , and which have been the subject of any
such barter , exchange , pledge , loan, or gift , shall
have any right , title or interest therein , but the same
may be seized and taken whenever found by any
officer of the State , civil or military , and shall there-
upon be delivered to the Adjutant General of the
State or other officer authorized to receive the same.
The possession of such clothes , arms , military out-
fits or accoutrements by any person not a soldier or

478 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
officer of the State shall be presumpive evidence of
such a sale , barter , exchange , pledge , loan or gift .
$ 67 . No officer or enlisted man shall be arrested
on civil process , except in case of a felony , while
going to , remaining at, or returning from a place
where he is ordered to attend for military service or
duty.
$ 68 . Officers and enlisted men of the Kentucky
National Guard while in active field service , going
to or returning from any parade , encampment , drill
or meeting which they may be required by law to
attend , shall together with their vehicular convey-
ances , animals and military property of the State
or the United States in their custody , be allowed to
pass free through toll gates and over all interstate
and intrastate toll bridges and ferries , if they be in
uniform or present a written order signed by an of-
ficer of the Kentucky National Guard .
$ 69 . Any person , master or keeper of a toll
gate , toll bridge or ferry, or any person in charge
thereof, who wilfully hinders or delays any member
of the National Guard or refuses free passage to
any such member going to or returning from any
parade , encampment , drill or meeting which he may
be by law required to attend , or wilfully hinders ,
delays or refuses free passage to any conveyance
or military property of the State in charge of a
member of said National Guard , is guilty of a mis-
demeanor , and shall be fined for each offense not
exceeding the sum of twenty -five dollars ($25.00 ) .
$ 70 . It shall be unlawful for any body of men.
whatsoever other than the National Guard of this
State and the troops of the United States to associ-
ate themselves together as a military company or
other armed organization , or to drill or parade with
arms anywhere within the State without the license
of the Governor therefor , which license of the Gov-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 479
ernor may at any time be revoked ; provided , that
nothing herein contained shall be construed to pre-
vent benevolent or social organizations from wear-
ing swords or students in educational institutions ,
chartered under the laws of the State , wherein mili-
tary science is part of the course of insruction , from
drilling and parading with arms in public , under the
superintendence of their instructors . Whoever
offends against the provisions of this section , or
belongs to or parades with any such unauthorized
body of men with arms , shall be punished by a fine
of not less than ten dollars ($10.00 ) nor more than
fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) , or imprisoned in the county
jail for a term not exceeding six months , or both .
§ 71. A person who , either by himself or with
another , wilfully deprives a member of the National
Guard of his employment , or prevents his being
employed by himself or another , or obstructs or
annoys said member of said National Guard , or his
employer , in respect of his trade, business or em-
ployment because said member of the National
Guard is such member , or dissuades any person
from enlistment in the said National Guard by
threat of injury to him in case he shall so enlist in
respect of his employment , trade or business , is
guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be punished by
a fine of not less than twenty-five dollars ( $25.00 )
nor more than one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) , or by
imprisonment in the county jail not less than ( 10 )
days , nor more than fifty ( 50 ) days , either or both ,
at the discretion of the court and jury .
$ 72. No association or corporation , constituted
or organized for the purpose of promoting the suc-
cess of the trade , employment or business of the
members thereof , shall by any constitution , rule ,
by-law, resolution , vote or regulation, discriminate
against any member of the National Guard of the

480 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
State of Kentucky because of such membership in
respect to the eligibility of such member of the
National Guard to membership in such association
or corporation , or in respect of his right to retain
said membership ; it being the purpose of this and
the preceding section to protect a member of the
said National Guard from disadvantage in his
means of livelihood and liberty , but not to give him
any preference or advantage on account of his mem-
bership in said National Guard . A person who aids
in enforcing any such provision against any member
of the said National Guard with the intent to dis-
criminate against him because of such membership
is guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be punished
as provided in the preceding section .
§ 73. The military courts of this State for the
National Guard , not in the service of the United
States , shall be :
(a ) Courts of inquiry .
(b ) General courts martial .
( c ) Special courts martial .
(d ) Summary courts martial .
They shall be constituted like and have cogniz-
ance of the same subjects and possess like power,
except as to punishments , as similar courts provided
for by the regulations and laws governing the Army
of the United States , and the proceedings of courts
martial of the National Guard shall follow the forms
and modes of procedure prescribed for said similar
courts in the Regular Army. The sentences of all
courts martial shall be carried into effect upon the
approval of the convening authority , which shall
have power to remit or mitigate any sentence .
$ 74 . Courts of inquiry shall consist of from one
to three officers of at least equal grade with the
officer , or with the senior officer if there be more
than one, in regard to whom the court is ordered ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 481
and may be ordered by the Governor or by his direc-
tion by the Adjutant General , to examine into the
nature of any transaction of or accusation or imputa-
tion against any officer or soldier , or of any hap-
pening , occurrence or condition which may affect the
National Guard or its efficiency or welfare . The
Court shall , without delay , report to the officer
ordering it the evidence adduced , a statement of
the facts and , when required, an opinion thereon .
Any officer may request a court of inquiry .
§ 75. General courts martial of the National
Guard may be convened by order of the Governor,
and such courts shall have the power to impose fines
not exceeding two hundred dollars ( $ 200.00 ) ; to sen-
tence to confinement not exceeding eight months ; to
sentence to forfeiture of pay and allowances ; to a
reprimand ; to dismissal or dishonorable discharge
from the service ; to reduction of non -commissioned
officers to the ranks ; or any two or more of such
punishments may be combined in the sentences im-
posed by such courts .
§ 76. The commanding officer of each garrison ,
fort, post , camp or other place , brigade , regiment ,
detached battalion or other detached command , may
appoint special courts martial for his command , but
such special courts martial may in any case be
appointed by superior authority when by the latter
deemed advisable . Special courts martial shall
have power to try any person subject to military
law, except a commissioned officer , for any crime or
offense made punishable by the military laws of the
State of Kentucky or the United States , and such
special courts martial shall have the same powers of
punishment as do general courts martial , except that
fines imposed by such courts shall not exceed one
hundred dollars ( $100.00 ) .
Acts- 16

482 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
$ 77 . The commanding officer of each garrison ,
fort , camp , post or other place , regiment or corps ,
detached battalion , company , troop , battery , or other
detachment of the National Guard , may appoint for
such place or command a summary court to consist
of one officer , who shall have power to administer
oaths and to try the enlisted men of such place or
command for breaches of discipline and violations
of military laws ; and said court may impose fines
not exceeding twenty-five ( $ 25.00 ) dollars for any
single offense ; may sentence non -commissioned
officers to reduction to the ranks ; may sentence to
forfeiture of pay and allowances . The proceedings
of such court shall be informal and the minutes
thereof shall be the same as prescribed for summary
courts of the Army of the United States .
$ 78. All courts martial of the National Guard ,
including summary courts , shall have the power to
sentence to confinement in lieu of fines authorized
to be imposed ; provided , that such sentences of con-
finement shall not exceed one day for each dollar of
fine authorized ; provided , further , that no sentence
of confinement imposed by a summary court shall
be carried into effect until the sentence shall have
been approved by an officer of field grade .
§ 79. An accused appearing before a general
court martial or summary court shall have the right
to be represented by counsel ; such counsel may be
a commissioned officer of the Kentucky National
Guard or any duly licensed lawyer in the State of
Kentucky .
§ 80. No sentence of dismissal from the service
or dishonorable discharge imposed by a National
Guard court martial shall be executed until ap-
proved by the Governor of the State of Kentucky .
$ 81 . Presidents of courts martial , courts of
inquiry and military boards , and summary court

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 483
officers shall have the power to issue warrants to
arrest , or cause to be arrested , accused persons and
to bring them before the court for trial whenever
such persons shall have disobeyed an order in writ-
ing from the convening authority to appear before
such court , a copy of the charge or charges having
been delivered to the accused with such order , and
to issue subpoenas duces tecum , and to enforce by
attachment attendance of witnesses , and the produc-
tion of books and papers . The refusal of a witness
to appear in answer to a subpoena , to be sworn or
to answer , shall be a contempt of said court and
shall be punishable by a fine of not to exceed thirty
dollars ( $ 30.00 ) , which may be imposed by such
court or board .
$ 82 . All processes and sentences of said courts.
shall be executed by sheriffs or other civil officers of
the State of Kentucky , and it shall be the duty of
such officers to execute all such processes and sen-
tences and make return thereof to the officer issuing
or imposing the same . Upon failure of any such
officer to execute such processes and sentences , they
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and subject to a
fine of not exceeding twenty-five dollars ($ 25.00 ) for
each offense .
§ 83. Any jail or prison of the State shall be
used as a place of imprisonment or detention of any
person convicted and sentenced to imprisonment
by a court martial or summary court, and every
jailer or wardenwarden shall , upon notification by
authority approving the sentence , receive and
confine each person so convicted , and shall carry
into execution the sentence of such court martial or
summary court as if they were the directions and
orders of properly constituted civil authorities ,
ordering the confinement of persons for criminal
offense . The cost of imprisonment or detention

484 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
shall be a proper charge against the county in which
the prisoner is confined , and shall be paid for at the
same rate as those prescribed for the detention of
other prisoners in the county jail . The authority
which has designated the place of confinement may
change the place of confinement of any prisoner
under the jurisdiction of such authority .
§ 84. The Act of the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky adopted at its 1924 session , creating the
Armory Commission of Kentucky and providing
armories for the Kentucky National Guard , and
which was approved March 15 , 1924 , and which is
now in effect , shall continue in effect and none of
its provisions are abridged or abrogated by this law
or any of its sections .
$ 85 . The Adjutant General shall for his services
receive an annual salary of three thousand dollars
($3,000.00 ) out of the State treasury , payable in
equal monthly installments .
§ 86. The Assistant Adjutant General shall for
his services receive an annual salary of one thou-
sand , eight hundred dollars ( $ 1,800.00 ) out of the
State treasury , payable in equal monthly install-
ments .
§ 87. The compensation of other officers and
employees in the Military Department shall be fixed
by the Adjutant General with the approval of the
Governor .
$ 88. Before entering upon the duties of his
office the Adjutant General shall be required to
execute bond running to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the sum of ten thousand dollars
( $10,000.00 ) , conditioned upon the faithful perform-
ance of his duties as Adjutant General .
$ 89. Before entering upon the duties of his
office the Assistant Adjutant General shall be
required to execute bond running to the Common: -

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 485
wealth of Kentucky in the sum of five thousand dol-
lars ( $5,000.00 ) , conditioned upon the faithful
performance of his duties as Assistant Adjutant
General.
$ 90. The Adjutant General shall have the power
to require such officers and employees in the Mili-
tary Department to execute bonds running to the
Commonwealth of Kentucky in such sums as he may
think reasonable to properly safeguard the interests
of the Commonwealth , considering the responsibili-
ties and the duties which such officers or employees
are required to perform .
§ 91. When said bonds given by the Adjutant
General, Assistant Adjutant General and other offi-
cers and employees in the Military Department are
surety company bonds , then the cost of the
premiums on said bonds shall be a proper charge
against the Military Fund , and said premiums shall
be paid out of said funds by vouchers approved by
the Adjutant General .
§ 92. Officers of the Kentucky National Guard
when ordered on active duty by the Governor shall
have the right to carry arms , and in the same man-
ner , as sheriffs and other peace officers of the Com-
monwealth are now permitted and authorized by
law to do .
$ 93 . Organizations , officers and enlisted men of
the Kentucky National Guard who are or may be
hereafter Federally recognized and authorized to
receive Federal pay , arms and equipment shall be in
Active Service within the meaning of this Act .
Troops of the Kentucky National Guard ordered
into service as prescribed in this Act for the en-
forcement of the law and for the preservation of the
peace and security of the rights and lives or prop-
erty of citizens shall be deemed to be in Active Field
Service . Officers employed under orders of the Gov-

486 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
ernor in making tours of inspection , mustering in
or mustering out troops making surveys of military
property , sitting on courts martial , summary courts ,
efficiency boards , courts of inquiry or boards of
officers , shall be deemed to be in Active Field Serv-
ice . Orders in every case shall state if pay and
travel are allowed and the expenses allowed .
§ 94. A person who excludes from the equal en-
joyment of any accommodation , facility or privilege
furnished by innkeepers or common carriers , or by
owners , managers or lessees of theaters or other
places of amusement or resort , any person lawfully
wearing the uniform of the Army , Navy , or Marine
Corps of the United States because of that uniform ,
is guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be punished by
a fine of not less than ten dollars ($10.00 ) nor more
than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) , or by confinement of not
less than ten days nor more than sixty days , or both
in the discretion of the court or jury .
95. That no red or black flag nor any banner ,
ensign or sign having upon it any inscription op-
posed to organized government , or which is sacri-
ligious , or which may be derogatory to public
morals, shall be carried in parade within this State
or displayed upon any street or building therein ;
and it shall be unlawful to display the flag of any
anarchistic society upon any public or private build-
ing or to carry or display such flag in any street pro-
cession or parade within this State . Nothing in this
Act shall apply to any pennant of any university ,
college or other recognized institution of learning .
§ 96. Whoever levies war against this State or
the United States or knowingly adheres to the
enemies of either , giving them aid and comfort , is
guilty of treason against the State of Kentucky and
shall be imprisoned in the penitentiary for life .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 487
$ 97 . Whenever the National Guard of this State
shall be called into active service by the Governor
for any purpose, requiring the service of members
thereof who may be students in any institution of
learning within the State , no teacher , professor ,
principal or president of any such institution shall
discriminate against any such student by reason of
such absence , but shall credit him with all academic
work accomplished to the date of such absence .
§ 98. The Adjutant General or such officers as
the Governor may designate shall inspect at least
once in each year , or oftener if the Governor deems
it necessary , all headquarters , arsenals , storehouses ,
armories , organizations and all military property
belonging to the State and military property that
has been issued to the State by the United States ,
and such inspector or inspectors , shall render a de-
tailed report to the Adjutant General of the condi-
tion of same .
$ 99. Officers and enlisted men of the National
Guard ordered into active service of the State by
proper authority shall not be liable civilly , or
criminally , for any act , or acts , done by them in pur-
suance of duty in such service .
§ 100. Each regiment and separate battalion may
have a council of administration , composed of its
officers , which shall be charged with the care and
control of the fiscal affairs of the organization , may
adopt suitable by-laws , effective on the approval of
the Governor , and may elect not more than twenty-
five honorary members for each company , battery,
troop or equivalent unit ; said honorary members to
be entitled to exemption from jury service , upon
their payment annually , or oftener as the council
shall so determine , of a sum not less than fifty dol-
lars ( $ 50.00 ) ; and the money thus obtained shall be
for the use of the regimental or battalion fund , to be

488 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
expended by the commanding officer in such manner
as , in his judgment , the interests of the organization
require . Each unit not under the control of a regi-
mental or battalion council of administration may
also have a council of administration , composed of
its officers , and it shall have the same powers as pre-
scribed herein for a regiment and separate battalion .
§ 101. All acts or parts of acts in conflict here-
with are to the extent of such conflicts hereby re-
pealed .
§ 102. If any section , subsection , sentence , clause
or phrase of this Act shall for any reason be
adjudged by any court of competent jurisdiction to
be invalid , such judgment shall not affect , impair
or invalidate the remainder of this Act , as it is the
intention of the General Assembly in the enactment
of this Act to enact each and every section sep-
arately and independently of each other .
$ 103 . Whereas it is important for the better-
ment of the Military Department of the State for
this Act to take effect immediately , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist and this Act shall be effec-
tive upon its passage and approval by the Goveronr .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 97 .
AN ACT to amend section 2103 , sub -section 2, of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes so as to authorize judges of the city or police
court to solemnize a marriage ceremony .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That so much of Section 2103 , sub - section 2 , of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes as relates to persons
authorized to solemnize a marriage be and the same
is hereby amended by adding after the words ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 489
" Judge of the County Court " the following words ,
to -wit :
Judges of the City or Police Court , so that so
much of said Section 2103 , sub -section 2 , as relates
to persons authorized to solemnize marriages which
reads amended as follows , to -wit :
1. Ministers of the gospel or priests of any de-
nomination in regular communication with any re-
ligious society .
2. Judges of the County Court , Judges of the
City or Police Court , and such Justices of the Peace
as the Governor of the State or the County Court
may authorize .
3. Or, where either party belongs to a religious
society having no officiating priest or minister ,
whose usage is to solemnize marriage at the usual
place of worship , and by consent given in the pres-
ence of the society , it may be so solemnized .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 98 .
AN ACT relating to the safe development and operation of
minerals ; providing regulations for the location of oil and gas
wells and the operation and abandonment of same through
workable beds of coal ; regulating coal mining operations
within the vicinity of such wells ; defining the respective duties
of the owners and operators for said minerals and the State
Department of Mines ; prescribing full procedure for carrying
into effect all the provisions and providing penalties for any
violation of this Act ; and , repealing all laws or parts of laws
in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the term " well " when used in this Act
means a bore hole drilled or proposed to be drilled
for the purpose of producing natural gas or petro-
leum , or through which natural gas or petroleum are

490 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
being produced ; the term " well operator " shall
include any person or persons , firm , partnership ,
partnership association or corporation that pro-
poses to or does locate , drill , operate or abandon
any well as herein defined ; the term " coal operator "
shall include any person or persons , firm , partner-
ship or partnership association or corporation that
proposes to or does operate a coal mine ; the term
"department " or " Department of Mines " includes
the duly constituted authorities under the laws of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky having jurisdiction
over coal mining operations ; the term " plat " means
a map , drawing or print showing the location of a
well or wells as herein defined ; the term " casing "
means a string or strings of pipe commonly placed
in wells drilled for natural gas and petroleum ; the
terms " oil " and " gas " when used in this act , are
synonyms for petroleum and natural gas respec-
tively ; the term " cement " means hydraulic cement
properly mixed with water only ; the term " work-
able bed " means a coal bed actually being operated
commercially or which in the exercise of reasonable
judgment the Department of Mines decides can com-
mercially and that it is reasonably to be expected
will within a reasonable period of time not exceed-
ing ten ( 10 ) years be so operated or any coal bed
which from outcrop indication or other definite evi-
dence proves , to the satisfaction of the Department
of Mines , to be workable and which , when operated ,
will require protection if wells are drilled through it .
§ 2. Before drilling a well on any tract known to
be underlaid with coal bearing strata the well opera-
tor shall have a plat prepared showing the exact
location of the well and shall forward by registered
mail a copy of this plat to the Department and at
the same time copies to the record owner of such
coal bearing strata unless same be under operation ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 491
in which event to each coal operator operating said
beds of coal beneath said tract of land .
If no objections are made to the proposed location
within five ( 5 ) days from receipt of the plat by the
Department and the coal operator , the well operator
may proceed to drill at the location indicated , and
the plat shall be filed and become a permanent
record , subject to inspection at any time by an inter-
ested person. Any agent of the coal operator or a
superintendent in actual charge of the mines may
be considered as a coal operator , and any process
agent or other agent or person in charge of the coal
for him may be considered the owner for the pur-
poses of mailing the required copy of plat .
§ 3. In case the drilling of a well at any such
location will endanger the use , of present or future
operation, of a workable coal bed , the owner or coal
operator or operators affected may , within five ( 5 )
days from the receipt of said plat by him or them
and the Department of Mines , file specific objections
in writing to such proposed location with the De-
partment , and if such objection is so filed , the
Department shall notify the well operator of the
character of the objections and shall fix a time and
place for a hearing , not more than ten ( 10 ) days
from the end of the said five ( 5 ) days period , at
which such objections shall be considered .
At the hearing , the well operators and the coal
operators or owners , or such of them as are present
or represented , shall proceed to consider the objec-
tions and to agree upon either the location as made
or may so change same as to satisfy all objections
and meet the approval of the Department and any
new location thus selected and agreed upon shall be
indicated on a plat in accordance with Section 2 ,
which shall be filed and become a permanent record ,
whereupon the Department shall issue to the well

492 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
operator a drilling permit approving the new loca-
tion and authorize the well operator to drill at´said
location . In case the coal operator and well operator ,
or , the owner and well operator are unable to agree ,
then the Department shall , in view of the purposes
and intent of this Act and in compliance therewith ,
fix a location on said tract as near to the original
location as possible and shall issue to the well
operator a permit to drill at the new location , if the
well operator so desires .
If no such objections are filed within said period
of five days the Department shall forthwith issue to
the well operator a drilling permit approving the
location and authorizing the well operator to pro-
Iceed to drill at said location .
It shall be the duty of the Department to keep a
proper index of such plats and locations and
accurate records of the proceedings in case of any
conference or dispute . In any case , however , where
the well operator is working under any agreement
with or from the owner , or with or from the coal
operator when under operation of the workable coal
beds under such tract , which permits the well opera-
tor to select his own locations , the location as orig-
inally chosen above by the well operators shall be-
come the authorized location and the Department
shall issue the proper permit for the drilling of the
well , but the well operator must comply with all
other requirements of this Act , including those cov-
ering the drilling of wells through coal beds and the
plugging of wells which may be abandoned .
§ 4. A well penetrating one or more workable
coal beds shall be drilled in such manner as will , if
practically possible , exclude all oil , gas or gas
pressure from the coal bed , except such as may be
found in the coal bed itself . Each string of casing
run through a workable bed of coal shall be seated at

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 493
least thirty ( 30 ) feet below said coal bed in twenty
( 20 ) feet of cement , mud , clay or such other non-
porous material as will make an effective seal . In
case a second workable coal bed is found less than
thirty ( 30 ) feet below the first workable coal bed
referred to above , the casing shall be seated and
mudded off as above at least thirty ( 30 ) feet below
the second bed and in case gas is found between
these two beds , it shall be treated as set forth in
Section 5. After any such string of casing has been
properly so seated , drilling may proceed forthwith .
5. In the event that gas is found beneath a
workable coal bed , before the hole has been reduced
from the size it had at the coal bed , a packer shall
be placed below the coal bed, and above the gas
horizon , and the gas by this means diverted to the
inside of the adjacent string of casing through per-
forations made in said string of casing and through
it passed to the surface without contact with the
coal bed . Should gas be found between two work-
able beds of coal , in a hole of the same diameter
from bed to bed , two packers shall be placed with
perforations in the casing between them , permitting
the gas to pass inside the adjacent casing to the sur-
face . In either of the cases herein specified , the
strings of casing shall extend from their seats to
the top of the well .
6. In the event that a well becomes productive
of natural gas or petroleum , all coal protecting
strings of casing shall remain in place during the
life of the well , and the top ends of all such strings
shall be provided with casing heads , or such other
suitable devices as will permit the free passage of
gas and prevent filling the annular spaces outside
such casing with dirt or debris .
§ 7. When a well is drilled through the horizon
of a coal bed where the coal has been removed , the

494 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
hole shall be drilled at least thirty ( 30 ) feet below
the coal bed, of a size sufficient to permit the placing
of a liner which shall start not less than twenty ( 20 )
feet beneath the horizon of the coal bed and extend
not less than twenty feet above it . Within this liner ,
which may be welded to the casing to be used , shall
be centrally placed the largest sized casing to be
used in the well , and the space between the liner and
the casing shall be filled with cement as they are
lowered into the hole . Cement shall be placed in
the bottom of the hole to a depth of twenty ( 20 ) feet
to form a sealed seat for both liner and casing . Fol-
lowing the setting of the liner , drillings shall pro-
ceed in the manner provided above . Should it be
found necessary to drill through the horizon of two
or more workable coal beds where the coal has been
removed , the said liner shall be started not less than
twenty feet below the lowest such horizon pene-
trated and shall extend to a point not less than
twenty ( 20 ) feet above the highest such horizon .
8. Prior to the abandonment of any well , the
well operator shall notify , by registered mail , the
coal operator or operators or owners to whom
notices are required to be given by section 2 of this
Act , and the Department of Mines , of the intention
to plug and abandon any such well ( using such form
of notice as the Department may provide ) , giving
the number of the well and its location and fixing the
time at which the work of plugging and filling will
be commenced , which time shall not be less than
five ( 5 ) days after the day on which such notice so
mailed is received or in due course should be re-
ceived by the Department of Mines in order that
a representative or representatives of the coal
operator or owner and of the department or any or
all , may be present at the plugging and filling of
the well . Whether such representatives appear or

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 495
do not appear , the well operator may proceed at the
time fixed to plug and fill the well in the manner
hereinafter described . When said plugging and fill-
ing have been completed , an affidavit , in triplicate ,
shall be made ( on a form to be furnished by the
Department ) by two experienced men who partici-
pated in the work , in which affidavit shall be set
forth the time and manner in which the well was
plugged ; one copy of this affidavit shall be retained
by the well operator , another ( or true copies of
same ) shall be mailed to the coal operator or opera-
tors or owners , and the third to the Department of
Mines .
§ 9. Upon the abandonment of any well drilled
through a workable coal bed , the well operator , at
the time of such abandonment shall plug the well to
a point forty ( 40 ) feet below the lowest workable
coal bed in the following manner : The hole shall
be filled with either mud , clay or non -porous mate-
rial from the bottom to a point twenty ( 20 ) feet
above the top of the lowest oil , gas or water bearing
strata ; or a permanent bridge shall be anchored
thirty ( 30 ) feet below its lowest oil , gas or water
bearing strata , and from said bridge it shall be filled
with mud , clay or other non - porous material to a
point twenty ( 20 ) feet above said strata ; at this
point there shall be placed a plug of cement or other
suitable material which will completely seal the
hole . Between this sealing plug and a point twenty
( 20 ) feet above the next higher oil , gas or water
bearing strata , the hole shall be treated in a like
manner and at said point there shall be placed an-
other suitable plug , which will completely seal the
hole . In a like manner the hole shall be filled and
plugged , or bridged , filled and plugged with refer-
ence to each of its oil , gas or water bearing strata .
Whenever such strata are not widely separated and

496 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
are free from water , they may be grouped and
treated as a single productive strata . After plug-
ging all strata as aforesaid , a final plug shall be
anchored approximately ten ( 10 ) feet below the
bottom of the largest casing in the well and from
this point to the surface the well shall be filled with
mud , clay or other non - pourous material . In case
any of the strata in the well shall have been shot ,
creating cavities which cannot readily be filled in
the manner above described , the well operator shall
follow either of the following methods : ( a ) Should
the stratum which has been shot be the lowest one
in the well , there shall be placed , at the nearest suit-
able point but not less than twenty ( 20 ) feet above
the stratum, a plug of cement or other suitable mate-
rial which will completely seal the hole . In the
event , however , that the shooting has been done
above one or more oil or gas bearing strata in the
well , plugging in the manner specified shall be done
at the nearest suitable point , but not less than
twenty feet below and above the stratum shot .
(b) When such cavity shall be in the lowest oil
or gas bearing stratum in the well , a liner shall be
placed which shall extend from below the stratum
to a suitable point , but not less than twenty ( 20 )
feet above the stratum in which the shooting has
been done . In the event , however , that the shooting
has been done above one or more oil or gas bearing
strata in the well , the liner shall be so placed that
it will extend not less than twenty ( 20 ) feet above ,
nor less than twenty feet below the stratum in which
shooting has been done . Following the placing of
the liner in the manner here specified . it shall be
compactly filled with cement , clay, or other non -por-
ous sealing material .
When a well has been filled and securely pugged
in the manner aforesaid to a point forty ( 40 ) feet

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 497
below the lowest workable coal bed , and if in the
judgment of the well operator , the coal operator and
the Department of Mines , a permanent outlet to the
surface is required , such outlet shall be provided in
the following manner : A plug of cement or other
suitable material shall be placed in the well at a
suitable point , not less than ten ( 10 ) feet below the
lowest workable coal bed . In this plug and passing
through the center of it shall be securely fastened
an open pipe , not less than two inches in diameter ,
which shall extend to the surface . At or above the
surface the pipe shall be provided with a device
which will permit the free passage of gas and pre-
vent obstructing of same . Following the setting of
the aforesaid plug and pipe , the hole shall be filled
with cement to a point ten ( 10 ) feet above the lowest
workable coal bed . In case there are additional
overlying workable coal beds , they shall be treated
similarly , if in the judgment of the well operator ,
the coal operator and the Department of Mines such
treatment is necessary and in event the parties can-
not agree , the decision of the Department of Mines
shall control . If no outlet to the surface is consid-
ered necessary by them from any workable coal bed ,
the plugging , filling and cementing shall be as first
above described .
10. Before hereafter removing any coal or
other material or driving any entry or passageway
within five hundred ( 500 ) feet of any well , and also
before hereafter extending the workings in any coal
mine beneath any tract of land on which wells are
also drilled or within five hundred (500 ) feet of any
well or under any tract of land in visible possession
of a well operator or operators , any coal operator
or operators shall forward , by registered mail , a
copy of the parts of its maps and plans which it is
required by law to prepare and file and bring to date

498 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
from time to time , showing its mine workings and
projected mine workings beneath such tract of land
and within five hundred feet of the outer boundaries
thereof , simultaneously , to the well operator and
the Department , and following the filing of such
parts of its said plans or maps as aforesaid , the coal
operator may proceed with its mining operations in
the manner and as projected on said plans or maps ,
but in case the conduct of its mining operations
nearer than five hundred ( 500 ) feet to any com-
pleted well or well that is being drilled , or for the
purpose of drilling which a derrick is being con-
structed , will endanger the use or drilling of such
well , the well owner or operator affected may within.
five days ( 5 ) days from receipt of said plat by him
or them and the Department of Mines , file specific
objections in writing to the mining operations within
less than five hundred ( 500 ) feet of such well , and if
such objection is so filed , the Department shall
notify the coal operator of the character of the
objections and shall fix a time and place for a hear-
ing not more than ten ( 10 ) days from the end of
said five ( 5 ) days period , at which such objections
shall be considered . At the hearing , the coal opera-
tors or owners and well operators or owners , or such
of them as are present or represented , shall proceed
to consider the objections and to agree upon the
character and extent of operations to be conducted
within less than five hundred ( 500 ) feet of such well
so as to satisfy the objections raised and meet
the approval of the Department , and in case the
coal operator or owner and well operator or owner
affected are unable to agree , then the Department ,
after a full hearing , shall make a decision defining
what coal , if any , is necessary to be left for the safe
protection of the use and operation of such well .
The decision shall be subject to appeal by either

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 499
party as in this act provided . A complete record
of all such hearings shall be kept by the Depart-
ment .
The coal operator shall, at least every six ( 6 )
months , bring such plans or maps , required by this
Section , to date , or file new plans and new maps
complete to date .
§ 11. The Department shall exercise supervi-
sion over the drilling, casing , plugging and filling of
all wells drilled through workable coal beds and of
all mining operations in close proximity to any well
and shall have such access to plans , maps and other
records and to the properties of the operators as
may be necessary or proper for this purpose . The
Department shall receive or file formal complaints ,
charging that drilling or mining operations are
being conducted contrary to the provisions of this
Act, or to the order of the Department, and shall
require formal hearings on such complaints . Imme-
diately following such hearing , the Department shall
make its findings of fact and enter such order as in
its judgment is just and right and necessary to
secure the proper administration of this Act .
§ 12. The Department may prescribe rules of
procedure and for offering evidence in all matters
brought before it and shall prepare and , on request ,
furnish to applicants copies of forms of notices and
other forms that the Department may require to be
used and prescribe the manner of serving the same .
Any matter in controversy before the Department
shall , after hearing or hearings , of which all per-
sons interested have had due notice and at which
they have been given an opportunity to appear and
be heard and to offer evidence and to make argu-
ment by counsel if desired , be decided by the De-
partment as may seem to it to be just and reason-

500 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
able and necessary or desirable for the proper en-
forcement of the provisions of this Act .
§ 13. (a ) Any party in interest being dissatis-
fied with an order of the Department may commence
an action in the circuit court of the county wherein .
the subject -matter involved in the order , or the well,
or workable coal bed , or part thereof, are located or
may lie , or in any other court of competent jurisdic-
tion against the Department and other interested
parties as defendants , to vacate or set aside , in
whole or in part , any such order .
Said action shall be in equity and proceeded with
and tried as an equitable action and the courts shall
have power to grant relief from any unlawful or
unreasonable order of the Department, and shall
render such judgment as to it may seem equitable
and just .
From final orders and judgments in any such pro-
ceeding , an appeal shall lie to the Court of Appeals ,
to be taken in the same manner as appeals are pros-
ecuted in equity causes .
(b) The pendency of such proceedings shall not
of itself stay or suspend operation of the order of
the Department, but during the pendency of such
proceedings the court in its discretion may stay or
suspend , in whole or in part , the operation of the
order of the Department on such terms as it deems
just and in accordance with practice of courts exer-
cising equity jurisdiction .
Any party shall have the right to secure from the
court in which the action is commenced or pending
an order suspending or staying the operation of an
order of the Department pending said action by
adequately securing the other parties against loss
due to the delay in enforcement of the order , in case
the order under review is not set aside , the security

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 501
to take such form as shall be directed by the court
granting the stay or suspension .
§ 14. Any person or persons , co -partnership ,
partnership , association or corporation willfully vio-
lating the provisions of this Act which prescribes
the manner of drilling and casing or plugging and
filling any well , or which fixes the distance from
wells within which mining operations shall not be
conducted without the approval of the Department ,
or violating the terms of any order of the Depart-
ment allowing mining operations within a lesser dis-
tance of any well than that prescribed by the Act ,
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and on conviction
be punished by a fine not exceeding two hundred
dollars , or imprisonment in jail for not exceeding
twelve months or both , in the discretion of the court,
and prosecutions under this section may be brought
in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in
the circuit court of the county in which the violation
of such provisions of the Act or terms of such order
was committed .
§ 15. The invalidity of any portion of this Act
shall not affect the validity of any other portion.
thereof which can be given effect without such in-
valid part.
§ 16. All acts and parts of acts inconsistent with
this act are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 99 .
AN ACT to further regulate motor vehicles for the carriage of
passengers for hire , not operating under certificates of public
convenience and necessity , and to increase the powers of the
State Tax Commission in respect thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

502 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 1. When used in this act the term " operator "
shall mean any corporation , association , company ,
receiver , assignee or trustee thereof, and any indi-
vidual or co -partnership engaged in the transpor-
tation of persons for hire on any public highway by
motor vehicle not operating under a certificate of
public convenience and necessity issued by the State
Tax Commission or other regulatory body of the
state ; the term " Commission " shall mean the State
Tax Commission ; the term " public highway " shall
mean every public road , street or highway in this
state within or without the corporate limits of any
municipality .
,
§ 2. No operator shall operate on any public
highway without having paid to the Commission the
annual registration fees provided by law for each
motor vehicle operated by him and without having
procured from the Commission a permit for such
operation . Before the Commission shall issue a
permit the operator shall file with the Commission
an application , in such form as the Commission may
require , which shall set forth, among other matters ,
the name and residence of the operator , the name of
the chief officer if the operator be a corporation , and
the names of the members if the operator be a part-
nership , the number and description of the vehicles
to be used by such operator, the place where the
operator proposes to operate , and a list of all
drivers employed or to be employed . If any changes
are made in the drivers or any additional drivers
are employed the operator shall, within thirty days
after the employment of any new or additional
driver or drivers , furnish a list thereof to the Com-
mission . Permit shall be renewed at such times and
in such manner and upon such conditions as the
Commission may prescribe.

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 503
3. Before any permit is granted by the Com-
mission , the operator shall file with the Commission
a good and sufficient bond with adequate corporate
surety , payable to the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
which shall bind the obligor therein to pay any final
judgment rendered against him arising out of the
death of or injury to any passenger or passengers ,
for loss or damage to property while in transit , or
death of or injury to other persons or damage to
their property , or any act or omission connected with
the operation of motor vehicles by the operator ,
which bond shall be in the penal sum of Five Thou-
sand Dollars ( $5,000.00 ) for each motor vehicle
operated , for death of or injury to persons , and in
the further sum of One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1 ,-
000.00 ) for each vehicle operated , for damage to
property , resulting from the operation of any such
motor vehicle .
Instead of such bond the Commission may accept
and file a policy of insurance issued by any insur-
ance company or carrier authorized to do business
in this State , or other contract in writing deemed
by the Commission to be adequate , by which such
insurance company or insurance carrier shall as-
sume the liability prescribed by this section .
The Commission may exempt , in whole or in part ,
from the requirements of this section any operator
upon application for such exemption and upon show-
ing to the Commission to its satisfaction that by
reason of the financial ability of the operator there
exists due assurance of the payment of any and all
damages for which the operator may be liable as a
result of the operation of the vehicles owned by the
operator . The exemption herein provided shall be
made only by written order of the Commission ,
which may from time to time require statements of
the financial condition of such operator and may

504 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
upon ten days notice in writing , for cause , revoke its
order granting such exemption , in which event said
operator shall immediately comply with the other
requirements of this section .
No insurance company or insurance carrier issu-
ing any policy filed with the Commission and no
surety or other obligor on any bond or contract filed
with the Commission shall be relieved from liability
under said policy , bond or contract until after the
expiration of fifteen days notice to the Commission
of an intention to cancel said insurance policy , bond
or other contract , and the acceptance of such notice
and the cancellation of such policy , bond or other
contract by the Commission shall not relieve the in-
surance company or insurance carrier or the surety
on such bond or other contract of any liability which
shall have accrued prior to the effective date of such
cancellation . The Commission shall have the right to
require all bonds or insurance policies to expire and
be renewed upon a given day .
If any operator shall violate anv provision of this
act the Commission shall give notice to such opera-
tor and if after hearing the Commission finds that
such operator has violated any of the provisions of
this act , it shall cancel the permit issued to such
operator , and after cancellation no operator shall
have the right to ask for a renewal of its permit for
a period of six months . Any operator whose license
is cancelled by the Commission shall have the right
of appeal to the Franklin Circuit Court , where said
case and the hearing thereon shall be tried de novo .
4. No operator shall employ as a driver of any
motor vehicle , and no person shall drive any such
motor vehicle , until each driver shall have been
granted a license by the Commission upon applica-
tion in such form as the Commission may prescribe,
which shall set forth , among other things , the name

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 505
of the applicant , his age , residence , experience in
the operation of motor vehicles , the date and num-
ber of his registration certificate as a chauffeur
under Section 2739g - 15 , 2739g -16 , 2739g - 18 and
2739g -19 , Kentucky Statutes , and be accompanied
by a certificate of medical examination by a reputa-
ble physician as to eyesight , hearing, freedom from
physical defects or malformations , and condition of
health and habits . The Commission may , in its dis-
cretion , require the applicant to take a practical test
in the operation of motor vehicles and an examina-
tion in his knowledge of the motor vehicle laws , and
may refuse to issue a license to any person who is
not qualified to operate a motor vehicle for hire , and
shall refuse to issue a license to any person under
twenty-one years of age. The applicant shall pay to
the Commission an annual fee of $2.00 , which shall
accompany his application , and which shall be in
addition to the fee required to be paid by chauffeurs
by Section 2739g - 16 , Kentucky Statutes . The Com-
mission shall issue special licenses and badges to
such persons as meet its reqquirements . All such
licenses shall be subject to revocation by the Com-
mission , after hearing, or opportunity for hearing ,
after ten days notice in writing to the address stated
in the application . The violation of any law of this
State , or of any ordinance of a city or town , shall
constitute cause for a revocation of such license .
All licenses shall be for the calendar year in which
they are issued , and shall be renewed annually upon
the payment of a fee of Two Dollars ( $2.00 ) , under
such rules and regulations as the Commission may
prescribe .
§ 5. All permits and licenses heretofore issued
by the Commissioner of Motor Vehicles , and in
force on the effective date of this act, in respect of
the operation of motor vehicles under the provisions

506 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of Chapter 78 of 1930 , or in respect of drivers '
licenses under the provisions of Section 2739j -32 ,
Kentucky Statutes , and all bonds , policies of insur-
ance or contracts accepted by and filed with said
Commissioner , shall continue in full force and effect
in accordance with their terms , unless sooner
revoked as in this act provided .
§ 6. The violation of any provision of this act
shall constitute a misdemeanor , punishable by a fine
of not less than Ten Dollars ($ 10.00 ) nor more than
Two Hundred Dollars ( $ 200.00 ) and costs of prose-
cution. Prosecutions under this act may be by
summary arrest , warrant or information , and
quarterly courts , and the judges thereof, justices of
the peace and police or city judges shall have con-
current jurisdiction with circuit courts of the trial
of all offenses under this act committed within their
respective jurisdictions .
§ 7. No operator of a motor vehicle for the car-
riage of passengers for hire , not holding a certifi-
cate of public convenience and necessity issued by
the Commission or other regulatory body of the
State and operating between fixed termini or over
regular routes , shall operate in competition with the
holder of a certificate as aforesaid over the route ,
or any part thereof , covered by such certificate , ex-
cept for casual trips thereon or thereover , and on
such casual trips the operator , or the driver of such
motor vehicle , shall not pick up any passengers
along the route and shall not on the return trip
carry any passengers other than those carried on
the original trip . The violation of the provisions of
this section shall constitute a violation of this act ,
punishable as in Section 6 hereof provided .
§ 8. After the payment of all expenses incident
to the enforcement of this act , all funds collected
under its provisions shall be credited to the fund for

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 507
the construction and maintenance of the State
system of highways .
§ 9. All laws or parts of laws in conflict or incon-
sistent with the provisions of this act are hereby
repealed . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 100 .
AN ACT relating to motor vehicles and to amend and re -enact
Section thirty -six of Chapter one hundred twelve of the Acts
of Nineteen hundred twenty-six ( now Section two thousand
seven hundred and thirty -nine J36 , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , nineteen hundred thirty Edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the

§ 1. That Section thirty-six of an Act approved

six Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , nineteen hundred
