CHAPTER 101 .
AN ACT to provide for the taxation of motor vehicles for hire
and primarily for the transportation of passengers and provid
ing for the disposition of the taxes so collected .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

508 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 1. For each motor vehicle for hire , used prima-
rily for the transportation of persons , operating
between fixed termini and over a regular route under
a certificate of convenience and necessity , with a
seating capacity of seven passengers or less , $3.00
for each passenger seat and 50c for each one hun-
dred pounds gross weight of each motor vehicle and
$10.00 for the bus tags issued for each bus by the
Commission , as provided by law.
On each such motor vehicle having a passenger
seating capacity of more than seven or less than
seventeen , $7.50 for each passenger seat and 50c for
each one hundred pounds gross weight of said motor
vehicle and $10.00 for the tags issued for each bus ,
by the Commission , as provided by law .
On each such motor vehicle having a passenger
seating capacity of seventeen and less than twenty-
six , $ 10.00 for each passenger seat and 50c for each
one hundred pounds gross weight of said motor
vehicle and $ 25.00 for the tags issued for each bus ,
by the Commission , as provided by law .
On each motor vehicle having a passenger seat-
ing capacity of twenty-six and less than thirty ,
$12.50 for each passenger seat and 50c for each one
hundred pounds gross weight and $50.00 for the tags
issued for each bus by the Commission , as provided
by law .
On each such motor vehicle having a passenger
seating capacity of thirty or more , $ 15.00 for each
passenger seat and 50c for each one hundred pounds
gross weight of said motor vehicle and $50.00 for the
tags issued for each bus by the Commission , as pro-
vided by law .
§ 2. If any such motor carrier has paid for the
calendar year 1932 , a tax on the passenger seating
capacity of a bus in excess of the tax herein imposed ,
then such carrier shall receive credit for the propor-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 509
tionate part of such excess for the remainder of the
calendar year after this act becomes effective upon
any taxes which it is required to pay under this act .
§ 3. All taxes collected under the provisions of
this bill shall, after the payment of the expenses of
the administration of this act , become a part of the
road fund of the Commonwealth .
§ 4. All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 102 .
AN ACT to make it compulsory for the owners of all trucks
used for the purpose of hauling live stock , in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , to have the name of the owner or owners
of said trucks painted on both sides and on the rear of said
trucks , in letters five inches high and providing a penalty for
the violation thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the owners of all trucks used for the
purpose of hauling live stock , in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , be required to have the name of the
owner or owners of said trucks painted on both sides
and on the rear thereof, in letters not less than five
inches high .
§ 2. Any person , firm or corporation violating
the provisions of this Act , shall be fined not less
than five dollars nor more than fifty dollars and each
day that any such truck is operated in violation of
the provisions of this act shall be deemed a separate
offense and punishable as such .
3. This act shall take effect according to the
provisions of the Constitution of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

510 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 103 ,
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Chapter 76 of the Acts of one
thousand nine hundred and thirty entitled " An Act regulating
the transportation by motor vehicles of all officers and em-
ployees of the State of Kentucky , excepting the Governor and
Lieutenant Governor and providing for the payment of the
traveling expenses by motor vehicles of all officers and em-
ployees of the State of Kentucky , and providing for the future
use and sale of all motor passenger vehicles now owned by
the State of Kentucky and repealing all laws and parts of
laws provided for the purchase of motor passenger vehicles
for use of State officers and employees , excepting the Governor
and Lieutenant Governor , providing a penalty for a violation
thereof and declaring an emergency to exist ," and providing
for the purchase and operation of motor passenger vehicles
for the use of the State Highway Commission of Kentucky and
the employees thereof , and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 76 of the Acts of one thousand
nine hundred and thirty of the General Assembly of
the State of Kentucky be , and the same is hereby
repealed and re -enacted so that as when re - enacted
it will read as follows : " That no part of any money
or revenue hereinafter paid into the State Treasury
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , shall be used or
expended for the purchase of any motor passenger
vehicle for the use of any officer of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky or any member , agent or em-
ployee of any Board or Commission created by the
laws of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or for the
use of any person or persons directly or indirectly
engaged in performing any services in behalf of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , other than the Gover-
nor and the Lieutenant Governor when acting as the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
the State Highway Commission of Kentucky and its
employees .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 511
2. That it shall be unlawful for the State
Treasurer , the Auditor of Public Accounts , officer
or member of any board or commission created by
the laws of the State of Kentucky , or any other
State officer of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to
purchase or contract for the purchase of any motor
passenger vehicle on behalf of or for the account
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , except for the
use of the Governor and the Lieutenant Governor
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky when acting as
the Governor and the State Highway Commission
of Kentucky and its employees , and it shall be un-
lawful for any payments to be made out of any
State funds or revenues for such purpose , other than
for the purchase of necessary motor passenger
vehicles for the use of the Governor and the Lieu-
tenant Governor when acting as the Governor of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway
Commission and its employees .
$ 3. When any officer , member of any board or
commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or
any subordinate officer , agent or employee thereof
is authorized under the laws of the State of Ken-
tucky to and does , in the discharge of his or her
official duties , incur any expense incident to travel-
ing in motor vehicles personally owned by such
officer , member , agent or employee , such expense
shall not exceed five cents per mile , over the nearest
traveled route over which such officer , member ,
agent or employee has traveled in the discharge of
such official duty in his or her personally owned
motor vehicle , which sum of five cents shall include
an allowance of one cent per mile for depreciation
and investment charges incident to the operation of
such motor vehicle , and such expense shall be paid
in the manner now provided by law for the payment
of expenses of State officials and employees of the

512 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
•
State of Kentucky , provided , however , that in no
event shall any sum be allowed or paid by the
Auditor of Public Accounts of the State of Ken-
tucky for the expenses in traveling motor vehicles ,
incident to the performance of official duties , in
excess of the actual expenses so incurred by such
official or employee .
§ 4. Nothing in this act contained shall be con-
strued as prohibiting the operation during the years
1930 and 1931 by State officers , members of boards
and commissions and agents and employees thereof,
of motor passenger vehicles heretofore purchased
by State funds and now owned by the State of Ken-
tucky , provided , such motor passenger vehicles shall
be used exclusively for the performance of official
duties and no claims for expenses incident to the
operation thereof shall be allowed or paid in excess
of the actual cost of operation thereof, which shall
not exceed four cents per mile for each mile traveled
in the performance of official duties .
§ 5. On or before January 1 , 1932 , all motor
passenger vehicles theretofore purchased by the
Commonwealth of Kentucky or any board or com-
mission created by the laws of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , other than such motor passenger
vehicle as may be in the use of the Governor and
Lieutenant Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky and its employees , shall be delivered to the
Sinking Fund Commission or its agent designated
for such purpose , by the board , commission , officer ,
agent or employees having the custody thereof , and
thereafter it shall be unlawful for any person whom-
soever , other than the Governor and Lieutenant
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
the State Highway Commission of Kentucky and its

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 513
employees to use or operate any passenger motor
vehicle owned by the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
On or before February 1 , 1932 , the Sinking Fund
Commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
shall advertise and sell all motor passenger vehicles
belonging to the Commonwealth of Kentucky , other
than such as may be in the use of the Governor and
Lieutenant Governor and the State Highway Com-
mission of Kentucky and its employees , and the pro-
ceeds derived from the sale thereof shall be paid
into the Sinking Fund of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and disbursed in payment of the existing
indebtedness of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
§ 6. Nothing in this Act contained shall be con-
strued as in any manner limiting the powers of the
State Highway Commission or any other board or
commission created by the laws of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , to purchase and operate motor
vehicles other than motor passenger vehicles .
$ 7. Any person violating any of the provisions
of this Act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
shall be fined not less than fifty dollars and not more
than five hundred dollars for each offense .
§ 8. Nothing in this Act contained shall be con-
strued as in any manner limiting or preventing the
powers of the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky to purchase and operate passenger motor
vehicles in the conduct of the affairs of the State
Highway Department of Kentucky . Provided , how-
ever , that no automobile purchased for said depart-
ment , shall cost in the aggregate more than $800.00 .
§ 9. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
§ 10. It being imperative for the efficient and
economical conduct of the State Highway Depart-
ment of the State of Kentucky that passenger motor
vehicles be purchased and operated by the State
Acts- 17

514 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Highway Commission of Kentucky and the employ-
ees thereof , an emergency is declared to exist and
this Act shall become effective from and after its
passage by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 104 .
AN ACT authorizing , regulating and governing the transporta-
tion for hire of persons and property by motor vehicles on
public highways ; providing for the payment of fees and for
the payment of taxes ; prescribing punishment and penalties ;
and repealing all laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Article one . Section one. As used in this Act,
unless the context otherwise requires : ( a ) " Motor
Vehicle" means any motor propelled vehicle , not
usually operated over or on rails , used for the trans-
portation on the public highways of this State of
persons or property for hire , and shall include any
such vehicle operated as a unit in combination with
another vehicle used for any such purpose ; (b )
" Operator " means any person , firm, partnership ,
association , joint stock company , corporation , lessee ,
trustee , or receiver , appointed by any court what-
soever owning , controlling , operating or managing
any motor vehicle used for the transportation of
persons or property for hire ; ( c ) " Common Car-
rier " means any operator of a motor vehicle for
hire in common carriage ; ( d ) " Contract Carrier"
means any operator of a motor vehicle for hire other
than a common carrier . " Contract carrier " shall
not be construed to mean or include any person, firm
or corporation who transports only his own prop-
erty , or who does not engage in the transportation

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 515
business but makes occasional or casual trips to
transport persons or the property of others for hire ;
( e ) " Public highway " means every public street
alley , road or highway in this State , whether within
or without the corporate limits of any municipality ;
( f ) " Certificate " means the certificate of public
convenience and necessity authorized to be issued
under the provisions of this Act ; ( g ) " Permit "
means the permit to operate authorized to be issued.
under the provisions of this Act ; (h ) " Commission "
means the State Tax Commission of Kentucky .
Section two . The Commission is authorized to
conduct any investigation , inquiry or hearing which
it deems proper in connection with the performance
of its duties hereunder , and same may be conducted
by or before any member of said Commission ; and
any decision or order made pursuant thereto shall ,
if approved and confirmed by said Commission as
shown by its records , be deemed for all purposes the
decision or order of the said Commission .
Section three . The State Tax Commission may
appoint such auditors , inspectors and other em-
ployees upon such terms and conditions as it may
deem necessary to enable it to perform the duties
imposed upon it by this act ; provided , however , that
the total compensation paid by the Commission for
the purposes herein prescribed shall be subject to
the approval of the Governor . The actual and
necessary traveling expenses incurred while en-
gaged in the discharge of their duties under this act
shall be paid to the members of the Commission and
to its employees .
After the payment of all expenses incurred by the
Commission in the performance of its duties under
this act , all fees collected shall be paid into the State
Road fund and shall be used for the maintenance
and construction of the public highways of this

516 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
State . If , however , such fees are insufficient for the
purpose stated , the amount necessary to make up
such shortage shall be paid out of the taxes collected
under this act . The balance of all such taxes shall
be paid into the State Road fund and shall be used
for the construction and maintenance of the public
highways .
Article two . Section one. No common carrier
shall operate any motor vehicle for hire for the
transportation of persons or property on any public
highway in this State without having obtained a
certificate from the Commission.
Section two . Every application for a certificate
shall be made in such form and contain such matters
as the Commission may prescribe , and shall be made
under oath , signed by the applicant ; or , if the appli-
cant be not a person , then by a person having knowl-
edge of the matters therein set forth and duly desig-
nated for that purpose by the applicant .
Section three . Upon the filing of any such appli-
cation and the payment of the fee hereinafter pre-
scribed , the Commission shall , within a reasonable
time , fix the time and place for a hearing thereof . A
written notice of such hearing , and of the right to
file a protest in accordance with said Commission's
requirements , shall be mailed by said Commission
at least ten (ten ) days before the hearing of such
application to the applicant , to all common carriers
(including steam and clectric railway companies )
serving any part of the route to be served by the
applicant, to the Chairman of the State Highway
Commission , to the county attorney and county
judge of each county in which the applicant pro-
poses to render service , and to any other person ,
firm or corporation who may, in the opinion of the
Commission, be interested in or affected by the
issuance of said certificate .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 517
Section four . At the time fixed in such notice , or
at such time thereafter as the Commission may de-
termine , a public hearing upon said application
shall be held by said Commission . Any person , firm
or corporation having an interest in the subject
matter shall have the right , in accordance with the
rules and regulations prescribed therefor by said
Commission, to file a protest to the granting , in
whole or in part of said application , to make rep-
resentations , and to introduce evidence in support
of said protest .
Section five . After such hearing the Commission
shall have the power to issue to the applicant a cer-
tificate , in the form to be prescribed by said Com-
mission, declaring that the public convenience and
necessity require the operation for which applica-
tion is made , or refuse to issue the same , or to issue
it for the partial exercise only of the privilege
sought , and may attach to the exercise of the rights
granted by such certificate such terms and condi-
tions as , in its judgment , the public convenience and
interest may require ; provided , however , if no pro-
test to the granting of the certificate be filed with
said Commission prior to the date fixed for the hear-
ing , and if said Commission is satisfied that the
privilege sought by the applicant is convenient and
necessary in the public interest the certificate may
be granted without a public hearing .
Section six . In granting or refusing to grant such
certificate , the Commission shall take into considera-
tion the effect that the proposed operation may have
upon public transportation business and facilities of
every character with the territory sought to be
served by the applicant , the public need for the
service the applicant proposes to render , the ability
of the applicant efficiently to perform the service for
which authority is requested , and the effect upon

518 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the highways and upon the safety of the public
using such highways that will probably result from
the granting of such application .
Section seven . No such certificate shall be issued
until the applicant has established to the satisfac-
tion of the Commission , upon consideration of the
matters mentioned in the preceding section , that the
privilege sought by the applicant is convenient and
necessary in the public interest . If two or more
operators who have been engaged in the transporta-
tion of property for compensation , before this Act
becomes effective , apply for a certificate authorizing
them to perform substantially the same service in
the same territory under similar conditions , and if
the said Commission shall be of the opinion that, in
accordance with the provisions of this Act , certifi-
cates should be granted to some but not all of such
applicants , preference shall be given to the operator
or operators who have been longest engaged in such
service , provided such service has been rendered
in accordance with the requirements of the law.
Section eight . When any application for a certifi-
cate has been heard and denied , the Commission
shall not entertain another application covering the
same or similar routes , schedules and/or service
until the expiration of six months from the date of
such denial .
Section nine . Any holder of a certificate of con-
venience and necessity , issued by the Commissioner
of Motor Transportation and in force at the time
this Act becomes effective , who has complied with
the requirements of the law now in force and the
orders of the said Commissioner , may surrender
said certificate within thirty (thirty ) days from the
time this Act becomes effective , and thereupon the
Commission shall , without the exaction of any fee or
charge , issue a certificate under this Act .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 519
Section ten , The Commission shall have the
power and authority , by general order or otherwise ,
to prescribe rules and regulations governing com-
mon carriers ; it shall have authority to fix or
approve the rates , fares , charges , classifications ,
rules and regulations of each such common carrier ,
to regulate operating schedules so as to insure ade-
quate and convenient transportation service , to pre-
scribe a uniform system and classification of
accounts to be used , to require the filing of annual
and other reports , and to supervise and regulate
such common carriers in all other matters in which
the public interest is involved .
Section eleven . No common carrier shall abandon
or change any route or service without an order of
the Commission that public convenience and neces-
sity permit such abandonment or change . Applica-
tions for any such abandonment or change shall be
made in accordance with the requirements of said
Commission , and the procedure on an application
for an abandonment or change of route shall be the
same as herein provided for the issuance of a certif-
icate ; provided , however , if it becomes necessary
on account of the condition of the roads or other
emergency , temporary changes in route , service and
schedules may be made .
Section twelve . Every rate demanded or received
by any common carrier shall be just and reason-
able .
Section thirteen . Every common carrier shall
furnish adequate , efficient , safe and reasonable
service .
Section fourteen. Under such rules and regula-
tions as the Commission may prescribe , every com-
mon carrier shall maintain on file with said Com-
mission a schedule of the rates , fares , charges and
classifications , if any , and a time schedule , if any ,

520 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of all motor vehicles operated under the authority
of said Commission , and shall keep open for public
inspection at designated offices so much of said
schedules , rates , fares , charges and classifications ,
as well as time schedules , as said Commission may
deem necessary for public information .
Section fifteen . No common carrier shall charge ,
demand , collect or receive a greater or less or dif-
ferent compensation for the transportation of per-
sons or property , or for any service in connection
therewith , than the rates , fares and charges speci-
fied in its tariffs and classifications filed with and
approved by said Commission and in effect at the
time ; nor shall any company refund or remit in any
manner or by any device any portion of the rates ,
fares or charges so specified , nor make or give any
unreasonable preference or advantage to any per-
son ; nor subject any person to any unreasonable
prejudice or discrimination .
Section sixteen . Unless Commission otherwise
orders, no common carrier shall make any change in
any rate which has been duly established under this
Act , except after thirty days ' notice to said Com-
mission , which notice shall plainly state the changes
proposed to be made in the rates then in force and
the time when the changed rates will go into effect .
Such carrier shall also give such notice of the pro-
posed change to other interested persons , and in
such manner , as said Commission , in its discretion ,
may direct . All proposed changes shall be shown
by filing new schedules or shall be plainly indicated.
on schedules filed and in force at the time and kept
open for public inspection . Said Commission , for
good cause shown , may allow changes in rates with-
out requiring the thirty (thirty ) day's notice under
such conditions as it may prescribe . All such

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 521
changes shall be immediately indicated upon its
schedules by such carrier .
Whenever there is filed with said Commission any
schedule stating a new rate or rates , said Commis-
sion may , either upon complaint or upon its own
initiative upon reasonable notice , enter upon a hear-
ing concerning the lawfulness of such rate or rates ,
and , pending such hearing and decision thereof ,
said Commission may suspend the operation of such
rate or rates for a period of not to exceed six ( six )
months . If after such hearing said Commission
finds any such rate or rates to be unjust , unreason-
able or unjustly discriminatory , or in any wise in
violation of the law, said Commission shall deter-
mine the just and reasonable rate or rates to be
charged and shall fix the same by order .
Section seventeen . If the Commission , after a
hearing held after reasonable notice upon its own
motion or upon complaint , finds any existing rate
or rates to be excessive , unreasonable or unjustly
discriminatory , or the services rendered or the
facilities employed by any common carrier to be un-
safe , inadequate , inconvenient or in any wise in
violation of law or the rules and regulations of said
Commission , it may determine the just and reason-
able rates to be charged thereafter , or the reason-
able , safe , convenient and adequate service to be
thereafter furnished , and shall fix the same by
order ; provided , however , that nothing in this Act
shall be construed to require the Commission to fix
the same rates for motor carriers subject to this
Act as are or may be fixed for carriers engaged in
other methods of transportation .
Article three . Section one . It is hereby declared
that the business of contract carriers is affected with
the public interest , and that the safety and welfare
of the public , the preservation and maintenance of

522 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the public highways , and the integrity of the regu-
lation of common carriers require the regulation of
contract carriers to the extent hereinafter provided .
Section two . No contract carrier shall operate
any motor vehicle for hire for the transportation of
persons or property on any public highway in this
State without having obtained a permit from the
Commission .
Section three . Applications for permits shall be
made in the manner and form provided for in the
Commission's regulations and said Commission
may , if it deems it advisable , require a public hear-
ing to be held thereon , and in this event it shall give
written notice thereof to all persons who may , in the
opinion of said Commission , be interested in or
affected by the issuance of such permit at least ten
days prior to the time fixed for such hearing .
Section four. Upon the payment of fees herein-
after prescribed , the Commission shall have power
to issue to the applicant a permit , in the form to be
prescribed by said Commission , authorizing the
operation for which application is made , provided
the applicant has established to the satisfaction of
said Commission that the privilege sought will not
endanger the safety of the public or interfere with
the public's use of the highways or impair the con-
dition or maintenance of such highways .
Section five . Every contract carrier is hereby
forbidden to give or cause any undue or unreason-
able advantage or preference to those whom he
serves as compared with the patrons of any com-
mon carrier , as that term is used in this Act , or the
patrons of any other common carrier , or to subject
the patrons of any such common carrier to any
undue or unreasonable discrimination or disadvan-
tage , or by unfair competition to destroy or impair
the service or business of any common carrier , as

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 523
that term is used in this Act , or of any other common
carrier , or the integrity of the State's regulation of
any such service or business ; and , to the end that
the Commission may enforce these provisions , each
such contract carrier shall maintain on file with said
Commission a statement of his charges , and of such
other matters as said Commission may require .
Section six . Whenever upon complaint filed with
the Commission , or upon said Commission's own
motion, it may find , after hearing to be conducted in
accordance with said Commission's general orders ,
that any such rate, fare , charge , classification or
any other rule or practice of any contract carrier
causes any violation of Article three , Section five
of this Act , it shall prescribe the rate, fare , charge ,
or the maximum or minimum , or maximum and
minimum thereafter to be charged , and the classifi-
cation , regulation , or practice thereafter to be
observed by such contract carrier , in such manner
as , in its judgment , will remove such violation ; pro-
vided , however that nothing in this Act shall be
construed to require the Commission to fix the same
rates for motor carriers subject to this Act as are
or may be fixed for carriers engaged in other
methods of transportation .
Section seven . The Commission is further
authorized , after such general investigation and
hearing as it may deem advisable , to issue , from
time to time , such general and special orders regu-
lating and controlling the business of contract car-
riers , their rates and practices , as it may deem
reasonably necessary to prevent violation of Article
three , Section five of this Act .
Article four . Section one. Each application for
a certificate or permit shall be accompanied by a
fee of ten dollars (ten dollars ) if filed by an appli-
cant to operate a motor vehicle for hire designed

524 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
primarily for the transportation of persons , with
a seating capacity of seven persons or less ; all other
applications for certificates shall be accompanied by
a fee of twenty -five dollars (twenty-five dollars ) ,
and all other applications for permits shall be
accompanied by a fee of twenty dollars ( twenty
dollars ) .
Section two . Distinguishing plates shall be pre-
scribed and furnished by the Commission for , and
shall be at all times displayed on , each motor vehicle
authorized by the Commission to operate under this
Act . The transfer of such plate from one vehicle to
another is prohibited except upon the authority and
consent of the Commission .
Section Three . Application for amendments of
certificates or permits may be made as are applica-
tions for the original certificates and may be
granted in the same manner as provided for certifi-
cates . No certificate or permit may be transferred
without the consent of the Commission authorizing
such transfer . Said Commission is authorized to
prescribe the conditions precedent to such transfer
and to make any necessary rules and regulations
pertaining thereto .
Section four . No certificate or permit granted
under this Act shall be effective after the first day
of January of the year following the year of its
issue . Renewals shall be issued upon application
made in accordance with said Commission's require-
ments and upon the payment of the fees prescribed
for original applications . But the Commission shall
have no power to refuse to renew any existing per-
mit or certificate , or any permit at any time granted
by it , or to cancel any such permit or certificate
whenever issued , except for just cause and after a
hearing, thirty days ' notice of which shall be given
to the holder of the certificate or permit , and if the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 525
Commission should , after such hearing , refuse to
renew or undertake to cancel any such permit or
certificate , the holder or owner of the permit or
certificate shall have the right to appeal from the
action of the Commission by filing a petition of
appeal with the Clerk of the Franklin Circuit Court
in the manner provided in Section eighteen , of
Article four of this Act ; but upon such hearing , any-
thing in this Act to the contrary notwithstanding , all
parties may be permitted to introduce new evidence
and the Court shall try the matter de novo , and
either party shall have a right to appeal from the
judgment of the Franklin Circuit Court to the Court
of Appeals of Kentucky , and , pending such appeals ,
the certificate or permit shall remain in effect .
Section five . No operator of a motor vehicle for
hire shall permit such vehicle to be operated upon
the highways of this State unless the driver or
chauffeur thereof has obtained , and from time to
time has renewed , in accordance with the require-
ments of the Commission , a statement from said
Commission certifying that said driver or chauffeur
has met its requirements with respect to his health ,
vision , sobriety , intelligence , care , ability and ex-
perience as the driver or chauffeur of such motor
vehicles . For the original issue , and for each re-
newal , of each such statement there shall be paid
a fee of two dollars ( two dollars ) . The holder of
each such statement shall at all times when operat-
ing a motor vehicle for hire have in his possession
said statement , and shall wear a badge , to be issued
by said Commission , indicating that the holder
thereof is a certified driver or chauffeur .
Section six . The Commission may, for good
cause , upon complaint or upon its own motion revoke
or suspend any such statement , whereupon it and
the badge shall be returned to said Commission .

526 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Section seven . It shall be unlawful for any com-
mon carrier , or contract carrier , to require or per-
mit any driver or chauffeur operating a motor
vehicle for hire under the provisions of this Act to
be or remain continuously on duty for a longer
period than twelve hours , and when such driver or
chauffeur has been continuously on duty for twelve
hours , he shall have at least eight consecutive hours
off duty ; and it shall be unlawful for any common
carrier , or contract carrier , to require or permit
any driver or chauffeur operating a motor vehicle
for hire under the provisions of this Act to be or re-
main on duty for a longer period than sixteen hours
in the aggregate in any twenty -four hours period ,
he shall have at least ten consecutive hours off duty.
The periods of release from duty herein required
shall be given at such place and under such
circumstances that rest and relaxation from the
strain of the duties of the employment may be
obtained . No period off duty shall be deemed to
break the continuity of service unless it be for at
least three consecutive hours and given at a place
and under such circumstances that rest and relaxa-
tion from the strain of the duties of the employment
may be obtained . In case of an unforseen emer-
gency not resulting from the negligence of the car-
rier, its agents , servants or employees , the driver
or chauffeur may complete his run or tour of duty,
if such run or tour of duty, but for the delay caused
by such emergency , could reasonably have been com-
pleted without a violation of this Section . The
Commission may require such reports as it may
deem necessary for the enforcement of the provi-
sions of this section .
Section eight . No certificate or permit shall be
issued until there has been filed with and accepted
by the Commission a good and sufficient indemnity

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 527
bond or insurance policy issued by some surety or
insurance company or other insurance carrier , duly
authorized to transact business as such with this
State , which shall provide by such terms , conditions
and provisions and in such penal sums or maximum
amounts as said Commission may deem necessary
for the reasonable protection of the patrons of the
operator of the motor vehicle for hire and of the
public in the collection of damages for which the
operator may be liable by reason of the operation
of any motor vehicle for hire ; provided , however ,
that the bond or insurance policy required of a con-
tract carrier or the operator of a motor vehicle for
hire engaged solely in interstate commerce shall not
be for the protection of the patrons of the motor
carrier ; and provided further that no certificate or
permit shall be issued until there has been filed with
the Commission a bond in the penal sum of one
thousand ( one thousand dollars ) dollars , payable to
the State of Kentucky , with some surety company
qualified to do business in the State as surety
thereon , conditioned that the applicant shall pay any
and all fees , taxes or penalties which may be due
under the provisions of this Act and for the faith-
ful compliance with all lawful decisions , orders ,
rules , regulations , demands and requirements of the
Commission made , rendered , issued or promulgated
under the provisions of this Act . Provided , how-
ever , if the Commission , in its discretion , shall deem
it necessary , it may require that the amount of the
bond to be given shall be increased to an amount not
to exceed Five Thousand (five thousand dollars )
Dollars . No bond or insurance policy may be can-
celed or otherwise terminated at any time prior to
its expiration for any reason whatever until there
have been filed with said Commission by the surety
or indemnity company a notice to such effect at

528 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
least fifteen (fifteen ) days prior to the date of such
termination or cancellation . This provision shall
be deemed to be a part of every such undertaking ,
and no other provision thereof, and no agreement
between the parties thereto , shall operate to avoid
the same . If any such bond or insurance policy
shall become inoperative , the authority under the
certificate or permit shall cease and be suspended
until a bond or insurance policy meeting the re-
quirement of this section shall become effective and
be filed with said Commission .
Section nine . If an operator be a non -resident of
this state he shall, prior to operating any motor
vehicle for hire , appoint , and shall , so long as be
operates a motor vehicle for hire in this state , main-
tain an agent upon whom process may be served , in
the manner provided in Section three thousand
seven hundred and twenty -d of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes .
Section ten . There shall be paid by every
operator of a motor vehicle for hire , subject to the
provisions of this Act , as an excise tax for the privi-
lege of using the highways the following rates per
mile traveled upon the highways of this State by
each motor vehicle for hire operated by him : ( a )
On each motor vehicle for hire , used primarily for
the transportation of persons , with a passenger
seating capacity of not more than seven ( seven )
persons , one-fourth ( one-fourth ) cent , eight ( eight )
to sixteen ( sixteen ) inclusive , one -half (one-half )
cent , seventeen ( seventeen ) to twenty -five ( twenty-
five ) inclusive , three -fourths ( three -fourths ) cent ,
twenty six (twenty- six ) to twenty-nine (twenty-
nine ) inclusive , one ( one ) cent, thirty (thirty ) or
more , three (three ) cents ; provided , however , the
tax imposed by this sub -section on account of the
operation of any such motor vehicle shall be in-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 529
creased one hundred ( one hundred ) per cent if the
annual registration fee prescribed by the law of this
State on like motor vehicles is not paid because such
motor vehicle is engaged in interstate commerce ;
(b) On each motor vehicle for hire , used primarily
for the transportation of property , which vehicle ,
when unloaded , weighs more than fifty -five hundred
pounds and not more than three ( three ) tons , one-
half ( one -half ) cent , and one-half ( one-half ) addi-
tional cent for each ton or fraction thereof, which
the unloaded motor vehicle weighs , in excess of
three (three ) tons ; provided , however , the tax im-
posed by this sub - section on account of the opera-
tion of any such motor vehicle shall be increased
thirty (thirty ) per cent if the annual registration
fee prescribed by the law of this State on like motor
vehicles is not paid because such motor vehicle is
engaged in interstate commerce . The tax imposed
by this section shall in each instance be increased
fifty (fifty per cent ) per cent on each motor vehicle
for hire not equipped wholly with pneumatic tires .
There shall be added a penalty of one (one per cent )
per cent per month for each month the payment of
the tax imposed by this section is in default . The
Commission is directed to collect all the taxes im-
posed by this section .
Section eleven . The fees and taxes prescribed by
this Act shall be in addition to the fees and taxes
prescribed by any other law of this State ; provided ,
however , no municipality shall be authorized to im-
pose a license fee or tax upon any motor vehicle
operated under a certificate or permit granted pur-
suant to the provisions of this Act .
Section twelve . In order to ascertain the mileage
traveled by motor vehicles subject to this act , the
Commission shall prescribe the records to be kept
by each operator of a motor vehicle for hire , and

530 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
on or before the fifteenth of each month each opera-
tor of a motor vehicle for hire shall file with the
Commission , in duplicate , a statement , verified
under oath , by the operator or by a person having
knowledge of the facts and duly designated there-
for by the operator , showing the mileage traveled by
each motor vehicle for hire operated by such
operator during the preceding calendar month , and
shall at the time of the filing of such report pay the
tax reflected by it.
Section thirteen . The State Tax Commission is
empowered to prescribe and require the character
of applicances which each operator of a motor
vehicle for hire shall use in order to establish the
correct mileage traveled by such vehicle , and to
adopt rules and regulations with respect to the care
and inspection of such applicances and reports of
the mileage shown by said appliances .
Section fourteen . Each application , which accord-
ing to the provisions of this Act must be accom-
panied by the payment of a fee , shall , together with
the prescribed fee , be sent to the Commission .
Section fifteen . The Commission may, in its dis-
cretion , require any operator of a motor vehicle for
hire , operating such vehicle within this State , to
keep within this State , subject to inspection at all
reasonable times by said Commission , or its agent ,
such books of account or records as may be reason-
ably necessary to enable said Commission to obtain
full information at all times regarding the amounts
due from such operator under the provisions of this
Act . The books and records of all common carriers
and contract carriers shall be at all times open to
inspection by the Commission , by any member .
thereof, or by any agent appointed for such purpose .
Section sixteen . Every operator of a motor
vehicle for hire shall register with the Commission

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 531
in accordance with its requirements , and shall com-
ply with the said Commission's rules and regulations
adopted for the purpose of ascertaining the mileage
traveled for hire upon the highways of this Stace
by each such motor vehicle . No fee shall be charged
for such registration .
Section seventeen . Upon the completion of any
hearing , the Commission shall render a written
report of its finding of facts and conclusions of law ,
and shall include a statement of such other matters
as may be pertinent to the question at issue . This
report shall be filed with the records of the proceed-
ings and a copy thereof shall be immediately sent
to all those who have become parties to the pro-
ceedings .
Section eighteen . When any application for a
certificate or permit has been refused , the applicant
shall have the right to appeal , or if the application
is granted, any person who has filed a protest to the
granting of such application shall have the right to
appeal as hereinafter provided . The appellant may ,
within twenty ( twenty ) days after the rendition of
the order of the Commission , file a petition of appeal
with the Clerk of the Circuit Court of Franklin
County . Such petition shall state completely the
grounds upon which the review is sought , shall
assign all errors relied on , and shall be accompanied
by a copy of the record , certified by said Commis-
sion , or an abstract thereof if agreed to by all par-
ties to the hearing before said Commission . The
appellant shall furnish copies of the petition to each
person who was a party to the hearing before said
Commission. Summons shall be issued upon the
petition , directing the adverse party or parties to
file answer within fifteen ( 15 ) days after service
thereof .

532 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Section nineteen . No new or additional evidence
may be introduced in the Circuit Court except as to
fraud or misconduct of some person engaged in the
administration of this Act and affecting the order ,
ruling or award , but the court shall otherwise hear
the case upon the certified record or abstract
thereof , and shall dispose of the case in summary
manner , its review being limited to determining
whether or not : One . The Commission acted with-
out or in excess of its power ; Two . The order , deci-
sion or award was procured by fraud ; Three . The
order , decision or award is in conformity to the
provisions of this Act ; Four . The finding of facts
in issue is supported by any substantial evidence .
Section twenty. The Commission and each party
shall have the right to appear in such review pro-
ceedings . The court shall enter judgment, confirm-
ing, modifying or setting aside the order , decision
or award, or , in its discretion , remanding the case
to the Commission for proceedings in conformity
with the direction of the court . The court may , in
advance of judgment and upon a sufficient showing
of facts , remand the cause to said Commission . Any
party may appeal from the decision of the Circuit
Court to the Court of Appeals . Such appeals to the
Court of Appeals shall have precedence of other
cases pending .
Section twenty -one. Whenever any common car-
rier or contract carrier is found to be violating the
provisions of this Act , or any of the rules and regu-
lations prescribed by the Commission , pursuant to
the provisions of this Act , or any of the laws of the
State of Kentucky touching on such carriers ' opera-
tion upon the public highways of this State , the
Commission may upon complaint or upon its own
motion issue its order to the said carrier notifying
him to appear before it at a fixed time and place , at

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 533
which time and place said Commission shall investi-
gate such violation , and if it shall be satisfied , after
a hearing , that said carrier has violated or refused
to observe such laws , rules or regulations , said Com-
mission may suspend , revoke , alter or amend any
certificate or permit issued to such carrier , but the
holder shall have the right of appeal as hereinbefore
provided .
Section twenty-two . Every device to evade , or to
prevent the application of any provision of this Act ,
or any lawful order , rule or regulation of the Com-
mission issued pursuant to the provisions of this
Act , is hereby forbidden and declared to be a viola-
tion of this Act .
Section twenty-three . Every operator of a motor
vehicle for hire , every officer, agent or employee of
any such operator , and every other person who
violates , or causes , aids or abets any violation of
this Act , or who violates or causes , aids or abets any
violation of any order, rule or regulation lawfully
issued pursuant to authority granted by this act , or
who knowingly makes any false or erroneous state-
ment , report or representation to the Commission ,
with respect to any matter placed under the juris-
diction of the Commission by this Act , or who shall
knowingly make any false entry in the accounts or
records required to be kept pursuant to the authority
granted by this Act , or who shall knowingly fail to
keep , or who shall knowingly destroy or mutilate
any such accounts or records , shall be punished by
a fine of not exceeding five hundred dollars ( five
hundred dollars ) , or by imprisonment in the county
jail for not exceeding one year, or by both such fine
and imprisonment . Prosecution may be had in the
circuit court of any county in which the offense , or
any part thereof, shall have occurred .

534 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Section twenty-four . Any common carrier , con-
tract carrier or any other person , firm or corpora-
tion may , at the instance of the Commission or of
any person having an interest in the subject -matter,
be enjoined by the courts of this State from any
violation of the provisions of this Act , or of any
order, rule , regulation or requirement of the Com-
mission .
Section twenty -five . Upon the request of the Com-
mission it shall be the duty of the Attorney General
or of any Commonwealth's Attorney or County
Attorney to represent within his jurisdiction and
without additional compensation , the Commission
in any action or proceeding arising under the provi-
sions of this Act in which it may be a party .
Section twenty- six. There shall be exempted from
the provisions of this Act ; One . Motor vehicles for
hire while being used exclusively for the transporta-
tion of school children and school teachers to and
from school , provided such vehicle is operated by,
or the compensation for such transportation is paid
by , a school district ; Two . Motor vehicles for hire ,
operating exclusively within the limits of a city or
incorporated town , or within ten miles of its limits.
if it be a city of the first , second , third , fourth or
fifth class , or within five miles of its limits if it be a
town of the sixth class . Provided , however , that the
operator of any such motor vehicle for the carriage
of passengers , operating between any city or incor-
porated town and a point or points within ten ( 10 )
miles of the limits thereof , if the city be of the first ,
second , third , fourth or fifth class , or within five
miles of the limits thereof , if the town be of the sixth
class , and over regular routes or between fixed
termini , may apply to the Commission for a certif-
icate . The provisions of Article two and of Article
four, except Sections ten , eleven , twelve , thirteen ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 535
fifteen and sixteen of Article four , shall apply to the
certificates issued under this proviso and operations
thereunder . After the issuance of such certificate ,
it shall be unlawful for the operator of any motor
vehicle for hire , not holding a certificate , to operate
in competition with the holder of such certificate
over the route , or any part thereof, covered by such
certificate . Three . Motor vehicles for hire , designed
primarily for the transportation of property , pro-
vided such motor vehicles , when unloaded , weigh
not more than fifty -five hundred pounds . Four . Any
motor vehicle designed primarily for the transporta-
tion of property owned or leased by a non -profit ,
co - operative association , carrying only property
belonging to the association or its members , shall be
considered to be an owner's truck and exempt from
the provisions of this Act .
Section twenty- seven . This Act and every part
thereof shall apply and be construed to apply to
interstate commerce , except insofar as the same
may be in conflict with the provisions of the Consti-
tution of the United States and the Acts of Congress .
Section twenty-eight . If for any reason any
article , section , sub-section , sentence , clause or
phrase of this Act shall be held unconstitutional ,
such decision shall not affect the validity of any
other portion of this Act. It is hereby declared to
be the legislative intent that this Act would have
been adopted had such void or ineffective article ,
section , sub -section , sentence , clause or phrase not
been included in this Act .
Section twenty-nine . All laws and parts of laws
in conflict with the provisions of this Act are hereby
repealed .
Approved March 17, 1932 .

536 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 105 .
AN ACT requiring all vehicles traveling any highway, public
road , or street , to carry lights or lanterns of sufficient bright-
ness to show the outline of the left side of such vehicle , an
amendment of Section 2739 G 24 , Carroll's Edition of Kentucky
Statutes , excepting therefrom wagons , buggies or slow moving
vehicles or motorless vehicles while traveling on roads , other
than State and Federal or State roads and highways .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2739 G 24 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes be amended by repealing all of said section
and re -enacting in lieu thereof the following :
That all automobiles , motor trucks , bicycles ,
wagons , buggies , motorcycles , and each and every
other kind of vehicle used or operated upon any
public highway, road , or street , in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , are required to display lights
as follows :
( 1 ) At the rear one lighted lamp showing red
visible from the rear two hundred feet away,
whether said vehicle be in operation or stationary .
( 2 ) When in operation bicycles and two -wheeled
motorcycles shall have at least one , and all other
automobiles , and trucks , including three -wheeled
motorcycles , shall have at least two lighted lamps in
front , showing white , or tinted other than red light ,
and of sufficient power to clearly reveal substantial
objects at least two hundred ( feet ahead ) except for
bicycles , fifty feet ahead ; provided , any light pro-
jected ahead or sidewise shall be so arranged or
deflected that no glaring rays from it or from any
reflector shall be at any time more than three and
one-half feet above a level surface at a distance of
seventy -five feet or more ahead of such vehicle , and
provided , further , that in case of accident or emer-
gency , automobiles or trucks may display lantern or
other lights of smaller power, but if insufficient to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 537
clearly reveal substantial objects at least seventy-
five feet ahead , shall not proceed at a rate of speed
in excess of eight miles an hour : Provided , that no
automobile or truck shall be sold in this Common-
.wealth unless the light projected by the headlights
meets the foregoing requirements at the time of the
sale .
( 3 ) When in operation wagons , buggies or any
other slow moving vehicles er motorless vehicle
while travelling on any State or Federal or State
and Federal road or highway, shall have at least one
lighted lamp on the left side of said vehicle whether
from the front or rear , showing white and of suffici
ent power to clearly reveal the outline of the left
side of said vehicle and so that said outline may be
observed by approaching vehicles from a distance
of at least one hundred feet .
(4) Cities or towns may by ordinance designate
certain well lighted streets or parts thereof during
certain periods as being sufficiently illuminated to
make lights on parked vehicles unnecessary , in which
case Paragraphs 1, 2 , and 3, shall not apply to such
vehicles parked in such streets during such period.
and may also provide that when in operation in such
designated streets or parts thereof during such
period, light or lights in front may be of lessor
power as is provided in Paragraphs 2 and 3 in which
case the provisions of paragraphs 2 and 3 shall not
apply .
( 5 ) All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 106 .
AN ACT to further regulate motor -trucks , semi -trailer trucks ,
semi -trailers and trailers to enlarge the powers of the State
Highway Commission , County Courts , and cities and incorpo-

538 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
rated towns in respect thereof, to provide for enforcement of
the act and penalties for violation thereof , and repealing all
laws inconsistent therewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Definitions : When used in this Act :
" Motor -truck ” means any motor- propelled vehicle
designed for carrying freight or merchandise and
not operated or driven on fixed rails or tracks ; but
it shall not include any self-propelled vehicles
designed primarily for passenger transportation ,
but equipped with frames , racks or bodies having a
load capacity not exceeding One thousand pounds .
'Semi -Trailer Trucks " means any motor-propelled
vehicle , not operated or driven on fixed rails or
tracks , designed to draw , and to support the front
end of, a semi -trailer , which, together with the
tractor , shall be considered to be one unit .
66
" Semi - Trailer " means a vehicle designed to be
attached to , and having its front end supported by ,
a motor truck or truck tractor , and intended for the
carrying of freight or merchandise and with a load
capacity of over One thousand pounds . " Trailer "
means any vehicle designed to be drawn by a motor
truck or truck -tractor , but supported wholly upon its
own wheels , and intended for the carriage of freight
or merchandise and with a load capacity of over
One thousand pounds .
"Person " shall include individuals , partnerships ,
associations , trusts and corporations , and the re-
ceivers , assignees or agents of any of them .
"Highway " shall include any public road , street ,
avenue , alley or boulevard , bridge, viaduct or trestle
and the approaches thereto , within the limits of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
" Commission " shall mean the State Highway
Commission of Kentucky .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 539
( Trailers . ) Section Two . No person shall operate
any trailer on any highway .
(Weight . ) Section Three . No person shall oper-
ate on any highway any motor -truck or semi -trailer
truck whose gross weight, including load , shall ex-
ceed Eighteen thousand pounds .
( Height . ) Section Four . No person shall operate
on any highway any motor-truck or semi -trailer
truck whose height , including any part of body or
load , shall exceed eleven and one-half feet , but noth-
ing herein contained shall be construed to require
the public authorities to provide sufficient vertical
clearances to prevent the operation of vehicles with
the height of eleven and one-half feet .
(Width . ) Section Five . No person shall operate
on any highway any motor-truck or semi -trailer
truck whose width , including any part of body or
load, shall exceed ninety-six inches .
( Length . ) Section Six . No person shall operate
on any highway any motor-truck whose length , in-
cluding any part of body or load, shall exceed
twenty -six and one-half feet , nor any semi - trailer
truck whose length , including any part of body or
load , shall exceed thirty feet .
(Speed . ) Section Seven . No person shall operate
any motor-truck or semi -trailer truck on any high-
way outside cities or incorporated towns at a greater
speed than thirty miles per hour , if its gross weight ,
including load, exceed Five thousand pounds , or at
a greater speed than forty miles per hour , if its
gross weight , including load shall be Five thousand
pounds or less , and inside the limits of cities and
incorporated towns the maximum speed limits shall
be twenty miles per hour and twenty -five miles per
hour , respectively .
( Speed Governor . ) Section Eight . No person
shall operate any motor truck or semi -trailer truck

540 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
on any highway unless said motor -truck or semi-
trailer truck shall be equipped with a suitable
mechanical device , in good order and working con-
dition , which shall automatically prevent the opera-
tion of such motor-truck or semi -trailer truck at a
speed in excess of the maximum speed authorized
for the class of motor-trucks or semi -trailer trucks
to which said motor-truck or semi -trailer truck may
belong , as provided in Section Seven hereof.
( Brakes on Semi -Trailers . ) Section Nine . No
person shall operate any semi -trailer on any high-
way unless said semi - trailer shall be equipped with
efficient brakes of such design and character that
they may be operated and controlled by the operator
of the truck or tractor to which said semi -trailer is
attached .
(Stop-Lights . ) Section Ten . No person shall
operate any motor-truck or semi -trailer truck on
any highway unless it shall be equipped with a yel-
low or red light which shall automatically indicate
the application of brakes and be visible from the
rear a distance of not less than two hundred feet .
(Clearance Lights . ) Section Eleven . No person
shall operate on any highway a motor-truck or semi-
trailer truck having a width of any part in excess
of eighty four inches , unless said motor truck or
semi -trailer truck shall carry at least two clearance
lights to indicate the outside left limit of said motor-
truck or semi -trailer truck , one light , colored white
or green , to be attached to and visible from the
front of said motor-truck or semi -trailer truck , and
one light , colored red , to be attached to and visible
from the rear thereof, in each case a distance of not
less than five hundred feet .
(Reduced Load and Speed Limits . ) Section
Twelve . Anything in this act to the contrary not-
withstanding, the Commission , in respect of State

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 541
Highways or roads designated as part of the State
system of primary roads , and county courts , or the
judges thereof , in respect of county roads , may pre-
scribe , by notice as hereinafter provided , load and
speed limits lower than the limits prescribed in
Sections Three and Seven hereof, respectively ,
whenever in its or their judgment any road or part
thereof, or any bridge or culvert, shall, by reason
of its design , deterioration , rain or other climatic
cr natural causes , be liable to be damaged or
destroyed by motor-trucks or semi -trailer trucks , if
the gross weight or speed thereof shall exceed the
limits prescribed in said notice ; and said Com-
mission , or county courts , or the judges thereof ,
may , by like notice , regulate or prohibit , in
whole or in part , the operation of motor-trucks or
semi -trailer trucks on State highways , or county
roads , or specified parts thereof , for limited periods
or specified days , or parts of days , whenever , in its
or their judgment , such regulation or prohibition
is necessary to provide for the public safety and
convenience on said highways or roads , or part
thereof, by reason of traffic density or intensive use
thereof by the traveling public. Said notice , or the
substance thereof , shall be posted at conspicuous
places at the termini of and at all intermediate
crossroads and road junctions with the section of
road to which said notice shall apply . After any
such notice shall have been so posted , the operation
of any motor truck or semi -trailer truck contrary to
its provisions shall constitute a violation of this act .
(Exemptions . ) Section Thirteen . (a ) The provi-
sions of this act shall not apply to motor trucks ,
semi -trailer trucks or trailers , owned by, or by any
agency of, the United States , the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or any county or city or incorporated
town therein .

542 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(b) If any motor-truck , semi -trailer truck or
trailer , shall be licensed by any city or incorporated
town in this State , and the registration plate or
plates issued as evidence of said license shall be
conspicuously exhibited on said motor-truck , semi-
trailer truck or trailer in the manner required by
ordinance , the provisions of Sections , Two , Three ,
Four , Five, Six , Nine , Ten and Eelven hereof shall
not apply to the operation of such motor-truck ,
semi -trailer truck or trailer , within the limits of the
city or incorporated town issuing such license , or
within ten miles of the limits thereof, if it be a city
of the first , second , third or fourth class , or within
five miles of the limits thereof , if it be a town of the
fifth or sixth class . Provided , that all cities and
incorporated towns are hereby empowered to pro-
vide , by ordinance , maximum limits with respect to
the weight , height , width and length of motor-
trucks , semi -trailer trucks and trailers , within their
respective boundaries , not less , however , than the
maximum limits prescribed in Sections Three , Four ,
Five and Six hereof, to authorize the operation of
trailers , therein , to impose license taxes on motor-
trucks , truck-tractors , semi -trailers and trailers , and
to require that all vehicles to which licenses may be
issued shall affix the registration plate or plates
issued as evidence of said licenses to said vehicles
in some conspicuous place , to be prescribed by
ordinance .
(c) The Commission is hereby authorized to
prescribe , by orders of general application , rules
and regulations for the issuance by it of permits for
the operation of motor trucks , tractors , semi -trailer
and trailer , whose gross weight including load ,
height , width or length may exceed the limits pre-
scribed by Sections Three , Four , Five , and Six
hereof, or which in other respects fail to comply

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 543
with the requirements of this act . Said permits may
be issued by said Commissioner for stated periods
and for special purposes and to meet unusual condi-
tions , and upon such terms and conditions in the
interest of public safety and the preservation of the
highways as the Commission may , in its discretion ,
require . Said Commission shall require , as a con-
dition to the issuance of such permit , that the ap-
plicant pay a reasonable fee therefor , to be fixed by
the Commission , and may require that the applicant
give bond , with approved surety , to indemnify the
State , and or the counties thereof , against damage
to roads or bridges resulting from the use thereof
by the applicant . The operation of motor trucks ,
tractors , semi -trailers or trailers , in accordance with
the terms of any permit shall not constitute a viola-
tion of this Act , provided the operator thereof shall
have said permit, or a copy thereof, authenticated
as the Commission may require in his possession .
The operation of any motor-truck, tractor , semi-
trailer or trailer , in violation of the terms of any
such permit , shall constitute a violation of this Act .
(d ) The limitations herein prescribed by Section
Three shall not apply , prior to January first , nine-
teen hundred and thirty -three , to any motor truck
or semi -trailer truck which shall have been regis-
tered and shall have paid the annual registration
fee as provided by the law of this State , before the
date upon which this Act shall be approved by the
Governor , or before the date , this Act shall become
a law without the approval of the Governor , but
shall , as to such vehicles , be and become in full force
and effect on and after January first , nineteen hun-
dred and thirty three .
(Penalties . ) Section Fourteen . The operation of
any motor truck , semi -trailer truck or trailer , in
violation of any section of this act , or of the terms of

544 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
any special permit issued hereunder , shall consti-
tute a misdemeanor , and shall subject the owner
thereof , if such violation was with his knowledge or
consent , and the operator thereof , to a fine of not
less than fifteen dollars , nor more than two hun-
dred dollars , and the costs of prosecution . Prosecu-
tions under this act may be by summary arrest ,
warrant or summons , as provided in the criminal
code, or by information as provided in Section
Eleven hundred and forty -one , Kentucky Statutes .
(Jurisdiction of Offenses .) Section Fifteen .
Quarterly courts and the judges thereof , justices
of the peace and police or city judges , shall have
concurrent jurisdiction with circuit courts in the
trial of all offenses under this act , committed within
their respective jurisdiction , and said courts shall
always be open for the trial of such offenses .
Section Sixteen . Any officer hereinafter in Sec-
tions Seventeen and Eighteen enumerated , who shall
be in uniform or who shall exhibit his badge or other
evidence of authority , having reason to believe that
the height , length , width or weight of any motor-
truck , semi -trailer truck or trailer , is in excess of
the maximum limits prescribed by this act or per-
mitted by any special permit issued under the terms
hereof, is authorized to measure or weigh the same,
either by means of portable or stationary scales ,
and may require such vehicle to be driven to the
nearest stationary scales , in the event such scales
are within the distance of five miles . Said officer
shall require the operator of said motor -truck , semi-
trailer truck or trailer , to unload such portion of
the load as may be necessary to decrease the gross
weight of such vehicle to the maximum gross weight
permitted by this act or by the terms of any special
permit in the possession of such operator and issued
under the provisions of Section Thirteen hereof

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 545
(which excess load, when unloaded , shall be at the
sole risk of the owner ) or , at the election of the
operator , said officer shall permit said operator to
move such vehicle , with its load, to the nearest in-
corporated town or the nearest court having juris-
diction, at which place said excess load shall be un-
loaded . The refusal of any such operator to permit
his motor -truck , semi -trailer truck or trailer to be
measured or weighed , or to proceed to a stationary
scales , or to unload the excess load , shall constitute
violations of this act .
(Enforcement . ) Section Seventeen . Any peace
officer , including sheriffs and their deputies , con-
stables and their deputies , police officers and mar-
shals of cities or incorporated towns , county police
or patrols , and special officers appointed by any
agency of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the
enforcement of its laws relating to motor vehicles ,
now existing or hereafter enacted , shall be author-
ized , and it is hereby made the duty of each of them ,
to enforce the provisions of this act and to make
arrests for any violation or violations thereof , and
for violations of any other law relating to motor
vehicles , without warrant if the offense be com-
mitted in his presence , and with warrant or sum-
mons if he does not observe the commission of the
offense . When in pursuit of an offender for any
offense committee within his jurisdiction , any such
officer may follow and effect an arrest beyond the
limits of his jurisdiction . If the arrest be made
without warrant , the accused may elect to be im-
mediately taken before the nearest court having
jurisdiction , whereupon it shall be the duty of the
officer to so take him . If the accused elect not to be
so taken , then it shall be the duty of the officer to
require of the accused a bail -bond in a sum not less
than Five hundred dollars , conditioned that the
*Acts- 18

546 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
accused binds himself to appear in the court of
jurisdiction at the time fixed in the bond , not how-
ever in any case later than six days from the day
of arrest . In case the arrested person fails to ap-
pear on the day fixed , the bond shall be forfeited in
the manner as is provided for the forfeiture of
bonds in other cases . No officer shall be permitted
to take a cash bond . The officer making the arrest
and taking the bond shall report the same to the
court having jurisdiction within eighteen hours after
taking such bond .
Section Eighteen . In addition to all other enforce-
ment agencies , the Commission and the State Tax
Commission are hereby authorized to issue commis-
sions to their employees , or any of them , as high-
way patrols , and such employees , when so commis-
sioned , shall have all the powers of peace officers in
respect of the enforcement of this act , and of all the
laws relating to motor vehicles , and particularly the
powers hereinabove in Section Seventeen granted to
other officers .
(Scales . ) Section Nineteen . The Commission is
authorized to designate , furnish instructions to , pre-
scribe rules and regulations for the conduct of , and
to supervise official stations for determining the
weight of motor vehicles at such points as to it may
seem necessary , but at not less than one point in
each county , which stations shall comply with the
requirements of the Commission , and shall be avail-
able for the use of all officers in the enforcement of
this act . Said Commission is authorized to pur-
chase portable scales suitable for weighing motor
vehicles , and to place said scales in the charge of
any officers authorized to enforce this law . The
expenses of said weighing stations and the cost of
purchasing and maintaining said scales shall be

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 547
paid out of any funds made available for the use
of the Commission .
( Rewards . ) Section Twenty . Any of said officers ,
other than said special officers or employees of the
Commission or State Tax Commission , making any
arrest of any person for any violation of this Act ,
shall upon final conviction of the defendant receive
a fee of Five dollars , to be taxed as costs and recov-
ered of the defendant as other costs .
Section Twenty - One . All fines and bonds for
forfeitures collected under the provisions of this
act shall be credited to the Commission and dis-
bursed by it for the enforcement of this act.
Section Twenty -Two . It is hereby declared to be
the public policy of this State that heavy motor-
trucks , alone or in combination with other vehicles ,
increase the cost of highway construction and main-
tenance , interfere with and limit the use of the high-
ways for normal traffic thereon , and endanger the
safety and lives of the traveling public , and that
the regulations embodied in the foregoing act are
necessary to achieve economy in highway costs , and
to permit the highways to be used freely and safely
by the traveling public . The provisions of this act
are severable , and if any of its provisions shall be .
held unconstitutional or void , the remaining provi-
sions shall continue in full force and effect , it being
the legislative intent that this act would have been
adopted had such unconstitutional or void provi-
sions not been included therein .
Section Twenty - Three . All laws , or parts of
laws , in conflict with the provisions of this act , to
the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .
Approved March 11 , 1932 .
