be "Are you in favor of the organization and gov-
ernment of the city of (naming the city
in which said vote is to be taken ) under the pro-
visions of an act entitled ' An Act to further amend
an act entitled ' An Act for the government of cities

596 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of second class in the Commonwealth of Kentucky "
which was approved March 19th , 1894 , and there-
after in due course became a law and as same has
since been amended , all of which said act and amend-
ments now appear as Article 3 of Chapter 89 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , which was adopted by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky at the
session of 1932 ? ' "'
It shall be the duty of the county clerk to give to
the sheriff of the county , or to such officer as may be
appointed to hold said election , a certified copy of
the order of the county court as it appears on the
order book , within five days after such order is
made ; and it shall be the duty of said sheriff or
other such officer to have said order published in
some daily or weekly newspaper published or circu-
lated in said county for at least two weeks before
the election and also to advertise the same by printed
or written notices posted in conspicuous places in
said city for the same length of time . If there is no
daily or weekly newspaper published in said city
or if the proprietor of said paper refuses to publish
such notices , the printed or written hand bills pro-
vided for shall be sufficient notice . The sheriff or
other such officer shall have the advertisement and
notices herein provided for posted as herein re-
quired within seven days after he receives the order
of the county court aforesaid . All elections pro-
vided for in this act , except the primary elections ,
shall be held on the regular election day by the
regular election officers .
The canvassing board having authority to canvass
the election returns for elections held within said
city and county shall canvass the returns of said
election and shall correctly certify the result of said
elections showing the total number of votes cast for
and the total number of votes cast against said

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 597
.
proposition , which certificate shall be delivered to
the clerk of the county court , and a copy thereof
delivered to the clerk of the city ; and at the next
regular term of said county court the county judge
shall cause said certificate to be spread on the order
book of said court , and the entry of said certificate in
the order book or copy thereof , shall be prima facie
evidence of the facts therein contained , and shall be
conclusive evidence of said election and the result
thereof .
If a majority of the votes cast at said election
shall be in favor of organizing and governing the
city under the provisions of this act , a certified copy
of the certificate of said canvassing board shall be
spread upon the records of said city at the next
regular meeting of its Board of Commissioners or
City Council . Thereupon said city shall be organ-
ized and governed by the provisions of this act .
But failure on the part of any of said ministerial
officers to perform their duties as required in this
section shall not prevent the organization and gov-
ernment of said city under the provisions hereof .
C.
All the present city officers save those of mayor
and police judge , shall at the expiration of that year
which shall next follow the year in which said elec-
tion is held be ipso facto abolished , if the vote at said
election shall be in favor of the organization and
government of the city under this act . Provided ,
however , that the commissioners or other officers
then or theretofore elected by the people shall con-
tinue in office and administer the government of such
city until the expiration of the terms for which they
were elected ; and provided further that elections
under this act shall not be held in the same year that
the mayor or police judge is elected .

598 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
D.
At the regular city election in the year next fol-
lowing the year in which the election is held , pur-
suant to Section B hereof , there shall be elected by
the qualified voters of said city two commissioners
who shall serve for a period of two years each re-
spectively , and such election for commissioners
shall be held biennally and at each alternate election
for commissioners there shall be elected a mayor or
police judge . Said Commissioners shall serve each
for a term of two years . Said mayor and police
judge shall serve each for a term of four years . The
mayor , police judge and commissioners shall be
nominated and elected from the city at large and as
herein prescribed .
E.
No person shall be elected without first having
been nominated in the manner hereinafter pre-
scribed . On the third Saturday before the day for
the regular election there shall be held a primary
election . Said primary election shall be conducted
by the same officers chosen and acting in the same
manner , with the same rights and duties , as in the
later regular election .
Each applicant for nomination shall , at least ten
days before the day for said primary election , file
with the county clerk a petition signed by at least
one hundred voters and in the following form :
"We , the undersigned , qualified voters of the
city of (Naming it ) , and residing each at
the place indicated opposite his name signed hereto ,
do hereby request that the name of ( nam-
ing the applicant ) , be placed on the ballot as that of
an applicant for nomination for the office of
(naming the office sought ) , at the primary election
to be held in our city on the third Saturday before

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 599
the next regular election . We know the applicant to
be a qualified voter of the city and a man of good
character ; and he is , in our judgment , qualified for
the duties of the office sought by him . "
Names Numbers Streets
Such petition shall be verified by the affidavit of
some person or persons as to the genuineness of the
signatures and addresses of the signers .
No voter shall sign more than one such petition
with reference to each office that is to be filled . In
the event that a person shall sign more than one
petition of applicants for nomination for mayor or
police judge or shall sign the petitions of more than
four applicants for nomination for commissioners ,
then the name of said petitioner shall not be counted
as a valid name on any of said petitions .
Immediately upon the expiration of the time for
filing such petitions the said county clerk shall cause
to be published for three successive days in the
official newspaper of the city, and in such other
papers as he may designate , in proper form , the
names of the persons as they will appear upon the
primary ballots ; and the said clerk shall thereupon
cause the primary ballots to be printed , authenti-
cated with a fac simile of his signature . Provided ,
however , that immediately upon the expiration of
the time of filing such petition , if it should appear
that there are only two applicants for nomination
for mayor or police judge , and only four applicants
so nominated by such petitioners for commissioners ,
such two applicants for mayor or police judge and
such four applicants for commissioners , as the case
may be, shall be thereupon nominated and the
names of such nominees shall not be placed upon

600 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the primary ballot for such primary election , and
no primary election shall be held for nomination for
such offices but the county clerk shall cause the
names of such applicants to be placed on the regular
ballot for the general election .
Upon said ballots the names of said applicants
for each nomination shall be placed in alphabetical
order , with a square at the right of each name , and
above the names of the applicants for nomination
for mayor or police judge , as the case may be , shall
be printed the words " Vote for one " , and above the
names of the applicants for nominations for com-
missioners shall be printed the words " Vote for
two " . The ballots shall contain no party designa-
tion or emblem of any kind , nor any sign indicating
any applicant's political belief or party affiliation .
Having caused said ballots to be printed , the said
county clerk shall cause to be delivered at each poll-
ing place a number of said ballots equal to twice the
number of votes cast in such polling precinct at the
last preceding general municipal election for mayor .
The persons who are qalified to vote at the general
municipal election shall be qualified to vote at such
primary election , and the law applicable to chal-
lenges made at a general municipal election shall be
applicable to challenges made at such primary
election .
At such primary election the two applicants re-
ceiving the highest number of votes for nomination
for mayor or police judge , as the case may be , shall
be thereby nominated ; and the four applicants
receiving the highest number of votes for nomina-
tions for commisioners shall be thereby nominated .
F.
At the regular election held under this act the
names of the nominees who shall be selected by the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 601
primary or who shall be certified by the county clerk ,
as herein provided, shall be placed in alphabetical
order upon ballots differing from those used in the
nomination only in that said regular election ballots
shall contain only the names of the successful nomi-
nees and said ballot shall contain the heading " City
Ballot ". The nomince receiving the greater num-
ber of votes cast for mayor or police judge , and the
two nominees receiving the greater number of votes
cast for commisioners shall be declared elected re-
spectively and certificates of election issued accord-
ingly .
G.
Every fourth year after the election at which a
mayor and two commissioners shall be elected , and
every fourth year after the election at which a police
judge and two commissioners are elected , a primary
election and a regular election shall be held in the
same manner for said offices . Any legal voter not
less than 25 years of age and possessing the other
qualifications prescribed by law shall be eligible to
election to any office under this act .
H.
The mayor shall be elected for a term of four
years beginnig on the first Monday in January fol-
lowing his election , and he shall receive an annual
salary of $4,000.00 ; and said two commissioners
shall be elected for a term of two years beginning
on the first Monday in January following their elec-
tion and each commissioner shall receive an annual
salary of $ 3,600.00 .
I.
The mayor and two commissioners shall consti-
tute a board of commissioners . In this board of

602 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
commissioners shall be vested all the legislative ,
executive and administrative power of the city save
as herein otherwise provided . Two members of the
board of Commissioners shall constitute a quorum ,
but the affirmative vote of at least two members
shall be necessary to the adoption of any motion ,
resolution or ordinance or the making or approval
of any contract or the passage of any measure .
Upon each vote the yea and nays shall be re-
corded and every motion , resolution and ordinance
shall be reduced to writing and read before the vote
is taken thereon . The mayor shall preside at meet-
ings of the board , but he shall have no veto power .
Every resolution , measure or ordinance and all con-
tracts shall be signed by him or by the two commis-
sioners and recorded before it shall take effect . The
board of commissioners shall at the beginning of its
term of office elect by a majority vote one commis-
sioner to act as mayor pro tem . , and the commis-
sioner so chosen shall be vested with all the power
and shall perform all the duties of mayor in the
event of his absence from the city or his inability to
attend the duties of his office .
J.
Every ordinance or resolution ordering the con-
struction or reconstruction of any street or sewer ,
or making or authorizing any contract involving
the expenditure of more than One Thousand Dollars
($1,000.00 ) or granting any franchise or the right
to use or occupy the streets , highways , bridges , or
public places of the city for any except a merely
temporary purpose shall after its introduction and
before its adoption remain on file at least one week
for public inspection in the completed form in which
it shall be put upon its final passage ; and no such
ordinance or resolution shall go into effect until the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 603
expiration of ten days after its passage , except in
case of emergency the public health or safety shall
require that it take immediate effect , which fact
shall be declared by the unanimous vote of the board
of commissioners .
K.
The board of commissioners shall meet at least
once a week for the transaction of its business . It
shall fix by ordinance the times of holding its regular
meetings . Any special meeting may be called by the
mayor or by two commissioners . All meetings shall
be public.
Lt.
The administrative functions of the city shall be
classified under three departments , as follows :
1. Department of Public Affairs ;
2. Department of Public Finance ;
3. Department of Public Works .
The board of commissioners shall determine the
functions of each department , and shall prescribe
the duties of its commissioner and of his employees .
It may assign any employee to one or more depart-
ments or require any employee to perform duties in
two or more departments . It shall make all such
rules and regulations as to the conduct of the
various departments as may be necessary and
proper for the efficient and economic conduct of the
business of the city .
N.
The mayor shall be the commissioner of the de-
partment of public affairs and shall have a general
advisory supervision of the affairs of the other de-
partments . The board of commissioners shall at
its first regular meeting designate by majority vote
one commissioner to have superintendence of the

604 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
department of public finance and one commissoner
to have superintendence of the department of public
works .
0.
The board of commissioners shall also at its first
meeting , or as soon thereafter as may be practicable ,
appoint all such employees as may be necessary for
the proper and efficient conduct of the affairs of the
city .
All such employees shall be agents , not officers of
the city ; and they shall perform such duties and for
such compensation as the board of commissioners
may by ordinance prescribe . Each employee shall
be selected with reference solely to his fitness and
for the good of the public service ( without reference
to his political faith or party affiliation ) and the
board of commissioners shall adopt such rules and
regulations looking to the appointment and dis-
charge of employees as will tend to carry out the
spirit of this provision and lead to the establish-
ment of the merit ( system ) in public service .
P.
The board of commissioners shall at the termina-
tion of each month of the fiscal year cause to be
printed in the official newspaper an itemized state-
ment of all receipts and expenses of the city during
the month .
At the termination of each fiscal year the board
of commissioners shall cause one or more competent
accountants to make a complete examination of the
books and accounts of the city, and shall cause the
result of such examination to be published in the
official newspaper and in pamphlet form , and a copy
of the report in pamphlet form to be given to each
voter who may apply for same at the proper office .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 605
Q.
In case of the death , resignation , or inability of the
mayor, causing a permanent vacancy in said office ,
the mayor pro tem . shall act as mayor and shall
possess all the rights and powers and perform all
the duties of the mayor and receive his salary , under
the official title , however , of mayor pro tem . , until
such vacancy in the office of mayor is filled by an
election ordered by the board of commissioners for
that purpose , at which time the mayor pro tem . , if
his term as commissioner be then unexpired , shall
resume his duties as such commissioner .
In the event that any commissioner shall for more
than thirty days be necessarily absent from the city ,
or for the same period shall be unable from sickness
or other cause to discharge the duties of his office , or
in the event the mayor pro tem . shall be filling the
office of mayor , as contemplated above , for such
period , the board of commissioners may fill such
office temporarily by appointment , and said
appointee shall discharge the duties of the commis-
sioner whose place may thus be temporarily filled
until such commissioner shall return or become fit
for and resume his duties , and any such temporary
appointee shall serve without compensation .
In the event of a permanent vacancy in the board
of commissioners caused by the death , resignation
or inability of any member thereof, or in the event
a permanent vacancy in the office of mayor shall be
filled by the mayor pro tem ., such vacancy shall be
filled by appointment by the other members of the
board until the vacancy shall be filled by the election
of a successor at the next regular election of
municipal officers , as is prescribed by law , and such
temporary appointee shall , for the time he serves as
such , receive the salary of the commissioner whose
place he has been appointed to fill .

606 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
A vacancy shall exist when any elective officer
fails to qualify within ten days after notice of his
election , or dies , ( or resigns ) , or moves his domicile
outside the city , or remains outside the city for a
period of six months , or is convicted of a felony ,
or is judicially declared insane , or is removed from
office in any manner .
R.
In case of misconduct , inability , or wilful neglect
in the performance of the duties of his office , the
mayor or any commissioner may be removed from
office by a unanimous vote of the other members of
the board of commissioners . But no such officer
shall be so removed without having been given the
right to have a full public hearing with representa-
tion by counsel and with witnesses summoned in his
behalf and required to testify . The findings of fact
at such hearing, and the reasons for any such re-
moval , shall be stated in writing and filed as matter
of public record .
S.
If during the ten days next following the passage
of any such ordinance as cannot within said ten
days become effective , a petition signed by a num-
ber of voters equal to at least twenty -five per centum
(25 % ) of the total number of votes cast for both
candidates for mayor at the last preceding regular
election for mayor , stating the residence of each
signer, and verified as to signatures and residences
by the affidavits of some one or more persons , shall
be presented to the board of commissioners , protest-
ing against the passage of such ordinance , such
ordinance shall be suspended from going into effect ,
and shall be reconsidered by the board of commis-
sioners . If such ordinance be not then repealed , the .
board shall submit to the voters of the whole city at
either a special or a regular election according to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 607
law , the following question " Shall the ordinance
(briefly describing it ) , go into effect ?" and if a
majority of the votes cast upon such question be in
the negative the ordinance shall not go into effect ..
But if the majority of the votes cast upon such ques-
tion be in the affirmative the ordinance shall go into
effect as soon as the result is officially ascertained
and declared .
T.
If a petition signed by a number of voters equal
to at least twenty -five per centum ( 25% ) of the total
number of votes cast for both candidates for mayor
at the last preceding regular election for mayor ,
stating the residence of each signer , and verified by
affidavits of some one or more persons as to the
signatures and residences , requesting the board of
commissioners to pass an ordinance therein set
forth- if such petition be presented to the board of
commissioners , and if the ordinance therein re-
quested to be passed be one that the board has a
legal right to pass , then the board shall either pass
such proposed ordinance without alteration , within
ten days after such petition is filed , or submit the
question of its passage to the voters of the city at
the next regular election . At such election the ques-
tion submitted shall be : " Shall the proposed ordi-
nance (briefly describing it ) , be passed ? " If a
majority of the votes cast upon said question be in
the affirmative , the proposed ordinance shall be
thereby passed , and shall become effective , as soon
as the result is officially ascertained and declared .
Any such ordinance shall not be amended or re-
pealed except by the voters at a regular biennial city
election . Any number of proposed ordinances
requested by petition as above provided for may be
voted on at any election .

608 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The board of commissioners may submit the ques-
tion or the repeal or amendment of any such ordi-
nance to the voters at any succeeding regular city
election ; and if a majority of the votes cast on such
question be in favor of the repeal or amendment ,
such ordinance shall be thereby repealed , or
amended , as the case may be.
U:
Whenever a question of passage of a proposed
ordinance , or of the going into effect , or the repeal ,

nances , or the ordinance and amendment , as the case

of the city, and in such other newspapers as the
board of commissioners may direct , before such
election .
V.
The expenses of all primary elections under the
provisions of this act shall be paid by the city .
W.
The mayor and each commissioner shall execute
a bond to the city, upon which an action may be

the penal sum of ten thousand dollars , ( $ 10,000.00 ) ,
conditioned upon the faithful performance of his
official duties , such bonds to be approved by the
county judge, and filed as matters of public record .
X.
Nothing herein shall be construed to apply to the

the Board of Education .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 609
CHAPTER 119 .
AN ACT enabling cities of the third class to acquire , construct ,
operate and maintain electric light , heat and power plant and
to issue revenue bonds of such cities to pay the cost thereof ,
payable solely from the revenues of such works without the
incurrance of indebtedness of said cities .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That cities of the Third Class , whether
organized under the general law or special charter
law , and including such cities where the Commission
form of government has been adopted , are hereby
authorized and empowered to purchase , establish ,
erect , maintain and operate electric light , heat and
power plants , together with extensions and neces-
sary appurtenances thereto , within or without the
corporate limits under the provisions of this act for
the purpose of supplying such city and the inhabi-
tants thereof with electric light , heat and power .
§ 2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of
acquiring any such electric light , heat and power
plant , either by purchase , or construction and
appurtenances or extensions thereto , any such city
may borrow money and issue negotiable bonds , pro-
vided no such bonds shall be issued unless and until
authorized by an ordinance specifying the proposed
undertaking, the amount of bonds to be issued and
the maximum rate of interest such bonds are to bear ,
which shall not be more than six (6 ) per cent per
annum . Such ordinance shall further provide that
the proposed electric light , heat and power plant
and appurtenances which is to be acquired or con-
structed or the proposed extensions thereto are to
be made pursuant to the provisions of this act .
§ 3. One or more electric light , heat and power
plants , owned by one or more persons or corpora-
Acts- 20

610 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tions , may be acquired hereunder as a single enter-
prise , and the common council or board of commis-
sioners of such city shall have authority to agree
with the owner or owners as to the value thereof and
to purchase the same at such value and the Com-
mon Council or Board of Commissioners of such
city shall have the authority to purchase the same
at such value after publishing said agreement of
purchase setting out the price , interest rate , condi-
tion of plant , possible depreciation and repairs in
all the newspapers of general circulation of such
city or in such county in which such city is located .
In case no such newspaper is published in such city
or county , then by posting large posters in ten ( 10 )
or more prominent places in such city , setting out

city for a period of forty-five ( 45 ) days or if no

612 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
common council or board of commissioners shall
deem for the best interests of such city , or any con-
tract for the purchase or acquisition of any light ,
heat or power plant may provide that payment
thereof shall be made in such bonds . In no event
shall any bonds be negotioted on a basis to yield
more than six per cent . Such bonds when issued
shall be payable solely from the revenue funds de-
rived from such light , heat and power plants as pro-
vided in Section 9 , hereof, and shall not constitute an
indebtedness of such city within the meaning of the
constitutional provisions or limitations . It shall be
plainly stated on the face of each bond that same
has been issued under the provisions of this act and
that it does not constitute an indebtedness of such
city within the meaning of any constitutional provi-
sions or limitations .
§ 6. All moneys received from any bonds issued
pursuant hereto shall be applied solely for the pur-
chase , establishment , or erection of such electric
light , heat and power plant and extensions thereto
and necessary appurtenances , provided such moneys
may be used also to advance the payment of the
interest on bonds during the first three years follow-
ing the date of such bonds . And there shall be and
there is hereby created a statutory mortgage lien
upon the electric light , heat and power plant and
appurtenances and extensions so acquired to and in
favor of the holders of said bonds and each of them
and to and in favor of the holders of the coupons of
said bonds .
§ 7. The light , heat and power plant so pur-
chased , acquired or constructed , together with the
extensions and appurtenances , shall remain subject
to such statutory liens until the payment in full of
the principal and interest of the bonds . Any holder
of said bonds or of any of the coupons may , either

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 613
at law or in equity by suit , action , mandamus , or
other proceedings , protect and enforce the statutory
mortgage lien hereby conferred and may by suit ,
action , mandamus , or other proceedings , enforce
and compel performance of all duties required by
this act , including the making and collecting of suffi-
cient rates , the segregation of the income and reve-
nue, and the application thereof .
§ 8. If there be any default in the payment of the
principal or interest of any of said bonds , any court
having jurisdiction of the action may appoint a re-
ceiver to administer said electric light , heat and
power plant on behalf of the city, with power to
charge and collect rates sufficient to provide for the
payment of any bonds or obligations outstanding
against said electric light , heat and power plant and
for the payment of the operating expenses and to
apply the income and revenues in conformity with
this act , and the ordinance referred to in Section 9 ,
hereof .
§ 9. At or before the issuance of such bonds the
common council or board of commissioners of such
city shall, by ordinance , set aside and pledge the
income and revenue of such electric light , heat and
power plant into a separate and special fund to be
used and applied in payment of the cost thereof and
in the maintenance , operation and depreciation
thereof . Said ordinance shall definitely fix and
determine the amount of revenue which shall be
necessary and be so set aside and applied to the pay-
ment of the principal and interest of the bonds , and
the proportion of the balance of such income and
revenues which is to be set aside as a proper and
adequate depreciation account , and the remaining
proportion of such balance shall be set aside for the
reasonable and proper operation and maintenance
thereof . The rates to be charged for the service

614 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
from such electric light , heat and power plant shall
be sufficient to provide for the payment of interest
upon all bonds and to create a sinking fund to pay
the principal thereof as and when the same becomes
due and to provide for the operation and mainte-
nance thereof an adequate depreciation account .
Such rates shall be fixed and revised from time to
time so as to produce these amounts .
§ 10. If any surplus shall be accumulated in the
operating and maintenance fund , which shall be
equal to the cost of maintaining and operating such
electric light , heat and power plant during the re-
mainder of the calendar , operating or fiscal year as
may be provided by the ordinance hereinbefore re-
quired and the cost of maintaining and operating
such electric light , heat and power plant during the
succeeding like calendar , year , operating or fiscal
year, any excess over such amount may be trans-
ferred at any time by the council or board of com-
missioners to the depreciation account to be used
for any improvements , extensions or additions to
such electric light , heat and power plant .
§ 11. The funds accumulating to the deprecia-
tion account shall be expended in balancing depre-
ciation in such electric light , heat and power plant
or in making new constructions , extensions or addi-
tions thereto . Any such accumulations may be in-
vested as the council may designate and if invested ,
the income from such investments shall be carried
into the depreciation account .
12. The reasonable cost and value of any serv-
ice rendered to such city by such electric light , heat
and power plant may be charged against the city
and shall be paid for monthly as the service accrues
from the current fund or proceeds of taxes which
such city is hereby authorized and required to levy
in an amount sufficient for that purpose . Such funds

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 615
so paid shall be accounted for in the same manner
as other revenues of such electric light , heat and
power plant .
§ 13. Such city may issue new bonds for the pur-
pose of providing funds for the payment of any out-
standing bonds , in accordance with the proceedure
described by this act . Such new bonds shall be
secured to the same extent and shall have the same
source of payment as the bonds which shall have
been thereby refunded .
§ 14. Should the common council or board of
commissioners find that the bonds authorized will be
insufficient to accomplish the purpose desired , addi-
tional bonds may be authorized and issued subject
to the procdeure as herein required .
§ 15. Any city acquiring any electric light , heat
or power plant pursuant to the provisions of this
act , may , at the time of the issuing of the bonds for
such acquisition provide for additional bonds for
extensions and permanent improvements to be
placed in escrow and to be negotiated from time to
time as such proceeds for that purpose may be neces-
sary . Such bonds when so negotiated shall have
equal standing with the bonds of the same issue .
16. In like manner , such city acquiring any
electric light , heat and power plant under the pro-
visions hereof, may provide for the extension , addi-
ton or improvement of such public utility by an
additional issue or issues of bonds in the manner
herein provided .
§ 17. Such city in acquiring any electric light ,
heat or power plant under the provisions of this
act, may provide by ordinance any such provisions
and stipulations for the administration of the in-
come and revenues and for the security of the bond-
holders as the common council or board of commis-
soners of such city may be necessary .

616 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 18. The ordinance required by Section 2 and 9
hereof may also set apart bonds hereunder author-
ized , equal to the amount of any secured debt or
charge , subject to which an electric light , heat and
power plant may be purchased or acquired , and
shall set aside for interest and sinking fund from the
balance of the income and revenues of the electric
light , heat and power plant remaining after setting
aside the funds for the payment of principal and
interest of bonds , a sum sufficient to comply with the
requirements of the instrument creating the lien or
securing the charge , or if such instrument does not
make any provision therefor , said ordinance shall
fix and determine the amount which shall set aside
into a securing debt account for interest on such
secured debt or charge and a fixed amount to pay
the principal thereof at maturity . Any surplus
after satisfying the secured debt or charge may be
transferred to the fund for the redemption of the
principal and interest of the bonds issued there-
under. Bonds may be issued hereunder subject to
the provisions hereof in exchange for or in satisfac-
tion of such secured debt or charge , or may be sold
in the manner herein provided and the proceeds
applied in payment of the same at or before
maturity .
$ 19. This act shall be deemed to create an addi-
tional and alternate method for the acquisition of
an electric light , heat and power plant by any city
of the third class and shall not be deemed to include ,
amend , alter , or repeal any other statute . No pro-
ceedings shall be required for the acquisiton of any
electric light , heat and power plant hereunder or the
issuance of bonds hereunder except such as are pre-
scribed by this act , any provision in the general jaws
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or the charter
of any city to the contrary notwithstanding .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 617
§ 20. Any city acquiring or constructing an
electric light , heat and power plant within or with-
out the city under the provisions of this act , shall
by ordinance appoint three commissioners to oper-
ate, manage and control said plant to be known as
the City Utility Commission .
Said Utility Commission shall have absolute and
exclusive control of said electric light , heat and
power plant and its operation and fiscal manage-
ment , regulation of rates and in every other respect
provided that in fixing rates said Commission shall
be governed by the provisions of Section 9 , hereof,
and of any ordinance enacted by the city council or
board of commissioners as provided in Section 9 ,
hereof .
Said Utility Commission shall provide rules , regu-
lations and by-laws for the management of said
plant and out of the revenue of the plant it shall pay
operating expenses , repairs and additions which
may be necessary to be made thereto , and provide
sufficient reserve fund to insure the said plant is kept
in repair and in good working order and to provide
against any emergency which may arise. Said
Commission shall from time to time pay to the city
the surplus revenue derived from the operation of
the said plant as is provided in Section 9 and 10 ,
hereof.
In order that said Commission may be non-
partisan and non -political , no person shall be ap-
pointed a member thereof who has , within the last
two years before his appointment held any city ,
county , state or federal office , or who within said
period has been a member of any committee , or any
political party , or who is related within the third
degree to the mayor or member of the board
of commissioners or member of the board of coun-
cil of such city . Said Utility Commission shall

618 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
not appoint to any subordinate office which they ,
may create any person who is related to any
of the commissioners or to the mayor of said
city or to any member of the board of commis-
sioners or to any member of the board of coun-
cil of said city . No officer or employee of said city
whether holding a paid or unpaid office shall be eligi-
ble to be appointed as a member of said Commission
or to be employed by the Commission in any capac-
ity. The members of such City Utility Commission
shall be citizens , taxpayers , and legal voters of such
city and shall not at the time of the appointment be
indebted to the city either directly or indirectly or
be surety on the official bonds of any officer of said
city .
If at any time during the term of office any mem-
ber of said Commission shall become a candidate .
for or be elected or be appointed to any public office
he shall automatically vacate his membership from
said Commission and another person shall be ap-
pointed in his place .
The city shall pay the cost of securing bonds for
said Commissioners from a surety company , and
said Commissioners shall each execute bond in the
penal sum of $ 10,000.00 conditioned upon the faith-
ful performance of his official duties . Said bonds
shall be approved by the board of Commissioners
or by the Board of Council as the case may be.
An action may be maintained upon any commis-
sioner's bond by any person injured by a violation
of the covenants therein contained .
The Board of Commissioners of the Council of
the city shall fix the salary to be paid to said mem-
bers of said Utility Commission but not to exceed
$300.00 per annum for each Utility Commissioner .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 619
The first Board of Utility Commissioners ap-
pointed under this act shall be appointed for terms
as follows : one for the term of one year ; one for the
term of two years ; one for the term of three years .
Upon the expiration of the first terms , successors
shall be appointed for a term of three years .
§ 21. This act being necessary for and to secure
public health , safety , convenience and welfare of
cities in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , shall be
liberally construed to effectuate the purpose hereof.
§ 22. If any section or provision of this act is
for any reason illegal it is the intention that the
remaining sections and parts thereof shall remain
in full force and effect .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 120 .
AN ACT to repeal an Act relating to Cities and Towns , being
Chapter 738 of the Act of General Assembly , Session 1858 ,
approved February 17 , 1858 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an Act approved February 17 , 1858 ,
being Chapter 738 of the Acts of the General Assem-
bly of 1858 , incorporating the town of New Roe ,
Allen County , Ky. , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed .
All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
This act shall take effect and become a law by its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER , 121 .
AN ACT authorizing , empowering and directing the Governor to
cause an audit, study and survey of all the departments ,

620 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
institutions , boards , commissions and agencies of the Common-
wealth ; empowering him to employ auditors , accountants ,
clerks , bookkeepers , and all other persons necessary to make
said audit , study and survey , defining the scope or character
of same , and appropriating the sum of one hundred and fifty
thousand dollars ( $150,000.00 ) to be used by the Governor in
carrying out the purpose of the Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Governor of this Commonwealth is
hereby authorized , empowered and directed to
cause to be made a full , thorough , comprehensive
and complete audit , study and survey of all the de-
partments , institutions , boards , commissions and
agencies of this Commonwealth . To this end he is
now specifically authorized , empowered and directed
to employ such reputable , qualified and experienced
accounts , auditors , clerks , bookkeepers , and any and
all other persons that may be necessary to carry
out , as expeditiously as possible , the duties imposed
upon him by this Act.
The audit , study and survey herein provided for
shall extend back over such a period of years as will
reveal the true financial condition of the Common-
wealth , and of every Department thereof, and shall
be of such character as to ascertain waste , duplica-
tion of effort , useless offices , faulty or inadequate
systems of bookkeeping , improper budgeting and
any and all other facts that may aid in placing the
Commonwealth upon a sound financial basis .
Upon completion of said audit , study and survey ,
the Governor shall make a detailed report thereof
to the General Assembly, together with his recom-
mendations concerning the same .
There is hereby appropriated out of any funds in
the State Treasury not otherwise appropriated , the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 621
sum of One Hundred and Fifty Thousand Dollars
($150,000.00 ) to be used by the Governor in per-
forming the duties required by this Act . Said funds
shall be paid out of the State Treasury on warrants
drawn by the State Auditor upon proper certifica-
tion by the Governor .
Whereas , the amount of the outstanding indebted-
ness against the Commonwealth is now unknown ,
but is increasing from year to year , and ,
Whereas, the happiness and prosperity of our
people depend largely upon sound financial and
economic policies which can be definitely outlined
and determined only after the true financial condi-
tion of our Commonwealth is revealed ,
Now Therefore , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist , and this Act shall become effective imme-
diately upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor .
Approved February 5, 1932 .
CHAPTER 122 .
AN ACT to extend the jurisdiction of notaries public into coun-
ties other than the county of their appointment .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. A notary public appointed for any county
of the state , upon filing in the county clerk's office
in any other adjoining county of the state , his
autograph signature andand a certificate of the
county clerk of the county for which he was ap-
pointed , setting forth the fact of his appointment
and qualification as such notary public , and paying
to said county clerk , where said signature and cer-
tificate are filed , a fee of one dollar , may exercise
all the functions of his office , in the county in which
such autograph signature and certificate are filed

622 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
with the same effect in all respects as if the same
were exercised in the county in which he resides
and for which he was appointed . The county clerk
of a county in whose office any notary public has
so filed his autograph signature and such certificate
shall , when so requested , subjoin to any certificate
of proof or acknowledgment signed by such notary
a certificate under his hand and seal , stating that
such notary public has filed a certificate of his ap-
pointment and qualifications with his autograph
signature in his office , and was at the time of taking
such proof or acknowledgment duly authorized to
take the same ; that he is well acquainted with the
handwriting of such notary public and believes that
the signature to such proof or acknowledgment is
genuine , and thereupon the instrument so proved or
acknowledged and certified shall be entitled to be
read in evidence or to be recorded in any of the coun-
ties of this state in respect to which a certificate of
a county clerk may be necessary for either purpose .
§ 2. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 123 .
AN ACT to make June 3rd " Confederate Memorial Day," a legal
holiday .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the 3rd day of June of the year 1932 and
the 3rd day of June of each year thereafter is
hereby declared a legal holiday , to be known as
" Confederate Memorial Day . " and the same shall
be recognized , classed and treated as other legal
holidays under the laws of this state .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 124.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 4619 and 4625 , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the appointment
and powers of the state inspector and examiner and his assist-
ants , providing compensation therefor, and fixing penalties for
violation of this act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4619 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby amended
and re-enacted so that , as amended and re-enacted ,
said section shall read as follows :
The governor shall, by and with the advice and
consent of the senate , appoint on the first Monday
in January , for a period of four years , an officer to
be known as the state inspector and examiner . The
person so appointed shall be at least thirty years
of age and possess all the qualifications of an
elector . He shall hold his office for four years from
the date of his appointment , and until his successor
is elected and qualified . He shall receive an annual
salary of three thousand dollars ( $ 3,000.00 ) , payable
in the same manner as the salary of the governor ,
and shall also receive his necessary traveling ex-
penses when on business connected with his office .
His expenses shall be paid upon the warrant of the
auditor , when he has filed with the auditor an item-
ized statement of said expenses , subscribed and
sworn to by him and approved by the Governor .
The state inspector and examiner may , with the
approval of the governor , employ such assistants
and stenographers to assist in the performance of
the duties required of him , under the law , as are
necessary . The compensation of such assistants
and stenographers shall be fixed in advance , by
agreement between the state inspector and exam-

624 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
iner , the governor and such assistants and stenog-
raphers .
In addition to the salary and compensation herein
authorized to be paid , the state inspector and exam-
iner and the governor may allow to such assistants
and stenographers employed hereunder their actual
necessary traveling expenses while engaged in the
service of the state . Such compensation and ex-
penses herein provided shall be paid upon the war-
rant of the auditor when there has been filed with
him an itemized statement of such salary and ex-
penses , subscribed and sworn to by such assistants
and stenographers , and approved by the state in-
spector and examiner and also approved by the gov-
ernor .
That Section 4625 , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby amended
and re-enacted so that, as amended and re- enacted ,
said section shall read as follows :
The state inspector and examiner and his assist-
ants shall , at all times , have access to the papers ,
books and records of any of the asylums , prisons ,
feeble -minded and eleemosynary institutions , public
works and offices that he is authorized to investi-
gate , and they shall have power to issue process
and compel the attendance of witnesses before them ,
and to administer oaths to and compel witnesses
to testify in any of the investigations the state in-
spector and examiner is authorized to make ; and ,
upon the failure of any witness to attend or testify ,
without legal excuse , he shall be deemed guilty of a
misdemeanor , and , upon conviction , fined not ex-
ceeding twenty-five dollars ( $25.00 ) for each offense .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 625
CHAPTER 125.
AN ACT to repeal Section 2554a -16 , Ky. Stats ., giving peace
officers five dollars for making arrests of persons for violating
the law prohibiting the sale of intoxicating liquors .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 2554a -16 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , 1930 Edition , same being Section 16 of Chapter
33 of the Acts of March 22 , 1922 , which section reads
as follows :
"Any peace officer making an arrest of any per-
son for any violation of this act shall, upon final
conviction of defendant , receive a fee of five dollars
to be taxed as costs , and if not paid as costs then
same shall be allowed by the fiscal court of the
county and paid out of the county funds . "
Be and the same is hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 126 .
AN ACT relating to jailers in counties containing cities of the
second class with a population of less than 70,000 and provid-
ing for the appointment of deputy jailers , guards and turnkeys
and regulating the salaries and pay of said jailers , deputy jail-
ers , guards and turnkeys.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in counties having a population of less
than 70,000 and containing cities of the second class ,
the jailer shall be paid from the fees now provided
by law for his services annually not exceeding $ 5 ,-
000.00 , and the number of his deputies , guards and
turnkeys and the salaries allowed to each shall be
fixed by the Fiscal Court ; but the annual salary of
the chief deputy shall be not more than $2,400.00 nor

626 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
less than $ 1,800.00 ; nor shall the annual salary of
the other deputies , guards or turnkeys be more than
$1,800.00 or less than $ 1,200.00 each . The order
fixing the salaries and the numbers of deputies ,
guards and turnkeys shall be spread upon the order
book of said court and signed by the presiding officer
thereof . All of the salaries herein provided shall
be paid out of the fees and compensation now al-
lowed to jailers for keeping , handling and dieting
prisoners , and for such other services as by law are
required of said jailers .
§ 2. For the purpose of determining the amount
of official income available for the purpose of pay-
ing the jailer , his deputies , guards and turnkeys , the
net amount of such income for each year from all
sources shall be deemed the income for such office ,
and after the payment of the amounts herein pro-
vided for the jailer , and the amounts that may be
provided by the order of the Fiscal Court for his
deputies , guards and turnkeys , the balance remain-
ing , if any , shall be paid to said counties by said.
respective jailers .
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are to the extent of such conflict hereby re-
pealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 127 .
AN ACT to amend section 28 of Carroll's Criminal Code of Prac-
tice , Baldwin's 1927 edition , and relating to bail in criminal
cases .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 28 of Carroll's Criminal Code
of Practice , Baldwin's 1927 edition , be amended by
adding thereto the following :

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 627
"If the offense charged be a felony and if it be
bailable , the magistrate or the judge of the county
court who issued the warrant may in his discretion
make such indorsement on the warrant fixing the
amount of bail and such indorsement shall likewise
authorize the sheriff , or his deputy or any such con-
stable to take the bail " ; so that said section when
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
"If the offense charged be a misdemeanor , the
person arrested may immediately give bail for ap-
pearing on a day to be named in the bail bond , either
before the magistrate who issued the warrant or the
judge of the county court, for an examination of the
charge , or before the court having jurisdiction to
try the offense ; and an indorsement to that effect ,
stating, the amount of the bail to be given , and that
the bail may be taken by the sheriff of the county
where the arrest is made or where the offense was
committed or by any constable making the arrest
shall be made on the warrant of arrest , and such
indorsement shall authorize the sheriff , or his
deputy or any such constable to take the bail . If the
offense charged be a felony and if it be bailable , the
magistrate or the judge of the county court who
issued the warrant may in his discretion make such
indorsement on the warrant fixing the amount of
the bail and such indorsement shall likewise author-
ize the sheriff , or his deputy or any such constable
to take the bail ." Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 128 .
AN ACT to amend section 36 of Carroll's Code of Criminal Prac-
tice , Baldwin's 1927 edition , relating to arrests .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

628 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 1. That subsection 3 of section 36 of Carroll's
Criminal Code of Practice , Baldwin's 1927 edition ,
be amended and re-enacted so that said section 36
when amended and re-enacted shall read as follows :
A peace officer may make an arrest-
1. In obedience to a warrant of arrest delivered
to him .
2. Without a warrant , when a public offense is
committed in his presence , or when he has reason-
able grounds for believing that the person arrested
has committed a felony .
3. That such peace officer with a warrant of
arrest may cross a county line and proceed as far as
two miles therefrom for the purpose of making the
arrest in the adjoining county or counties ; pro-
vided , that such peace officer , when in the actual
pursuit of an offender , may go so far as may be nec-
essary for the purpose of making such arrest .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 129 .
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Sections 4130 , 4135 , 4143 ,
4147 and 4148 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition ,
which sections relate to revenue and taxation , and repealing
all acts , parts of acts , and laws in conflict with this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4130 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby
repealed and re-enacted , so that said section when
thus repealed and re-enacted shall read as follows :
" The sheriff or collector shall , on or before the
first day of June next succeeding his election , and
on or before the said day annually thereafter , enter
into bonds with surety for the faithful performance

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 629
of his duties as revenue collector . A quietus by the
auditor of public accounts , and from the fiscal court
of this county for the preceeding year following
the first one shall be produced by each sheriff or col-
lector of the county court on or before that day , and
no tax-book shall be delivered to the sheriff or col-
lector after the first year of his term who shall fail
to exhibit such quietus on or before that date . He
may execute bond at any time after he qualifies ,
up to and including the first day of June succeeding
his election , and it shall be the duty of the judge of
the county court to hold a court at any time the
sheriff may request for that purpose.
The county judge shall judge of the sufficiency of
the surety, and in no case shall sureties be taken
who are not jointly worth , subject to execution after
the payment of all their debts and liabilities , a sum
equal to the aggregate amount of money which may
probably be received by the sheriff or collector dur-
ing the year succeeding the execution of the bond .
The Commonwealth , the county and taxing dis-
trict shall have a lien from the date the sheriff
begins to act upon the real estate of the sheriff
therein secured or afterward acquired by him , which
shall not be discharged until the whole amount of
money collected by the sheriff or collector , or for
which he may be liable to them respectively , shall
have been paid , and the same lien shall exist upon
the real estate of a usurper of the office of sheriff
or collector , or a de facto sheriff or collecter , or any
person who may act as sheriff or collector , provided ,
however , that if in any county the tax records are
destroyed by fire , lost , stolen , or mutilated , so as
to require a reassessment of the property in any
county or recertification of the tax bills , that the
sheriff in such counties shall have five months from

630 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the time he receives said re -certified tax bills within
which to make the settlements with the fiscal court
and auditor as herein provided and to execute the
bond referred to in this section , and to receive his
quietus from the auditor of public accounts and
from the fiscal court of his county for the preceed-
ing year .
§ 2. That section 4135 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed and re -enacted , so that said section when
thus repealed and re -enacted shall read as follows :
The outgoing sheriff , as soon as his successor has
been qualified and inducted into office and his official
bond approved , shall immediately vacate his office ,
deliver to his successor all books , papers , records
and other property held by virtue of his office and
shall make a full and complete settlement of his
accounts as sheriff . Except that the outgoing
sheriff upon the execution , as herein provided , a new
revenue bond , shall keep in his possession all unpaid
tax bills and shall collect and account for same as
provided by law , and shall have until the first day of
June after his term of office has expired to make his
settlement with the auditor of public accounts and
the fiscal court of his county and to receive his
quietus , and immediately thereafter he shall deliver
these records to his successor . On the failure of
any outgoing sheriff for ten days to comply with
the provisions of this section , he shall be deemed
guilty of a misdemeanor and , on conviction , be fined
in a sum not less than fifty dollars ( $ 50 ) nor more
than five hundred dollars ( $500 ) and be liable on
his bond for any default . Provided , that the income
of the outgoing sheriff arising for commissions
for collection of taxes , shall be treated as a part of
the income of the sheriff for the preceeding year .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 631
3. That Section 4143 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby
repealed and re -enacted , so that said section when
thus repealed and re -enacted shall read as follows :
The sheriff or collector of revenue in each county
of this Commonwealth shall on or before the 10th
day of each month report under oath to the Auditor
of Public Accounts the amount of all taxes he has
collected for the preceding month , and shall , on or
before the making of such report , pay into the State
Treasury all taxes which he has collected for the
State for said preceding month . Any sheriff or
collecting officer who shall fail to report as herein
required , shall be liable to indictment in the Frank-
lin Circuit Court , and upon conviction shall be fined
not less than one hundred dollars ($ 100 ) nor more
than five hundred dollars ( $ 500 ) , for each offense ,
and it shall be the duty of the Auditor of Public
Accounts to report to the grand jury of Franklin
county at each term of said court any such sheriff or
collector failing to report as herein required . Any
sheriff or collector failing to pay over any taxes
collected by him and due the state as hereinabove
set out , shall be required by the Auditor of Public
Accounts to pay a penalty of six per centum ( 6 % ) ,
and interest at the rate of six per centum (6% )
on such taxes , and the Auditor of Public Accounts ,
in his settlement with the sheriff or collector , shall
charge him with the penalties accruing under the
provisions of this Act.
§ 4. That Section 4147 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition, be and the same is hereby
repealed and re- enacted , so that section when thus
repealed and re -enacted shall read as follows :
The sheriff or collector of the state and county
revenue of each county of this Commonwealth , shall ,

632 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
on or before the 10th day of each month , in each
year , report under oath to the county court of his
county the amount of state and county taxes he has
collected during the month preceding , together with
all fines , forfeitures or money , or any other account
that shall have been received or collected by him for
said preceding month , showing in said report the
amount collected for and belonging to each particu-
lar fund , for which such revenue or money may be
intended , and the disposition of such revenue or
money collected by him . The sheriff or collecting
officer shall be required by the fiscal court to pay a
penalty of six per centum ( 6 % ) on all taxes or other
moneys collected by him and unpaid by him to the
county treasurer by the 10th day of each month fol-
lowing the month in which such collection was made .
The reports herein referred to shall be filed and
recorded in separate books furnished by the county
court for that purpose , which shall be open for
inspection in the office of the county clerk . Any
sheriff or collector who shall fail to report as herein
required shall be liable to indictment in the county
of his residence , and fined not less than one hundred
dollars ($ 100 ) nor more than five hundred dollars
($500 ) .
§ 5. That Section 4148 , of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby
repealed and re -enacted so that said section when
thus repealed and re-enacted shall read as follows :
All state , county and district taxes , except as
otherwise specially provided , shall be due and pay-
able on and after the first day of March after the
assessment , and all taxpayers whose taxes are not
paid on or before the last day of February after
the same are due , shall be deemed delinquent , and
such taxes shall bear interest at the rate of six per

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 633
cent . (6% ) per annum from the last day of Febru-
ary after they are due until paid , and any person
or persons failing to pay their taxes on or by the
last day of February after they are due , shall pay a
penalty of six per centum ( 6 % ) additional on the
taxes due and unpaid . Providing any taxpayer who
pays his or her or its state , county of district taxes
on or before November 1 , after same becomes due
in any year shall be entitled to 2 % discount thereon ,
and the collector , in case of all such payments will
allow such reduction and give a receipt in full to the
taxpayer . The sheriff or collector whose duty it is
to receive or collect the taxes shall collect the inter-
est and penalty and account for the same in the
same way in which they are required to collect and
account for the taxes .
6. If any section of this Act shall be held to be
invalid or unconstitutional , such fact shall not affect
any other section of this Act , it being the intention
of the General Assembly in enacting this act to
enact each section separately ; and if any proviso or
exception contained in any section of this act shall
be held invalid or unconstitutional , such fact shall
not affect the remaining portion of such section , it
being the intention of the General Assembly to enact
each section of this Act and each proviso and each
exception thereto separately .
7. All acts and laws in conflict herewith , to the
extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 130 .
AN ACT to amend Section One Thousand , Seven Hundred and
Thirty , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , providing for the fees of
jailers in the counties of this Commonwealth .

634 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section One Thousand , Seven Hundred and
Thirty , Chapter 47 , Kentucky Statutes , be , and the
same is, hereby amended by striking from Sub-
section 4 after the word " Court , " " One ( $ 1.00 )
Dollar " and inserting in lieu thereof " Seventy-
Five ( .75 ) Cents " ; and said section when so
amended shall read as follows :
" 1730. Fees allowed to jailers . The fees of the
jailers shall be as follows :
For attending County and Quarterly Courts , to
be paid out of the County levy, per day , not
exceeding Two Dollars ( $ 2.00 ) .
For furnishing fuel and lights to County and
Quarterly Courts , a reasonable compensation , not
exceeding two dollars ( $ 2.00 ) per day , to be paid
out of the County levy .
For putting a prisoner in irons , for an offense
other than a felony , or contempt besides the cost of
irons , to be paid out of County levy , fifty cents ( 50c ) .
For keeping and dieting prisoners in jail when
confined for an offense other than a felony or con-
tempt of court seventy -five ( 75c ) cents per day , to
be paid out of the county levy , unless confined for
a breach of the by-laws or ordinances of a city or
town, or for the violation of a statute where the city
or town gets the benefit of the fine ; in that case to
be paid by such city or town : Provided , that in
counties having within their boundaries cities of
the first class , the charge for keeping and dieting
prisoners in jail when confined for an offense other
than a felony or contempt of court, shall be one
dollar ( $ 1.00 ) per day , to be paid out of the county
levy, unless confined for a breach of the by -laws or
ordinances of a city or town , or for the violation of

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 635
a statute where the city or town gets the benefit of
the fine ; in that case to be paid by such city or town .
For imprisoning and releasing a prisoner charged
with a misdemeanor , sixty cents ( 60c ) , to be paid out
of the county levy, unless confined for a breach of the
by-laws or ordinances of a city or town , or for viola-
tion of statute , where the city or town gets the
benefit of the fine ; in that case , to be paid by such
city or town . And for imprisoning and releasing a
prisoner charged with a felony, sixty cents ( 60c ) , to
be paid out of the state treasury . For all other
services performed by him the same fees as paid
sheriffs ."
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 131 .
AN ACT to amend section 1072 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 edition .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section 1072 of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 edition, be amended by adding the
words : " Provided , however , that said salaries shall
be fixed not later than the first day of May in the
year in which said officers are nominated and
elected ," so that said section when so amended will
read as follows : " The county judge , clerk of the
fiscal court and county attorney shall receive an
annual salary , to be fixed at a reasonable amount by
the fiscal court , and paid in monthly installments by
the county ; provided, however , that said salaries
shall be fixed not later than the first Monday in May
in the year in which said officers are nominated and
elected ."
Approved February 24 , 1932 .

636 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 132 .
AN ACT to amend Section 4168k, Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition , Supplement 1931 , the same being Section 11 of Chap-
ter 150 , of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1930 , approved
March 15 , 1930 , and to fix the fees of County Clerks for record-
ing Certificates of Transfer and Releases of Tax Claims evi-
denced by Certificates of Transfer and repealing laws in con-
flict herewith to the extent of such conflict .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4168k, Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's Edition 1930 , Supplement 1931 , being Section
11 of Chapter 150 of the Acts of the General Assem-
bly of 1930 , relating to the transfer of tax claims
held by the Commonwealth of Kentucky or any
county , municipality or taxing district thereof , be
amended by striking out the words " twenty-five
cents " in said Section and inserting in lieu thereof ,
the words " twenty-five cents " and adding thereto
the words : " All Acts or parts of Acts in conflict
with this Act are hereby repealed to the extent of
such conflict " so that when amended said Section
will read as follows :
The County Court Clerk shall be entitled to
charge a fee of twenty-five cents and no more for
recording the hereinabove certificate of transfer on
the Record of Encumbrances and County Court
Clerk shall be entitled to charge a fee of twenty-five
cents and no more for each Release on the Record of
Encumbrances of the hereinabove certificate of
transfer , or for each deed of release of said cer-
tificate of transfer .
Section 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflict
herewith are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 637
CHAPTER 133 .
AN ACT to create the Office of District Detective , to the Com-
monwealth's Attorney , in certain Judicial Districts , composed
of two counties , and having a population of 100,000 , or more ,
according to and as established by the 1930 Federal Census ;
providing the method of appointment , his compensation and
prescribing his duties and tenure of office .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in all Judicial Districts , composed of
two counties and having a population of 100,000 or
more , according to the Federal Census of 1930 ,
there is hereby created , the office of District Detec-
tive , to the Commonwealth's Attorney . Said Dis-
trict Detective shall be appointed by the Common-
wealth's Attorney for a term of four years , subject
however , to be removed at any time , by the Com-
monwealth's Attorney , without notice or hearing.
§ 2. It shall be the duty of said District Detec-
tive , to assist the Commonwealth's Attorney in the
preparation of all criminal cases in the courts of
said district , and to make investigations of facts
and circumstances surrounding and violation of the
criminal statutes and to report the same to the
Commonwealth's Attorney for such action as said
attorney may deem necessary . Said detective shall
perform such other duties as the said attorney shall ,
from time to time , designate and shall make his
reports in conformity to the directions of the said
attorney .
§ 3. Said detective , shall have all the powers of
a sheriff and shall make arrests when deemed
necessary to protect the public peace or public wel-
fare and shall be and is empowered to administer
oaths for the purpose of making investigations
concerning the violation of any criminal statute
and may , if deemed necessary , go beyond the county

638 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
lines composing his said District , to make investi-
gations and to perform any duty incumbent upon
him .
§ 4. Said District Detective shall be paid a
salary of not exceeding twenty-four hundred dollars
per year, to be paid monthly by the Fiscal Courts of
the counties composing said district . Said salary to
be paid out of the county levy of each county and
divided equally between said counties . Said Fiscal
Courts shall allow and pay said salary monthly .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 134.
AN ACT amending Sections 3766e -2, Kentucky Statutes , Bald-
win's Kentucky Statutes Service , supplementing Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , 1931 Edition , and 3766e -9, Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , being Section 2, Chapter 131 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1930 , and Section 9 , Chapter 164
of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 , respectively , and
declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 3766e - 2 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's Kentucky Statute Service , supplementing
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1931 Edition , being
Section 2 , Chapter 131 , of the Acts of the General
Assembly of 1930 , be and the same hereby is
amended by repealing said section in its entirety ,
and enacting in lieu thereof the following so that
said section as amended shall read as follows :
Said Commission shall be composed of seven (7)
members to be appointed by the Governor . Each
member of said Commission shall be a citizen and
resident of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
the members of said Commission shall serve for a
term of four ( 4 ) years , and until their successors
are appointed and qualified . The Governor shall

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 639
make said appointments within ten ( 10 ) days from
the effective date of this act . If any vacancy or
vacancies shall occur in said Commission by death ,
resignation , or otherwise , the same shall be filled
for the unexpired term by appointment by the
Governor . Before entering upon the discharge of
their duties the members of said Commission shall
each take and subscribe to the constitutional oath
of office , which oath shall be filed in the office of the
Secretary of State . The members of said Com-
mission shall meet as soon as practicable after their
appointment and qualification and organize by elect-
ing one member of said Commission as Chairman ,
another as Vice Chairman , a Secretary and a
Treasurer , and such other officers , clerical help and
assistants as they deem necessary may be appointed
by said Commission . Said Commission shall also
adopt by-laws to govern its procedure .
§ 2. That Section 3766e -9 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , being Section 9 , Chapter 164
of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 , be
and the same hereby is amended by repealing said
section in its entirety, and enacting in lieu thereof
the following so that said section as amended shall
read as follows :
Upon request of the Commission , it shall be the
duty of the Attorney General to act in his official
capacity for and on behalf of the Commission in
any matter or proceeding wherein his services may
be necessary , and to furnish the Commission his
written advice and opinion touching any matter in
which the Commission , as such , may be properly
interested . Provided , however , that the Commis-
sion shall have the right and it hereby is authorized
and empowered to employ such special counsel ,
employees , and assistants as it may deem necessary
to efficiently discharge the duties imposed upon it

640 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
by law , and it hereby is authorized and empowered
to fix the salary or compensation of each person so
employed .
§ 3. If any section , clause , or provision of this
act shall be held to be invalid or unconstitutional ,
such holding shall not affect any other section ,
clause , or provision , it being the intention of the
General Assembly in enacting this act to enact each
section and each provision separately, and if any
provision or section contained in any part of this
act shall be held invalid or unconstitutional , it is
hereby declared that the remaining portion of said
section and of said act would have been enacted
without such invalid or unconstitutional provision .
§ 4. Whereas , on May 25 , 1926 , the President
of the United States approved an act of Congress
providing for the establishment of a National Park
in Kentucky , the conditions precedent of which have
not been fully met or performed by the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and
Whereas, great benefits to the Commonwealth of
Kentucky will result from the prompt compliance
with said conditions precedent and the establish-
ment of a National Park in Kentucky , and in order
that the Governor may select and appoint , without
outside interference or restrictions , a Commission
under the terms of this act for the better promotion
and earlier compliance with and full performance
of said conditions precedent , and all of the premises
considered , it is hereby declared that an emergency
exists and this act shall take effect immediately
upon its passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved February 5, 1932 .
CHAPTER 135 .
AN ACT relating to the conveyance of the Perryville Battlefield
to the United States of America for national cemetery or park
purposes .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 641
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That whenever the United States Congress
enacts a law authorizing the United States of
America to take over and become possessed of the
Perryville Battlefield , now in the hands of the com-
missioner of the Perryville Battlefield , for and on
behalf of the Commonwealth as is provided by the
Acts of 1928 , Chapter 107 , and approved by the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky March
21 , 1928 , the Governor of Kentucky is hereby
authorized and directed to appoint a commissioner
to make and execute , and said commissioner is
hereby authorized and directed to make and execute
for and on behalf of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, a deed conveying fee simple title to the United
States of America for national cemetery or park
purposes , in conformity with the provisions of the
said act of Congress when enacted , seventeen and
three -fourths ( 1734 ) acres of land, more or less , in
Boyle County, Kentucky , now owned and held by
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , together with all
improvements thereon and appurtenances there-
unto belonging ; such conveyances to be made free
of any cost or charge to the United States of
America for the property so conveyed . The said
commissioner is hereby authorized to ascertain by
survey or otherwise the metes and bounds of said
land so conveyed and to include in said deed an
appropriate description of said land ; and also a
clause or proviso to the effect that , if and when the
United States government shall cease to use or
maintain the land thus conveyed as a national ceme-
tery or park , the same if demand to that effect shall
be made by the Governor of Kentucky , shall revert
to the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and complete
jurisdiction over same shall thereupon be again
Acts- 21

642 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
vested in said Commonwealth of Kentucky . The
said deed shall be in such forms as shall be agree-
able to the provisions of said act of Congress , when
enacted , and this act ; and the action of said com-
missioner shall be and constitute the action of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky in the premises .
§ 2. When such conveyance to the United States
of America shall be made for and on behalf of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , exclusive jurisdiction
shall then and thereby be ceded by the Common-
wealth of Kentucky to and vested in the United
States of America over the land so conveyed for
all purposes , except that civil and criminal process
issued by or under the authority of said Common-
wealth may be executed thereon the same as hereto-
fore ; but the jurisdiction hereby ceded shall con-
tinue no longer than the United States of America
shall own said land .
3. When such conveyance shall be made to the
United States of America for and on behalf of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , land so conveyed with
all structures and improvements erected or main-
tained, so long as same may remain the property
of the United States , shall be exempt from all taxes
and assessments imposed or levied by or under the
authority of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or
any county , municipality or district thereof.
4. When said land shall be duly conveyed to
the United States of America agreeable to and for
the purposes named in said act of Congress , exclu-
sive jurisdiction of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
over same shall be then and thereby ceded and shall
vest in the United States of America so long as
same shall be used for national cemetery or park
purposes ; except that civil and criminal process
issued by or under the authority of said Common-
wealth may be executed thereon the same as here-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 643
tofore ; and in like manner as provided in Section 3
hereof, all structures and improvements erected or
