maintained thereon so long as same will remain the
property of the United States , shall be exempt from
all taxes and assessments imposed or levied by or
under the authority of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky or any county , municipality or district .
thereof .
§ 5. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 136 .
AN ACT to authorize the Kentucky State Park Commission to
acquire by the exercise of the right of eminent domain in
condemnation proceedings , or by gift , devise , purchase or any
other lawful means for the transfer of title , the lands described
therein for a public State Park or for public park purposes ,
and providing that said park be named jointly by the State
Commission on Conservation and Development of Virginia and
the Kentucky State Park Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That the lands situated in the State of Ken-
tucky and described in Section 2 of this Act be and
they are hereby specifically designated and set apart
as lands suitable for use as a public State Park and
for public park purposes , and the Kentucky State
Park Commission is hereby expressly authorized
and empowered to acquire title to said lands or any
part thereof for a State Park .
2. The lands in the State of Kentucky herein.
specifically designated and set apart as lands for a
State Park are as follows :
That tract or boundary of land in Cumberland
Mountain area of Kentucky , together with all waters

644 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
included therein or appurtenant thereto , approxi-
mately 3,500 acres in extent and bounded as follows :
Beginning at the mouth of Grassy Fork Creek on
the Virginia and Kentucky State line ; thence north
with top of ridge approximately 1.9 miles and thence
down Pool point to Russell's Fork of the Big Sandy
River ; thence down the River to Little Island
Branch ; thence with back line of Elkhorn City to
mouth of Elkhorn Creek ; thence up Elkhorn Creek
to Water Branch ; thence up Water Branch to the
head thereof ; thence a straight line to the Virginia
and Kentucky State Line and with the Virginia and
Kentucky State Line back to the point of beginning .
§ 3. Subject to the provisions of this Act the
Kentucky State Park Commission is hereby ex-
pressly vested with the power of eminent domain
to condemn all or any part of said lands for use as
a public State Park or for public park purposes , and
to acquire title to all or any part of said lands ,
including dwelling houses , outbuildings , orchards ,
yards , gardens and other improvements on such
lands and all or any right , title , or interest in or
to all or any part of such lands and the improve-
ments thereon by the exercise of the right of
eminent domain in condemnation proceedings or by
gift , devise , purchase , or any other lawful means
for the transfer of title provided , nevertheless , that
in acquiring title to any of said property by con-
demnation proceedings or otherwise , the Kentucky
State Park Commission shall have no authority to
pledge the credit of the State or incur any indebted-
ness on behalf of itself or of the State , or spend
any money of the State for the purpose of buying
all or any part of said land during the next biennial
period , but said Commission may solicit and accept
donations from any person , firm or corporation for
the purpose of purchasing any or all of said lands

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 645
and may purchase any or all of said lands with any
money donated for such purpose at any time in its
discretion and it may solicit and accept any or all of
said lands by gift at any time after this Act becomes
effective .
Title to all or any part of such lands or any inter-
est in lands within the areas described in this Act
which may be acquired by the Kentucky State Park
Commission under authority of provisions of this or
any other Act , shall be held by the Commission in
trust for the State of Kentucky .
§ 4. The lands described herein and established
as a State Park shall be named jointly by the State
Commission on Conservation and Development of
Virginia and the Kentucky State Park Commission .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 137 .
AN ACT to repeal Section 3766e -9, Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
1930 edition , which is Section 9 of Chapter 164 , Acts of 1928 ,
approved by the Governor on February 29 , 1928 , and Section 2
of Senate Bill No. 4 , which was approved by the Governor on
February 5 , 1932 , both relating to the employment of attorneys
for the Kentucky National Park Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 3766e - 9 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 edition , which is Section 9 of Chapter
164 , Acts of 1928 , and Section 2 of Senate Bill No. 4 ,
which was approved by the Governor of the Com-
monwealth on February 5 , 1932 , both relating to the
employment of attorneys for the National Park
Commission, be and the same are hereby repealed
in their entirety.
§ 2. Whereas , the employment of attorneys as
provided for by the foregoing Statute and the fore-

646 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
going Senate Bill is an unnecessary expenditure of
the taxpayers ' money , for the purpose of the prac-
ticing of economy and the saving of said taxpayers '
money an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect upon its passage by the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 138 .
AN ACT prohibiting cattle from running or trespassing on state
and national parks , encampment grounds , scout camps , and
grounds dedicated to religious, educational and recreational
purposes , and providing penalties therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The word " cattle " as used herein is defined
by Section 456 of the Statute .
§ 2. Any person who shall permit any cattle to
run or trespass upon any state and national parks ,
encampment grounds , scout camps , and grounds
dedicated to religious , educational and recreational
purposes in this state , shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor and shall upon conviction be fined not less
than $10.00 nor more than $100.00 for each offense ,
and each head of cattle running and trespassing
upon such property shall be a seperate offense .
§ 3. An emergency is declared to exist and this
act shall become effective upon passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 139 .
AN ACT providing for the practice of Barbering and Beauty
Specialists requiring the registration of Barbers and Appren-
tices and Beauty Specialists and Apprentices, and the super-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 647
vising of Barber Shops and Beauty Shops ; defining the practice
of Barbering , of Barbers and Beauty Specialists and Appren-
tices ; exemptions from the operation of the Act ; qualifications
for the certificate of registration as registered barbers , regis-
tered apprentices and registered Beauty Specialists ; provid-
ing for application for examination , and examination prepara-
tory to practice as registered barbers , registered apprentices
and registered Beauty Specialists ; providing for the estab
lishment of a minimum standard of professional education
of barbers and Beauty Specialists ; providing for the issu-
ance of certificates of registration of barbers , apprentices
and Beauty Specialists ; regulating the admission of Barbers
and Beauty Specialists to practice in this state who have
practiced barbering or beauty culture in another state or
country ; providing for the present practitioners in this
state ; for the display of certificates of registration ; for the
renewal and the restoration of certificates ; providing for
the establishment of a Board of Barbers and Beautician
Examiners to be known as The Kentucky State Board of
Barbers and Beautician Examiners ; for the hearing by said
board of refusal , renewal , or suspension of any certificate of
registration ; for perfecting appeal from the board ; for fees
to be charged for the issuance of registration certificates and
for fee to accompany application for examination ; for penalty
for violation of any provision of this Act ; for compensation
of members of board and method of appointment by Governor ;
for organization of said board and its officers ; duties of said
board ; for delegating to its power to make and publish rules
and regulations for the administration of this Act and the post-
ing of same in the Barber Shops , Barber and Beauty Culture
Schools , and Beauty Shops of this state ; for the keeping of
the records of the board's proceedings and its publicity ; for
method of call meetings of the board ; for the duties of the
board's secretary for compensation of secretary and each mem-
ber of board and limiting salary of any inspector , clerk or
assistant ; for the declaration by courts of unconstitutionality
of any section or part of this Act not affecting any part of this
Act not declared unconstitutional .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Requirement of Registration .
( a ) That after July 1 , 1932 , no person shall

648 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
practice or attempt to practice barbering , or that of
beauty culture , without a certificate of registration
as a registered barber , or beauty specialist issued
pursuant to the provisions of the herein Act , by the
Board of Barber and Beautician Examiners , herein-
after established . This Act shall not apply to any
barber or beautician operating in any town or city
of the fifth and sixth class .
(b ) After July 1 , 1932 , no person shall serve or
attempt to serve as an apprentice under a registered
barber or beauty specialist without a certificate of
registration as a registered apprentice issued by the
Board .
( c ) After July 1 , 1932 , it shall be unlawful to
operate a Barber Shop , or Beauty Shop unless it is
at all times under the direct supervision and man-
agement of a registered Barber or Beauty Special-
ist.
§ 2. Practice Defined .
Any one or combination of the following practices
(when done upon the upper part of the human
body for cosmetic purposes and not for the treat-
ment of diseases or physical or mental ailments and
when done for payment either directly or indirectly ,
or without payment for the public generally ) con-
stitutes the practice of barbering , or beauty culture .
( a ) Shaving or trimming the beard or cutting
the hair ;
(b ) Giving facial and scalp massage or treat-
ments with oils , creams , lotions or other prepara-
tions either by hand or mechanical appliances
(c) Singeing , shampooing or dyeing the hair or
applying hair tonics ;

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 649
(d ) Applying cosmetics preparations , powders ,
oils , clay or lotions to scalp , face , neck or upper part
of the body.
The practice of Beauty Culture shall include each
above named section ; But , Ladies ' Hair Cutting
Only of Section A. Permanent Waving , Marcelling ,
and Finger Waving .
3. Practice of Apprentice .
No registered apprentice may independently prac-
tice barbering or practice as a beauty specialist ,
but he or she may as an apprentice , do any or all of
the acts constituting the practice of barbering , and
that of a beauty specialist , under the immediate
supervision of a registered barber or beauty cultur-
ist , and only one shall be employed in any licensed
shop .
§ 4. Exemptions .
The following persons are exempt from the pro-
visions of this Act , while in the proper discharge of
their professional duties .
(a) Persons authorized by the law of this State
to practice medicine and surgery .
( b) Commissioned medical or surgical officers of
the United States Army, Navy , or Marine Hospital
Service.
( c) Registered nurses .
§ 5. Qualification for Certificate of Registration
as Registered Barber and Registered Beauty Spe-
cialist .
A person who is qualified to receive a certificate
of registration to practice barbering and beauty cul-
ture .
(a ) Who is qualified under section 6 of this Act .
(b) Who is at least eighteen ( 18 ) years of age.
(c ) Who is of good moral character and tem-
porate habits ;

650 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(d ) Who has practiced as a registered appren-
tice for eighteen months under the immediate super-
vision of a registered member of his or her profes-
sion, and
( e ) Who has passed a satisfactory examination
conducted by the Board to determine his or her
fitness to practice barbering or beauty culture .
An applicant for a certificate of registration to
practice as a registered barber or beauty specialist,
who fails to pass a satisfactory examination con-
ducted by the Board must continue to practice as an
apprentice for an additional six ( 6 ) months before
he or she is entitled to take the examination for a
registered barber or beauty specialist .
§ 6. Qualifications for Certificate of Registra-
tion as Registered Apprentice .
A person is qualified to receive a certificate of
registration as a registered apprentice :
(a ) Who is at least sixteen and one-half ( 162 )
years of age ;
(b) Who is of good moral character and temper--
ate habits ;
( c) Who has graduated from an accredited
school of barbering or beauty culture ; and who has
passed a satisfactory examination conducted by the
Board to determine his or her fitness to practice as
a registered apprentice .
An applicant for a certificate of registration to
practice as an apprentice , who fails to pass a satis-
factory examination is required to complete a fur-
ther course of study of not less than five hundred
(500 ) hours , to be completed within three months.
of not more than eight ( 8 ) hours in any working
day in an accredited school of barbering or beauty
culture .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 651
§ 7. Minimum Standard of Professional Educa-
tion .
No school of barbering or beauty culture shall
be approved by the board unless it is accredited and
requires as a prerequisite to graduation , a course of
instruction of not less than seven hundred hours to
be completed in four months of not more than ten
hours a day ; such course of instruction to include
the following subjects : Histology of the hair , skin ,
nails , muscles , and nerves of the face and neck ; ele-
mentary chemistry , relating to sterilization and an-
ticeptics ; diseases of the skin , hair , glands , massag-
ing and manipulating the muscles of the upper
body ; hair cutting , shaving , arranging , dressing,
coloring , bleaching and tinting the hair , and such
other courses as scientific research and progress in
the future may render desirable .
§ 8. Application for Examination .
Each applicant for examination shall make appli-
cation to the board on blank forms prepared and
furnished by the board such application to contain
proof under the applicant's oath of the particular
qualifications of the applicant .
Furnish the board two 3-inch by 5-inch signed
photographs of the applicant , to be presented to the
board when the applicant appears for examination ,
one to accompany the application , and one to be
returned to the applicant . Pay to the board re-
quired fee .
$ 9. Examination .
The board shall conduct examinations of appli-
cant for certificates of registration to practice as a
registered barber , a registered beauty specialist , and
a registered apprentice , not less than four times
each year , at such times and places as the board may
determine .

652 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The examination of applicants for certificates of
registration as registered barbers , registered beauty
specialists , and registered apprentices shall include
both practical demonstration and written and oral
test , and shall embrace the subjects usually taught
in schools of barbering and of beauty culture ap-
proved by the board.
$ 10 . Issuance of the Certificate .
Whenever the provisions of the act herein have
been complied with , the board shall issue a certifi-
cate of registration to the applicant .
§ 11. Non - Residents .
Persons having practiced barbering and beauty
culture in another state or country ;
(a ) A person who is at least eighteen years of
age .
(c) Who is of good moral character and temper-
ate habits ; and
Who has a license or certificate of registration as
a practicing barber or beautician from another state
or country, which has substantially the same re-
quirements for licensing or registering ; or
Who can prove by affidavits that he or she has
practiced as barber or beautician in another state
or country for at least three years prior to making
application in this state ; shall
Upon payment of required fee , be issued a per-
mit to practice as a journeyman barber or beauti-
cian until she or he is called by the board for exam-
ination to determine his or her fitness to receive a
certificate of registration to practice barbering or
beauty culture . Should he or she fail to pass the
required examination he or she shall be allowed to
practice as a journeyman barber or beautician until
he or she is called by the board for the next term of
examinations . Should he or she fail at the third

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 653
examination , he or she must cease to practice bar-
bering or beauty culture in this state .
(a) A person who is at least sixteen and one-
half years of age .
(c ) Who is of good moral character and tem-
perate habits ; and
( e ) Has a certificate of registration as an ap-
prentice in a state or country which has substan-
tially the same requirements as an apprentice as is
approved by this act , shall upon payment of the
required fee , be issued a permit to work as an ap-
prentice until called by the board for examination
to determine his or her fitness to receive a certificate
of registration as an apprentice . Being able to pass
the required examination he or she will be issued
a certificate of registration as a registered appren-
tice , and the time spent in such State or country
shall be credited upon the period of apprenticeship
required by this Act as a qualification to take the
examination as registered barber or registered
beauty specialist .
A person who has practiced as an apprentice in
another State or country which does not have sub-
stantially the same requirements for registration as
an apprentice as required by this Act , and who has
the qualifications as required by section 7 of this
Act , shall be credited with the time so spent as an
apprentice in such other State or country upon
period of apprenticeship required by this Act as a
qualification to take the examination to determine
his or her fitness to receive a certificate of registra-
tion as a member of the barber or beauty culture
profession .
12. Present Practitioner .
Any person, resident of this State , who either for
one year immediately preceding July 1 , 1932 , was
continuously engaged in the practice of barbering or

654 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
that of a beauty specialist at one or more established
places of business in this State , or has , prior to
July 1 , 1932 , graduated from a school of barbering
or beauty culture , shall be granted a certificate of
registration in his or her particular profession ,
without examination , by making application to the
board on or before the first day of July , 1932 , and
paying the required fee of $ 5.00 and shall be given
credit for time previously spent in such practice .
Any person who on and prior to July 1 , 1932 , was
a student in a school of barbering or beaty culture
is qualified upon graduation from such school , to
take the examination for a certificate of registration
to practice as an apprentice , without regard to
whether such a school complied with the standards
for approval specified in Section 7 of this Act .
§ 13. Display of Certificate .
Every holder of a certificate of registration shall
display it in a conspicious place adjacent to or near
his or her work chair .
14 . Renewal and Restoration of Certificates .
Every registered barber or beauty specialict and
every registered apprentice who continues in active
practice or service , shall annually , on or before July
of such year , renew his or her certificate of registra-
tion and pay required fee . Every certificate of
registration which has not been renewed during
the month of July in any year , shall expire on the
1st day of July in that year .
A registered barber or beauty specialist or a reg-
istered apprentice whose certificate of registration
has expired may have his or her certificate restored
immediately upon payment of the required restora-
tion fee .
Any registered barber or registered beauty spe-
cialist who retires from practice of his or her pro-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 655
fession for not more than five years , may renew his
or her certificate of registration upon payment of
the required registration fee .
$ 15 . Refusal and Revocation of Certificate .
The board may either refuse to issue or renew or
may suspend or revoke any certificate of registra-
tion for any one or combination of the following
causes :
(a ) The conviction of a felony shown by a certi-
fied copy of the court of conviction ;
(b) Gross malpractice or gross incompetency ;
( c) Continued practice by a person knowingly
having an infection or contagious disease ;
(d ) Habitual drunkness or habitual addiction to
inorphine , cocaine , or other habit forming drugs ;
(e) The use of any room or place for barbering
or beauty culture which is also used for residential
or business purposes , ( except the sale of hair tonics ,
lotions , creams , cultery, toilet articles , cigars ,
tobacco , confectionery and such commodities as are
used and sold in barber or beauty shops ) , unless a
substantial partition of ceiling height separates the
portion used for residential or business purposes .
The violation of any of the provisions of this
Act , and the failure to enforce sanitary regulations
prescribed for in Section 21 of this Act .
§ 16. Hearing by the Board .
The board may neither refuse to issue , nor refuse
to renew nor suspend , nor revoke any certificate or
registration , however , for any of these causes unless
the person accused has been given at least twenty
days notice in writing of the charge against him or
her and a public hearing by the board.
Upon the hearing of such proceeding the board
may administer oaths and may procure by its
supoenas , the attendance of witnesses and the pro-
duction of relevant books and papers .

656 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
If the applicant is aggrieved by the action of the
board , he or she may appeal to the Franklin Circuit
Court at Frankfort , Kentucky , by filing with the
clerk of said court a certified copy of the written
charge or charges , as heard by the board , said board
being required to make certify copy of said charge
or charges on demand of applicant and when same
is lodged with the clerk of said circuit court at
Frankfort , Kentucky , and advance cost required
paid , within ten days after the board's finding the
appeal will have been perfected and same shall be
docketed and will stand for trial .
$ 17 . Fees .
The fee to be paid by an applicant for an examina-
tion to determine his or her fitness to receive a cer-
tificate of registration to practice barbering or to
practice as a beauty specialist , is Ten ( $ 10 ) Dollars ,
and for the issuance of a certificate Five ( $5 ) Dol-
lars .
The fee to be charged an applicant for an exami-
nation to determine his or her fitness to receive a
certificate of registration to practice as an appren-
tice is Five ( $ 5 ) Dollars , and for the issuance of the
certificate One ( $ 1 ) Dollar .
The fee to be paid for the renewal of a certificate
of registration to practice barbering or as a beauty
beautician , is Three ( $ 3 ) Dollars , and for the
restoration of an expired certificate Five ( $5 ) Dol-
lars .
§ 18. Penalty for Violation of Any of the Provi-
sions of this Act or Any Rule or Regulation Promul-
gated by the Board .
Any person violating any of the provisions of this
act shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and
upon conviction thereof shall be punished by a fine
not to exceed Two Hundred ( $ 200 ) Dollars , and not
less than Twenty -five ( $ 25 ) Dollars .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 657
§ 19. There is hereby created a Board of Bar-
bers and Beauticians Examiners , to be known as the
Kentucky State Board of Barbers and Beautician
Examiners .
The governor will appoint as members of said
board : one master barber, one unorganized master
barber , one journeyman barber , and one beautician .
Each member must be resident of the common-
wealth of Kentucky at the time of his or her appoint-
ment , and must also have practiced the profession
for five years immediately prior to appointment .
Two members of the first board shall serve for
two years from the date of his or her appointment
and qualification and the other two years members
appointed shall serve for four years from the time
of his or her appointment and qualification . At the
expiration of the term of office of the two members
appointed for two years their successors shall be
appointed for a term of four years thereafter . At
the expiration of the term of office of the two mem-
bers whose terms of office expire in four years , their
successors shall be appointed for a term of four
years thereafter . The governor will make such ap-
pointments within thirty days after this act .
Members appointed by the governor to fill vacan-
cies shall be appointed from a list of three submitted
by the one of the four heretofore mentioned organi-
zations not represented on said board by reasons of
said vacancy , and he or she shall hold office during
the unexpired term of their predecessor but this
provision does not preclude reappointment .
§ 20. Officers , Compensation , Etc.
The appointees to the board shall meet within ten
days after their appointment and perfect an organ-
ization by electing a president and secretary and
adopting reasonable rules and regulations for the
transaction of business and for putting into effect.

658 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the provisions of this act . It shall use and adopt
a common seal for authentication of its orders and
records and establish and keep an office at Frankfort
or elsewhere in Kentucky .
The secretary shall keep a record of all proceed-
ings of the board and within ten days , after the close
of every meeting of the board will deposit with the
State Treasurer of Kentucky all money collected and
take his receipt therefore and all money received by
the secretary at any and all times shall be so
deposited . The secretary of the board shall execute
the State of Kentucky a good and sufficient bond ,
approved by the governor , in the sum of Ten
Thousand ( $ 10,000 ) Dollars , to insure the faithful
performance of his or her duties .
A majority of the members of the board present
in meetings legally assembled may perform and
exercise all the duties and powers devolving upon
the board . The secretary of the board shall receive
a salary of not to exceed three thousand ( $3,000 )
dollars per annum , and shall be reimbursed for his
or her necessary traveling expenses incurred and
incident to the actual discharge of his or her duty ,
and each member of the board shall receive as com-
pensation for actual attendance on legally assembled
meetings of the board ten ( $ 10 ) dollars , for each day
such member attends as shown by the record and
shall be reimbursed for necessary traveling expenses
to be paid only from the fund created by fees col-
lected in the administration of this act and subject
to such restrictions as is required of other officers
of the Commonwealth as to traveling expenses .
The board shall have authority to employ such
inspectors , clerks and assistants as it may deem
necessary to carry out the provisions of this act ;
provided however that such employment is with the
knowledge to such person or persons so employed

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 659
that a pro -rata of the fees to be collected will insure
such payment and not be in excess of receipts . The
board shall meet quarterly at the same time and
place as provided in section 9 of this act , and shall
then conduct all examinations of applicants . It will
conduct such other business as that is before the
board to meet for any urgent purpose , a call meet-
ing may be had on notice from the president or
should president refuse call meeting of the board
to meet such urgent necessity then in that event a
rotice from the secretary signed by any two mem-
bers of the board will legally convene said board
for said purpose , but no other business can be trans-
acted at such call meetings except the business speci-
fied in the notice .
§ 21. The board is authorized to make and
promulgate any rule or regulation in its opinion.
necessary , not in conflict with law, for the purpose
of effectively enforcing the provisions of this act ,
and when such rules or regulations is passed and
published by said board, it shall be subject to en-
forcement the same as if incorporated in and made a
part of this act and said rules and regulations shall
include the prescribing of sanitary requirements for
barber shops and beauty shops and barber and
beauty schools .
Any member of the board, or its agents or assist-
ants when authorized in writing by the board , shall
have the authority to enter upon the premises and
into any barber shop or beauty shop or barber
school , or beauty culture school at any reasonable
time during business hours for purpose of official
inspection . A copy of such rules and regulations
so promulgated by the board shall be provided the
owner or manager of such shops and schools , and
by such owner or manager displayed in a conspicu
ous place in each shop or school .

660 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
The board shall keep accurate and complete
record of all its proceedings and especially its pro-
ceedings relating to the issuance , refusal , renewal ,
suspension and revocation of certificates of registra-
tion . Such record shall contain the name , place
of business and residence of each registered barber
and registered apprentice and each registered
beauty specialist , and the date and number of his
or her certificate of registration . This shall be a
public record open to public inspection at all reason-
able times .
§ 22. Partial Unconstitutionality .
If any section or part of this act on review by the
courts of the State should be declared to be in con-
flict with the constitutions of the State , it shall not
effect the validity or force of any other section or
part thereof not declared to be unconstitutional .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 140 .
AN ACT defining plumbing , master plumbers and journeyman
plumber in counties containing cities of the first and second
class of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; providing for exami-
nations and issuing certificates to qualified registered master
plumbers and registered journeyman plumbers and placing
same under the jurisdiction of the State Board of Health of
Kentucky ; and also granting to the State Board of Health of
Kentucky the power to adopt and promulgate a code regulating
the installation of plumbing , house drainage , sewerage and
sewage disposal ; designating the kind of materials and fixtures
including supply pipes , waste , ventilation , soil pipe and sewer-
age to be used in plumbing and drainage and the methods of
their installation ; prescribing the powers and duties of the
State Board of Health in relation to this act and providing
penalties for violation of the provisions of this act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Defining Plumbing , Master Plumber and
Journeyman Plumber : Plumbing is the art of in-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 661
stalling in buildings the pipes for distributing the
water supply, the fixtures for using water , and
drainage pipes for removing waste water and sewer-
age, together with fittings and appurtenances of
various kinds all within or adjacent to the building
and including also the service pipe " which forms the
connection between the shut -off valve and curb line
and the building , and the " house sewer " which con-
veys the waste water and sewage from the building
to the street sewer or other point of disposal , includ-
ing private sewage disposal contraptions or other
methods and also includes storm or rain water pip-
ings , if same is discharged through or connected
with house sewer or house drain . In other to safe-
guard life , health and property , any person , firm or
corporation engaged in or working at the business
of plumbing , either as a master plumber or a jour-
neyman plumber , in counties containing cities of the
First and Second Class of this Commonwealth , shall
hereafter be required to submit evidence that he or
she is qualified and shall be required to register , as
hereinafter provided.
A master plumber is hereby defined to be a person
who assumes responsible charge and direction of
other persons in the installation of plumbing and
drainage and is skilled in the planning , superintend-
ing and the practical installation of plumbing and
drainage .
A journeyman plumber is hereby defined to be a
person who performs the work of installing plumb-
ing and drainage under the direction of master
plumber .
Any person who assumes responsible charge and
direction of other persons in the installation of
plumbing and drainage , and who employs for gain
or reward a person not registered , as provided
herein , to perform and install plumbing and drain-

662 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
age , shall be guilty of violating the provisions of
this Act and shall be punishable under the terms
thereof . Provided , however , the provisions of this
Act shall not apply to anyone who is employed as or
acting as a maintenance man by any person , firm or
corporation , and who does not practice as his prin-
cipal trade of business the occupation of a journey-
man plumber ; and , provided further , the provisions
of this Act shall not apply to person employed by
any water company in the laying , maintenance and
operations of its water mains and other appurte-
nances in the conduct of its business .
§ 2. For the purpose of carrying out the provi-
sions of this Act and for the purpose of enforcing
all of its provisions , the State Board of Health of
Kentucky shall have jurisdiction , with power to
issue and revoke certificates , as herein provided ,
conduct and hold examinations as herein provided ,
to make all the necessary rules and regulations for
conducting the examination of applicants and gov-
erning the method and time for filing applications
for examinations and the time within which an appli-
cant must be examined after his application has
been filed , and shall have the power to adopt all
other reasonable rules and regulations not incon-
sistent herewith , as may be necessary to carry into
effect the objects and purposes of this Act .
For the purpose of conducting the examination or
examinations for persons to qualify as a registered
master plumber or as a registered journeyman
plumber under the provisions of this Act , the State
Board of Health of Kentucky shall appoint as such
examiners for such examination or examinations ,
the following persons . The State Health Officer ,
the State Sanitary Engineer and three other per-
sons , all of whom shall be registered , under the pro-
visions of this Act , either as a master plumber or

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 663
a journeyman plumber . Provided , however , that
one of said persons shall be a registered master
plumber and one of said persons shall bebe a
registered journeyman plumber .
For the purpose of defraying the expense of such
examination or examinations , and other expenses
incident to the operation of this Act , the following
fees shall be paid to the State Board of Health of
Kentucky :
( a ) By an applicant for an examination to de-
termine his fitness to receive a certificate of regis-
tration as a master plumber , twenty -five ( $25.00 )
dollars .
(b) By an applicant for a certificate of registra-
tion as a master plumber, twenty-five ($25.00 )
dollars .
( c ) By an applicant for an examination to de-
termine his fitness to receive his certificate as a
registered journeyman plumber , five ( $ 5.00 ) dollars .
(d ) By an applicant for a certificate of registra-
tion as a journeyman plumber , five ( $ 5.00 ) dollars .
(e ) By an applicant for the annual renewal of
the certificate as a registered master plumber , ten
($10.00 ) dollars .
(f) By an applicant for the annual renewal of
the certificate as a registered journeyman plumber ,
three ( $ 3.00 ) dollars .
All of the above fees must accompany the appli-
cation , and no part of said fees shall be refunded .
An applicant failing to receive a certificate upon
his first application , or an applicant who fails to
pass his first examination , shall be entitled to enter
any examination held within six months from the
date of his failure to procure a certificate , as pro-
vided for in this Act, without the payment of any
additional fee . A

664 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
3. The State Board of Health of Kentucky , for
the purpose of carrying out the provisions of this
Act , shall establish , adopt , promulgate and put into
effect a code regulating the installation of plumbing,
house drainage and sewerage and sewage disposal ,
using as a minimum the basis principles adopted by
the sub -committee on plumbing of the Building Code
Committee of the United States , Department of
Commerce , as shown in the final report of said com-
mittee under date of July 3 , 1923 , with such addi-
tions as local , climatic and other conditions may re-
quire , and also to fix reasonable fees for the pur-
pose of carrying into effect the provisions of this
Act.
§ 4. MATERIALS - Kind That May Be Used.-
The use of all unsafe and defective material in the
work of plumbing and drainage is prohibited . Only
the best known methods of installing of materials
and fixtures , including supply pipes , waste , ventila-
tion , soil pipe and sewerage shall be employed . It
shall be unlawful to use in the work aforesaid light
lead pipe , bends and traps , and all combination lead
and iron or combination lead and brass ferrules ,
bends or traps ; but there shall be employed only the
best known methods of joining lead or iron pipes by
the use of heavy cast brass ferrules properly wiped
with solder to lead .
$ 5 . Persons to Whom Certificates As Registered
Master Plumbers or Registered Journeyman
Plumbers are to be Issued .- The State Board of
Health of Kentucky , upon application of any per-
son who is a citizen of the United States and at least
twenty -one ( 21 ) years of age, shall issue a certifi-
cate as a registered master plumber or a registered
journeyman plumber without examination to any of
the following :

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 665
(a ) Any person , firm or corporation who fur-
nishes the proper evidence that he or she were
known as a master plumber and practiced as a
master plumber as his or her principal vocation
within counties contaning cities of the First and
Second Class of the Commonwealth of Kentucky at
the time that this Act became a law , for at least five
(5 ) years prior thereto and who applies for such
certificate within six ( 6 ) months after the passage
of this Act and its approval by the Governor .
(b ) Any person who furnishes the proper evi-
dence that he or she were known as Journeyman
plumbers and practiced as journeyman as his or her
principal vocation within counties containing cities
of the First and Second Class of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky at the time that this Act became a law ,
for at least six ( 6 ) years prior thereto and who
applies for such certificate within six (6 ) months
after the passage of this Act and its approval by the
Governor .
(c ) Any person who furnishes the proper evi-
dence that he or she now holds a license as a journey-
man plumber , or who is a registered journeyman
plumber and has passed the examination of any
plumbing board of examiners for journeyman
plumbers provided for under the laws of this State ,
and who applies for a certificate as a journeyman
plumber within six ( 6 ) months after the passage of
this Act and its approval by the Governor .
( d ) Any applicant not possessing the qualifica-
tions as hereinabove set forth, who desires to be-
come a registered master plumber or a registered
journeyman plumber under the provisions of this
Act , shall satisfactorily pass an examination de-
signed to test applicant's practicability and tech-
nical knowledge of plumbing work , ventilation ,
water supply , etc.-provided such applicant is a

666 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
citizen of the United States and at least twenty - one
(21 ) years of age .
§ 6. Who May Be Styled as Registered Master
Plumber or Registered Journeyman Plumber .-Any
person having a certificate pursuant to the require-
ments of this Act may be styled or known as a reg-
istered master plumber or a registered journeyman
plumber .
§ 7. No Person Shall Assume the Title of Master
Plumber or Journeyman Plumber Unless Applica-
tion For Certificate Has Been Applied For.- No
person , firm or corporation presumed to have the
right to secure such certificate because of his or her
use of the title " Master Plumber " or " Journeyman
Plumber " prior to the time this Act goes into effect ,
shall assume any title indicating that he or she is a
master plumber or journeyman plumber, or any
words , letters or figures to indicate that the person
using them is a master plumber or a journeyman
plumber, unless he or she shall have qualified and
obtained a certificate of registration as a master
plumber or journeyman plumber , as provided for in
this Act , or stated his or her intention in writing to
the State Board of Health to apply for certificate
within six (6 ) months after the passage of this Act
and its approval by the Governor .
§ 8. A Corporation or a Partnership May En-
gage in the Business of Plumbing .-A corporation
or a partnership or an individual principal may en-
gage in the business of plumbing within counties
containing cities of the First and Second Class in
this State , provided a person or persons connected
with such corporation or partnership or individual
principal in responsible charge of such plumbing
work is or are registered as herein required of
master plumbers , or is or are otherwise authorized
to practice . The same exemption shall apply to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 667
corporations and partnerships as apply to indi-
viduals under this Act .
It shall be the duty of any person who is a
registered master plumber who is in responsible
charge of such plumbing work for the corporation ,
partnership or individual principal , engaged in the
plumbing business , for such person to notify the
State Board of Health when such person severs his
connection with the said corporation , partnership
or individual principal .
9. Any municipality in this Commonwealth in-
cluded under the terms of this Act which shall adopt
the code promulgated by the State Board of Health
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for plumbing
hereinabove set forth through municipal ordinances
regulating the installation of plumbing, shall have
the right to appoint a municipal plumbing inspector
and a deputy plumbing inspector ; said municipal in-
spection and deputy municipal inspectors shall have
concurrent jurisdiction with the county plumbing
inspector within the boundaries of the municipality
in the enforcement of the provisions of this Act ;
provided , however , that at the time of their appoint-
ments the said municipal plumbing inspector and
deputy municipal plumbing inspectors are either
registered master plumbers , under provisions of
this Act , or are registered journeyman plumbers
under any law of this Commonwealth regulating the
registration of Journeyman plumbers or any munici-
pal ordinance passed thereunder . No provisions of
this Act shall deprive cities of the First or Second
Class from collecting its occupational license fees
on either master or journeyman plumbers , or from
the collection of fees for the issuance of permits for
the installation of plumbing fixtures .
§ 10. For the purpose of carrying out the pro-
visions of this Act , the State Board of Health of

668 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Kentucky shall appoint for counties containing
cities of the First and Second Class in this Common-
wealth , a County Plumbing Inspector ; also the State
Board of Health of Kentucky may appoint such
Deputy County Plumbing Inspectors as it may deem
necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act.
Such County Plumbing Inspector and Deputy
County Plumbing Inspector or Inspectors shall have
at least ten ( 10 ) years experience as a journeyman
plumber , or has had five ( 5 ) years experience as a
journeyman plumber , and five ( 5 ) years experience
as a master plumber , or who has had ten ( 10 ) years
experience either as a journeyman plumber and a
master plumber . Provided , however , that such per-
son must have had five ( 5 ) years experience as a
journeyman plumber and who , at the time of his
appointment , is either a registered master plumber
or a registered journeyman plumber , under the pro-
visions of this Act . The salary of said County
Plumbing Inspector and the deputy or deputies shall
be fixed by the State Board of Health at the time of
or immediately after his or their appointment .
§ 11. Penalties for Violating the Provisions of
the Act.-Any person , firm or corporation vio-
lating any of the provisions of this Act , shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and , upon con-
viction , shall be fined not less than ten ( $ 10.00 )
dollars , nor more than one hundred ( $ 100.00 )
dollars , or imprisonment in the county Jail for a
period of not more than ninety ( 90 ) days , or both so
fined and imprisoned , in the discretion of the court
or jury .
§ 12. If any clause , sentence , paragraph , or part
of this Act shall for any reason , be adjudged by any
court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid , said
judgment shall not affect , impair or invalidate the
remainder of said Act , but shall be confined in its

ACTS OF THE General ASSEMBLY 669
operation to the clause , paragraph or part thereof
directly involved in the controversy in which said
judgment shall have been rendered .
$ 13. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
the provisions of this Act are hereby repealed .
Approved February 23 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 141 .
·
AN ACT to amend section one hundred and seventy -two of the
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky so as to
authorize the General Assembly to enact laws exempting real
estate and /or tangible personal property from taxation for
state purposes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That upon the concurrence of three -fifths of
all members elected to each house the yeas and nays
being taken thereon and entered in full on their
respective journals , Section one hundred and seven-
ty -two of the Constitution be , and is , amended and
revised, so that it shall read as follows : All prop-
erty, not exempted from taxation by this Constitu-
tion , shall be assessed for taxation at its fair cash
value , estimated at the price it would bring at a
fair voluntary sale ; but the General Assembly shall
have power to provide by law that real estate and
or tangible personal property may be exempted
from taxation for state purposes . Any officer , or
other person authorized to assess values for taxes ,
who shall commit any wilful error in the perform-
ance of his duties shall be guilty of misfeasance and
upon conviction thereof shall forfeit his office , and
be otherwise punished as provided by law .
2. This amendment shall be submitted to the
voters of the Commonwealth for ratification or re-
jection at the time and in the manner provided for
under Section two hundred and fifty -six of the Con-

670 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
stitution of Kentucky and Section one thousand four
hundred and fifty -nine of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , nineteen hundred and thirty edition .
Approved March 17 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 142 .
AN ACT to repeal Section 4151-2 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of Kentucky of 1928 approved March 3, 1928 , relat-
ing to taxation and revenue as set out in chapter 129 , in said
Acts of 1928 , General Assembly of Kentucky , which section
4151-2 provided for interest and penalty on lands when sold
for taxes , and amending and re -enacting said section 4151-2
and providing for interest and penalty on lands sold for
taxes and providing for redemption thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That section 4151-2 of Chapter 129 , Acts of the
General Assembly of Kentucky of 1928 , approved
March 3rd , 1928 , be and the same is hereby repealed
and amended and re - enacted to read as follows :
Section 4151-2 , land sold for taxes , redemption ,
possession , clerk may collect delinquent tax, duties
of county judge , sheriff , clerk, county attorney .
When real estate is sold for taxes if no one will
bid for and purchase such real estate at the amount
of tax , interest , penalty and cost including the cost
for advertising and the sheriff's or collector's com-
mission it shall be the duties of the sheriff or collec-
tor to purchase the same for the state , county and
taxing district having taxes against the delinquent
for the amount of tax due , interest , penalty, cost
and commission thereon and shall make returns to
the county clerk as provided in section 40 of article.
8 , and the clerk shall certify the same to the auditor .
of public accounts and the sheriff or collector shall
have credit for the amount of such tax due State
in the settlement with the auditor of public accounts .
www

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 671
'The owner of such real estate , his representatives ,
heirs or assigns shall have the right to redeem the
same from the State , County and District or any
other purchaser at any time within five years after
the day of sale by paying the purchase money with
interest at the rate of 6 % per annum , and in addi-
tion 2 % penalty upon the total amount of the pur-
chase price amount of all costs . The State , County
and District shall have the right to possession of
land purchased by them at any time after the expira-
tion of the five years allowed for redemption by
giving thirty days ' notice to the owner , and the pur
chaser other than state , county and district shall
have the right to possession of land purchased by
him after the expiration of five years allowed for
redemption by giving six months ' notice to the
owner thereof .
In redemption of lands sold for the county , state
and district for delinquent taxes at any time within
a period five years after the sale or until the revenue
agent under the direction of the auditor of public
accounts assuming charge of the same or otherwise
the county clerk is hereby vested with the authority
to collect such delinquent taxes , interests , costs ,
commission and penalties prescribed by law, and he
shall make report thereof to the auditor of public
accounts , the treasurer of the county and the proper
officers of the district as often as the latter officers
may require , and not less than once every thirty
days , and he shall pay into the state treasury all
monies collected by him due the state ; and the
county treasurer all monies due the county , and all
monies due the district to the proper officer thereof ,
as herein authorized , on the first day of every month
and oftener if required by auditor of public accounts ,
or either of the other officers mentioned . Said re-
port of county clerk, showing the name of each

672 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
owner and each piece of property redeemed , the
year's taxes for which delinquent , the assessed
valuation of same for each year , and the total
amount paid to the state , county and district for
redemption shall be examined by the county court ,
and if found correct , certified to the auditor of pub-
lic accounts ; together with the total sum due the
state as shown on said report less 5 % commission
for the collection which he shall retain for his serv-
ices .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 143.
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections 26 and 27 of Chapter
141 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1893 as amended
by Chapter 78 of the Acts of the General Assembly of 1910 ,
being now sections 2316 and 2317 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes ( Baldwin's Revision of 1930 ) , relating to Landlord and
Tenant and the lien of the Landlord .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 26 of Chapter 141 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1893 , now Section 2316
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ( Baldwin's Revision
of 1930 ) , be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended and re -enacted , so that the same when so
amended and re-enacted shall read as follows :
All valid liens upon the personal property of a
lessee , assignee , or under -tenant , created before the
property was carried upon the leased premises ,
shall prevail against a distress warrant or attach-
ment for rent . If such lien be created whilst the
property is on the leased premises , and on property
upon which the landlord hath a superior lien for
his rent , then to the extent of four ( 4 ) months ' rent ,
whether the same accrued before or after the crea-
tion of the lien , the distress or attachment shall

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 673
have preference , and be first satisfied , provided the
same is sued out in one hundred and twenty ( 120 )
days from the time the rent was due .
§ 2. That Section 27 of Chapter 141 of the Acts
of the General Assembly of 1893 as amended by
Chapter 78 of the Acts of the General Assembly of
1910 , being Section 2317 of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes (Baldwin's Revision of 1930 ) , be and the same
is hereby repealed , amended and re -enacted , so that
the same when so amended and re-enacted shall read
as follows :
A landlord shall have a superior lien on the pro-
duce of the farm or premises rented for farming
purposes and the fixtures , household furniture , and
other personal property of the tenant , or under-
tenant , owned by him after possession is taken
under the lease ; but such lien shall not be for more
than one ( 1 ) year's rent due and to become due , nor
for any rent which has been due for more than
cleven ( 11 ) months , and every other landlord shall
have a superior lien on the fixtures , household fur-
niture , and other personal property of the tenant ,
or under-tenant , from the time possession is taken
under the lease to secure the landlord in the pay-
ment of four ( 4 ) months ' rent, due or to become
due , but such lien shall not be effective for any rent
which is past due for a longer time than the lien is
given . And if any such property be removed openly
from the leased premises , and without fraudulent
intent , and not returned , the landlord shall have a
superior lien on the property so removed for fifteen
( 15 ) days from the date of its removal , and may
enforce his lien against the property wherever
found . Provided , that the provision of this act shall
not apply to or in any manner effect the rights of
landowners who lease lands for coal mining pur-
poses .
Acts- 22

674 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 3. It is hereby declared to be the intent of the
Legislature in enacting this act not to repeal or in-
terfere in any wise or way with Sections 2323 and
2325 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930 Edition .
§ 4. All acts or parts of acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .
Approved March 17 , 1932 .
