CHAPTER 146.
AN ACT to change the twentieth ( 20th ) , thirty -second ( 32nd ) ,
and thirty -seventh ( 37th ) Judicial Districts of Kentucky , and
to provide for the holding of courts in said districts ; creating
a court of continuous session in the Thirty - second Judicial
District and providing the procedure therein .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 677
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Twentieth ( 20th ) Judicial District
of Kentucky shall consist of the counties of Greenup ,
Lewis , and Carter .
§ 2. That the Thirty - second ( 32nd ) Judicial
District of Kentucky shall consist of the County of
Boyd .
§ 3. That the Thirty - seventh ( 37th ) Judicial
District of Kentucky shall consist of the counties of
Lawrence, Elliott , and Morgan .
§ 4. The courts in the 20th , 32nd , and 37th Judi-
cial Districts and in the several counties comprising
the same as herein constituted shall be held at the
following times and places and for the periods
herein set out :
(A) Twentieth ( 20th ) Judicial District , Greenup
County, at Greenup first Monday in January , and
continue twenty -four juridical days ; first Monday
in April and continue for twenty-four juridical
days ; first Monday in July , and continue twenty-
four juridical days ; first Monday in November and
continue thirty-six juridical days .
Lewis County , at Vanceburg, first Monday in
February and continue eighteen juridical days ; first
Monday in May and continue eighteen juridical
days ; the first Monday in September and continue
eighteen juridical days .
Carter County , at Grayson , first Monday in March
and continue twenty -four juridical days ; first Mon-
day in June and continue eighteen juridical days ;
first Monday in October and continue twenty- four
juridical days .
( B ) Thirty -second Judicial District , Boyd Coun-
ty , at Catlettsburg , and it shall be a court of continu-
ous session and subject to all the laws of the Com-
monwealth governing courts of continuous session ,

678 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
in counties having a population of less than One
Hundred and Fifty Thousand .
( C ) Thirty -seventh (37th ) Judicial District ,
Lawrence County , at Louisa , the second Monday in
January , and continue eighteen juridical days ; first
Monday in May and continue eighteen juridical
days ; first Monday in October and continue eighteen
juridical days .
Elliott County , at Sandy Hook , on the second
Monday in February , and continue twelve juridical
days ; first Monday in June and continue twelve
juridical days ; first Monday in November , and con-
tinue twelve juridical days .
Morgan County, at West Liberty, on the first
Monday in March and continue twelve juridical
days ; third Monday in June and continue twelve
juridical days ; third Monday in November , and con-
tinue eighteen juridical days .
§ 5. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with , are hereby repealed .
6. If any part of this Act shall be held uncon-
stitutional , it shall not invalidate or impair the
effect of the remainder thereof .
§ 7. The provisions of this Act shall become
effective on and after January 1st , 1933 .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 147 .
AN ACT to provide for the more efficient and economical admin-
istration of justice in courts of justices of the peace in coun-
ties containing a population in excess of two hundred fifty
thousand ( 250,000 ) , and to reduce the number of magisterial
districts therein , by amending and re -enacting section one
thousand seventy -nine ( 1,079 ) of the Kentucky Statutes ; to
provide for the costs and fees thereof and for the payment of
same to the county ; for certain reports relating thereto ; to fix
the compensation and duties of justices of the peace , con-
stables and recorders therein ; for the appointment and pay-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 679
ment of recorders and assistants , defining their duties and
requiring bonds therefrom , with surety ; defining certain duties
of constables and deputy constables , requiring bonds there-
from , fixing the places of holding such courts and providing
for the equipment and maintenance of such places ; fixing the
filing fees , and providing for rules of practice in said courts
and fixing penalties for the violation by said officers of their
duties .
WHEREAS , in counties containing a population in excess of two
hundred fifty thousand ( 250,000 ) intensive industrial and com-
mercial development and consequent density of population
have combined to produce definitely changed conditions of life ,
by reason of which the demands upon such courts in such
counties are contiuous , voluminous , and exacting , and by
reason of which justices of the peace in such counties have
become primarily judicial officers , as distinguished from the
fiscal character of said officers elsewhere in this State , and
by reason of which the existing system for the administration
of justice within the jurisdiction of such courts is now wholly
inadequate , now therefore :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That this act shall be effective in each
magisterial district in every county of this Common-
wealth , which county contains a population in ex-
cess of two hundred fifty thousand ( 250,000 ) ; Pro-
vided , that the provisions hereof shall not begin to
apply to the office of justice of the peace , or the office
of constable , until the expiration of the term of
office , in each instance , which is current and unex-
pired upon the effective date of this act . At the
regular election for justices of the peace in 1933 ,
such justices shall be elected for the districts here-
inafter provided for .
§ 2. Within each of the said magisterial dis-
tricts , all moneys received or collected as compensa-
tion , cost , expense , for , or otherwise resulting from
or on account of, the performance of the duties or
the exercise of the powers incident to the offices of

680 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the offices of justice of the peace , constable , and
deputy constable , shall become, upon receipt or col-
lection , by or for any person hereafter holding any
of said offices , a part of the general fund of the
county .
§ 3. Each person hereafter elected or appointed
to and thereafter holding the office of justice of the
peace in any of said districts shall be exclusively
compensated for the performance of the duties of
his office by salary , in the sum of four thousand
($4,000.00 ) dollars per annum , which shall be paid ,
in equal monthly installments , out of the general
fund of the county ; Provided , that no justice of the
peace shall receive or be paid a salary exceeding
three hundred thirty -three dollars and thirty-four
cents ($333.34 ) during or for any month , or four
thousand ( $4,000.00 ) during or for any consecutive
twelve ( 12 ) months .
§ 4. In each of the said magisterial districts the
justice of the peace shall employ one clerical assist-
ant to be known as the recorder, whose employment
shall become effective upon the first Monday in
January , 1934 , and shall continue for the period.
during which the justice of the peace making the
appointment holds office , but not beyond the current
term of office of said justice . Each justice of the
peace may employ , not exceeding three clerical
assistants known as deputy recorders. Each
recorder shall be compensated for his services by
salary in the sum of two hundred ( $ 200.00 ) dollars
per month , and each deputy recorder shall be com-
pensated for his services by salary in the sum of one
hundred ( $ 100.00 ) dollars per month , which salaries
shall be paid monthly, out of the general fund of
the county . The said salaries shall be in lieu of all
other compensation . The fiscal court or county com-
mission of the county shall require bonds , with good

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 681
and sufficient sureties , of recorders and deputy
recorders , for the faithful performance of their
duties . The employment of each recorder and each
deputy recorder shall be noted of record by the jus-
tice of the peace by the entry of a suitable order
upon the records of the court , and the employment
of any recorder or deputy recorder may be termi-
nated at any time , for cause , by order of record by
the justice of the peace holding office within the dis-
trict . Any of said employees shall be eligible to
serve in any district within the purview of this Act .
§ 5. It shall be the duty of each recorder , after
executing bond as required in section four (4 )
hereof , to receive for the county the correct amounts
of money which , under the provisions of this act ,
become a part of the general fund of the county .
Each recorder shall keep such records , in the man-
ner and form as prescribed by the fiscal court , as
will enable the recorder , and the fiscal court , to
ascertain from time to time the correct amounts of
money received , or due and not received , for the
general fund of the county . Each recorder , and
cach deputy recorder, shall perform such clerical.
services , under the direction of the justice of the
peace, as will assist and enable said justice to
promptly dispatch litigation , and such clerical serv-
ices as will assist and enable the justice to
accurately keep such records thereof as are required.
by law . Recorders and deputy recorders shall have
the powers to administer oaths .
§ 6. Each justice of the peace , constable and
deputy constable shall daily deliver or cause to be
delivered to the recorder for the district in which
the said officers hold office all moneys received or
collected by each of them by virtue of their respec-
tive offices , or the recorder may receive such moneys
for the said officers , and in either event the recorder

682 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
shall daily issue to the said officers receipts for
moneys received from or for each of them , respec-
tively . Each recorder shall keep such records of
such daily transaction , in such manner and form ,
and showing such information , as the fiscal court
of the county may require .
§ 7. Before the tenth day of each calendar month
the recorder for each magisterial district shall pre-
pare a sworn statement , which shall show the cor-
rect amounts of money received or collected during
the next preceding calendar month from the admin-
istration of the offices of justice of the peace , con-
stable , and deputy constable existing in the district .
The statement shall be in the form , and shall dis-
close the information required by the fiscal court .
Each monthly statement , and as many copies thereof
as the fiscal court may require, shall be subscribed
and sworn to by the recorder . On or before the
tenth day of each calendar month each recorder
shall deliver one or more copies of the said monthly
statement to the fiscal court , or to such county officer
or agent as the fiscal court may designate , and each
recorder shall simultaneously deliver by certified
check payable to the order of the county treasurer
the total sum of money shown by the said statement
to have been received by the recorder under the
provisions of this Act during the next preceding
calendar month . During the interims between such
monthly statements each recorder shall safely pre-
serve the funds coming into his hands by virtue of
this Act in a State or National Bank designated
by the justice of the peace , which bank shall duly
execute a depository bond in a sufficient amount to
cover monthly balances .
§ 8. Each recorder, or deputy recorder, and the
sureties upon his bond , shall be liable for the proper
disposition of any money , property , or other thing

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 683
of value coming into possession of said recorder , or
deputy recorder, by reason of his employment , and
for the payment of all money received or collected
by the recorder , or deputy recorder , for the general
fund of the county to the county treasurer or proper
county officer or agent , until the recorder , or deputy
recorder, and the sureties upon his bond , are
released and fully discharged from further liability
by the fiscal court of the county , by order entered
of record .
9. Each person hereafter elected or appointed
and thereafter holding the office of constable in any
of said districts shall be exclusively compensated for
the performance of the duties of his office by salary
in the sum of twenty- four hundred ( $2,400.00 ) dol-
lars per annum , which shall be paid in equal monthly
installments out of the general fund of the county ,
and each deputy constable similarly appointed and
thereafter holding the office of deputy constable ,
shall be similarly exclusively compensated for the
performance of the duties of his office by salary in
the sum of fifteen hundred ( $ 1,500.00 ) dollars per
annum which shall be paid in equal monthly install-
ments , provided , however , that in no event shall the
amount paid to any constable or deputy constable
for any month exceed the amount paid into the gen-
eral fund of said county by the said constable or
deputy constable , pursuant to section ( 2 ) hereof,
during the preceding month .
§ 10. In each county in which this act is effective
the fiscal court thereof shall , by order entered of
record , designate a place within each magisterial
district within the county at which designated place
the justice of the peace of the said district shall
hold all sessions of court and shall at all times there
keep all records pertaining to his office . The place
so designated in each magisterial district shall be

684 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
located in or as closely adjacent to the county court
house as is practicable , the purpose of this require-
ment being to effect as nearly as is possible a cen-
tral location for the holding of sessions of courts of
the justices of the peace within the same county .
The justice of the peace , constable , deputy con-
stables , and recorders in each of the respective dis-
tricts shall have a common office at the place so
designated , and no justice of the peace , constable ,
deputy constable , or recorder shall have , directly
or indirectly , an office or other place within the dis-
trict , or within the county, for the transaction of
official business , nor shall either of them transact
any official business elsewhere except as authorized
by law . No attorney at law or other person shall ,
directly or indirectly , have or maintain an office at
or in conjunction with the common office of the jus-
tice of the peace , constables and recorders . The
expense of maintaining the various offices so estab-
lished within the several magisterial districts of a
county shall be paid in usual course , out of the gen-
eral fund of the county , in such manner , and subject
to such preliminary approval , as the fiscal court may
require . Each of the said offices shall be originally
equipped and shall thereafter be kept provided with
necessary furniture , clerical and other equipment ,
record books , stationery , and supplies , which shall
be paid for , in usual course , out of the general fund
of the county , by authority of the fiscal court .
§ 11. The justices of the peace shall collect , upon
each original civil action or proceeding commencing
with original process in the courts conducted by
them within the said magisterial districts , the sum
of one ( $1.00 ) dollar , which sum shall be collected
when each action or proceeding is filed , and applied
as a credit upon the costs accruing in the respective
actions or proceedings , and upon receipt or collec-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 685
tion shall become a part of the general fund of the
county , and shall be reported and disposed of as
provided in sections 2 and 7 of this act .
§ 12. The justices of the peace of each of the
counties in which this act is effective shall , immedi
ately after the effective date of this act , meet and
adopt appropriate and uniform rules of court , not
inconsistent with the Civil and Criminal Codes of
Practice , for the governance of justices ' courts
within the said counties , and upon approval of a
majority of the justices within the same county ,
each justice of the peace therein shall immediately
cause an order to be entered upon the records of
each justice's court held within the county , publish-
ing the said rules at length and dopting them as the
rules of each of the said courts . The rules of court
may be amended at any time by a majority of the
justices of the peace holding office within the same
county , at a joint meeting held for that purpose
upon notice signed by one or more of said justices .
§ 13. If any recorder or deputy recorder shall
make , or knowingly permit others to make a false
entry in any books , ledgers or accounts which such
recorder or deputy recorder is required by this act
to keep , or shall fail to make any proper entry in
such books , ledgers or records of accounts , with in-
tent to cheat or defraud this Commonwealth , or the
county , or any officer , person , or corporation ; or, if
any recorder or deputy recorder shall knowingly
embezzle , or misapply , misappropriate , or withhold
any money or property or other thing of value
belonging to the State or the county , or any
money which is declared by this act to be a part
of the general fund of the county , or which has come
into his possession or control by reason of his em-
ployment ; or , if any recorder or deputy recorder
shall knowingly make , or participate in the making

686 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of any false statement or report , with such intent,
such recorder or deputy recorder shall, upon con-
viction thereof, be deemed to be guilty of a felony
and shall be confined in the penitentiary not less
than two nor more than ten years .
§ 14. If any justice of the peace , constable , or
deputy constable shall , directly or indirectly , cause ,
aid, abet , or connive in the making of a false entry
in any official books or records , or shall directly or
indirectly cause , aid , abet , or connive in the omission
of any proper entry from any books or records , with
intent to cheat or defraud this Commonwealth , or
any county , or any officer, person , or corporation ,
either for the benefit of himself or another ; or if
any justice , constable , or deputy constable shall
embezzle or misapply or misappropriate or with-
hold any money or property or other things of
value belonging to any county or any money which
is declared by this act to be a part of the general
fund of the county or which has come into his posses-
sion or control officially or if any justice , constable
or deputy constable shall cause , counsel , aid ,
abet , encourage , or participate in the making of
any false statement or report with such intent ,
such justice of the peace , constable , or deputy
constable shall, upon conviction thereof, be deemed
to be guilty of a felony and shall be confined in
the penitentiary not less than two or more than
ten ( 10 ) years , the office held by such person
shall become vacant , and the judgment of conviction
shall so recite , but such justice , constable , or deputy
constable shall have the right to appeal to the Court
of Appeals .
The crimes and penalties herein defined shall be
cumulative and in addition to the crimes and penal-
ties which apply to justices of the peace and con-
stables , otherwise provided by law .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 687
$ 15. That , Section One Thousand Seventy - Nine
( 1,079 ) of the Kentucky Statutes (Carroll's 1930
Edition ) be and the same is hereby amended as
follows , to -wit :
By striking out the words :-"Provided , That in the counties containing a city
of hundred fifty thousand population or more there
shall be established eight magisterial districts , four
of which shall be laid off within the corporate limits
of such city and four in that portion of the county
outside the limits of such city ."
and by substituting in lieu thereof the words :
"Provided , that in counties containing a popula-
tion in excess of two hundred fifty thousand
( 250,000 ) , there shall be established three magis-
terial districts which shall be laid off diverging
from , as nearly as may be , the county court house
of the county . The County Judge of such counties
shall , in open court on the first Monday in August ,
one thousand nine hundred and thirty -two , appoint
three competent citizens of such county or counties ,
over twenty -one years of age , and residing in dif-
ferent portions of the county , as commissioners to
divide the county into three magisterial districts
laid off as herein provided . "
so that said section , when . amended and re - enacted
shall read as follows :
" The County Judge of each county shall , in open
court , on the first Monday in August , one thousand
cight hundred and ninety-two ( 1932 ) appoint three
competent citizens of the county over twenty -one
years of age , and residing in different portions of
the county and in different justices ' districts , as
commissioners to divide the county into not less
than three nor more than eight justices ' districts ,
as nearly equal in territory and population as may
be ; Provided , that in counties containing a popula-

688 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
tion in excess of 250,000 and there shall be estab-
lished three magisterial districts which shall be laid
off diverging from , as nearly as may be , the county
court house of the county . The County Judge of
such counties shall , in open court on the first Mon-
day in August , one thousand nine hundred and thir-
ty -two , appoint three competent citizens of such
county or counties , over twenty -one years of age,
and residing in different portions of the county , as
commissioners to divide the county into three mag-
isterial districts laid off as herein provided . " The
commissioners , before they proceed to act shall be
sworn to faithfully perform their duties .
§ 16. If any section of this Act is held to be un-
constitutional , such holding shall not affect the
validity of any other section or clause of this Act ,
it being the intent of the General Assembly to enact
each section of this Act separately and independent
of all other sections .
§ 17. All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict with
this Act are , to the extent of such conflict , hereby
repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 148 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 4148 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to the time and manner of pay-
ment of taxes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4148 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended by adding thereto the following proviso :
Provided , however , that the sheriff or collecting
officer whose duty it is to collect or receive taxes
may , at any time after the taxes mentioned in this

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 689
section shall become due , receive less than the face
amount of the tax bill and credit the same on account
of the amount due, including the amount of interest
and penalties then due , and all such payments shall
be credited upon the tax bill , or upon sheets annexed
thereto for that purpose , and acknowledged in writ-
ing or by a rubber stamp , indicating the amount or
amounts from time to time so paid to such sheriff or
collecting officer .
So that said section as amended and re - enacted
shall read as follows : All state , county and district
taxes , except as otherwise specially provided , shall
be due and payable on and after the first day of
March after the assessment , and all taxpayers whose
taxes are not paid on the thirty-first day of Decem-
ber after the same are due shall be deemed
delinquent , and such taxes shall bear interest at the
rate of six per cent ( 6 % ) per annum from the thirty-
first day of December after they are due until paid ,
and any person or persons failing to pay their taxes
by thirty-first day of December in the year follow-
ing the assessment for such taxes , shall pay a pen-
alty of six per centum ( 6 % ) additional on the taxes
due and unpaid . Providing any taxpayer who pays
his or her or its state , county or district taxes on
or before September 1 , after same becomes due in
any year shall be entitled to two per cent (2 % )
discount thereon , and the collector , in case of all
such payments will allow such reduction and give
a receipt in full to the taxpayer . The sheriff or
collector whose duty it is to receive or collect the
taxes shall collect the interest and penalty and
account for the same in the same way in which they
are required to collect and account for the taxes .
Provided , however , that the sheriff or collecting
officer whose duty it is to collect or receive taxes
may , at any time after the taxes mentioned in this

690 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
section shall become due , receive less than the face
amount of the tax bill and credit the same on account
of the amount due , including the amount of interest
and penalties then due , and all such payments shall
be credited upon the tax bill , or upon sheets annexed
thereto for that purpose , and acknowledged in
writing or by a rubber stamp , indicating the amount
or amounts from time to time so paid to such sheriff
or collecting officer .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 149 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 3184 and Section 3185 of
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition and relating to the
payment and collection of taxes in cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 3184 of Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be , and the same is hereby
amended and re-enacted , so that said section when
amended and re -enacted , shall read as follows :
Section 3184. Auditor to deliver books to clerk ;
tax bills ; delivery of same to auditor and by him to
treasurer ; notice to taxpayer . As soon as the tax
levy ordinance becomes a law , the Auditor shall
deliver the assessment books to the City Clerk , who
shall from them make out tax bills . Said bills shall
show each lot , the assessment thereon , separately ,
shall give the name of the supposed owner , and
shall give the net total assessment of personal
property against said person and the tax thereon ;
and shall be so arranged that the tax bill for each
half year may be detached and have each part in-
telligible . There shall be a stub to each bill which
shall show in condensed form the items and amounts
in the bill, and the bills and stubs shall be numbered

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 691
consecutively , the stub to correspond to the bill to
which it is attached . Bills and stubs to be bound
in book form . On or before the first day of April
in each year, the Clerk shall deliver the assessment
books and tax bills fully made out to the Auditor and
together they shall ascertain whether the sum of the
tax bills equals the amount of the tax which , at the
rate fixed , the total assessment should realize , and
if it does not , they shall find and arrest the error ;
when the two agree , the Auditor shall receipt assess-
ment books and tax bills . On or before the first day
of April in each year , the Auditor shall deliver all
tax bills to the treasurer and take his receipts for
the gross amount of them . As soon as practicable
after the tax bills are delivered to the Treasurer ,
he shall, by himself , deputy or employees , mail to
each person against whom a tax bill has been listed ,
or to the guardians and committees of infants and
persons of unsound mind, a post paid notice ,
directed to the best of his knowledge , stating the
number and amount of the bill , the date when due
and the penalties imposed for non -payment ; but this
provision as to notice , is directory , and the failure
of the Treasurer to send such notice , or the tax-
payer, guardian or committee to receive it, shall not
invalidate the tax, or the interest or penalties , or
any subsequent proceeding for the collection thereof .
§ 2. That Section 3185 of Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be , and the same is hereby
amended and re -enacted so that said section , when
amended and re - enacted , shall read as follows :
Section 3185. When and how taxes due and pay-
able ; penalty for non -payment . Ad valorem taxes
shall be due and payable , without demand therefor ,
at the treasurer's office , one -half each on the first
day of June and the first day of October in each
year , but the whole tax for the year may be paid

692 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
as hereinafter provided . The treasurer may receive
the whole tax at any time after the tax bills have
been made out and delivered to him, as provided in
Section 3184 , Kentucky Statutes , and said taxes paid
during the month of April in the year in which said
taxes are due and payable shall be reduced by a dis-
count of three per cent ( 3 % ) . Said taxes paid
during the month of May in the year in which said
taxes are due and payable shall be reduced by a dis-
count of two per cent ( 2 % ) . Taxes not paid within
one calendar month after they are due , shall be
deemed delinquent and shall have added to them a
penalty of ten per centum ( 10 % ) of the amount
thereof, and shall thereafter bear interest at the .
rate of one-half of one per cent (one -half of one per
cent ) for each month or fraction of a month until
paid , or until the property of the delinquent has
been sold for the tax , as hereinafter provided . When
taxes are paid the treasurer shall note on the stub
of the bill the amount paid on same and the date
thereof, and he will deliver the receipted bill ,
countersigned by the auditor to the taxpayer. When
any person shall desire to pay taxes on any one item
of real estate in a tax bill he shall be allowed to do
so , if the city's interest will not be prejudiced
thereby , and will be given a receipt showing the date
and the amount of the payment , the number of the
tax bill , the name of the person against whom the
tax bill is made out and a sufficient description to
identify the property on which the taxes are paid .
A corresponding notation shall be made on the tax
bill and the stub and the city's lien will thereupon
stand released as to said property , but the tax bill
will not be surrendered until paid in full .
§ 3. Whereas , there is no provision for a dis-
count for the prompt payment of taxes in cities of
the second class , an emergency is hereby declared to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 693
exist , and this act shall take effect and be in force
from and after its passage and approval .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 150 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation , imposing an excise
or license tax on gasoline and all other liquid fuels , including
all liquids ordinarily , practically and commercially usable in
internal combustion engines for the generation of power , and
all distillates of, and condensates from petroleum , natural
gas , coal tar , vegetable ferments , and from all other products ,
so usable and sold in this Commonwealth at wholesale , as the
words "at wholesale " are defined in this act ; except kerosene ,
fuel oil , and crude oil as such ; providing for the collection of
such tax, for the keeping of records , reporting of sales , defin-
ing the duties of the State Tax Commission , and requiring
reports to be made to the State Tax Commission by railroads
and other persons , firms or corporations showing the trans-
portation and delivery of gasoline ; exempting gasoline from
local excise and license taxes ; the distribution and use of the
revenue derived therefrom , the administration of said law ,
fixing fines and penalties for the violation of this act ; repeal-
ing chapter one hundred and twenty -seven of the Acts of nine-
teen hundred and twenty -eight , and chapter one hundred and
fifty -three of the Acts of nineteen hundred and thirty , both of
said chapters constituting the present gasoline tax law .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
GASOLINE TAX LAW.
§ 1. That the word " gasoline " as used in this
act shall include all liquid fuels , including liquids
ordinarily , practically and commercially usable in
internal combustion engines for the generation of
power , and all distillates of, and condensates from
petroleum , natural gas , coal , coal tar, vegetable
ferments , and all other products , so usable and sold
in this Commonwealth at wholesale , as the words
"at wholesale " are defined herein, except kerosene ,

694 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
fuel oil and crude oil as such . A State tax of five
(5 ) cents per gallon is hereby imposed on all gaso-
line , as defined herein , sold in this Commonwealth at
wholesale as the words " at wholesale " are herein-
after defined . No other excise or license tax shall
be levied or assessed on gasoline by the State or any
county , city , town or other district . The tax hereby
provided for shall be paid by the person , firm , asso-
ciation or corporation so selling gasoline , and shall
be paid by said person , firm, association or corpora-
tion into the general fund of the State Treasury in
the manner and within the time hereinafter speci-
fied . The words " at wholesale " , as used in this act
shall be held and construed to mean and include any
and all sales made for the purpose of re -sale or dis-
tribution , or for use , and , as well, the gasoline fur-
nished or supplied for distribution within this State
whether the distributor be the same person who so
furnished the same , his agent or employe , or another
person ; and also to mean and include any person
who shall purchase or obtain gasoline without the
State and sell or distribute or use the same within
the State . Provided , that nothing herein contained
shall be construed as authorizing or requiring the
collection of such tax upon any gasoline after the
same shall have been already once taxed under the
provision of this act , and the words " at wholesale "
shall not apply to any gasoline which has been so
taxed .
2. All taxes collected under the provisions of
this act shall be paid into the State Treasury , and
shall be used only for the purpose of the construc-
tion , reconstruction , maintenance , and repair of
roads , highways , and bridges .
§ 3. ( a ) Every person , or the treasurer or other
proper officer or agent of every association , co -part-
nership or corporation selling gasoline at wholesale ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 695
as defined herein , shall keep and preserve , for a
period of two ( 2 ) years , an accurate record of all
such sales distribution , receipts , and uses of gaso-
line , together with invoices bills -of-lading , and
other pertinent records and papers as may be re-
quired by the State Tax Commission : and shall on
or before the last day of each month transmit to the
State Tax Commission a statement , under oath or
affirmation , on such forms as the State Tax Commis-
sion may prescribe , of the total number of gallons
of gasoline sold , distributed or used in each county
of the state from their respective place or places of
business so registered under the provisions of sec-
tion seven a (7a ) hereof during the preceding
calendar month , which gallons are made taxable by
this act , and shall accompany said report by a certi-
fied or cashier's check, payable to the State Treas-
urer of Kentucky , for the amount of tax due for
such preceding calendar month . It shall be the duty
of every person , purchasing gasoline taxable under
this act from an " at wholesale " dealer for the pur-
pose of resale , to maintain and keep for a period of
one ( 1 ) year a record of gasoline received , the
amount of tax paid to the dealer as part of the
purchase price , together with delivery tickets , in-
voices , bills -of -lading , and such other records as the
State Tax Commission shall require .
(b ) Every person , association , co -partnership , or
corporation , operating a refinery for the production
of gasoline within the State of Kentucky , and sell-
ing gasoline for delivery to points within the state
of Kentucky , shall collect and pay to the State
Treasurer all taxes assessed under this act , on such
gasoline . The State Tax Commission , in computing
the amount of tax due under this act , shall make an
allowance of one per cent for leakage , evaporation ,
bad debts and collection costs .

696 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
( c ) Every person , associaton , co -partnership or
corporation , operating a refinery in this state , shall
keep and preserve an accurate record of all such
sales , distribution , manufacture , and uses of gaso-
line within the state of Kentucky , and all shipments
of gasoline to points outside the state , and shall , not
later than the tenth day of the month , make a report
to the State Tax Commission , of all gasoline shipped
to points in the state , and all shipments of gasoline
to points outside the state , during the preceding
calendar month , giving the date of shipment , car
number and initial , net gallons , and the name of the
person , firm or corporation to whom shipped .
(d) The State Tax Commission shall allow to
every person , association , co -partnership , or cor
poration operating a refinery in Kentucky for keep-
ing a record of the sale and shipments of gasoline ,
as provided herein , and for collecting and paying
the tax thereon, one per cent of such tax reported
and paid.
(e ) If any person , association , co -partnership or
corporation shall purchase gasoline outside the
state of Kentucky , for sale within the state of Ken-
tucky, such purchaser shall, on or before the last
day of each month in which such gasoline is
received , transmit to the State Tax Commission a
statement , under oath or affirmation , on such forms
as the State Tax Commission may prescribe , of the
total number of gallons of gasoline so purchased
and received for sale in Kentucky , during the month
next preceding , and shall accompany said report by
a certified or cashier's check , payable to the State
Treasurer of Kentucky , for the amount of tax due
on such gasoline received provided that the State
Tax Commission in computing the amount of such
tax shall make an allowance of one per cent for
leakage , evaporation, bad debts , collection costs ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 697
handling and reporting the tax . Provided that such
allowance shall be made only on gasoline purchased
in carload quantities .
(f) Every railroad or other transportation com-
pany and every other person , firm , association ,
partnership or corporation transporting gasoline as
defined in Section 1 of this act by whatsoever man .
ner from without the state to points within this
state , or between points within the state , shall re-
port to the State Tax Commission , on forms pre-
scribed by the Commission , showing the name and
address of each person , firm or corporation to whom
deliveries of gasoline have been made , the name and
address of the original consignee , if deliveries are
made to any other than the original consignee , the
name and address of the consignor , the point of
origin , the point of delivery , the date of delivery ,
the number and initials of each car, if shipped by
rail , the quantity of each shipment and delivery in
gallons , the manner of shipment and delivery , and
such other information as the State Tax Commis-
sion may require relative to the transportation and
delivery of such gasoline . Such reports are to in
clude intracity switching movements of gasoline in
tank cars or otherwise . Such reports shall be made
under oath and shall be filed on or before the last
day of every month covering all such deliveries
made within this state during the preceding calendar
month .
(g ) The State Tax Commission of the state of
Kentucky shall , upon request duly received from
the officials to whom are entrusted the enforcement
of the gasoline tax laws of any other state , forward
to such officials any information which it may have
in its possession relative to the manufacture ,
receipt , sale , use , transportation and/or shipment
by any person of gasoline .

698 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
(h ) By virtue of the allowance provided here - in
to "at wholesale " dealers for collecting the tax and
remitting the same to State Tax Commission, every
" at wholesale " dealer is a trust officer of the State ,
and failure to remit gasoline tax to the state as pro-
vided herein constitutes embezzlement of state
funds , and upon conviction under this section any
individual , or partner , or officer , or agent of any
association , partnership or corporation shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and shall be fined not ex-
ceeding one thousand dollars ($1000.00 ) or im-
prisoned in the county jail not exceeding six months ,
or both so fined and imprisoned, and upon convic-
tion on a second offense under this section , shall be
guilty of a felony and shall be imprisoned for not
less than one year or more than five years in the
state prison .
§ 4. If any such person , association , co -partner-
ship or corporation neglects or refuses to make said.
return , or pay the tax at the time hereinbefore pro-
vided, the amount thereof , with an addition of a
penalty of twenty per centum thereof, with interest
at the rate of six per cent per annum from the date
when due until paid , shall be paid on an account
settled by the State Tax Commission , as other taxes
are paid , settled and collected ; and , if collected by
proceedings , in any court , an additional penalty
of twenty per centum shall be collected as is author-
ized to be collected in revenue agents proceedings
under section 4263-4 of the Kentucky Statutes .
The said twenty per centum penalty thereon and
interest due shall be paid into the State Treasury as
part of the tax , and the twenty per centum penalty
collected in revenue agents ' proceedings shall be
distributed as provided in section 4263-4 of the Ken-
tucky Statutes .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 699
§ 5. The State Tax Commission , or any assist-
ant or representative thereof, appointed in writing
by it, is hereby authorized to examine the books ,
records , papers , files and equipment of any person ,
association , co -partnership or corporation pertain-
ing to the business made taxable by this act , and to
that end , shall have the power to examine witnesses
under oath to verify the accuracy of any return made
under the provisions of this act, or to ascertain
whether or not any tax is due by any person , asso-
ciation , co -partnership or corporation under the
provisions of this act, and in the collection of said
tax, the State Tax Commission shall have the power
to institute legal proceedings for the purpose of
ascertaining the amount of tax due and the collec-
tion thereof with the penalties thereon , and the
State Tax Commission shall be vested with all the
powers under the law of the State for the collec-
tion of taxes . The State Tax Commission may , at
or after the commencement of an action for the pur-
pose of collecting the amount of tax due and penal-
ties and interest due thereon as set out herein have
an attachment against the property of the defend-
ant , including garnishees , without the execution of
a bond upon the filing of an affidavit showing the
nature of the claim , that it is just , the sum which
the State Tax Commission believes the plaintiff
ought to recover and that the collection of the de-
mand will be endangered by delay in obtaining
judgment or a return of no property found . Any
information gained by the State Tax Commission ,
or any assistant , agent or representative thereof ,
as a result of the reports , investigations or verifica
tions herein required to be made , shall be confiden-
tial , and any person divulging such information
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and , upon convic-
tion , shall be fined not exceeding five hundred dollars

700 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
($ 500 ) ; providing , however , that this prohibition
shall not extend to information required in prosecu-
tions for making false reports or returns or in the
preparation and prosecution of legal proceedings
for the collection of any tax due under the provi-
sions of this act , or any prosecutions for any other
infraction of the tax laws of this State . If any wit-
ness shall fail or refuse to appear at the request of
the State Tax Commission , or refuse access to any
books , records , files , papers , or equipment , the State
Tax Commission shall certify such fact and the
name of the witness , or witnesses , so failing and
refusing to appear , or refusing access to any books ,
records , papers , files or equipment to the circuit
court of the county having jurisdiction of the matter
and said court shall thereupon issue a summons for
the said party to appear at a place designated
within the jurisdiction of the court on a day fixed ,
and give such evidence and open for inspection such
books , records , files and papers as may be required
for the purposes of ascertaining whether or not
any return so made is true and correct , or whether
or not any person , association , copartnership or
corporation has failed or refused to make a true and
correct return under the provisions of this law ;
and the circuit court shall have in any such proceed-
ing all the power of a court of equity in punishing
for contempt . In order that the State Tax Commis-
sion may have additional means of checking up the
accuracy of the reports made , and determining the
amount of tax due by any person , association co-
partnership or corporation , the records , books , and
other documents of common carriers , relative to the
transportation of gasoline , are hereby declared to be
accessible to the State Tax Commission .

6. Any person , association , co -partnership or
corporation , or any officer , director , trustee , or mem-
ber of any association , co -partnership or corpora-
tion , who shall fail , neglect or refuse to make the
returns and pay the tax herein prescribed , or shall
refuse to permit the State Tax Commission , its as-
sistants , agents or representatives appointed by it
in writing , to examine the books , records , papers ,
files and equipment of such person , association , co-
partnership or corporation pertaining to the busi-
ness made taxable by this act , or who makes any

or who does , or attempts to do , anything whatever
to avoid a full disclosure of the amount of business

ceived , used , sold or delivered , as provided in this
act , shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon con-
viction , shall be fined not exceeding one thousand
dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) , and in the case the offender is
an individual , he may be imprisoned in the county
jail not exceeding six months , or both so fined and
imprisoned . Such penalty shall be in addition to the
penalty imposed hereinbefore in section 4 of this
act .
§ 7a . On and after 30 days after this act shall
go into effect , it shall be unlawful for any " at
wholesale " dealer to receive , use , sell or distribute
any gasoline or to engage in the sale of gasoline
within this state unless such dealer is the holder of
an uncancelled license issued by the State Tax Com-
mission to engage in such business . To procure
such license every dealer shall file with the State
Tax Commission an application under oath or af-

702 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
firmation and in such form as the State Tax Com-
mission may prescribe , setting forth :
a . The name under which the dealer will trans-
act business within the state of Kentucky ;
b . The location , with street number address , of
its principal office or place of business within this
state ; and if such dealer has more than one place
of business within the State , the locations of each
place of business ;
C. The name and complete residence address of
the owner or the names and addresses of the part-
ners , if such dealer is a partnership , or the names
and addresses of the principal officers , if such dealer
is a corporation or association ; and if such dealer is
a corporation organized under the laws of another
state , territory or country , it shall also file with such
application a certified copy of the certificate or
license issued by the secrtary of state of Kentucky
showing that such corporation is authorized to
transact business in the state of Kentucky . Upon
the filing of an application for a license , and concur-
rently therewith , a bond of the character stipulated
and in the amount provided for in Sect . 7 -b of this
act , shall be filed with the State Tax Commission .
No license shall be issued upon any application
unless accompanied by such a bond . In the event
that any application for a license certificate to
transact business as a dealer in the state of Ken-
tucky shall be filed by any person whose license
shall at any time heretofore have been cancelled for
cause by the State Tax Commission , or in case said
State Tax Commission shall be of the opinion that
such application is not filed in good faith , or that
such application is filed by some person as a sub-
terfuge for the real person in interest whose license

or registration shall theretofore have been cancelled
for cause by said State Tax Commission , then and
in any of said events the State Tax Commission ,
after a hearing, of which the applicant shall have
been given five ( 5 ) days ' notice in writing and in
which said applicant shall have the right to appear
in person or by counsel and present testimony , shall
have and is hereby given the right and authority to

tucky. The application in proper form having been

cepted and approved , the State Tax Commission

shall keep and file all applications and bonds with

of all licensed dealers .
(b) Every " at wholesale " dealer shall file with
the State Tax Commission a bond in the minimum
amount of $5,000.00 and up to a maximum amount
sufficient in the opinion of the State Tax Commis-
sion , the financial rating and reputation of the com-
pany considered , to insure payment to the State of
Kentucky of the amount of tax and any penalties
and interest for which the dealer may become liable ,
on a form to be approved by the State Tax Commis-
sion and with a surety company approved by the

704 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
State Tax Commission as surty thereon , and upon
which such dealer shall be the principal obliger and
the state of Kentucky shall be the obligee , condi-
tioned upon the prompt filing of true reports and
the payment by such dealer to the treasurer of the
State of Kentucky of any and all gasoline excise
taxes which are now or which hereafter may be
levied or imposed by the state of Kentucky together

and generally upon faithful compliance with the

Tax Commission shall be discharged or reduced ,
whether by judgment rendered , payment made or
otherwise , or if in the opinion of the State Tax

given shall have become unsatisfactory or unaccept-
able , then the State Tax Commission may require

ties in the same amount , failing which the State Tax

ficate of said dealer . If such new bond shall be fur-
nished by said dealer as above provided , the State
Tax Commission shall cancel and surrender the
bond of said dealer for which such new bond shall

which the dealer shall be given five ( 5 ) days ' notice
in writing , the State Tax Commission shall decide.
that the amount of the existing bond is insufficient
to insure payment to the state of Kentucky of the
amount of the tax and any penalties and interest
for which said dealer is or may at any time become
liable , then the dealer shall forthwith , upon the
written demand of State Tax Commission file an
additional bond in the same manner and form with

a surety company thereon approved by the State
Tax Commission in any amount determined by the
State Tax Commission to be necessary to secure at

shall be released and discharged from any and all

ity already accrued , or which shall accrue , before

in the amount and form hereinbefore in this section
provided , the State Tax Commission shall forthwith
cancel the license of said dealer . If such new bond

the State Tax Commission shall cancel and surren-
der the bond of said dealer for which such new bond

(c) If an " at wholesale " dealer shall at any
time file a false monthly report of the data or in-
formation required by this act , or shall fail , refuse

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 707
accurate reports or to pay said tax and/or penalties ,
then the State Tax Commission shall cancel and sur-
render the bond theretofore filed by said dealer .
(d) Any person who shall engage in business in
the state of Kentucky as an " at wholesale " dealer
without being the holder of an uncanceled license to
engage in such business , or who shall , without such
license , sell any gasoline upon which the tax herein
imposed shall not have been reported and paid , shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction
thereof shall be punished by a fine of not less than
one hundred dollars ($ 100.00 ) nor more than one
thousand dollars ($ 1,000.00 ) or by imprisonment in
the county jail for not less than thirty ( 30 ) days
nor more than one ( 1 ) year or both so fined and im-
prisoned in the discretion of the jury . Each day or
part thereof during which any person shall engage
in business as a dealer without being the holder of
an uncancelled license shall constitute a separate
offense within the meaning of this section .
8. It shall be the duty of the State Tax Com-
mission to administer and enforce the provisions
of this act , and it is hereby authorized and empow-
ered to prescribe all blanks , forms and reports nec-
essary to carry out the provisions of this act . It
shall certify each month to the State Auditor the
total amount of tax paid or collected from each
wholesale dealer during the preceding month under
the provisions of this act , and shall accompany said
certification with the checks received by it for the
payment of said tax and penalties , if any . All costs.
and expenses incurred by the State Tax Commission
in the administration of this act shall be paid out
of the funds collected hereunder .

708 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
9. No suit shall be maintained in any court to
restrain or delay the collection or payment of the
tax herein imposed upon any ground whatever , but
the aggrieved taxpayer shall pay the taxes when
due , and if paid under protest may at any time
within two years from the date of such payment sue
the Auditor of Public Accounts in an action of law
to recover the tax so paid , with legal interest
thereon from the date of payment . If it is legally
determined that said tax, or any part thereof , was
wrongfully collected for any reason , it shall be the
duty of the Auditor of Public Accounts then in office
to issue his warrant on the Treasurer of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky for the amount of such tax
so adjudged to have been wrongfully collected ,
together with interest thereon , and the Treasurer
shall pay same out of public road fund of the State .
A separate suit need not be filed for such separate
payment made by any . taxpayer , and a recovery
may be had in one suit for as many illegal payments
as may have been made , and which are not barred
by the limitation of two years herein imposed .
§ 10. The provisions of this act are severable
and if any of its provisions or any penalty provided
by this act shall be held unconstitutional , such pro-
vision or the particular penalty held to be invalid
shall not affect or impair the remainder of the act or
such part of the penalties as are not held to be in-
valid and the decisions of the courts shall not affect
or impair the remaining provisions of this act or any
of the other penalties anywhere in this act provided .
It is hereby declared as a legislative intent that this
act would have been adopted had any unconstitu-
tional provisions or such invalid penalties not been
included therein .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 709
§ 11. All of the provisions of Chapter one hun-
dred and twenty - seven of the Acts of the General
Assembly of nineteen hundred and twenty -eight and
of Chapter one hundred and fifty -three of the Acts
of the General Assembly of nineteen hundred and
thirty are hereby repealed and this Act is enacted

§ 12. WHEREAS , the new provisions of this act
are designed to strengthen the administrative fea-
tures of the gasoline tax law and to prevent the eva-

ernor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 151 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section 4238 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to revenue and taxation , so as
to increase the State tax on the recording of certain instru-
ments and marriage licenses .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 4238 , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's 1930 Edition , be and the same hereby is
amended and re -enacted so that said section as
amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
On each original actionoriginal action or proceeding in the
circuit court, commencing with or without original
process , and on each original action or pro-
ceeding in other courts , when the amount in

710 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
controversy , exclusive of interest and cost , ex-
ceeds fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) ; on each appeal to
the circuit or quarterly court ; on each traverse
or forcible entry or detainer ; on each proceeding
to revive a judgment and fifty cents (fifty cents ) on
each deed or power of attorney to convey real or
personal estate , or any mortgage or conveyance of
real or personal property , or lien or conveyance of
coal , oil , gas or other mineral right or privilege , one
dollar ( $ 1.00 ) , and on each marriage license , three
dollars and fifty cents ( $3.50 ) , and on the seal of
any court , required by law to keep a seal , fifty cents
(50c ) , except for license authorized by the state ; on
each appeal to the court of appeals , two dollars
( $2.00 ) , on each verdict of a petit jury in civil cases
in the circuit court, four dollars ( $ 4.00 ) . The tax
shall be paid when the parties withdraw a jury or
the plaintiff sustains a non - suit after the jury is
sworn . On filing and registering a plat and certifi-
cate of survey, and issuing and recording patent
thereon , one charge , which shall be paid before the
survey is filed, but not to apply to a survey hereto-
fore filed in register's office , two dollars and fifty
cents ( $2.50 ) for each survey consisting of ten acres
or less , and five dollars ( $ 5.00 ) for each survey in
excess of ten acres ; for filing a caveat , twenty-five
cents ( 25c ) ; for filing a copy of judgment of a court
in a case of a caveat , mandamus or other proceed-
ings affecting the land office , or the duties of the reg-
ister , twenty-five cents ( 25c ) ; for issuing a land war-
rant , one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) ; for each official certificate
with seal of office affixed , one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) ; for a
copy of a plat , fifty cents ( 50c ) ; for a copy of a
course and distances of any patent or survey, fifty
cents (50c ) ; for a copy of an entry or land warrant ,

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 711
fifty cents ( 50c ) ; for a copy of an assignment ,
twenty-five cents (25c ) ; for a copy of a caveat , two
cents ( 2c ) for every twenty words ; for a copy of any
writing not specifically provided for , two cents ( 2c )
for every twenty words .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

That Chapter 143 of the 1930 Acts of the General

being Section 2061a -42 , Baldwin's 1930 Supplement
to Carroll's 1930 Kentucky Statutes , entitled “ Cer-
tain counties may divert funds for support and
maintenance of sanatoria , when , " be repealed and
re-enacted so as to read as follows :
" That any county , which has levied a tax and
collected funds from such levy for the purpose of
studying , treating and preventing tuberculosis
through the establishment of a County Sanatoria , or
otherwise , but where said county is not financially
able , or inclined to erect said sanatoria, the Fiscal
Court of said County is hereby authorized and
directed to divert any and all funds collected under
the provisions of the Acts of 1908 and 1918 for pur-
poses herein described to the purchase of a county
farm or poor farm , and for the erection of proper
and necessary buildings thereon , and for the sup-

712 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
port and maintenance of said county farm , or poor
farm , wherein the unfortunates of said county are
kept and maintained on account of their inability
to support themselves either from being afflicted
with tuberculosis , or otherwise . "
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 153 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Chapter 19 of the Acts of 1920 ,
approved by the Governor March 16 , 1920 , entitled "An Act
imposing a license tax on race tracks at which races are run
for purses or prizes under the jurisdiction of the Kentucky
State Racing Commission , and which license tax is in lieu of
all other license , income , excise , special or franchise tax of the
State of Kentucky , or any county , city, town , municipality or
other political subdivision thereof."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Chapter 19 of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly of 1920 , approved March 16 , 1920 ,
be and the same is hereby repealed and re -enacted
so that said Act when amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows :
§ 2. That every person , firm , association , or cor-
poration engaged in the business of conducting a
race track , at which races are run for purses or
prizes , under the jurisdiction of the State Racing
Commission , shall pay to the State of Kentucky a
license tax therefor , as follows :
§ 3. Any Race Track within twenty ( 20 ) miles
of a city of two hundred thousand ( 200,000 ) popula-
tion located within or without the State of Ken-
tucky , shall pay for each day upon which races are
run a license tax of twenty - five hundred ( $ 2,500.00 )
dollars per day, and any track situated more than
twenty ( 20 ) miles from a city of two hundred thou-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 713
sand ( 200,000 ) population located within or without
the State of Kentucky , shall pay for each day upon
which races are run a license tax of five hundred
( $500.00 ) dollars per day.
§ 4. It shall be the duty of every person , firm ,
association , or corporation engaged in the business
of conducting a Race Track , to furnish to the
Auditor of Public Accounts on or before December
1st of each year , a true report of the number of
days on which races were run on the said race tracks
during the year , and at the same time pay to the
Auditor of Public Accounts the correct amount due
to the State of Kentucky , by said Race Tracks , at
the rate per day above fixed and provided .
§ 5. The license tax herein imposed shall be in
lieu of all other license , income , excise , special or
franchise tax to the State of Kentucky , or any
County , City, Town , Municipality or other political
subdivision thereof ; and no County, City, Town ,
Municipality or other political subdivision of the
State of Kentucky shall be authorized or empowered
to levy or impose any license , income , excise , special
or franchise tax on any such person , firm , associa-
tion , or corporation engaged in the business of con-
ducting a race track at which races are run for
purses or prizes under the jurisdiction of the State
Racing Commission , or on the operation or mainte-
nance of any pari -mutuel machine or similar device ,
or on the sale of any commodity or merchandise
during the running of races thereon or at any such
race tracks by any such person , firm , association ,
or corporation .
§ 6. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
7. Whereas certain race tracks in this Com-
monwealth will be unable to operate in the Spring

714 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of 1932 under the law as it existed before the amend-
ment herein proposed , unless relief is granted to
them , and
Whereas , it is to the interest of the breeding in-
dustry of this Commonwealth to have race meet-
ings in the Spring of 1932 ,
Now , therefore , an emergency is hereby declared
to exist , and this Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 25 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 154 .
AN ACT relating to Chiropractic , amending and re -enacting sec-
tion 8 of chapter 123 of Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That Section 8 of Chapter 123 of the Ses-
sion Acts of the General Assembly of 1928 , same
relating to Chiropractic , be and the same is
amended , and as so amended said section of said.
Chapter shall read and be as follows , to -wit :
Section 8. All license heretofore or hereafter
issued to persons to practice Chiropractic in the
Commonwealth of Kentucky shall pay to this Board
an annual renewal fee of $3.00 on or before the first
day of January of each year after this law becomes
effective , and any person engaging in the practice
who having held license shall upon failure to renew
same as is herein provided , be punished as in the
manner provided by Section 13 of this Act . The
Secretary of the Board , shall not later than Novem-
ber 1st of each year , mail to all licensed chiroprac-
tors in this Commonwealth a notice to the effect that
the said renewal fee is due on or before the 1st day
of the succeeding January . All renewal certificates

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 715
shall be signed by the Secretary, under the seal of
the Board . All licensees shall file their license with
the County Court Clerk of the county in which such
licensee resides , before engaging in practice , and
the clerk shall record same in a book kept in his
office .
§ 2. All laws in conflict with this amendment are
hereby repealed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 155 .
AN ACT to authorize cities of the Second Class to levy a tax
for the purpose of providing a fund for the organization , em-
ployment and maintenance of a band , orchestra or drum and
bugle corps .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
All cities of the Second Class organized under the
laws of this State , are hereby authorized to levy
each year a tax not to exceed ( 2 ) two mills , for the
purpose of providing a fund for the organization ,
employment and maintenance of a band , orchestra ,
or drum and bugle corps .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 156 .
AN ACT to authorize the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky to lengthen or delay the time of payment of taxes ,
interest on taxes , and to give the proper relief to sheriffs when
such delays be granted by the Governor's proclamation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That in case of extreme drought and the
failure in whole or in part of crops in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky ; that in case of continued un-

716 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
employment due to any and all economic changes ;
that in case of extreme destruction of property , live
stock or crops by wind , rain , lightning , earthquakes
or tornadoes or any other act of Providence destruc-
tive to the financial interest of the peoples of the
state --that in case due to any one or more of the
above listed causes , financial embarrassment and
distress lays a heavy hand upon the taxpayers of
the Commonwealth , and wherein the public good ,
the public happiness and the general welfare of the
people of the state can best be served and protected
-that the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky be vested with full authority when in his wise
and better judgment he be convinced that an
extreme emergency exists and extreme financial dis-
tress is present among the taxpayers of the state
and that relief in the premises would serve the best
interest of the taxpayers of Kentucky or any part
thereof, then the Governor of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky shall by a proclamation delay or lengthen
the time for payment on any taxes or interest , either
or both , due to the state or counties , for a period
not less than thirty days or more than ninety days
at the discretion of the Governor . Said proclama-
tion may apply to one or more counties or to the
entire state .
$ 2. The sheriffs of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky shall have full relief for any taxes , interest or
penalties exacted of them by any law in the event
of such proclamation by the Governor of this Com-
monwealth .
§ 3. All laws in conflict are hereby repealed
to the extent of such conflict .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 717
CHAPTER 157 .
AN ACT to repeal and re- enact Section No. 4223c -1 of Carroll's
Statutes of Kentucky ; Pertaining to tax on oil production ;
Produced in the State of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section 4223c -1 of Carroll's Statutes , 1930
Edition , be and the same is hereby repealed and
re-enacted and when so re -enacted that same shall
read as follows :
Every person , firm , corporation or association
producing crude petroleum oil in this state shall in
lieu of all other taxes on the wells producing said
crude petroleum , annually pay a tax equal to one-
half ( ½ ) of one per cent ( 1 % ) of the market value
of all crude petroleum so produced ; and such tax
shall be for state purposes , and in addition any
county in the state may impose a like tax for road
purposes , county purposes or school purposes , not
to exceed one per cent ( 1 % ) of the market value
of all crude petroleum produced in such county ; and
the Fiscal Court of any county may levy said tax
for county purposes ; and shall determine what fund
or funds shall receive the tax when collected .
when crude petroleum is produced in any separate
taxing district in a county the Fiscal Court shall
equitably distribute such taxes between the county
and such taxing district .
And
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 158.
AN ACT to define oleomargarine and to prescribe regulations to
govern the manufacture , storage and sale thereof ; to provide
for the issuance of license to the manufacturers and venders
thereof on payment of fees therefor , and for revocation of
such license for violations of this act ; to provide a privilege
tax on sales of oleomargarine ; to provide for the enforcement

718 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of the provisions of this act by the State Tax Commission and
its agents ; and declaring a violation of this act to be a
misdemeanor and providing punishment therefor, and authoriz-
ing the State Tax Commission to make rules and regulations
to carry into effect this act , and repealing all laws or parts
of laws in conflict herewith , and declaring an emergency to
exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the term person as used in this act
shall include individuals , firms , co -partnerships ,
associations and corporations , both foreign and
domestic .
§ 2. That for the purpose of this act certain
manufactured substances , certain extracts , and cer-
tain mixtures and compounds , including such mix-
tures and compounds with butter , shall be known
and designated as Oleomargarine, namely : All sub-
stances heretofore known as oleomargarine , ole ,
oleomargaine oil , butterine , lardine , suine , and
neutral ; all lard extracts and tallow extracts ; and all
mixtures and compounds of tallow , beef fat , suet ;
lard , lard oil , fish or fish oil , palm oil , vegetable oil ,
annetto and other coloring matter , intestinal fat ,
and offal fat ,-if ( 1 ) made in imitation or semblance
of butter , or ( 2 ) calculated or intended to be sold as
butter or for butter , or ( 3 ) churned , emulsified , or
mixed in cream , milk , water or other liquid , and con-
taining moister in excess of one per centum , or salt
in any quantity whatsoever . This section shall not
apply ( 1 ) to puff pastry , shortening not churned or
emulsified in milk or creain , and having a melting
point of one hundred and eighteen degrees Fahren-
heit or more , nor ( 2 ) to any of the following con-
taining condiments and spices : salad dressings ,
mayonnaise dressing or mayonnaise products , nor
(3) to pharmacutical preparations .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 719
§ 3. That whoever , in person or by agent , sells ,
exposes for sale , or has in possession with intent
to sell , oleomargarine as defined in Section 2 of this
act , shall have the word " oleomargarine " branded
upon the top , sides and bottom of every tub , firkin ,
box or package . cotaining any of said articles , in
plain Gothic letters of not less than twenty point
type. This brand must cover a space of not less
than three and one -eighth inches in length on each
panel , and shall be printed in a conspicuous color ,
which forms a strong contrast with the color of the
package ; and whoever , in person or by agent ex-
poses or offers for sale any oleomargarine not in the
original package , shall attach to said article , in a
conspicuous place , a label bearing the word " oleo-
margarine " as provided for above and in case of
retail sales of any said article , substances or com-
pound from bulk, tub or other container , the seller
shall in person or by agent , attach to each package
so sold and delivered therewith to the purchasers , a
label or wrapper bearing in a conspicuous place
upon the outside of the package , the word " oleo-
margarine " printed in plain Gothic letters of not
less than twenty point type .
§ 4. That no person , by himself or his agent or
servant , shall manufacture , sell , offer for sale ,
expose for sale or have in his possession , with intent
to sell , any article compound or product , made
wholly or partly out of any fat , oil or oleaginous
substance or compound thereof , not produced di-
rectly and at the time of manufacture , from unadul-
terated milk or cream from the same , which shall be
in imitation of butter , produced from pure unadul-
terated milk , cream , or the combination of same.
Provided that nothing in this act shall be construed
to prohibit the manufacture or sale of oleomarga-
rine , as defined in Section 2 of this act , in a separate

720 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
and distinct form , and in such a manner as pre-
scribed in said section .
§ 5. That every person desiring to manufacture ,
sell or offer for sale , or have in possession with
intent to sell oleomargarine as defined in Section 2
of this act , shall make application for a license to
do so , which shall be in such form as shall be pre-
scribed by the State Tax Commission or its agents ,
said application shall be furnished by the State Tax
Commission in such form with such information
thereon as may be deemed necessary or expedient
for the fulfillment of the provisions of this act , such
application shall contain an accurate description of
the place where the proposed business is intended
to be carried on , the name , style of firm and such
other information as may be prescribed to conduct
the said busines , such licenses shall be furnished to
the firm , person , manufacturer , or retailer and the
State Tax Commission shall issue such license
properly signed and certified to , charging the fol-
lowing fees : Manufacturer's license , five dollars
($5.00 ) ; jobber's license , three dollars ( $ 3.00 ) , retail

to be for the period of one year ; after obtaining the

granted under the said license , hang up and display
in a conspicuous place , on the walls of the room ,

said ; and shall also procure from the State Tax
Commission a sign, or signs , which in number , size
and lettering shall be uniform throughout the state ,
clearly setting forth that he is engaged in the manu-
facture , or sale of oleomargarine .
6. That any person operating a restaurant ,
lunch -counter , boarding house , or other place where

•
food is served to the public using and serving oleo-
margarine , shall have cards placed in plain view

8. That no person shall use in any way in con-

inations as will best carry out the provisions of this

"This tax to be in addition to the license fees de-

§ 9. That no state , county , municipal or other
institution supported in whole or in part by public
funds , shall furnish to its inmates , patrons , or em-
ployees , or use in the preparation of their food any
oleomargarine or similar substitutes for butter as

§ 10. All revenue and fees collected by the State
Tax Commission under the provisions of this act ,
"

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 723
shall be paid into the State Treasury to the credit of

§ 11. The State Tax Commission is hereby
authorized to appoint the necessary agents or depu-
ties for the purpose of carrying out the provisions
of this act , and said agents , deputies , and employees ,
are hereby authorized and empowered to enter , dur- .
ing business hours , for the purpose of carrying out
the provisions of this act , all places of business that
may come under the provisions of this act .
12. That to facilitate the inspection of oleo-
margarine as prescribed in this act , all manufac-
turers , jobbers , and wholesalers making shipments
into or within the state are hereby required to for-
ward to the State Tax Commission on forms to be

transportation company , the name and address of
the consignees , together with the brand or brands
making up such shipment .
§ 13. The State Tax Commission is hereby em-
powered to promulgate such rules and regulations
as are consistent with the provisions of this act , pro-
vided , however , that when requested by any person
interested , the State Tax Commission shall furnish
copies of all proposed rules and regulations at least
ten days before such rules and regulations become
effective so that the person may be heard thereon .
§ 14. Any person violating any of the provisions
of this act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and upon
conviction thereof , for the first offense shall be fined
not less than ten dollars ($ 10.00 ) , nor more than
fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) , and for such subsequent
offense , shall be fined not less than twenty-five dol-
lars ( $ 25.00 ) , or be imprisoned in the county jail for

724 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
not more than twelve months , nor less than sixty

§ 15. That if any section , subsection , clause , pro-

$ 16. All laws or parts of laws in conflict with

§ 17. Whereas , the present state revenue does

and development of the state government and its
