and this act shall become a law and become effec-
tive upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 19 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 159 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Section 4356r of Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's 1930 Edition , relating to county road over-
seers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That section 4356r of Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's 1930 Edition , be and the same is hereby
repealed and in lieu thereof that there by and is
hereby enacted the following :
The duties of overseers shall be as follows : In
counties where the roads are worked by hands
allotted as hereinbefore provided , the overseers
shall have charge of the roads and bridges in their
respective precincts . Said overseers shall construct
bridges , work the roads in the manner directed by
the fiscal court , shall summon the hands to work the
roads in their precincts , superintend and direct said

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 725
work , and keep the roads and bridges thereon free
of obstructions and in good condition for travel , and
may employ the necessary wagons , plows , scrapers ,
teams and such additional implements as may be
needed to work said roads , provided that no bridge
construction nor road work , nor labor , nor teams ,
nor tools , which shall require the expenditure of
money , shall be done or undertaken or employed by
said overseers unless previous written authority ,
authorizing them to do so and fixing the amount
they may expend for said purposes , shall first be
obtained from the fiscal court of the county wherein
the proposed bridge construction or road work is to
be done . The respective counties of the Common-
wealth shall not be liable for any indebtedness or
expenditures in excess of that authorized as afore-
said and shall not be liable for any indebtedness
contracted or created or agreed to by any overseer
when done in violation of this section .
Said overseers shall report to the nearest justice
of the peace or to the county judge any failure or
refusal of any of the hands on their roads to appear
and do good work in obedience to their summons ,
and the number and length of time of such failure ,
and they shall also report promptly to the county
judge any damage , injury or obstruction caused by
any one to said roads or bridges thereon . And
when the fiscal court has provided for paying hands
for working on the roads , it shall be the duty of the
overseers to report to the fiscal court the number
of hands and the time worked by each who have
worked on the roads of their respective precincts ,
and to furnish to each hand a certificate of the time
so worked by him .
§ 2. Whereas at present county road overseers
have the unlimited authority to create indebtedness
against the counties of this Commonwealth incident

726 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
to road work and bridge construction , and whereas
the fiscal courts of the counties of the state have no
discretion in paying the indebtedness so created ,
and whereas the past and the present operation of
the overseer system in the various counties of Ken-
tucky has created an unwarranted amount of
indebtedness against the counties of the state of
Kentucky , an emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this law shall become effective immediately
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
§ 3. All laws and parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Neither approved or disapproved .
CHAPTER 160 .
AN ACT enabling cities of the Second Class to acquire , con-
struct , improve , operate and maintain bridges across navigable
streams , and to acquire and operate ferries thereover , so as
to connect such cities with an adjoining State ; providing for
the creation of bridge commissions of such cities and pre-
scribing their powers and duties ; authorizing the issuance of
revenue bonds of such cities , payable solely from bridge and
ferry earnings , to pay the cost of such bridges and improve-
ments thereof and such ferries ; providing for the collection
of bridge and ferry tolls for the payment of such bonds and
for the cost of maintenance , operation and repair of the
bridges and ferries ; setting forth the conditions upon which
such bridges shall become free bridges ; providing that no
debt of such cities shall be incurred in the exercise of any of
the powers granted by this Act ; providing for condemation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. Issuance of revenue bonds for bridges .
Cities of the second class are hereby authorized and
empowered to acquire by purchase and to construct ,
or partly acquire and partly construct , and to im-
prove , operate and maintain bridges over navigable
streams so as to connect such cities with an adjoin-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 727
ing state , and , in order to pay the cost of such
acquisition and/or construction and of such im-
provements , to issue bridge revenue bonds of such
cities , as hereinafter provided .
§ 2. Definitions . As used in this Act , the fol-
lowing words and terms shall have the following
meanings ;
( a ) The word " bridge " shall be deemed to em-
brace a bridge in its entirety or the highway and
pedestrain portion of any existing bridge , and shall
include all property , rights , easements and
franchises relating thereto , including approaches .
(b) The word " improvements " shall mean such
repairs , replacements , additions and betterments of
and to a bridge acquired by purchase as are deemed
necessary to place it in a safe and efficient condition
for the use of the public , if such repairs , replace-
ments , additions and betterments are ordered prior
to the sale of any bonds for the acquisition of such
bridge .
(c) The term " cost of bridge " as applied to a
bridge to be acquired by purchase shall include the
purchase price , cost of improvements , financing
charges , interest during any period of disuse before
completion of improvements and for a period of six
months after such purchase or such completion , cost
of traffic estimates and of engineering and legal
expenses , plans , specifications and surveys , esti-
mates of cost and of revenues , other expenses nec-
essary or incident to determining the feasibility or
practicability of the enterprise , and such adminis-
trative and other expense as may be necessary or
incident to the financing herein authorized and the
acquisition of the bridge and the placing of the
bridge in operation .
(d) The term " cost of bridge " as applied to a
bridge to be constructed shall embrace the cost of

728 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
construction , the cost of all land, property rights ,
easements and franchises acquired which are
deemed necessary for such construction , financing
charges , interest during construction and for a
period of six months thereafter , cost of traffic esti-
mates and of engineering and legal expenses , plans ,
specifications , surveys, estimates of cost and of
revenues , other expenses necessary or incident to
determining the feasibility or practicability of the
enterprise , and such administrative and other ex-
pense as may be necessary or incident to the financ-
ing herein authorized and the construction of the
bridge and the placing of the bridge in operation .
( e ) The term " bridge commission " shall mean
the bridge commission hereinafter provided for of
any such city, or if such commission shall be
abolished , any board or officer succeeding to the
principal functions thereof or upon which or whom
the powers given by this Act to such commission
shall be given by law.
(f) The word " owner" shall include all individ-
uals , co -partnerships , associations or corporations
having any title or interest in any property , rights ,
easements or franchises authorized to be acquired
by this Act.
§ 3. Bridge commission . The Mayor or other
chief executive officer of such city , with the approval
of the legislative body of the city, may appoint four
persons who , with the Mayor or other chief execu-
tive officer , ex officio , shall constitute the bridge
commission of said city . Each appointee shall be
at least twenty-five years of age , and not more than
two of such appointees shall be members of the
same political party . No officer or employee of said
city , whether holding a paid or unpaid office , shall
be eligible to hold an appointment on said bridge
commission . Such appointees shall be originally

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 729
appointed for terms of four years . Upon the ex-
piration of such terms , appointments shall be made
in like manner except that the terms of the four
appointees shall be for one year , two years , three
years and four years , respectively , and the suc-
cessors of such appointees shall be appointed in like
manner , each for a period of four years . Any mem-
ber of the bridge commission shall be eligible for
reappointment . Vacancies shall be filled for any
unexpired term in the same manner as the original
appointment . Each appointee , before entering
upon the discharge of his duties , shall qualify by
taking , subscribing and filing an oath of office as
required by law, and by executing a bond for the
faithful performance of his duties , to be approved
by the legislative body of such city , in the penal sum
of $5,000 , which bond shall be filed with the other
official bonds of the city .
§ 4. Organization of commission . Upon the ap-
pointment and qualification of the members of the
bridge commission as above provided , they , together
with the Mayor or other chief executive officer , shall
constitute a body corporate under the name of ( here
insert name of city ) Bridge Commission and shall
have power to contract and be contracted with , to
sue and be sued in that name and to adopt a seal
and alter same at pleasure . Said bridge conimis-
sion shall elect a chairman and a vice chairman from
its members , and a secretary -treasurer who need
not be a member of such commission . The members
of the bridge commission , except the Mayor , shall
receive such compensation as may be fixed by the
legislative body of the city, either as a salary or as
payment for meetings attended , but the maximum
compensation of the chairman in any year shall not
exceed One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) and of
each other member shall not exceed Five Hundred

730 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Dollars ( $ 500.00 ) . The bridge commission shall fix
the compensation of the secretary -treasurer in its
discretion . The bridge commission shall have power
to establish by-laws , rules and regulations for its
own government and to make and enter into all con-
tracts or agreements necessary or incidental to the
performance of its duties and the execution of its
powers under this Act . The bridge commission may
employ engineering , architectural and construction
experts and inspectors and attorneys , and such other
employees as may be necessary in its opinion , and
fix their compensation , all of whom shall do such
work as the bridge commission shall direct . All
salaries and compensation shall be paid solely from
funds provided under the provisions of this Act ,
and no such bridge commission shall proceed to
exercise or carry out any authority or powers given
it to bind such bridge commission beyond the extent
to which money has been or may be provided under
the provisions of this Act .
§ 5. Commissioners not interested in contracts .
No person while in the employe , of , or holding any
official relation to any person , firm or corporation
selling or furnishing any materials entering into the
construction , improvement , repair or maintenance
of any such bridge , or who is pecunarily interested
therein , as a stockholder or otherwise , shall be 'a
member of any city bridge commission ; nor shall
any such bridge commission appoint hereunder any
person or persons as engineers , superintendents , or
foremen sustaining such relation to such person ,
firm or corporation ; nor shall such engineers ,
superintendents or foremen thereafter become in
any way connected with or interested in any such
person , firm or corporation while in the employe of
such bridge commission ; nor shall any of such com-
missioners , or any engineer , superintendent or fore-

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 731
man appointed by the bridge commission be a candi-
date for , or hold any public office other than that of
commissioner or be an employee under said bridge
commission or be a member of any political com-
mittee either while acting as such commissioner or
while holding employment thereunder . In case any
of such commissioners or servants or employees
shall be a candidate for or hold any other public
office , or shall be a member of any political com-
mittee , his office as commissioner or position as em-
ployee , as the case may be, shall be ipso facto
vacated .
6. Purchase of bridges . The bridge commis-
sion of any such city is hereby authorized and em-
powered to acquire by purchase any toll bridge over
a navigable stream so as to connect such city with
and adjoining state , or any such toll bridge wholly
or partly constructed and/or any franchises , ease-
ments , permits and/or contracts for the construction
of any such bridge , upon such terms and at such
prices as may be considered by such bridge commis-
sion to be reasonable and can be agreed upon be-
tween it and the owner thereof, title thereto to be
taken in the name of such city .
§ 7. Condemnation of Property . The bridge
commission of any such city , whenever a reasonable
price cannot be agreed upon, is hereby authorized
and empowered to acquire by condemnation any
land, rights , easements , franchises and other prop-
erty deemed necessary or convenient for the im-
provement and/or the efficient operation of any
property acquired or constructed under this Act , or
for the purpose of constructing any bridge or por-
tion thereof hereunder , or for securing right - of - way
leading to any such bridge or its approaches , in the
manner provided by law for the appropriation of
private property for similar or other public pur-

732 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
poses . Such bridge commission is and shall be
further empowered to exercise in this Common-
wealth and in any adjoining states such powers of
eminent domain as may be conferred upon such city
or such bridge commission by any act of Congress
of the United States now in force or which may
hereafter be enacted . Title to any property con-
demned by the bridge commission shall be taken in
the name of the city . The city shall be under no
obligation to accept and pay for any property con-
demned , or any costs incidental to any condemna-
tion proceedings , and shall in no event pay for the
same except from the funds received under the pro-
visions of this Act , and in any condemnation , pro-
ceedings the court having jurisdiction of the suit ,
action or proceeding , may make such orders as may
be just to the city and to the owners of the property
to be condemned , and may require an undertaking
or other security to secure such owners against any
loss or damage to be sustained by reason of the fail-
ure of the city to accept and pay for the property ,
but such undertaking or security shall impose no
liability upon the city, except such as may be paid
from the funds received under the provisions of this
Act .
§ 8. Improvement of bridges acquired . It shall
be the duty of the bridge commission , at or before
the time any such bridge shall be acquired by pur-
chase , to determine what repairs , replacements ,
additions or betterments , if any, will be necessary to
place the bridge in safe and efficient condition for
the use of the public , and to cause an estimate of
the cost of such improvements to be made and sub-
mitted to such bridge commission by an engineer or
engineers appointed by it . The bridge commission
shall order such improvements before the sale of

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 733
any bridge revenue bonds hereinafter authorized
for the acquisition of such bridge .
§ 9. Construction of bridges . The bridge com-
mission of any such city is hereby authorized and
empowered to construct any toll bridge over a
navigable stream , so as to connect such city with an
adjoining state . Any such bridge commission is
hereby authorized to purchase within this Common-
wealth and within any adjoining state , such lands ,
structures , rights of way , franchises , easements and
other interests in lands , including lands under water
and riprarian rights of any person , railroad or other
corporation or municipality or political sub-division ,
deemed necessary for the construction of any such
bridge, upon such terms and at such prices as may
be considered by it to be reasonable and can be
agreed upon between it and the owner thereof, title
thereto to be taken in the name of the city . No con-
tract or agreement with any contractor or contrac-
tors for the construction of any such bridge exceed .
ing in amount the sum of Twenty -five Hundred
Dollars ( $ 2,500.00 ) shall be made without advertise-
ment for bids , which bids shall be opened publicly
and an award made to the best bidder , with power
in the Commission to reject any or all bids .
§ 10. Removal of obstructions . All individuals
or corporations having buildings , structures , works ,
conduits , mains , pipes , tracks, or other physical
obstruction in , over or upon the public streets , lanes ,
alleys , highways or in , under or over the stream
over which any bridge is to be constructed , and
which shall interfere with or impede the progress of
said bridge when in progress of construction or im-
provement , shall upon reasonable notice from the
bridge commission promptly so shift , adjust ,
accomodate or remove the same at their own cost
and expense , except as otherwise provided or re-

734 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
quired by law, as fully to meet the exigencies occa-
sioning such action and the city legislative body
shall have full power by ordinance to prescribe the
penalty for such failure . Any publicways or public
works damaged or destroyed by reason of the build-
ing of such bridge or approaches shall be restored
or repaired by the bridge commission and placed in
their original condition as near as practicable .
§ 11. Bridge Revenue Bonds . The bridge com-
mission of any such city is hereby authorized to
provide by resolution for the issuance of bridge
revenue bonds of such city for the purpose of pay-
ing the cost as hereinabove defined of any such
bridge , which resolution shall recite an estimate of
such cost . The principal and interest of such bonds
shall be payable solely from the special fund herein
provided for such payment . Such bonds shall bear
interest at not more than six per centum per annum ,
payable semi -annually , shall mature at such time or
times not more than thirty years from their date or
dates as may be determined by such bridge commis-
sion , and may be made redeemable before maturity
at the option of the city, to be exercised by such
bridge commission , at ( nor ) not more than the par
value thereof and a premium of five per cent , and
under such terms and conditions as may be fixed by
such bridge commission prior to the issuance of the
bonds . The principal and interest of such bonds
may be made payable in any lawful medium . The
bridge commission shall determine the form of the
bonds , including the interest coupons to be attached
thereto , and shall fix the denomination or denomina-
tions of the bonds and the place or places of pay-
ment of principal and interest thereof , which may
be at any bank or trust company within or without
the Commonwealth . Such bonds shall be signed by
the chairman of the bridge commission and by the

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 735
Mayor or other Chief executive officer of the city,
under the City's seal , attested by the city clerk or
city recorder , and the coupons shall bear the fac-
cimile signatures of said chairman . All bonds
issued under this Act shall contain a statement on
their face that the city shall not be obligated to pay
the same or the interest thereon except from the
revenues of such bridge . In case any of the officers
whose signatures appear on the bonds or coupons
shall cease to be such officers before the delivery of
such bonds , such signatures shall nevertheless be
valid and sufficient for all purposes the same as if
they had remained in office until such delivery . All
such bonds shall be , and shall have and are hereby
declared to have all the qualities and incidents of
negotiable instruments under the negotiable instru-
ments law of the Commonwealth . Such bonds and
the income thereof shall be exempt from taxation by
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and by the munici-
palities and political subdivisions thereof . Such
bonds shall be lawful investments of banks , savings
banks , trust companies , trustees and of the commis-
sioners of the sinking funds of municipalities and
counties , and shall be acceptable as security for the
deposit of public moneys . Provision may be made
for the registration of any of the bonds in the name
of the owner as to principal alone the bridge com-
mission issuing such bonds may sell the bonds in
such manner and for such price as it may determine
to be for the best interests of the city, taking into
consideration the financial responsibility of the pur-
chaser and the terms and conditions of the purchase
and especially the availability of the proceeds of the
bonds when required for payment of the cost of the
bridge . Such bonds may be sold at private sale
without advertisement . The proceeds of such bonds
shall be used solely for the payment of the cost of

736 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
the bridge and shall be checked out by the chairman
or vice - chairman of the bridge commission and the
secretary -treasurer thereof and under such restric-
tions , if any , as such bridge commission may pro-
vide . If the proceeds of such bonds , by error of
calculation or otherwise , shall be less than the cost
of the bridge , additional bonds may in like manner
be issued to provide the amount of such deficit and ,
unless otherwise provided in the trust indenture
hereinafter mentioned , shall be deemed to be of the
same issue and shall be entitled to payment from ·
the same fund , without preference or priority of the
bonds first issued for the same bridge . If the pro-
ceeds of bonds issued for any bridge shall exceed the
cost thereof , the surplus shall be paid into the fund
hereinafter provided for the payment of principal
and interest of said bonds . Prior to the preparation
of definitive bonds , such bridge commisson may
under like restrictions issue temporary bonds with
or without coupons , exchangeable for definitive
bonds upon the issuance of the latter . Such revenue
bonds may be issued without a vote of the voters and
without any other proceedings or the happening of
any other condition or things than those proceed-
ings , conditions and things which are specified and
required by this Act or by the Constitution of the
Commonwealth .
§ 12. Lien Upon Bond Proceeds . All moneys re-
ceived from any bonds issued pursuant to this Act
shall be applied solely to the payment of the cost of
the bridge or to the appurtenant sinking fund and
there shall be and hereby is created and granted a
lien upon such moneys , until so applied , in favor of
holders of such bonds or the trustee hereafter pro-
vided for in respect of such bonds .
$ 13. Trust Indenture . In the discretion of the
bridge commission each or any issue of such bonds

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 737
may be secured by a trust indenture by and between
the city and a corporate trustee , which may be any
trust company or bank having the powers of a trust
company within or outside of the Commonwealth ,
or such corporate trustee and an individual or in-
dividuals as co -trustee or co -trustees , but no such
trust indenture shall convey or mortgage any bridge
or any part thereof . Such trust indenture may con-
tain such provisions for protecting and enforcing
the rights and remedies of the bondholders as may
be reasonable and proper , not in violation of law ,
including covenants setting forth the duties of the
bridge commission in relation to the acquisition ,
construction , improvement , maintenance , operation ,
repair and insurance of the bridge, and the custody ,
safe -guarding and application of all moneys . The
trust identure may also provide that the bridge
shall be acquired , constructed , or partly acquired
and partly constructed , and paid for under the
supervision and approval of consulting engineers
employed or designated by such bridge commission
and satisfactory to the original purchasers of the
bonds issued therefor , their successors , assigns or
nominees , who may be given the right to require
that the security given by contractors and by any
depository of the proceeds of the bonds or revenues
of the bridge or other moneys pertaining thereto
be satisfactory to such purchasers , successors ,
assigns or nominees . Such indenture may set forth
the rights and remedies of the bondholders and
trustee , restricting the individual right of action of
bondholders as is customary in trust indentures
securing bonds and debentures of corporations . Ex-
cept as in this Act otherwise provided , such bridge
commission may provide by resolution or by such
trust indenture for the payment of the proceeds of
the sale of the bonds and the revenues of the bridge
Acts- 24

738 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
to such officer , board or depository or depositories
as it may determine for the custody thereof , and
for the method of disbursement thereof , with such
safe -guards and restrictions as it may determine .
All expenses incurred in carrying out such trust
indenture may be treated as a part of the cost of
maintenance , operation and repairs of the bridge
affected by such indenture .
§ 14. Bridge Tolls . The bridge commission shall
fix, charge and collect tolls for transit over each
such bridge , and such tolls shall be so fixed and
adjusted , in respect of the aggregate of tolls from
cach bridge for which bonds are issued , as to pro-
vide a fund sufficient to pay such bonds and the
interest thereon , and to provide an additional fund
to pay the cost of maintaining , repairing and operat-
ing such bridge , subject , however , to any applicable
law or regulation of the United States of America
now in force and hereafter to be enacted or made .
The tolls from each bridge for which bonds are
issued , except such part thereof as may be necessary
to pay the cost of maintaining , repairing and operat-
ing during any period in which such cost is not
otherwise provided for ( during which period the
tolls may be reduced accordingly ) shall be set aside
each month in a sinking fund which is hereby
pledged to and charged with the payment of ( a ) the
interest upon such bonds as such interest shall fall
due and (b ) the necessary fiscal agency charges for
paying bonds and interest and ( c) the payment of
such bonds , such sinking fund to be a fund for all
such bonds without distinction or priority of one
over another . Prior to the issuance of the bonds
the bridge commission may provide by resolution
or by such trust indenture for using the sinking fund
or any part thereof in the purchase of any of the
outstanding bonds payable therefrom at the market

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 739
price thereof , but not exceeding the price, if any,
at which the same shall at the next interest date be
payable or redeemable , and all bonds redeemed or
purchased shall forthwith be cancelled and shall not
again be issued . The moneys in the sinking fund ,
if not used within a reasonable time for the purchase
of bonds for cancellation as above provided , shall
be applied to the redemption of bonds by lot at the
redemption price then applicable .
$ 15. Cessation of tolls . When the particular
bonds issued for any bridge and the interest thereon
shall have been paid or a sufficient amount shall
have been provided for their payment and shall con-
tinue to be held for that purpose , the bridge com-
mission shall cease to charge tolls for the use of
such bridge and thereafter such bridge shall be free
unless tolls shall be required for maintaining , re-
pairing and operating such bridge .
§ 16. Action by trustee and bondholders . Any
holder of any of such bonds or any of the coupons
attached thereto , and the trustee , if any , except to
the extent the rights herein given may be restricted
by resolution passed before the issuance of the bonds
or by the trust indenture , may, either at law or in
equity , by suit , action , mandamus , or other pro-
ceeding protect and enforce any and all rights
granted hereunder or under such resolution or trust
indenture and may enforce and compel performance
of all duties required by this Act or by such resolu-
tion or trust indenture to be performed by the city
issuing the bonds , or by the bridge commission or
any officer thereof, including the fixing , charging and
collecting of tolls for transit over such bridge .
§ 17. Contributions . The bridge commission of
any such city shall have power to enter into agree-
ments with any adjoining state or any municipality
or political subdivision thereof , whereby such ad-

740 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
joining state , municipality or political subdivision
may contribute either money or property to the cost
of the construction , acquisition or the operation and
maintenance of any bridge connecting such city
with such other state , municipality or political sub-
division . The bridge commission , in addition to the
revenues which may be received from the sale of
bridge revenue bonds and from the collection of
tolls and other bridge revenues derived under the
provisions of this Act , shall have authority to re-
ceive and accept contributions of either money or
property or other things of value , to be held , used
and applied for the purposes in this Act provided .
§ 18. Competing bridges and ferries . No bridge
for the use of the traveling public connecting this
Commonwealth with an adjoining state shall here-
after be constructed and operated by any county ,
municipality or political subdivision of the Com-
monwealth , or by any person , co -partnership asso-
ciation or corporation , within five miles of any toll
bridge for the acquisition or construction of which
bridge revenue bonds shall have been authorized
under this Act , and no franchise shall hereafter be
granted for the operation of a ferry within the same
limits , except under a written permit granted by the
State Highway Commission . No such permit shal !
be granted by the State Highway Commission until
it shall ascertain by an investigation , including a
hearing upon such notice and under such rules as
the State Highway Commission may prescribe , that
there is an urgent public need for the operation of
such bridge or such ferry, and that its operation
will not affect the revenues of any such toll bridge
so as to impair the security of any bridge revenue
bonds issued for the acquisition or construction of
such toll bridge .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 741
§ 19. Acquisition of ferries . The bridge com-
mission of any city authorizing bridge revenue
bonds under this Act is hereby authorized to pur-
chase all or any ferries , together with all necessary
lands , easements and appurtenances thereto , which
shall then be operating across the stream to be
spanned by the bridge to be acquired or constructed
and shall be within five miles of the location of such
bridge . In the event of such purchase , the pur-
chased price thereof shall be deemed apart of the
cost of the bridge and shall be paid solely from
funds received under the provisions of this Act .
The bridge commission may maintain and operate
any ferry or ferries so purchased and fix such rates
of toll for the use thereof as it may deem proper ,
or it may in its discretion sell or otherwise dispose
of any ferry so purchased , or it may abandon or
dismantle the same whenever in its judgment it may
seem expedient so to do . All tolls collected for the
use of the ferry or ferries and the proceeds of any
sale or disposition of any ferry or ferries shall be
used to pay the cost of maintaining , repairing and
operating the same , and any residue thereof shall
be paid into the sinking fund bereinabove provided
for the bonds .
§ 20. General powers of the commission . In
addition to the powers and duties enumerated in
this Act , such bridge commission shall have power
to do and perform any and all things and acts nec-
essary or convenient in the construction or acquisi-
tion , maintenance and operation of any bridge to be
constructed or acquired under the provisions of this
Act , and in the financing thereof , to the end that
such bridge may become and be operated free of
tolls as early as possible and practicable , subject
only to the express limitations of this Act and con-
stitutional provisions applicable thereto .

742 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 21. Dissolution of bridge commission . When
all such bonds of any such city and the interest
thereon shall have been paid or their payment fully
provided for, the bridge commission shall stand dis-
solved . Thereupon the legislative body of such city
shall assume the operation , maintenance and repair
of any such bridge , and , unless the cost of operation ,
maintenance and repair shall be otherwise provided
for , shall assume the collection of tolls for the pay-
ment of such cost , and shall do any and all other
acts and things that may be necessary and con-
venient for the maintenance of such bridge .
§ 22. Act provides alternative method . This
Act shall be deemed to provide an additional and
alternative method for the doing of the things
authorized hereby and shall be regarded as sup-
plementary and additional to powers conferred by
other laws .
§ 23. Constitutional construction . The provi-
sions of this Act are severable , and if any of its
provisions shall be held unconstitutional by a court
of competent jurisdiction the decision of such court
shall not affect or impair any of the remaining pro-
visions of this Act.
24. This Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor as pro-
vided by law .
Approved March 14, 1932 .
CHAPTER 161 .
AN ACT authorizing the State Highway Commission to improve
Capital Avenue in the city of Frankfort from the North side
of the intersection of Todd Street , and running north to the
South side of Second Street .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 743
That all that part of Capital Avenue in the City of
Frankfort , beginning at the North side of the inter-
section of Todd Street with the same , and running
north to the south side of Second Street in said
city , be improved by the State Highway Commis-
sion , the improvement to be paid out of the State
Road Fund ; the type and character of the improve-
ment to be determined by the State Highway Com-
mission.
Approved March 17 , 1932 .
CHAPTER 162 .
AN ACT to regulate traffic over and upon bridges and ferries
now or hereafter owned or operated by the Commonwealth
of Kentucky through its agent , The State Highway Commis-
sion of Kentucky ; making it unlawful for any person to injure ,
deface or damage any bridge or ferry , or any part thereof , so
owned and operated ; making it unlawful for any person ,
except those specifically exempted by this Act , to pass over
or upon any bridge or ferry so owned or operated without
paying the required toll ; vesting in collectors of tolls stationed
at or upon any bridge or ferry so owned or operated with the
powers of peace officers ; vesting in The State Highway Com-
mission of Kentucky power to make rules and regulations for
the enforcement of this Act , and , also , prescribing penalties
for the commission of offenses defined in this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to injure , deface or damage , or to attempt
to injure , deface or damage in any manner what-
soever , any bridge or ferry , or any part thereof ,
now or hereafter owned or operated by the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ; and any person or persons
so offending shall , upon conviction for each offense ,
be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor and shall be
fined not less than $ 10.00 nor more than $300.00 , or
confined in the county jail not less than ten days

744 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
nor more than six months , or both so fined and im-
prisoned in the discretion of the jury .
§ 2. That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons , except employees of The State Highway
Commission of Kentucky while engaged in the dis-
charge of their duties , and except as otherwise pro-
vided in this Act , to pass over or upon any bridge
or ferry now or hereafter owned or operated by The
State Highway Commission of Kentucky , upon
which bridge or ferry tolls are at such time regu-
larly collected , without paying to the collector of
tolls then stationed at such bridge or ferry at the
time of such passage , in legal tender , such amount
of toll as may be prescribed by rule or regulation of
The State Highway Commission of Kentucky , or by
law , for passage over such bridge or ferry , includ-
ing vehicular traffic over or upon any such bridge or
ferry ; and any person or persons so offending, upon
conviction of this offense shall be guilty of a misde-
meanor and shall be fined not less than $ 10.00 nor
more than $100.00 for each offense .
3. Nothing contained in Section 2 of this Act
shall be construed as requiring the personnel of the
Kentucky National Guard , while engaged in the dis-
charge of official business , to pay to the collector of
tolls stationed at any bridge or ferry within the pur-
view of this Act the tolls regularly collected at such
bridge or ferry , either for the passage of himself or
for the conveyance of the military property of the
State in his charge ; provided , however , that a mem-
ber of such personnel shall , at the time of passage
over or upon any such bridge or ferry , exhibit to
the collector of tolls , stationed thereat , a written
official order indicating that such person or persons
are at the time engaged upon official business , and
unless as so exhibited such passage shall be
unlawful .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 745
$ 4 . That for purposes of the enforcement of this
Act every person appointed or employed by The
State Highway Commission of Kentucky to be a col-
lector of tolls upon or at any bridge or ferry now or
hereafter owned or operated by the Commonwealth
of Kentucky , through its agent , The State Highway
Commission of Kentucky , shall possess and exercise
all of the powers now vested by law in peace officers
within this Commonwealth ; provided , however , that
the powers herein conferred upon collectors of tolls
are expressly limited to the arrest and prosecution
of persons committing public offenses upon the high-
ways , bridges and ferries so owned or operated , and
the approaches thereto , within this Commonwealth ,
and all things incident thereto .
§ 5. That every person so appointed by The
State Highway Commission . of Kentucky to be a
collector of tolls upon any bridge or ferry so owned
or operated with this Commonwealth shall , before
entering upon the discharge of his duties , execute
bond to the Commonwealth with good and sufficient
surety , conditioned for the faithful performance of
his duties as such collector of tolls , and shall take
and subscribe the constitutional oath of office . Said
bond and said oath so subscribed by every collector
of tolls shall be executed either in the County Court
of the County in which such collector resides , or in
the office of the The State Highway Commission of
Kentucky , and shall be approved by said commission
or the Chairman thereof , the originals or certified
copies thereof , as the case may be , shall be duly filed
and entered of record in the office of the The State
Highway Commission of Kentucky . All such col-
lectors of toll shall be subject to all the penalties ,
liabilities and obligations now imposed by law on
peace officers .

746 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
§ 6. For the further purpose of enforcing this
Act, The State Highway Commission of Kentucky
is hereby vested with power and authority to make
and promulgate such rules and regulations , not in-
consistent with the Constitution and laws of this
Commonwealth , as may be necessary to effectuate
the purpose of this Act.
§ 7. All laws or parts of laws conflicting here-
with are hereby repealed .
§ 8. Whereas , considerable difficulty has arisen
in connection with the maintenance and operation
of bridges and ferries now owned or operated by the
Commonwealth through its agent , The State High-
way Commission of Kentucky , resulting in conflicts
between collectors of tolls stationed thereat and
numerous persons seeking passage upon such
bridges and ferries , and
Whereas, the need is urgent and necessary for
immediate relief for the protection of the Commis-
sion , its agents , servants and employees , of the
maintenance and operation of such bridges and fer-
ries and in the collection of tolls thereon .
Therefore , an emergency is hereby declared to
exist and this Act shall take effect immediately upon
and after its passage by the General Assembly and
approval by the Governor .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 163 ,
AN ACT relating to roads and bridges and establishing as a
part of the State Primary Highway System a road leading
from Smithland , Livingston County , Kentucky , to Madison-
ville , Kentucky , to be known hereafter , as the Ruby Laffeon

sembly of Kentucky ; 1928 , bearing upon road proj-
ect 433 , as designated in Kentucky Statutes , con-
cerning the points by which said road shall pass , be

man , Kentucky , and running by the City Cemetery

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 807
of Hickman , Kentucky , thence in a southeasterly

southeasterly direction to the N. C. & St. L. Railroad
at a point near the Dresden Crossing ; thence south

where the said road connects with Union City High-
way as now surveyed . The entire length of projects
approximately seven miles , be and same is hereby
established as a part of Primary System of State
Highways of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to be
known as State Highway Project No.
The construction, maintenance , supervision and
control of said road shall be subject to all the terms
and conditions set out and provided for in the act
establishing and creating a primary system of state
highways in the Commonwealth of Kentucky . And
the said project shall be entitled to and receive all
benefits of such laws as are now, or may hereafter
be in effect with relation to the primary system of
state highways of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .
Whereas , said project as enacted by Chapter 398
and established thereby has been surveyed by the
state highway commission , properly locating said
road as set out in this act as amended , and whereas
bids have been received for the grade and drainage
of same , but being held up because said road as
surveyed does not comply with the requirements of
act 398 , and whereas it will be necessary to pass
upon said bill and award said contract within the
next few weeks , and whereas the present restrictions
in the law will prevent the state highway commis-
sion from proceeding with said contract , which

appears to be advantageous to the state highway
department therefore an emergency is declared to
exist and this act shall become effective upon its

CHAPTER 516 .
RESOLUTION authorizing A. J. Bush to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or either .
Whereas , on the 2nd day of February , 1924 , A. J. Bush, of
Elizabethtown , Kentucky , who was then employed by the State
Highway Commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , whilst
in the performance of his duties as such employee , was injured
by a five -ton truck turning over on him , said truck being the
property of the State of Kentucky and being used and operated
at the time by the employees of the State Highway Commission
in the performance of their duties , and

966 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Whereas , his hip bone was broken and driven through the
hip joint , and he was otherwise seriously , painfully and perma-
Lently injured , disabling and incapacitating him from manual
labor for the remainder of his life , and
Whereas , said A. J. Bush is now 64 years of age and is
unable because of said injuries , to do manual labor , and was at
the time of said injuries earning $4.00 per day , and was required
to expend the sum of $ 1,200.00 for medical attention , hospital
bills , etc. , and
Whereas, the said A. J. Bush's eyes were effected by his
said injuries and he is now practically blind by reason thereof,
and
Whereas , he has sustained these serious and permanent
injuries by reason of the alleged carelessness and negligence
of the employees of the State Highway Commission in the con-
duct of the States business , therefore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That A. J. Bush be , and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission, or either ,
in the Circuit Court of the county of his residence
for such damages as he may have suffered , if any ,
by reason of any injury received by him through the
carelessness or negligence of the State Highway
Commission, its agents , or employees . Said suit
shall be for any amount , not exceeding the sum of
Ten Thousand Dollars , and in the event any judg-
ment is recovered by said Bush in said suit for
injuries to himself, or same is compromised or
settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public
Accounts by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer ,
and paid out of the General Fund .
§ 2. Either party to said suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be entered therein as in
any other civil suit , and the case may be settled and
adjusted with the consent and approval of the Attor-
ney General of Kentucky in the same way as any
other civil case, Neither approved nor disapproved .

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 967
CHAPTER 517 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION of the Senate of Kentucky and
the House of Representatives of the General Assembly,
memorializing the Congress of the United States to make
adequate appropriation and appoint a Commission for the
purpose of fittingly celebrating the sesqui -centennial of the
Siege of Bryan Station and the Battle of Blue Licks , " The
Last Battle of the Revolution " , in August, one thousand nine
hundred and thirty -two.
Whereas, great preparation have been made by the Congress
of the United States and the States of the Union to celebrate ,
throughout this year of one thousand nine hundred and thirty-
two, the Two Hundredth Anniversary of the Birth of George
Washington , the Father of Our Country, and
Whereas , the major events of the War for Independence ,
notably the Siege of Yorktown and the Surrender of Cornwallis ,
have been re -enacted in a glorious and inspiring manner under
the direction and by appropriation of the Congress of the United
States , and
Whereas , the George Washington Bicentennial year is also
the sesqui -centennial anniversary of the Siege of Bryan Station
and the Battle of Blue Licks , "The Last Battle of the Revolu-
tion", where every American officer in the action was killed ,
with the exception of the dauntless Colonel Daniel Boone ; there-
fore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring :
That the Congress of the United States be
memorialized to appropriate adequate funds and
appoint a Commission for the celebration in August ,
one thousand nine hundred and thirty -two , of the
Siege of Bryan Station and the Battle of Blue Licks
on a scale commensurate with the importance of the
occasion and as a fitting tribute to the patriotism
and bravery of the Kentuckians who laid down their
lives, in these two memoriable conflicts , for the
cause of Freedom and Independence ;
Be it further resolved , That the Chief Clerk of the
Senate forward one copy each of this resolution to
the President of the United States , to the President

968 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
of the Senate and the Speaker of the House of Rep-
resentatives of the United States and the Senators
and Representatives from Kentucky in the United
States Congress .
Approved February 2, 1932 .
CHAPTER 518 .
Whereas , The printers of North America , through the Inter-
national Typographical Union , have established at Colorado
Springs, Colorado , a tubercular sanitorium and home for the
sick and aged of this craft , which , from a small beginning in
one thousand eight hundred and ninety-two, now comprises two
hundred and fifty acres , with a total property value of more
than three million dollars ; and
Whereas , The Printers ' Home now cares for more than
three hundred residents continuously , expending more than
three hundred thousand dollars yearly for the care of their
sick and infirm , returning many stricken printers to their homes
rehabilitated , to live long and useful lives in their community ;
and
Whereas , The International Typographical Union at its
recent convention held in Boston , Massachusetts , adopted a
resolution which places itself squarely as desiring an auxiliary
Home in the East to be nearer the greater number of its mem-
bers , as is shown by these words : ". . . but goes on record as
being desirous of establishing an eastern sanitorium similar to
the one at Colorado Springs . . . ." ;
And Whereas , many sections of Kentucky are unequaled by
any State in the nation for the treatment and cure of tubercu-
losis , and where the scenic beauty and temporate climate are
such as to surround the aged with peace and contentment in
their declining years ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the House and Senate
concurring :
§ 1. That the International Typographical Union
be invited to establish its Eastern Printers ' Home
in the State of Kentucky .
§ 2. That the Governor of Kentucky extend this
invitation to the International Typographical Union

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 969
(Charles P. Howard , President , and Woodruff
Randolph , Secretary -Treasurer ) whose headquar-
ters are at Indianapolis , Indiana .
Approved February 2, 1932 .
CHAPTER 519 .
JOINT RESOLUTION Directing the Governor to Proclaim
October eleventh of each year "General Pulaski's Memorial
Day" for the observance and commemoration of the death of
Brigadier General Casimir Pulaski ;
Whereas , the eleventh day of October , one thousand seven
hundred and seventy -nine , is the date in American history of
the heoric death of Brigadier General Casimir Pulaski , who died
from wounds received on October ninth , one thousand seven
hundred seventy -nine , at the seige of Savannah , Georgia ; and
Whereas , the States of Indiana , Wisconsin , Michigan , Ohio ,
Arkansas , Missouri , West Virginia , Tennessee , Texas , Delaware ,
Fennsylvania , New York , Minnesota , Maryland , Illinois , New
Hampshire , Nebraska, and other States of the Union have by
legislative enactment designed October eleven of each year to
be " General Pulaski's Memorial Day" ; and
Whereas , it is fitting that the recurring anniversary of this
day be commemorated with suitable partiotic and public exer-
cises in observing and commemorating the death of this great
American hero of the Revolutionary war ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
State of Kentucky :
That the Governor of the State of Kentucky is
authorized and directed to issue a proclamation
calling upon officials of the Government to display
the flag of the United States on all governmental
buildings on October eleventh of each year and in-
viting the people of the State of Kentucky to
observe the day in schools and churches , or other
suitable places , with appropriate ceremonies in
commemoration of the death of General Casimir
Pulaski .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

970 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 520 .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , now in regular session :
One . That it is the sense of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that there exists and has existed
for several years of the immediate past the necessity of making
adequate survey of sections of the Cumberland River lying
within and immediately near to the corporate limits of the
cities of Harlan , Middlesboro , Pineville and Barbourville , in
Harlan , Bell and Knox Countes , Kentucky , and certain affected
portions of said cities and certain of the outlying contiguous
territory , all constantly menaced by and subject to damage
caused by overflows and inundations by the waters of said river
in times of tides , and that, in order to prevent such overflows
and inundations of said river and Yellow Creek, to thereby save
the people and their property in the said cities and those of
said outlying contiguous sections of unbearable damage con-
sequent upon said water inundations , and that there should be
appropriated by the Congress of the United States a sufficient
sum of money out of the Treasury of the United States to
remedy said deplorable situation , and
Two . Whereas , portions of said cities are situate on both
sides of said Cumberland River and Yellow Creek , at or near
to a worked and acute bend in the river and creeks , thereby
causing the river and creeks , in times of its tides , to overflow
the banks and inundate sections of said cities and immediately
outlying sections and causing the damage aforesaid . These
affected sections embrace the homes of many families and the
business enterprises and farm lands and homes of many of
the cities who are and always have been loyal to the government
of the United States of America . They are industrious but
poor in a financial sense and by their efforts and contributions
along with others of said cities they have erected splendid
schools and churches within said cities . Many of these people
and their wives and children have often been driven from their
homes by said tides to seek temporary places to stay until the
water subsided , when they return to their homes and find them
filled with mud and other refuse caused by the revages of the
river and creeks .
And , Three . Whereas , the hills and valleys lying and slop-
ing from the mountains of Tennessee and within the river
watershed above said cities have been , in the recent past ,
largely denuded of the forests and that of itself has , it is
believed , contributed to the frequency of the tides and increased

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 971
volume thereof. So that the affected sections and people men-
tioned are in well -nigh constant dread of the devastating angry
waters . The people of the said sections are absolutely unable
to financially and otherwise relieve the situation and relief of
same is a public necessity .
Four . Now , Therefore ,
Be it further resolved :
That the Kentucky Senators and Representatives
in Congress be and they are hereby requested to
exercise their utmost endeavors in securing from
our Federal Government the enactment of a statute
directing an early survey of the premises aforesaid
and the appropriation of a sufficient sum of money
to remedy the situation aforesaid by having con-
structed such canal or spillways at the proper place
near said cities , necessary to divert sufficient por-
tions of the waters of the Cumberland River and
Yellow Creek, in times of tides , to prevent the
damaging over- flows and inundations aforesaid .
Approved February 4, 1932 .

CHAPTER 522 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION ratifying the proposed amend-
ment to the Constitution of the United States of America .
Whereas : The seventy -second Congress of the United States
of America , at the first session , begun and held at the city of
Washington on Monday , the seventh day of December , 1931 A.
D. , adopt a joint Resolution proposing the following amendment
to the Constitution of the United States , a copy of which signed
by the Speaker of the House of Representatives and the Vice
President of the United States , and duly certified by the Gov-
ernor of this Commonwealth , and which is in words and figures
as follows , to -wit :
Joint Resolution proposing an amendment to the Constitu-
tion of the United States .
Resolved by the Senate and House of Representatives of
the United States of America in Congress assembled (two -thirds
of each House concurring therein ) , that the following amend-
ment to the Constitution be , and hereby is , proposed to the
States , to become valid as a part of said Constitution when
ratified by the legislatures of the several states as provided in
the Constitution :
Article , Section 1. The terms of the President and Vice
President shall end at noon on the twentieth day of January ,
and the terms of Senators and Representatives at noon on the
third day of January , of the years in which such terms would
Lave ended if this article had not been ratified ; and the terms
of their successors shall then begin .
2. The Congress shall assemble at least once in every year ,
and such meeting shall begin at noon on the third day of
January , unless they shall by law appoint a different day .
3. If, at the time fixed for the beginning of the term of
the President , the President elect shall have died , the Vice
President elect shall become President . If the President shall not
have been chosen before the time fixed for the beginning of
his term , or if the President elect shall have failed to qualify
then the Vice President elect shall act as President until a

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 973
President shall have qualified , declaring who shall then act as
President , or the manner in which one who is to act shall
be selected , and such person shall act accordingly until a
President or Vice President shall have qualified .
4. The Congress may by law provide for the case of the
death of any of the persons from whom the House of Representa-
tives may choose a President whenever the right of choice shall
have devolved upon them , and for the case of the death of any
of the persons from whom the Senate may choose a Vice Presi-
dent whenever the right of choice shall have devolved upon
them .
5. Sections 1 and 2 shall take effect on the fifteenth day
of October following the ratification of this Article .
6. This article shall be inoperative unless it shall have
been ratified as an amendment to the Constitution by the legis-
latures of three -fourths of the several States within seven years
from the date of its submission . Now , therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the foregoing and above -recited amendment
to the Constitution of the United States be and the
same is hereby ratified by the said General Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as a part of
and amendment to the Constitution of the United
States .
That the Governor of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky forwith forward to the Secretary of State of
the United States an authenticated copy of the
foregoing joint Resolution .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 523 .
RESOLUTION for help for enrolling clerks for Senate and
House .
Whereas , it will necessitate the employment of additional
aid by the enrolling clerks of the House and Senate in order
to expedite the business , now therefore :

974 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One . That the enrolling clerks of the House
and Senate are authorized and directed to employ
all necessary assistance who shall be entitled to
thirty-five cents per page for all bills enrolled by
them and the clerks , House and Senate , are directed
to issue their vouchers to the Auditor of Public
Accounts , who shall draw his warrant in their favor
for amounts certified by the respective clerks on
itemized accounts rendered . Said amounts shall
not include any charge by either the enrolling clerks
of the House or Senate for any work done by them .
Two . Provided , however , that not more than
Four hundred Dollars shall be expended for this
purpose in either house .
Three . It being necessary to have the work
herein provided for done at once, and the officers
charged with the duty are not able to perform the
volume of work personally , an emergency is de-
clared and this resolution becomes in force and
effect upon its adoption .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 524 .
Whereas , it has long been the custom for State Legisla-
tures to invite distinguished people to visit them and deliver
an address before the joint session , and ,
Whereas , the Honorable Franklin D. Roosevelt , Governor of
the Empire State , has been endorsed by his people for his
fidelity to their interest , as such official , and has taken his
place in the front rank of American Statesmen and Patriots ,
therefore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate , the House of Rrepre-
sentatives concurring therein ,
That Governor Roosevelt , Ex-Governor Byrd , of
Virginia , Newton D. Baker and Ex-Governor Al

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 975
Smith , of New York , be invited to pay a vist to
our Capitol , and deliver an address to the joint,
session , assembled for that purpose , during the
present session of the General Assembly at such
time as may be convenient to him .
Approved February 1, 1932 .
CHAPTER 525 .
Whereas , The William Howard Taft Memorial Highway
Association has launched a movement to have designated and
numbered a highway from Machinaw City , Michigan to Fort
Myers , Florida , as a memorial of the late William Howard Taft ,
and
Whereas , The said Memorial Highway has been designated
through many of the large centers of population of the north
leading from Cincinnati through Frankfort , Lawrenceburg ,
Harrodsburg , Danville , Liberty , Jamestown , and Albany , Ken-
tucky , and
Whereas , A Federal program of highway improvements as
an emergency aid to unemployment has been proposed , and
Whereas , The William Howard Taft Memorial Highway is
an important north -and -south highway providing a shorter and
more direct route through the central section of these United
States and binding the northern and southern communities
closer together for commercial exchange and tourist travel ,
therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That we memorialize Congress through our Ken-
tucky delegation to provide , as an emergency ap-
propriation for the unemployed , sufficient funds
without State aid , for the completion of unfinished
links and for straightening , widening and surfacing
of this memorial Highway as a purely Federal
Highway project .
Be it further resolved :
That the State Highway Commission be requested
to furnish to the William Howard Taft Highway

976 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Association , through its President , George A.
Stauffer , of Ottawa , Ohio , an estimate of the prob
able cost of completing , straightening , widening and
surfacing of said Memorial Highway as above
designated through Kentucky , and the copies of
this resolution be sent to the William Howard Taft
Highway Association and the members of the Ken-
tucky delegation in Congress by the Secretary of
State .
Approved February 6, 1932 .
CHAPTER 526 .
RESOLUTION providing for the printing of fifty copies of the
Governor's message for the use and information of the Senate
and House of Representatives .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That fifty copies of the message of the Governor
of this Commonwealth to the General Assembly of
1932 be printed for the use of each member of the
Senate and House of Representatives . The same are
to be prepared for mailing .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 527.-
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Whereas , the Statutes of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky requires any person or persons who pro-
mote or oppose legislation and acting in the capacity
as lobbyist to register in the office of the Attorney
General , giving their names and addresses , and the
company they represent, and
Whereas , there are persons now engaged in
lobbying in favor of and against legislation now
1

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 977
rending before the General Assembly of Kentucky ,
and
Whereas , the members composing the General
Assembly come from all parts of the State and are
unacquainted with such persons and are unable to
judge of the sincerity of their motives and pur-
poses without knowing who they are and what they
represent , and
Whereas , it is not convenient for the members of
the General Assembly to visit the Attorney Gen-
erals office and examine said register from time to
time without great loss of time ,
Therefore , Be it resolved :
That the Attorney General of this State be
requested to furnish a list on Tuesday, February
second , nineteen hundred and thirty -two , and on
each Monday thereafter of all registered lobbyists ,
giving their names , residence and the company that
they represent , and that he be requested to furnish
on each Monday thereafter, as long as this legisla-
ture is in session , such additional lobbyists with
their names , addresses and company that they
represent to the House and Senate so they may be
informed as to who are lobbyist and what company
they represent . That the clerk be requested to have
printed and placed on the desk of each member of
the House and Senate a list of all said lobbyist so
furnished , giving their names , addresses and the
companies that they represent .
Approved .
CHAPTER 528 .
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of copies of the Legis-
lative Digest for the members of the General Assembly and
State and other public officials .

978 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
Whereas , it is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Senator and Representative be advised as to the various
and detailed steps of legislation , and
Whereas , this General Assembly for the past twelve sessions
has approved and adopted as its official publication The Legis-
lative Digest , a publication the purpose of which is to fully
advise from day to day the officials and public of the Common-
wealth as to matters affecting them in an intelligent and con-
cise manner , therefore ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring therein :
That the publishers of the Legislative Digest are
hereby directed to furnish daily a copy to each
member and the clerks of the General Assembly ,
and to the heads of each State Department during
the time of this session . That the publishers are
allowed the sum of ten dollars for each subscription
for the session and the Auditor of Public Accounts
is directed to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer
for said amount when such service has been con-
cluded . The total number shall not exceed two hun-
dred copies , which are to be paid for under this
resolution .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 529 .
JOINT RESOLUTION of the Senate and the House of Repre-
sentatives of the General Assembly of Kentucky to meet in
Joint Session at eleven o'clock a . m . on February fourth ,
1932 , to pay a tribute to George Washington , signer of the
Act of Congress admitting Kentucky to Statehood , and to
signalize the formal opening of Kentucky's participation in
the Nation -wide George Washington Bicentennial Celebration .
Whereas , all of the States of the Union are planning a
series of celebrations this year in honor of the Two Hundredth
Anniversary of the Birth of George Washington , and
Whereas , President George Washington on February fourth ,
Seventeen Hundred and ninety-one , signed the Act of Congress .
admitting Kentucky to Statehood and thus extended to Ken-
tucky the distinct honor of being the first State authorized to

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 979
join the Thirteen Colonies after the formation of the United
States , and
Whereas , the Congress of the United States , through the
United States Commission and the Kentucky Committee for
the George Washington Bi -centennial , has presented to Ken-
tucky as a memento of this historic event a bust of George
Washington ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky :
That the Senate and the House of Representatives
meet in Joint Session at eleven o'clock a . m . on
February fourth , to witness the presentation of the
bust of George Washington by Congressman Virgil
Chapman , on behalf of the Congress of the United
States , to Governor Ruby Laffoon , for the State of
Kentucky , and that this hour and occasion signalize
the formal opening of the George Washington
Bicentennial Celebration in Kentucky .
Approved February 4, 1932 .
CHAPTER 530 .
Whereas , The Printers of North America , through the
International Typographical Union , have established at Colorado
Springs , Colorado , a tubercular sanitorium and home for the
sick and aged of this craft, which , from a small beginning in
Eighteen hundred and ninety -two, now comprises two hundred
and fifty acres , with a total property value of more than three
million dollars ; and
Whereas, The Printers ' Home now cares for more than
three hundred residents continuously , expending more than
three hundred thousand dollars yearly for the care of their sick
and infirm returning many stricken printers to their homes
rehabilitated , to live long and useful lives in their community ;
and
Whereas , The International Typographical Union at its
recent Convention held in Boston , Massachusetts , adopted a
resolution which places itself squarely as desiring an auxiliary
Home in the East to be nearer the greater number of its mem-
bers , as is shown by these words : ". but goes on record

980 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
as being desirous of establishing an eastern sanitorium similar
to the one at Colorado Springs. ." ; and
Whereas , many sections of Kentucky are unequaled by any
State in the Nation for the treatment and cure of tuberculosis ,
and where the scenic beauty and temporate climate are such
as to surround the aged with peace and contentment in their
declining years ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , the House and Senate
concurring :
1. That the International Typographical Union
be invited to Establish its Eastern Printers Home
in the State of Kentucky .
2. That the Governor of Kentucky extend this
invitation of the International Typographical
Union (Charles P. Howard , President and Wood-
ruff Randolph , Secretary-Treasurer ) , whose head-
quarters are at Indianapolis , Indiana .
Approved .
CHAPTER 531 .
JOINT AND CONCURRENT RESOLUTION by the two houses
of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Whereas , among the reasons for the selection of the city
of Frankfort as the location of the Capitol of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , was the natural beauty of its setting, its
surrounding hills , the beauty of the country adjacent thereto ,
and the winding of the Kentucky river through it , and
Whereas , tourist and strangers are largely attracted thereby
and caused to visit the Capitol city , and
Whereas , certain commercial interests have recently en-
gaged , for commercial purposes only , in the defacing of the
natural surface of the hills by the operation of rock quarries ,
so as to mar and destroy the natural beauty thereof, and
Whereas , such operations , unless soon abandoned , will
render the perspective of such natural surrounding unpleasant
and distasteful , as well as destructive of all ornamental effect,
and
Whereas , the condition of the public treasury of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky at this time forbids the appropriation

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 981
of sufficient funds to prevent and subvert such vandalism , but
which should be done if conditions were different , and
Whereas , for the purpose of calling attention to such
destruction and expressing condemnation thereof, Now , There-
fore,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Kentucky State Park Commission , or
any State agency that might hereafter succeed it in
the performance of its functions , is hereby urged , as
soon as it comes into possession of sufficient funds
by public donation or private subscription to take
such steps as it may be authorized under the law , to
acquire the necessary property around and encir-
cling the city of Frankfort , for the purpose of dis-
continuing and forever preventing such destruction ,
and for the purpose of preserving such natural
scenery unmolested , untouched and unharmed .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 532 .
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Congress of the United States be , and
the same is hereby memorialized to pass legislation
at its present session , which will provide for pension
or compensation to be paid to widows and orphans
of all deceased ex-service men , who may apply
therefor and show that they do not possess inde-
pendent resources sufficient to provide proper living
conditions for themselves and their dependents , re-
gardless as to whether or not the death of said
ex- service man was directly traceable to disability
caused by his service .
Approved February 6, 1932 .

CHAPTER 533 .

burg , and authorizing the State Highway Commission of
Kentucky to ascertain the cost of construction of Federal

to the City of Hardinsburg .
Whereas , The State Highway Commission has constructed

Whereas , the City Council of said city , without electing to
act under and in pursuance of the terms and provisions of
Chapter 178 of the Acts of 1928 , has constructed or caused to
be constructed , by an ordinance duly enacted after the con-
struction of Federal Highway No. 60 , a street running through
said city, upon the ten-year bond plan , as provided by Ken-
tucky Statutes , Section 3706 , of a type of construction different
from the type theretofore constructed by the Highway Com-
mission up to the corporate limits on either side of said city ,
said street being eighty -four one hundredths ( .84 ) miles in
length and described as follows : Depot Street from easterly
city limits to Main Street ; Main Street from Depot Street to
Owensboro Avenue ; Owensboro Avenue from Main Street to
westerly city limits , and
Whereas , the City of Hardinsburg now desires to avail
itself of the provisions of Chapter 178 of the Acts of 1928 ; now ,
therefore ,
Be it enacted by the Senate ( the House of Repre-
sentatives concurring ) :
That the Commonwealth of Kentucky , by and
through its agent , The State Highway Commission
of Kentucky , be and it hereby is authorized to ascer-
tain the actual cost of the construction of Federal
Highway No. 60 over the route within the corporate
limits of the City of Hardinsburg hereinabove de-
scribed , and after having done so , The State High-
way Commission of Kentucky be and it hereby is
authorized to enter into a contract with said city
wherein and whereby the State of Kentucky , by and
through its agent , The State Highway Commission

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 983
of Kentucky , as party of the first part , binds itself
and agrees to pay to the City of Hardinsburg , as
party of the second part , such sum as The State
Highway Commission may , in its discretion , de-
termine to be the actual cost of construction through
the city for eighty -four hundredths ( .84 ) miles of
the same type of road already constructed by said
Commission on Federal Highway No. 60 outside of
the city limits of said city , and providing that the
City of Hardinsburg shall pay or assume the cost
of the additional cost of construction by reason of
the higher type of road within the limits of said city ,
and the Auditor of Public Accounts , upon the due
execution of said contract and the filing of same in
the office of the Secretary of State and upon presen-
tation to the said Auditor of a certified copy thereof,
be and he hereby is authorized to draw his warrant
upon the State Treasurer payable to the order of
the City of Hardinsburg for such sum as may be
agreed upon, as provided in said contract and as

Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 535 .
RESOLUTION for the benefit of Owsley County .
Whereas : The County of Owsley in the month of January ,
1929 , had the misfortune to lose its court house by fire , together
with all records , including its Law Library .
Whereas : Said county has since that date built a new
court house at great expense , but being a small county and
heavily in debt are unable to pay the cost of replacing its
records or Law Library , which are necessary for the use of the
court of the county and district ;
Be it therefore resolved by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the State Librarian be directed and author-
ized to furnish to said county free of cost a complete
set of decisions of the Court of Appeals of Kentucky ,
as compiled in the Kentucky Report and Decisions .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

988 COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
CHAPTER 539 .
CONCURRENT RESOLUTION of the Senate of Kentucky and
the House of Representatives of the General Assembly .
memorializing the Congress of the United States to make
adequate appropriation and appoint a Commission for the
purpose of fittingly celebrating the sesqui -centennial of the
Siege of Bryan Station and the Battle of Blue Licks , "The
Last Battle of the Revolution " , in August , seventeen hundred
and thirty -two. Whereas great preparations have been made
by the Congress of the United States and the States of the
Union to celebrate , throughout this year of Nineteen hundred
and Thirty -two , the Two Hundredth Anniversary of the Birth
of George Washington , the Father of Our Country, and
Whereas , the major events of the War for Independence ,
notably the Siege of Yorktown and the Surrender of Cornwallis ,
have been re -enacted in a glorious and inspiring manner under
the direction and by appropriation of the Congress of the United
States , and
Whereas , the George Washington Bicentennial year is also
the sesqui -centennial anniversary of the Siege of Bryan Station
and the battle of Blue Licks , "The Last Battle of the Revolu-
tion ," where every American officer in the action was killed ,
with the exception of the dauntless Colonel Daniel Boone ; there-
fore,
Be it resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring :
That the Congress of the United States be
memorialized to appropriate adequate funds and
appoint a Commission for the celebration in August ,
Nineteen hundred and Thirty -two , of the Siege of
Bryan Station and the Battle of Blue Licks on a
scale commensurate with the importance of the
occasion and as a fitting tribute to the patriotism
and bravery of the Kentuckians who laid down
their lives in these two memorable conflicts , for the
cause of Freedom and Independence ;
Be it further resolved :
That the Chief Clerk of the Senate forward one
copy each of this resolution to the President of the
1

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY 989
United States , to the President of the Senate and
the Speaker of the House of Representatives of the
United States and to the Senators and Representa-
tives from Kentucky in the United States Congress .
Approved February 6, 1932 .

ence of the society , it may be so solemnized .
